Genesis1:1 In the beginning God created the heavens and the earth.
Genesis1:2 The earth was formless and void
Genesis1:3 God said
Genesis1:4 God saw that the light was good
Genesis1:5 God called the light Day
Genesis1:6 Then God said
Genesis1:7 So God made the expanse and separated the waters which were under the expanse from the waters which were above the expanse. And it was so.
Genesis1:8 God called the expanse Heaven. So the evening and the morning were the second day.
Genesis1:9 Then God said
Genesis1:10 God called the dry land Earth
Genesis1:11 Then God said
Genesis1:12 The earth produced vegetation
Genesis1:13 So the evening and the morning were the third day.
Genesis1:14 And God said
Genesis1:15 Let them be lights in the expanse of the heavens to give light on the earth.” And it was so.
Genesis1:16 God made two great lights: the greater light to rule the day and the lesser light to rule the night. He made the stars also.
Genesis1:17 Then God set them in the expanse of the heavens to give light on the earth
Genesis1:18 to rule over the day and over the night
Genesis1:19 So the evening and the morning were the fourth day.
Genesis1:20 Then God said
Genesis1:21 So God created great sea creatures and every living thing that moves
Genesis1:22 Then God blessed them
Genesis1:23 So the evening and the morning were the fifth day.
Genesis1:24 Then God said
Genesis1:25 So God made the beasts of the earth according to their kind
Genesis1:26 Then God said
Genesis1:27 So God created man in His own image; in the image of God He created him; male and female He created them.
Genesis1:28 God blessed them and said to them
Genesis1:29 And God said
Genesis1:30 To every beast of the earth and to every bird of the air and to everything that creeps on the earth which has the breath of life in it
Genesis1:31 God saw everything that He had made
Genesis2:1 So the heavens and the earth
Genesis2:2 On the seventh day God completed His work which He had done
Genesis2:3 Then God blessed the seventh day and made it holy
Genesis2:4 This is the account of the heavens and the earth when they were created. In the day that the Lord God made the earth and the heavens
Genesis2:5 no shrub of the field was yet on the earth
Genesis2:6 But a mist arose from the earth and watered the whole surface of the ground.
Genesis2:7 Then the Lord God formed man from the dust of the ground and breathed into his nostrils the breath of life
Genesis2:8 The Lord God planted a garden in the east
Genesis2:9 Out of the ground the Lord God made to grow every tree that is pleasant to the sight and good for food. The tree of life was also in the midst of the garden
Genesis2:10 A river flowed out of Eden to water the garden
Genesis2:11 The name of the first is Pishon; it encompasses the whole land of Havilah
Genesis2:12 The gold of that land is good; bdellium and the onyx stone are there.
Genesis2:13 The name of the second river is Gihon; it encompasses the whole land of Cush.
Genesis2:14 The name of the third river is Tigris; it goes toward the east of Assyria. The fourth river is the Euphrates.
Genesis2:15 The Lord God took the man and put him in the garden of Eden to till it and to keep it.
Genesis2:16 And the Lord God commanded the man
Genesis2:17 but of the tree of the knowledge of good and evil you shall not eat
Genesis2:18 Then the Lord God said
Genesis2:19 Out of the ground the Lord God formed every beast of the field and every bird of the sky
Genesis2:20 The man gave names to all the livestock
Genesis2:21 So the Lord God caused a deep sleep to fall on Adam
Genesis2:22 Then the rib which the Lord God had taken from man
Genesis2:23 Then Adam said
Genesis2:24 Therefore a man will leave his father and his mother and be joined to his wife
Genesis2:25 They were both naked

Genesis3:2 And the woman said to the serpent
Genesis3:3 but from the fruit of the tree which is in the midst of the garden
Genesis3:4 Then the serpent said to the woman
Genesis3:5 For God knows that on the day you eat of it your eyes will be opened and you will be like God
Genesis3:6 When the woman saw that the tree was good for food
Genesis3:7 Then the eyes of both were opened
Genesis3:8 Then they heard the sound of the Lord God walking in the garden in the cool of the day
Genesis3:9 The Lord God called to the man and said to him
Genesis3:10 He said
Genesis3:11 And He said
Genesis3:12 The man said
Genesis3:13 Then the Lord God said to the woman

Genesis3:15 I will put enmity between you and the woman

Genesis3:22 Then the Lord God said
Genesis3:23 therefore the Lord God sent him out from the garden of Eden
Genesis3:24 He drove the man out
Genesis4:1 Adam had relations with his wife Eve
Genesis4:2 Then she gave birth again to his brother Abel. And Abel was a keeper of flocks
Genesis4:3 In the course of time Cain brought an offering to the Lord of the fruit of the ground.
Genesis4:4 Abel also brought the firstborn of his flock and of their fat portions. And the Lord had respect for Abel and for his offering
Genesis4:5 but for Cain and for his offering
Genesis4:6 The Lord said to Cain
Genesis4:7 If you do well
Genesis4:8 Cain told Abel his brother. And it came about
Genesis4:9 The Lord said to Cain
Genesis4:10 And then He said
Genesis4:11 Now you are cursed from the ground which opened its mouth to receive your brother’s blood from your hand.
Genesis4:12 From now on when you till the ground
Genesis4:13 Then Cain said to the Lord
Genesis4:14 Behold
Genesis4:15 So the Lord said to him
Genesis4:16 Then Cain went out from the presence of the Lord and settled in the land of Nod
Genesis4:17 Cain had relations with his wife
Genesis4:18 To Enoch was born Irad
Genesis4:19 Lamech took two wives. The name of one was Adah
Genesis4:20 Adah gave birth to Jabal. He was the father of those who dwell in tents and have livestock.
Genesis4:21 His brother’s name was Jubal. He was the father of all those who play the harp and flute.
Genesis4:22 Zillah gave birth to Tubal-Cain
Genesis4:23 Lamech said to his wives: “Adah and Zillah
Genesis4:24 If Cain will be avenged sevenfold
Genesis4:25 Adam had relations with his wife again
Genesis4:26 To Seth also was born a son
Genesis5:1 This is the book of the generations of Adam. In the day when God created man
Genesis5:2 He created them male and female. He blessed them and called them Mankind in the day when they were created.
Genesis5:3 Adam lived one hundred and thirty years and fathered a son in his own likeness
Genesis5:4 The days of Adam after he fathered Seth were eight hundred years
Genesis5:5 So all the days that Adam lived were nine hundred and thirty years
Genesis5:6 Seth lived one hundred and five years and fathered Enosh.
Genesis5:7 Seth lived after the birth of Enosh eight hundred and seven years and had other sons and daughters.
Genesis5:8 So all the days of Seth were nine hundred and twelve years
Genesis5:9 Enosh lived ninety years and fathered Kenan.
Genesis5:10 Enosh lived after the birth of Kenan eight hundred and fifteen years and had other sons and daughters.
Genesis5:11 So all the days of Enosh were nine hundred and five years
Genesis5:12 Kenan lived seventy years and fathered Mahalalel.
Genesis5:13 Kenan lived after the birth of Mahalalel eight hundred and forty years and had other sons and daughters.
Genesis5:14 So all the days of Kenan were nine hundred and ten years
Genesis5:15 Mahalalel lived sixty-five years and fathered Jared.
Genesis5:16 Mahalalel lived after the birth of Jared eight hundred and thirty years and had other sons and daughters.
Genesis5:17 So all the days of Mahalalel were eight hundred and ninety-five years
Genesis5:18 Jared lived one hundred and sixty-two years and fathered Enoch.
Genesis5:19 Jared lived after the birth of Enoch eight hundred years and had other sons and daughters.
Genesis5:20 So all the days of Jared were nine hundred and sixty-two years
Genesis5:21 Enoch lived sixty-five years and fathered Methuselah.
Genesis5:22 Enoch walked with God after the birth of Methuselah for three hundred years and had other sons and daughters.
Genesis5:23 So all the days of Enoch were three hundred and sixty-five years.
Genesis5:24 Enoch walked with God
Genesis5:25 Methuselah lived one hundred and eighty-seven years and fathered Lamech.
Genesis5:26 Methuselah lived after the birth of Lamech seven hundred and eighty-two years and had other sons and daughters.
Genesis5:27 So all the days of Methuselah were nine hundred and sixty-nine years
Genesis5:28 Lamech lived one hundred and eighty-two years and had a son.
Genesis5:29 He named his son Noah
Genesis5:30 Lamech lived after the birth of Noah five hundred and ninety-five years and had other sons and daughters.
Genesis5:31 So all the days of Lamech were seven hundred and seventy-seven years
Genesis5:32 Noah was five hundred years old and fathered Shem

Genesis6:2 the sons of God saw that the daughters of men were fair and took as wives any they chose.
Genesis6:3 The Lord said
Genesis6:4 The Nephilim were on the earth in those days

Genesis6:7 So the Lord said
Genesis6:8 But Noah found grace in the eyes of the Lord.
Genesis6:9 These are the generations of Noah. Noah was a just man and blameless among his contemporaries. Noah walked with God.

Genesis6:11 The earth was corrupt before God and filled with violence.

Genesis6:17 For behold

Genesis7:5 And Noah did according to all that the Lord commanded him.

Genesis7:16 So they went in

Genesis7:18 The water prevailed and increased greatly upon the earth
Genesis7:19 The water prevailed exceedingly on the earth
Genesis7:20 The waters prevailed upward and the mountains were covered fifteen cubits deep.
Genesis7:21 All flesh that moved on the earth died: birds and livestock and beasts
Genesis7:22 All in whose nostrils was the breath of life
Genesis7:23 So He blotted out every living thing which was on the face of the ground
Genesis7:24 The waters prevailed on the earth for one hundred and fifty days.

Genesis8:2 Also the fountains of the deep and the floodgates of heaven were closed
Genesis8:3 The water receded steadily from the earth
Genesis8:4 The ark rested in the seventh month
Genesis8:5 The water continually decreased until the tenth month. In the tenth month
Genesis8:6 Then at the end of forty days
Genesis8:7 and he sent forth a raven
Genesis8:8 Then he sent out a dove to see if the waters had subsided from the face of the ground.
Genesis8:9 But the dove found no rest for the sole of her foot
Genesis8:10 He waited yet another seven days
Genesis8:11 Then the dove came to him in the evening
Genesis8:12 He waited another seven days and sent out the dove again

Genesis8:14 In the second month

Genesis8:18 So Noah and his sons and his wife and his sons’ wives went out.
Genesis8:19 Every beast

Exodus12:17 You shall observe the Feast of Unleavened Bread
Exodus12:18 In the first month
Exodus12:19 Seven days shall there be no leaven found in your houses
Exodus12:20 You shall eat nothing leavened. In all your dwellings you shall eat unleavened bread.

Exodus12:40 Now the sojourning of the Israelites who lived in Egypt was four hundred and thirty years.
Exodus12:41 And at the end of the four hundred and thirty years
Exodus12:42 It is a night to be observed to the Lord for bringing them out from the land of Egypt. This is that night for the Lord to be observed by all the Israelites in their generations.
Exodus12:43 So the Lord said to Moses and Aaron: This is the ordinance of the Passover: No foreigner may eat of it.
Exodus12:44 But every man’s servant bought with money
Exodus12:45 A foreigner or a hired servant shall not eat it.
Exodus12:46 In one house shall it be eaten. You shall not carry any of the flesh outside of the house
Exodus12:47 All the congregation of Israel shall keep it.
Exodus12:48 Now when a stranger sojourns with you and keeps the Passover to the Lord
Exodus12:49 The same law shall apply to him that is a native and to the stranger who sojourns among you.
Exodus12:50 So all the Israelites did it. They did just as the Lord commanded Moses and Aaron.
Exodus12:51 And that same day the Lord brought the Israelites out of the land of Egypt by their hosts.

Exodus20:6 and showing mercy to thousands of them who love Me and keep My commandments.

Exodus20:10 but the seventh day is a Sabbath to the Lord your God. On it you shall not do any work
Exodus20:11 For in six days the Lord made heaven and earth
Exodus20:12 Honor your father and your mother
Exodus20:13 You shall not murder.
Exodus20:14 You shall not commit adultery.
Exodus20:15 You shall not steal.

Exodus21:1 Now these are the judgments which you will set before them.
Exodus21:2 If you buy a Hebrew servant
Exodus21:3 If he came in by himself
Exodus21:4 If his master gives him a wife
Exodus21:5 However
Exodus21:6 then his master will bring him to the judges
Exodus21:7 If a man sells his daughter to be a female servant
Exodus21:8 If she does not please her master
Exodus21:9 If he has designated her for his son
Exodus21:10 If he marries another wife
Exodus21:11 If he does not provide these three for her
Exodus21:12 He that strikes a man so that he dies shall surely be put to death.
Exodus21:13 However
Exodus21:14 But if a man willfully comes upon his neighbor in order to kill him cunningly
Exodus21:15 He who strikes his father or his mother shall surely be put to death.
Exodus21:16 He who kidnaps a man and sells him
Exodus21:17 He who curses his father or his mother shall surely be put to death.
Exodus21:18 If men fight and one strikes the other with a stone or with his fist
Exodus21:19 and then if he gets up and walks around on his staff
Exodus21:20 If a man strikes his male or female servant with a rod and the servant dies at his hand
Exodus21:21 But if he survives for a day or two
Exodus21:22 If men fight and hurt a pregnant woman so that her child is born prematurely
Exodus21:23 But if there is any serious injury
Exodus21:24 eye for eye
Exodus21:25 burn for burn
Exodus21:26 If a man strikes the eye of his male or female servant
Exodus21:27 If he knocks out the tooth of his male or female servant
Exodus21:28 If an ox gores a man or a woman to death
Exodus21:29 But if the ox has had the habit of goring
Exodus21:30 If a ransom is set for him
Exodus21:31 Whether it gored a son or gored a daughter
Exodus21:32 If the ox gores a male servant or a female servant
Exodus21:33 If a man opens a pit or if a man digs a pit and does not cover it
Exodus21:34 the owner of the pit must make restitution. He must give money to their owner
Exodus21:35 If one man’s ox hurts another’s so that it dies
Exodus21:36 Or if it be known that the ox has had the habit of goring and its owner has not kept it in
Exodus22:1 If a man steals an ox or a sheep and kills it or sells it
Exodus22:2 If a thief is caught breaking in and is struck so that he dies
Exodus22:3 If the sun has risen on him
Exodus22:4 If the stolen item is in fact found alive in his possession
Exodus22:5 If a man causes a field or vineyard to be eaten and puts out his beast so that it feeds in another man’s field
Exodus22:6 If fire breaks out and catches in thorn bushes
Exodus22:7 If a man gives his neighbor money or items to be kept for him
Exodus22:8 If the thief is not caught
Exodus22:9 For any kind of trespass
Exodus22:10 If a man gives his neighbor a donkey

Exodus22:12 However
Exodus22:13 If it is torn in pieces by beasts
Exodus22:14 If a man borrows anything from his neighbor
Exodus22:15 But if the owner was with it
Exodus22:16 If a man seduces a virgin who is not engaged and has relations with her
Exodus22:17 If her father absolutely refuses to give her to him
Exodus22:18 You must not allow a sorceress to live.
Exodus22:19 Whoever has relations with a beast must surely be put to death.
Exodus22:20 He who sacrifices to any god other than the Lord alone shall be utterly destroyed.
Exodus22:21 You must neither wrong a foreigner nor oppress him
Exodus22:22 You shall not afflict any widow or orphan.
Exodus22:23 If you afflict them in any way and they cry at all to Me
Exodus22:24 And My anger will burn
Exodus22:25 If you lend money to any of My people who is poor among you
Exodus22:26 If you take your neighbor’s garment as a pledge
Exodus22:27 for that is his only covering; it is his garment for his body. In what else will he sleep? And when he cries out to Me
Exodus22:28 You shall not curse God or curse the ruler of your people.
Exodus22:29 You must not delay to offer the first of your harvest and of your vats. You must give to Me the firstborn of your sons.
Exodus22:30 Likewise you must do the same with your oxen and with your sheep. Seven days it shall remain with its mother
Exodus22:31 You will be holy men to Me; therefore you must not eat any flesh that is torn by beasts in the field. You must throw it to the dogs.

Exodus23:24 You must not bow down to their gods
Exodus23:25 You shall serve the Lord your God
Exodus23:26 No one shall be miscarrying or be barren in your land. I will fulfill the number of your days.

Exodus28:5 They shall take the gold
Exodus28:6 They shall make the ephod of gold
Exodus28:7 It shall have the two shoulder pieces attached to its two corners
Exodus28:8 The skillfully woven waistband of the ephod
Exodus28:9 You shall take two onyx stones and engrave on them the names of the Israelites
Exodus28:10 six of their names on one stone
Exodus28:11 With the work of an engraver in stone
Exodus28:12 You shall put the two stones on the shoulders of the ephod as stones of memorial for the Israelites
Exodus28:13 You shall make filigree settings of gold
Exodus28:14 and two chains of pure gold. You shall make them of twisted cord and fasten the braided chains to the filigree settings.
Exodus28:15 You shall make the breastplate of judgment
Exodus28:16 It is to be square when doubled: one span in length and one span in width.
Exodus28:17 You shall set in it four rows of stones. The first row shall be a sardius
Exodus28:18 the second row an emerald
Exodus28:19 the third row a jacinth
Exodus28:20 and the fourth row a beryl
Exodus28:21 The stones shall be according to the names of the Israelites
Exodus28:22 You shall make for the breastplate braided chains of pure gold.
Exodus28:23 You shall make on the breastplate two rings of gold and shall put the two rings on the two ends of the breastplate.
Exodus28:24 You shall put the two braided chains of gold on the two rings which are on the ends of the breastplate.
Exodus28:25 You shall fasten the other two ends of the two braided chains in the two filigree settings and put them on the front of the shoulder pieces of the ephod.
Exodus28:26 You shall make two rings of gold and shall put them on the two ends of the breastplate
Exodus28:27 You shall make two other rings of gold and shall put them on the two shoulder pieces of the ephod underneath toward the front
Exodus28:28 They shall bind the breastplate by its rings to the rings of the ephod with a blue cord
Exodus28:29 Aaron shall bear the names of the Israelites on the breastplate of judgment over his heart when he goes into the holy place
Exodus28:30 You shall put the Urim and the Thummim in the breastplate of judgment
Exodus28:31 You shall make the robe of the ephod completely blue.
Exodus28:32 There shall be a hole at the top of it
Exodus28:33 You shall make on its hem pomegranates of blue
Exodus28:34 a golden bell and a pomegranate
Exodus28:35 It shall be on Aaron when he ministers. And its sound shall be heard when he goes into the holy place before the Lord
Exodus28:36 You shall make a plate of pure gold and engrave on it
Exodus28:37 You shall put it on a blue cord
Exodus28:38 It shall be on Aaron’s forehead
Exodus28:39 You shall embroider the tunic of fine linen
Exodus28:40 For Aaron’s sons you shall make tunics. You shall make sashes for them
Exodus28:41 You shall put them on Aaron your brother
Exodus28:42 You shall make linen undergarments for them to cover their naked skin. They shall reach from the waist to the thighs.
Exodus28:43 They shall be on Aaron and on his sons when they come into the tent of meeting or when they come near to the altar to minister in the holy place
Exodus29:1 Now this is the thing that you shall do to them to consecrate them
Exodus29:2 and unleavened bread
Exodus29:3 You shall put them into one basket and bring them in the basket with the bull and the two rams.
Exodus29:4 Then you shall bring Aaron and his sons to the door of the tent of meeting and wash them with water.
Exodus29:5 You shall take the garments and clothe Aaron with the tunic and the robe of the ephod and the ephod and the breastplate
Exodus29:6 And you shall put the turban on his head and put the holy crown on the turban.
Exodus29:7 Then shall you take the anointing oil and pour it on his head and anoint him.
Exodus29:8 You shall bring his sons and put tunics on them.
Exodus29:9 You shall gird them with sashes
Exodus29:10 Then you shall bring a bull before the tent of meeting
Exodus29:11 You shall kill the bull before the Lord by the door of the tent of meeting.
Exodus29:12 You shall take of the blood of the bull
Exodus29:13 You shall take all the fat that covers the entrails
Exodus29:14 But the flesh of the bull
Exodus29:15 You shall also take one ram
Exodus29:16 You shall slay the ram
Exodus29:17 Then you shall cut the ram in pieces
Exodus29:18 You shall burn the whole ram on the altar. It is a burnt offering to the Lord. It is a soothing aroma
Exodus29:19 Then you shall take the other ram
Exodus29:20 You shall kill the ram and take some of its blood and put it on the tip of the right ear of Aaron
Exodus29:21 You shall take some of the blood that is on the altar and some of the anointing oil and sprinkle it on Aaron and on his garments
Exodus29:22 Also you shall take the fat and the rump of the ram
Exodus29:23 and one loaf of bread
Exodus29:24 And you shall put all of these in the hands of Aaron and in the hands of his sons
Exodus29:25 Then you shall take them from their hands and burn them on the altar for a burnt offering
Exodus29:26 You shall take the breast of Aaron’s ram of consecration
Exodus29:27 You shall consecrate the breast of the wave offering that is waved and the thigh of the priest’s portion that is contributed from the ram of the consecration
Exodus29:28 It shall be for Aaron and his sons by a statute forever
Exodus29:29 The holy garments belonging to Aaron are to belong to his sons after him
Exodus29:30 The son who is priest in his succession shall wear them seven days when he comes into the tent of meeting to minister in the holy place.
Exodus29:31 You shall take the ram of the consecration and boil its flesh in a holy place.
Exodus29:32 Aaron and his sons shall eat the flesh of the ram and the bread that is in the basket by the door of the tent of meeting.
Exodus29:33 They shall eat those things by which the atonement was made in order to consecrate and sanctify them
Exodus29:34 If any of the flesh from the consecrations or from the bread remains until the morning
Exodus29:35 Thus shall you do to Aaron and his sons according to all that I have commanded you. You shall consecrate them for seven days.
Exodus29:36 Every day you must offer a bull as a sin offering for atonement
Exodus29:37 For seven days you must make atonement for the altar and consecrate it
Exodus29:38 Now this is what you are to offer on the altar: two one-year-old lambs every day
Exodus29:39 The one lamb you must offer in the morning
Exodus29:40 And with the first lamb will be one-tenth of an ephah of flour mingled with one-fourth of a hin of beaten oil
Exodus29:41 The other lamb you must offer at sundown and must offer with it the same grain offering and the same drink offering as in the morning
Exodus29:42 This will be a continual burnt offering throughout your generations at the door of the tent of meeting before the Lord
Exodus29:43 I will meet there with the Israelites
Exodus29:44 I will sanctify the tent of meeting and the altar. I will also sanctify both Aaron and his sons to minister as priests to Me.
Exodus29:45 I will dwell among the Israelites and will be their God.
Exodus29:46 Then they will know that I am the Lord their God who brought them out of the land of Egypt
Exodus30:1 Also
Exodus30:2 It must be one cubit in length
Exodus30:3 You must overlay it with pure gold

Exodus30:7 Aaron must burn sweet incense on it. Every morning

Exodus30:10 Aaron must make atonement on its horns once a year with the blood of the sin offering of atonement. Once a year he must make atonement on it throughout your generations. It is most holy to the Lord.

Exodus30:12 When you take the census of the Israelites according to their number
Exodus30:13 This is what everyone who is counted must give: half a shekel according to the shekel of the sanctuary (one shekel is twenty gerahs). The half shekel will be the offering to the Lord.
Exodus30:14 Everyone who is counted
Exodus30:15 The rich must not give more and the poor must not give less than half a shekel

Exodus30:35 You shall make of these an incense
Exodus30:36 You must beat some of it very fine and put part of it before the testimony in the tent of meeting where I will meet with you. It will be most holy to you.
Exodus30:37 As for the perfume which you will make
Exodus30:38 Whoever makes anything like it in order to use it as perfume must be cut off from his people.

Exodus34:1 Now the Lord said to Moses

Exodus34:7 keeping mercy for thousands

Exodus34:14 (for you shall not worship any other god

Exodus34:22 You must observe the Feast of Weeks
Exodus34:23 Three times in the year all your males must appear before the Lord God
Exodus34:24 For I will drive out the nations before you and enlarge your borders. No man will covet your land when you go up to appear before the Lord your God three times in the year.
Exodus34:25 You must not offer the blood of My sacrifice with leaven
Exodus34:26 The first of the firstfruits of your land you must bring to the house of the Lord your God. You must not boil a young goat in its mother’s milk.

Exodus36:1 So Bezalel and Oholiab

Exodus36:4 And all the skilled men who were doing all the work of the sanctuary came from the work they were doing
Exodus36:5 and they said to Moses
Exodus36:6 So Moses issued a command
Exodus36:7 For the material they had was sufficient for all the work and more than enough to do it.
Exodus36:8 Every skilled man among those who were doing the work made the tabernacle with ten curtains of fine twisted linen
Exodus36:9 The length of each curtain was twenty-eight cubits
Exodus36:10 He joined the five curtains to one another
Exodus36:11 He made loops of blue on the edge of the outermost curtain in the first set. He did likewise along the edge of the outermost curtain in the second set.
Exodus36:12 He made fifty loops in the one curtain and made fifty loops on the edge of the curtain which was in the second set. The loops were opposite one another.
Exodus36:13 He made fifty gold clasps and joined the curtains to one another with the clasps
Exodus36:14 He made curtains of goats’ hair for a tent over the tabernacle. He made eleven curtains in all.
Exodus36:15 The length of each curtain was thirty cubits
Exodus36:16 He joined five curtains by themselves and six curtains by themselves.
Exodus36:17 He made fifty loops on the outermost edge of the curtain in the first set
Exodus36:18 He made fifty bronze clasps to join the tent together
Exodus36:19 He made a covering for the tent out of rams’ skins dyed red and a covering of porpoise skins above.
Exodus36:20 Then he made boards for the tabernacle out of acacia wood
Exodus36:21 The length of each board was ten cubits
Exodus36:22 Each board had two tenons
Exodus36:23 He made boards for the tabernacle: twenty boards for the south side.
Exodus36:24 He made forty sockets of silver under the twenty boards; two sockets under one board for its two tenons and two sockets under another board for its two tenons.
Exodus36:25 For the other side of the tabernacle
Exodus36:26 and their forty sockets of silver: two sockets under one board

Exodus39:34 and the covering of rams’ skins dyed red
Exodus39:35 the ark of the testimony and its poles and the mercy seat;
Exodus39:36 the table
Exodus39:37 the pure gold lampstand
Exodus39:38 and the gold altar
Exodus39:39 the bronze altar
Exodus39:40 the hangings of the courtyard
Exodus39:41 the woven garments of service to do service in the holy place
Exodus39:42 The Israelites did all the work according to all that the Lord commanded Moses.
Exodus39:43 And Moses saw all the work
Exodus40:1 Now the Lord spoke to Moses
Exodus40:2 On the first day of the first month you shall set up the tabernacle of the tent of meeting.
Exodus40:3 You shall put the ark of the testimony in it and screen the ark with the veil.
Exodus40:4 You shall bring in the table and arrange the things that are to be arranged on it
Exodus40:5 You shall set the gold altar of incense before the ark of the testimony and put the curtain for the entrance to the tabernacle.
Exodus40:6 You shall set the altar of the burnt offering in front of the entrance of the tabernacle of the tent of meeting.
Exodus40:7 You shall set the basin between the tent of meeting and the altar and put water in it.
Exodus40:8 You shall set up the courtyard around it and hang up the curtain at the courtyard gate.
Exodus40:9 You shall take the anointing oil and anoint the tabernacle and all that is in it and shall consecrate it and all its furnishings
Exodus40:10 Then you shall anoint the altar of the burnt offering and all its utensils
Exodus40:11 You shall anoint the basin and its stand
Exodus40:12 Then you shall bring Aaron and his sons to the entrance of the tent of meeting and wash them with water.
Exodus40:13 You shall put the holy garments on Aaron and anoint him and consecrate him
Exodus40:14 You shall bring his sons and clothe them with tunics.
Exodus40:15 Then you shall anoint them just as you anointed their father
Exodus40:16 Thus Moses did. According to all that the Lord commanded him
Exodus40:17 In the first month of the second year
Exodus40:18 Moses erected the tabernacle and fastened its sockets
Exodus40:19 Then he spread the tent over the tabernacle and put the covering of the tent on top of it
Exodus40:20 He took the testimony and put it into the ark
Exodus40:21 And he brought the ark into the tabernacle
Exodus40:22 Then he put the table in the tent of meeting on the north side of the tabernacle outside the veil.
Exodus40:23 He set the bread in order on it before the Lord
Exodus40:24 And he put the lampstand in the tent of meeting
Exodus40:25 He lighted the lamps before the Lord
Exodus40:26 Then he put the gold altar in the tent of meeting before the veil.
Exodus40:27 And he burned fragrant incense on it
Exodus40:28 Then he set up the curtain at the entrance of the tabernacle.
Exodus40:29 He put the altar of burnt offering by the entrance of the tabernacle of the tent of meeting and offered on it the burnt offering and the grain offering
Exodus40:30 Then he set the basin between the tent of meeting and the altar and put water in it for washing.
Exodus40:31 It was there Moses and Aaron and his sons washed their hands and their feet.
Exodus40:32 Whenever they went into the tent of meeting
Exodus40:33 He erected the courtyard all around the tabernacle and the altar
Exodus40:34 Then the cloud covered the tent of meeting
Exodus40:35 Moses was not able to enter into the tent of meeting because the cloud settled on it
Exodus40:36 When the cloud was lifted up from over the tabernacle
Exodus40:37 But if the cloud was not lifted up
Exodus40:38 For the cloud of the Lord was on the tabernacle by day
Leviticus1:1 And the Lord called Moses and spoke to him from the tent of meeting
Leviticus1:2 Speak to the Israelites and say to them: When an individual among you brings an offering to the Lord
Leviticus1:3 If his offering is a burnt sacrifice
Leviticus1:4 Then he shall lay his hand on the head of the burnt offering
Leviticus1:5 And he shall slaughter the bull before the Lord
Leviticus1:6 Then he shall skin the burnt offering and cut it up into parts.
Leviticus1:7 The sons of Aaron the priest shall put fire on the altar and arrange the wood on the fire.
Leviticus1:8 Then the priests
Leviticus1:9 But he shall wash its entrails and its legs in water
Leviticus1:10 If his gift for a burnt offering is from the flocks
Leviticus1:11 He shall slaughter it at the north side of the altar before the Lord. And the sons of Aaron
Leviticus1:12 He shall cut it up into parts
Leviticus1:13 But he shall wash the entrails and the legs with water. The priest shall bring it all and burn it on the altar. It is a burnt sacrifice
Leviticus1:14 If his offering to the Lord is a burnt sacrifice of birds
Leviticus1:15 The priest shall bring it to the altar
Leviticus1:16 He shall remove its entrails with its feathers and throw it to the east side of the altar to the place of the ashes.
Leviticus1:17 And he shall split it open by its wings
Leviticus2:1 When a person offers a grain offering to the Lord
Leviticus2:2 And he shall bring it to the sons of Aaron
Leviticus2:3 And the remainder of the grain offering shall belong to Aaron and to his sons
Leviticus2:4 When you bring an oven-baked grain offering
Leviticus2:5 If your offering is grain on a griddle
Leviticus2:6 Break it into pieces and pour oil on it. It is a grain offering.
Leviticus2:7 If your offering is grain in a skillet
Leviticus2:8 You shall bring the grain offering that is made of these things to the Lord
Leviticus2:9 The priest shall remove a memorial portion from the grain offering and burn it on the altar
Leviticus2:10 The remainder of the grain offering shall belong to Aaron and to his sons
Leviticus2:11 No grain offering that you bring to the Lord shall be made with leaven
Leviticus2:12 As an offering of firstfruits
Leviticus2:13 You shall season all your grain offerings with salt. You shall not fail to use the salt of the covenant of your God on your grain offering. With all your offerings you shall offer salt.
Leviticus2:14 If you offer a grain offering of your firstfruits to the Lord
Leviticus2:15 You shall put olive oil on it and frankincense on it. It is a grain offering.
Leviticus2:16 The priest shall burn its memorial portion and some of its coarsely ground new grain and oil
Leviticus3:1 If his offering is a peace sacrifice
Leviticus3:2 He shall lay his hand on the head of his offering and slaughter it at the door of the tent of meeting
Leviticus3:3 He shall offer from the peace sacrifice
Leviticus3:4 and the two kidneys with the fat that is above them
Leviticus3:5 Then the sons of Aaron shall burn it on the altar on the burnt sacrifice that is on the wood that is on the fire
Leviticus3:6 If his offering for a peace sacrifice to the Lord is from the flock
Leviticus3:7 If he is offering a sheep for his offering
Leviticus3:8 He shall lay his hand upon the head of his offering and slaughter it before the tent of meeting
Leviticus3:9 He shall offer from the peace sacrifice
Leviticus3:10 and the two kidneys with the fat that is above them
Leviticus3:11 The priest shall burn it on the altar as a food offering made by fire for the Lord.
Leviticus3:12 If his offering is a goat
Leviticus3:13 He shall lay his hand on its head and slaughter it before the tent of meeting
Leviticus3:14 He shall offer from it as his offering
Leviticus3:15 and the two kidneys with the fat that is above them
Leviticus3:16 The priest shall burn them on the altar as a food offering made by fire for a pleasing aroma. All the fat belongs to the Lord.
Leviticus3:17 As a continual statute for your generations in all your settlements
Leviticus4:1 And the Lord spoke to Moses
Leviticus4:2 Speak to the Israelites
Leviticus4:3 if the anointed priest sins
Leviticus4:4 He shall bring the bull to the opening of the tent of meeting before the Lord
Leviticus4:5 The anointed priest shall take some of the bull’s blood and bring it into the tent of meeting.
Leviticus4:6 The priest shall dip his finger in the blood and sprinkle some of the blood seven times before the Lord in front of the veil of the sanctuary.
Leviticus4:7 And the priest shall put some of the blood on the horns of the altar of fragrant incense before the Lord
Leviticus4:8 Then he shall remove all the fat of the bull that is for the sin offering—the fat that covers the entrails—and all the fat that is on the entrails
Leviticus4:9 and the two kidneys with the fat that is above them
Leviticus4:10 just as these are removed from the bull of the peace sacrifice
Leviticus4:11 The skin of the bull
Leviticus4:12 all the rest of the bull
Leviticus4:13 If the whole congregation of Israel commits an unintentional sin
Leviticus4:14 and the sin that they committed against the commandment becomes known
Leviticus4:15 The elders of the congregation shall lay their hands on the head of the bull before the Lord
Leviticus4:16 The anointed priest shall bring some of the bull’s blood to the tent of meeting.
Leviticus4:17 And the priest shall dip his finger in some of the blood and sprinkle it seven times before the Lord in front of the veil.
Leviticus4:18 He shall put some of the blood on the horns of the altar that is before the Lord
Leviticus4:19 He shall remove all the fat from it and burn it on the altar.
Leviticus4:20 He shall do to this bull just as he did to the bull of the sin offering; this is what he will do to it. And the priest shall make atonement for them
Leviticus4:21 He shall bring the bull outside the camp
Leviticus4:22 Whenever a leader sins
Leviticus4:23 or his sin that he committed was made known to him
Leviticus4:24 and he shall lay his hand on the head of the goat
Leviticus4:25 The priest shall take some of the blood of the sin offering on his finger and put it on the horns of the altar of the burnt offering
Leviticus4:26 And he shall burn all its fat on the altar
Leviticus4:27 If any one of the common people should sin unintentionally by doing one of the things that by the commandments of the Lord should not be done
Leviticus4:28 or his sin that he committed was made known to him
Leviticus4:29 He shall lay his hand on the head of the sin offering
Leviticus4:30 The priest shall take some of its blood on his finger and put it on the horns of the altar of the burnt offering
Leviticus4:31 And he shall remove all its fat
Leviticus4:32 And if he brings a sheep for a sin offering
Leviticus4:33 and lay his hand on the head of the sin offering
Leviticus4:34 The priest shall take some of the blood of the sin offering on his finger and put it on the horns of the altar of the burnt offering
Leviticus4:35 And he shall remove all the fat
Leviticus5:1 When a person sins in hearing the spoken oath
Leviticus5:2 Or when a person touches any unclean thing
Leviticus5:3 Or when he touches human uncleanness
Leviticus5:4 Or when a person swears by speaking rashly with his lips to do evil or to do good
Leviticus5:5 When he becomes guilty of one of these things
Leviticus5:6 And he shall bring his guilt offering to the Lord for his sin which he has committed
Leviticus5:7 If he cannot afford an animal
Leviticus5:8 He shall bring them to the priest who shall offer the one for the sin offering first. He will wring off its head at its neck
Leviticus5:9 Then he shall sprinkle some of the blood of the sin offering on the side of the altar
Leviticus5:10 But the second he shall treat as a burnt offering according to the regulation. The priest shall make atonement for him for his sin that he committed
Leviticus5:11 But if he cannot afford to bring two turtledoves or two pigeons
Leviticus5:12 Then he shall bring it to the priest
Leviticus5:13 The priest shall make atonement for him concerning his sin that he committed from any of these offenses
Leviticus5:14 The Lord spoke to Moses
Leviticus5:15 When a person acts unfaithfully and sins unintentionally in regard to the holy things of the Lord
Leviticus5:16 And he shall repay the sin that he committed with regard to the holy thing and shall add one-fifth to it and give it to the priest. The priest shall make atonement for him with the ram of the guilt offering
Leviticus5:17 If a person sins unintentionally and does any one of the things that by commandments of the Lord should not be done and he is found guilty
Leviticus5:18 He shall bring to the priest a ram without blemish from the flock—or its equivalent value —for a guilt offering. The priest shall make atonement for him concerning his error that he made unintentionally
Leviticus5:19 It is a guilt offering: He has indeed incurred guilt before the Lord.
Leviticus6:1 And the Lord spoke to Moses
Leviticus6:2 If a person sins and commits a trespass against the Lord by lying to his neighbor concerning something left in storage or entrusted to him
Leviticus6:3 or if he has found a lost item and lies about it
Leviticus6:4 then it shall be
Leviticus6:5 or all that about which he has sworn falsely. He shall repay it in full and shall add one-fifth to it. He shall give it to whom it belongs on the day that he is found guilty.
Leviticus6:6 And he shall bring his guilt offering to the Lord
Leviticus6:7 And the priest shall make atonement for him before the Lord

Leviticus6:9 Command Aaron and his sons
Leviticus6:10 The priest shall put on his linen robe
Leviticus6:11 Then he shall take off his garments and put on other garments
Leviticus6:12 The fire on the altar shall be kept burning on it. It shall not go out. The priest shall feed it with wood every morning. He will arrange the burnt offering on it
Leviticus6:13 A perpetual fire shall be kept burning on the altar. It shall never go out.
Leviticus6:14 This is the law of the grain offering. The sons of Aaron shall offer it before the Lord on the altar.
Leviticus6:15 He shall take from it a handful of the flour of the grain offering
Leviticus6:16 Aaron and his sons shall eat the remainder of it; it shall be eaten without leaven in a holy place. They shall eat it in the court of the tent of meeting.
Leviticus6:17 It shall not be baked with leaven. I have given it to them for their portion of My food offerings made by fire. It is most holy
Leviticus6:18 All the males among the sons of Aaron shall eat of it. It shall be a perpetual statute in your generations concerning the food offerings of the Lord made by fire. Everyone who touches them shall become holy.
Leviticus6:19 The Lord spoke to Moses
Leviticus6:20 This is the offering of Aaron and of his sons which they shall offer to the Lord in the day when he is anointed: one-tenth of an ephah of wheat flour for a regular grain offering
Leviticus6:21 It shall be made on a griddle with olive oil. When it is well mixed
Leviticus6:22 The priest from among the sons of Aaron who is anointed to succeed him shall offer it. It is a perpetual statute to the Lord. It shall be completely burned up.
Leviticus6:23 For every grain offering for the priest shall be completely burned up. It shall not be eaten.
Leviticus6:24 The Lord spoke to Moses
Leviticus6:25 Speak to Aaron and to his sons
Leviticus6:26 The priest who offers it for sin shall eat it. In a holy place it shall be eaten in the court of the tent of meeting.
Leviticus6:27 Whatever touches its flesh shall be holy
Leviticus6:28 And the clay vessel where it is boiled shall be broken
Leviticus6:29 All the males among the priests shall eat from this offering. It is most holy.
Leviticus6:30 Any sin offering where blood is brought into the tent of meeting to make atonement in the holy place shall not be eaten. It shall be burned up in the fire.

Leviticus11:1 The Lord spoke to Moses and Aaron
Leviticus11:2 Speak to the Israelites
Leviticus11:3 Whatever animal has a parted hoof (that is
Leviticus11:4 Nevertheless these you shall not eat of those that chew the cud or of those that have a divided hoof: The camel
Leviticus11:5 the rock badger
Leviticus11:6 the hare
Leviticus11:7 the pig
Leviticus11:8 you shall not eat of their flesh
Leviticus11:9 These you shall eat of all that are in the waters: Whatever has fins and scales in the waters
Leviticus11:10 all that do not have fins and scales in the seas and in the rivers
Leviticus11:11 they shall be detestable to you. You shall not eat of their flesh
Leviticus11:12 Whatever has no fins or scales in the waters is detestable to you.
Leviticus11:13 These you shall detest among the birds; they shall not be eaten; they are detestable: the griffon vulture
Leviticus11:14 the kite
Leviticus11:15 the raven of any kind
Leviticus11:16 and the eagle owl
Leviticus11:17 and the little owl
Leviticus11:18 and the white owl
Leviticus11:19 and the stork
Leviticus11:20 All flying insects that walk
Leviticus11:21 Yet these you may eat of every flying insect that creeps on all fours: those that have jointed legs above their feet with which to hop on the ground.
Leviticus11:22 Even of these you may eat: the locust of any kind
Leviticus11:23 But all other flying insects that have four feet shall be detestable to you.
Leviticus11:24 And by these you shall be unclean. Whoever touches the carcass of these shall be unclean until the evening.
Leviticus11:25 Whoever carries any of the carcasses of these shall wash his clothes and be unclean until the evening.
Leviticus11:26 The carcass of every animal that divides the hoof
Leviticus11:27 Whatever animal that walks on its paws
Leviticus11:28 He who carries the carcass of these shall wash his clothes and be unclean until the evening; they are unclean to you.
Leviticus11:29 These also shall be unclean to you among the crawling things that move on the ground: the mole rat
Leviticus11:30 and the gecko
Leviticus11:31 These are unclean to you among all that crawl. Whoever touches them when they are dead shall be unclean until the evening.
Leviticus11:32 And anything on which any of them falls when they are dead shall be unclean
Leviticus11:33 Every clay vessel into which any of these falls
Leviticus11:34 Any food in it that may be eaten on which water is poured shall be unclean
Leviticus11:35 Everything on which any part of their carcass falls shall be unclean; whether it is an oven or stove
Leviticus11:36 Nevertheless a fountain or cistern where there is plenty of water shall be clean
Leviticus11:37 If any part of their carcass falls on any grain seed that is to be sown
Leviticus11:38 But if any water is put on the seed
Leviticus11:39 If any animal from which you may eat dies
Leviticus11:40 And the person who eats of the carcass shall wash his clothes and be unclean until the evening. The person who carries the carcass shall wash his clothes and be unclean until the evening.
Leviticus11:41 Every crawling thing that moves on the ground shall be detestable. It shall not be eaten.
Leviticus11:42 Whatever goes on its belly
Leviticus11:43 You shall not make yourselves detestable with any crawling thing that moves
Leviticus11:44 For I am the Lord your God. You shall therefore sanctify yourselves
Leviticus11:45 For I am the Lord who brings you up out of the land of Egypt to be your God. Therefore you shall be holy
Leviticus11:46 This is the law for the animals
Leviticus11:47 to differentiate between the unclean and the clean

Leviticus12:2 Speak to the Israelites
Leviticus12:3 On the eighth day the flesh of his foreskin shall be circumcised.
Leviticus12:4 And she shall then continue in the blood of her purifying for thirty-three days. She shall not touch anything holy
Leviticus12:5 But if she gives birth to a female child
Leviticus12:6 When the days of her purifying are fulfilled
Leviticus12:7 who shall offer it before the Lord and make atonement for her. Then she shall be cleansed from the issue of her blood. This is the law for the woman who has given birth to a male or a female child.
Leviticus12:8 If she cannot afford a lamb
Leviticus13:1 And the Lord spoke to Moses and Aaron
Leviticus13:2 When a man has on the skin of his body a swelling
Leviticus13:3 And the priest shall examine the mark on the skin of the body
Leviticus13:4 If the spot is white on the skin of his body
Leviticus13:5 Then the priest shall examine him on the seventh day
Leviticus13:6 And the priest shall examine him again on the seventh day
Leviticus13:7 But if the scab spreads in the skin after he has been seen by the priest for his cleansing
Leviticus13:8 And if the priest sees that the scab has spread in the skin
Leviticus13:9 When a person has leprosy
Leviticus13:10 And the priest shall examine him
Leviticus13:11 it is a recurring leprosy in the skin of his body
Leviticus13:12 If the leprosy breaks out all over the skin
Leviticus13:13 then the priest shall examine
Leviticus13:14 But when raw flesh appears on him
Leviticus13:15 The priest shall see the raw flesh and pronounce him to be unclean
Leviticus13:16 Or if the raw flesh turns again
Leviticus13:17 And the priest shall see him
Leviticus13:18 And when the skin has a boil
Leviticus13:19 and in the place of the boil there is a white swelling or a reddish-white spot
Leviticus13:20 And if the priest sees it
Leviticus13:21 But if the priest looks at it
Leviticus13:22 If it spreads around the skin
Leviticus13:23 But if the spot stays in its place and does not spread
Leviticus13:24 Or if there is some of the skin on the body where there is a burn
Leviticus13:25 then the priest shall examine it
Leviticus13:26 But if the priest looks at it
Leviticus13:27 And the priest shall examine him on the seventh day
Leviticus13:28 If the spot stays in its place and it does not spread in the skin
Leviticus13:29 If a man or woman has a sore on the head or in the beard
Leviticus13:30 then the priest shall examine the sore
Leviticus13:31 If the priest examines the scaly sore
Leviticus13:32 And on the seventh day the priest shall examine the sore
Leviticus13:33 then he shall shave
Leviticus13:34 And on the seventh day the priest shall examine the scale. If the scale has not spread in the skin and is no deeper than the skin
Leviticus13:35 But if the scale spreads over the skin after his cleansing
Leviticus13:36 then the priest shall examine him. If the scale has spread over the skin
Leviticus13:37 But if the scale appears not to have changed and there is black hair growing in it
Leviticus13:38 If a man or a woman has spots on the skin of the body
Leviticus13:39 then the priest shall examine
Leviticus13:40 When a man has lost hair from his head
Leviticus13:41 And when a man has lost hair from his forehead
Leviticus13:42 But if there is on the bald head or bald forehead a reddish-white sore
Leviticus13:43 Then the priest shall examine it. If the diseased swelling is reddish-white on his bald head or on his bald forehead
Leviticus13:44 he is leprous. He is unclean. The priest shall pronounce him unclean. His disease is on his head.
Leviticus13:45 The man who has the leprous disease shall have torn clothes and disheveled hair; and he shall cover his mustache and shall cry
Leviticus13:46 All the days that he has the disease
Leviticus13:47 If a garment has a leprous disease
Leviticus13:48 whether it is in the warp or woof of the linen or wool fabric
Leviticus13:49 and if the disease is greenish or reddish in the garment
Leviticus13:50 And the priest shall examine the disease
Leviticus13:51 And he shall examine the disease on the seventh day. If the disease has spread in the garment
Leviticus13:52 He shall therefore burn that garment
Leviticus13:53 If the priest examines it
Leviticus13:54 then the priest shall command that they wash the article in which the disease appears
Leviticus13:55 The priest shall examine the infected article after it has been washed. If the infected spot has not changed its color
Leviticus13:56 If the priest examines and the disease has faded after washing it
Leviticus13:57 If it still appears in the garment
Leviticus13:58 The garment that you have washed
Leviticus13:59 This is the law of the leprous disease in a garment of wool or linen
Leviticus14:1 The Lord spoke to Moses
Leviticus14:2 This shall be the law of the leper in the day of his cleansing: It shall be reported to the priest
Leviticus14:3 and the priest shall go out of the camp
Leviticus14:4 Then the priest shall command that two live clean birds and cedar wood and scarlet fabric and hyssop be brought for him who is to be cleansed.
Leviticus14:5 The priest shall command that one of the birds be slaughtered in a clay vessel over running water.
Leviticus14:6 As for the living bird
Leviticus14:7 Then he shall sprinkle it seven times on him who is to be cleansed from leprosy
Leviticus14:8 He that is to be cleansed shall wash his clothes
Leviticus14:9 But it shall be on the seventh day that he shall shave all his hair off his head and his beard and his eyebrows
Leviticus14:10 On the eighth day he shall take two male lambs without blemish
Leviticus14:11 The priest who makes him clean shall present the man who is to be made clean and those things before the Lord at the entrance of the tent of meeting.
Leviticus14:12 Then the priest shall take one male lamb and offer it for a guilt offering
Leviticus14:13 Then he shall slaughter the lamb in the place where they kill the sin offering and the burnt offering in the holy sanctuary. For as the sin offering is the priest’s
Leviticus14:14 And the priest shall take some of the blood of the guilt offering
Leviticus14:15 And the priest shall take some of the log of oil and pour it into the palm of his own left hand.
Leviticus14:16 Then the priest shall dip his right finger in the oil that is in his left hand
Leviticus14:17 And some of the rest of the oil that is in his hand the priest shall put on the tip of the right ear of him who is to be cleansed
Leviticus14:18 And the rest of the oil that is in the priest’s hand he shall pour upon the head of him who is to be cleansed
Leviticus14:19 The priest shall offer the sin offering
Leviticus14:20 Then the priest shall offer the burnt offering and the grain offering on the altar
Leviticus14:21 But if he is poor and cannot afford so much
Leviticus14:22 and two turtledoves or two pigeons
Leviticus14:23 He shall bring them on the eighth day for his cleansing to the priest at the entrance of the tent of meeting before the Lord.
Leviticus14:24 The priest shall take the lamb of the guilt offering
Leviticus14:25 Then he shall kill the lamb of the guilt offering; and the priest shall take some of the blood of the guilt offering and put it on the tip of the right ear of him who is to be cleansed and on the thumb of his right hand and on the big toe of his right foot.
Leviticus14:26 Next the priest shall pour some of the oil into the palm of his own left hand
Leviticus14:27 and the priest shall sprinkle with his right finger some of the oil that is in his left hand seven times before the Lord.
Leviticus14:28 Then the priest shall put some of the oil that is in his hand on the tip of the right ear of him who is to be cleansed
Leviticus14:29 The rest of the oil that is in the priest’s hand he shall pour on the head of him who is to be cleansed to make atonement for him before the Lord.
Leviticus14:30 He shall offer one of the turtledoves or the pigeons
Leviticus14:31 even such as he is able to get
Leviticus14:32 This is the law for a person who has a leprous sore who cannot afford the offerings for his cleansing.
Leviticus14:33 The Lord spoke to Moses and to Aaron
Leviticus14:34 When you have come into the land of Canaan
Leviticus14:35 then he who owns the house shall come and tell the priest
Leviticus14:36 Then the priest shall command that they empty the house before the priest goes into it to examine the disease
Leviticus14:37 He shall examine the disease and see if the disease is in the walls of the house with greenish or reddish spots
Leviticus14:38 Then the priest shall go out to the door of the house and close off the house seven days.
Leviticus14:39 The priest shall return on the seventh day and shall examine and see if the disease has spread in the walls of the house.
Leviticus14:40 Then the priest shall command that they take away the stones in which the disease appears and throw them into an unclean place outside the city.
Leviticus14:41 And he shall cause the house to be scraped all around
Leviticus14:42 And they shall take other stones and put them in the place of those stones
Leviticus14:43 If the disease comes again and breaks out in the house after he has taken away the stones and after he has scraped the house and after it is plastered
Leviticus14:44 then the priest shall come and examine and see if the disease has spread in the house. If the disease has spread in the house
Leviticus14:45 He shall break down the house
Leviticus14:46 Moreover
Leviticus14:47 And he who sleeps in the house shall wash his clothes
Leviticus14:48 If the priest comes in and examines it and sees the disease has not spread in the house after the house was plastered
Leviticus14:49 He shall take two birds
Leviticus14:50 Then he shall kill one of the birds in a clay vessel over running water.
Leviticus14:51 He shall take the cedar wood
Leviticus14:52 Then he shall cleanse the house with the blood of the bird
Leviticus14:53 But he shall let the living bird loose outside the city into the open fields and make atonement for the house
Leviticus14:54 This is the law for all manner of leprous sore and scale
Leviticus14:55 and for the disease of a garment
Leviticus14:56 and for a swelling
Leviticus14:57 to discern when it is unclean and when it is clean. This is the law of leprosy.
Leviticus15:1 Then the Lord spoke to Moses and to Aaron
Leviticus15:2 Speak to the Israelites
Leviticus15:3 This is the instruction for his uncleanness in his discharge
Leviticus15:4 Every bed on which the man with the discharge lies shall be unclean
Leviticus15:5 Whoever touches the bed shall wash his clothes
Leviticus15:6 And whoever sits on anything where the man sat who has the discharge shall wash his clothes
Leviticus15:7 And whoever touches the body of him who has the discharge shall wash his clothes
Leviticus15:8 If the man with the discharge spits on someone who is clean
Leviticus15:9 Any saddle on which the man with a discharge rides shall be unclean.
Leviticus15:10 Whoever touches anything that was under him shall be unclean until the evening
Leviticus15:11 Whomever he who has the discharge touches
Leviticus15:12 The earthen vessel that he who has the discharge touches shall be broken
Leviticus15:13 When he who has a discharge is cleansed of his issue
Leviticus15:14 On the eighth day he shall take two turtledoves or two pigeons and come before the Lord at the entrance of the tent of meeting and give them to the priest.
Leviticus15:15 And the priest shall offer them
Leviticus15:16 If any man has a seminal emission
Leviticus15:17 Every garment and all leather on which there is seminal emission shall be washed with water and be unclean until the evening.
Leviticus15:18 If a man lies with a woman
Leviticus15:19 If a woman has a discharge
Leviticus15:20 Everything that she lies on in her menstrual impurity shall be unclean. Also
Leviticus15:21 Whoever touches her bed shall wash his clothes and bathe himself in water
Leviticus15:22 Whoever touches anything on which she sits shall wash his clothes and bathe himself in water
Leviticus15:23 Whether it is the bed or anything on which she sits
Leviticus15:24 If any man lies with her
Leviticus15:25 If a woman has a discharge of blood for many days
Leviticus15:26 Every bed that she lies on all the days of her discharge shall be for impurity
Leviticus15:27 Whoever touches those things shall be unclean and shall wash his clothes and bathe himself in water and be unclean until the evening.
Leviticus15:28 But if she is cleansed of her discharge
Leviticus15:29 On the eighth day she shall take two turtledoves or two pigeons and bring them to the priest at the entrance of the tent of meeting.
Leviticus15:30 The priest shall offer one for a sin offering and the other for a burnt offering
Leviticus15:31 Thus you shall separate the Israelites from their uncleanness
Leviticus15:32 This is the law for him who has a discharge and for him who has a seminal emission and thus becomes unclean
Leviticus15:33 and for her who is sick from her menstruation
Leviticus16:1 The Lord spoke to Moses after the death of the two sons of Aaron
Leviticus16:2 The Lord said to Moses: Speak to Aaron your brother so that he does not come at any time into the Holy Place within the veil before the mercy seat
Leviticus16:3 Thus Aaron shall come into the Holy Place with a young bull for a sin offering and a ram for a burnt offering.
Leviticus16:4 He shall put on the holy linen tunic
Leviticus16:5 He shall take from the congregation of the Israelites two male goats for a sin offering and one ram for a burnt offering.
Leviticus16:6 Aaron shall offer his bull for the sin offering
Leviticus16:7 Then he shall take the two goats and present them before the Lord at the entrance of the tent of meeting.
Leviticus16:8 Aaron shall cast lots for the two goats: one lot for the Lord and the other lot for the scapegoat.
Leviticus16:9 Aaron shall bring the goat on which the lot of the Lord falls and offer him for a sin offering.
Leviticus16:10 But the goat on which the lot falls to be the scapegoat shall be presented alive before the Lord to make atonement with it
Leviticus16:11 Aaron shall bring the bull of the sin offering
Leviticus16:12 And he shall take a censer full of burning coals of fire from the altar before the Lord
Leviticus16:13 And he shall put the incense on the fire before the Lord
Leviticus16:14 And he shall take of the blood of the bull
Leviticus16:15 Then he shall kill the goat of the sin offering
Leviticus16:16 And he shall make atonement for the Holy Place
Leviticus16:17 There shall be no man in the tent of meeting when he goes in to make atonement in the Holy Place
Leviticus16:18 Then he shall go out to the altar that is before the Lord and make atonement for it
Leviticus16:19 He shall sprinkle from the blood on it with his finger seven times
Leviticus16:20 When he has made an end of atonement for the Holy Place
Leviticus16:21 And Aaron shall lay both his hands on the head of the live goat
Leviticus16:22 And the goat shall bear on it all their iniquities to a desolate land
Leviticus16:23 Then Aaron shall come into the tent of meeting
Leviticus16:24 And he shall wash his body with water in a holy place
Leviticus16:25 The fat of the sin offering he shall burn on the altar.
Leviticus16:26 He who releases the goat as the scapegoat shall wash his clothes
Leviticus16:27 The bull for the sin offering and the goat for the sin offering
Leviticus16:28 He who burns them shall wash his clothes
Leviticus16:29 This shall be a perpetual statute for you so that in the seventh month
Leviticus16:30 For on that day the priest shall make atonement for you to cleanse you
Leviticus16:31 It shall be a sabbath
Leviticus16:32 The priest
Leviticus16:33 And he shall make atonement for the Holy Sanctuary
Leviticus16:34 This shall be a perpetual statute for you to make atonement for the Israelites for all their sins once a year. And Moses did as the Lord commanded him.
Leviticus17:1 And the Lord spoke to Moses
Leviticus17:2 Speak to Aaron
Leviticus17:3 If anyone of the house of Israel ritually slaughters an ox
Leviticus17:4 and does not bring it to the entrance of the tent of meeting to offer it as a gift to the Lord before the tabernacle of the Lord
Leviticus17:5 This is so that the Israelites may bring their sacrifices which they offer in the open field
Leviticus17:6 The priest shall sprinkle the blood on the altar of the Lord at the entrance of the tent of meeting
Leviticus17:7 They shall no more offer their sacrifices to goat demons
Leviticus17:8 You shall say to them: Any man from the house of Israel
Leviticus17:9 and does not bring it to the entrance of the tent of meeting to offer it to the Lord
Leviticus17:10 Whoever from the house of Israel
Leviticus17:11 For the life of the flesh is in the blood
Leviticus17:12 Therefore I said to the Israelites: No person among you shall eat blood
Leviticus17:13 Whoever from the Israelites
Leviticus17:14 For the life of every creature is its blood; in its blood is its life. Therefore I said to the Israelites: You shall not eat the blood of any creature
Leviticus17:15 Every person who eats that which died of itself
Leviticus17:16 But if he does not wash them or bathe his body
Leviticus18:1 The Lord spoke to Moses
Leviticus18:2 Speak to the Israelites
Leviticus18:3 After the practices of the land of Egypt
Leviticus18:4 You shall follow My decrees and keep My ordinances to walk in them: I am the Lord your God.
Leviticus18:5 You shall therefore keep My statutes and My judgments
Leviticus18:6 None of you shall approach any of his near relatives to have relations: I am the Lord.
Leviticus18:7 You shall not have relations with your father or have relations with your mother. She is your mother. You shall not have relations with her.
Leviticus18:8 You shall not have relations with your father’s wife
Leviticus18:9 You shall not have relations with your sister
Leviticus18:10 You shall not have relations with your son’s daughter or your daughter’s daughter
Leviticus18:11 You shall not have relations with your father’s wife’s daughter who is raised in your father’s family
Leviticus18:12 You shall not have relations with your father’s sister
Leviticus18:13 You shall not have relations with your mother’s sister
Leviticus18:14 You shall not expose the nakedness of your father’s brother. You shall not approach his wife for relations; she is your aunt.
Leviticus18:15 You shall not have relations with your daughter-in-law
Leviticus18:16 You shall not have relations with your brother’s wife
Leviticus18:17 You shall not have relations with a woman and her daughter
Leviticus18:18 And you shall not have a woman as a rival wife
Leviticus18:19 Also you shall not approach a woman to have relations as long as she is in her menstrual uncleanness.
Leviticus18:20 Moreover
Leviticus18:21 You shall not let any of your children be offered through the fire to Molech
Leviticus18:22 You shall not lie with a man as one does with a woman. It is an abomination.
Leviticus18:23 You shall not lie with an animal to defile yourself
Leviticus18:24 Do not defile yourselves in any of these ways
Leviticus18:25 And the land has become defiled; therefore I have punished its iniquity
Leviticus18:26 But you shall therefore keep My statutes and My decrees
Leviticus18:27 (for the people of the land
Leviticus18:28 lest the land vomit you out also when you defile it
Leviticus18:29 For whoever shall commit any of these abominations
Leviticus18:30 Therefore you shall keep My ordinances
Leviticus19:1 And the Lord spoke to Moses
Leviticus19:2 Speak to all the congregation of the Israelites
Leviticus19:3 Every one of you shall revere his mother and his father
Leviticus19:4 Do not turn to idols
Leviticus19:5 When you offer a sacrifice of peace offerings to the Lord
Leviticus19:6 It shall be eaten the same day you offer it or on the next day
Leviticus19:7 If it is eaten at all on the third day
Leviticus19:8 Therefore everyone who eats it shall bear his iniquity
Leviticus19:9 When you reap the harvest of your land
Leviticus19:10 You shall not glean bare your vineyard
Leviticus19:11 You shall not steal
Leviticus19:12 You shall not swear falsely by My name
Leviticus19:13 You shall not defraud your neighbor or rob him. The wages of him who is hired shall not stay with you all night until the morning.
Leviticus19:14 You shall not curse the deaf
Leviticus19:15 You shall do no unrighteousness in a court. You shall not be partial to the poor
Leviticus19:16 You shall not go around as a slanderer among your people
Leviticus19:17 You shall not hate your brother in your heart. You shall surely reason honestly with your neighbor
Leviticus19:18 You shall not take vengeance
Leviticus19:19 You shall keep My statutes. You shall not let your livestock breed with a different kind. You shall not sow your field with mixed seed
Leviticus19:20 Whoever lies with a slave woman who is betrothed to another man and not yet ransomed or given freedom
Leviticus19:21 He shall bring his guilt offering to the Lord at the entrance of the tent of meeting
Leviticus19:22 The priest shall make atonement for him with the ram of the guilt offering before the Lord for his sin which he has done
Leviticus19:23 When you come into the land and plant any kind of tree for food
Leviticus19:24 But in the fourth year all the fruit shall be holy and an offering of praise to the Lord.
Leviticus19:25 And in the fifth year you shall eat of the fruit that it may yield an increase to you: I am the Lord your God.
Leviticus19:26 You shall not eat anything with the blood in it
Leviticus19:27 You shall not round the corners of the hair on your head
Leviticus19:28 You shall not make any cuttings in your flesh for the dead
Leviticus19:29 Do not prostitute your daughter and cause her to be defiled
Leviticus19:30 You shall keep My Sabbaths and revere My sanctuary: I am the Lord.
Leviticus19:31 Do not turn to spirits through mediums or necromancers. Do not seek after them to be defiled by them: I am the Lord your God.
Leviticus19:32 You shall rise up before a gray head
Leviticus19:33 When a foreigner sojourns with you in your land
Leviticus19:34 The foreigner who dwells with you shall be to you as one born among you
Leviticus19:35 You shall do no unrighteousness in judgment regarding measures in length
Leviticus19:36 You shall have honest balances
Leviticus19:37 Therefore you shall observe all My statutes and all My judgments and do them: I am the Lord.
Leviticus20:1 Then the Lord spoke to Moses
Leviticus20:2 Again
Leviticus20:3 I will set My face against that man and will cut him off from among his people
Leviticus20:4 If the people of the land in any way hide their eyes from the man when he gives of his children to Molech and do not put him to death
Leviticus20:5 then I will set My face against that man and against his family; and I will cut them off from their people
Leviticus20:6 The person who turns to spirits through mediums and necromancers in order to whore after them
Leviticus20:7 Consecrate yourselves therefore
Leviticus20:8 You shall keep My statutes
Leviticus20:9 For anyone who curses his father or his mother shall be surely put to death. He has cursed his father or his mother
Leviticus20:10 If a man commits adultery with another man’s wife
Leviticus20:11 If a man lies with his father’s wife
Leviticus20:12 If a man lies with his daughter-in-law
Leviticus20:13 If a man lies with another man as with a woman
Leviticus20:14 If a man lies with a woman and also her mother
Leviticus20:15 If a man lies with an animal
Leviticus20:16 If a woman approaches an animal and lies with it
Leviticus20:17 If a man lies with his sister
Leviticus20:18 If a man lies with a woman during her menstrual period and exposes her nakedness
Leviticus20:19 You shall not have relations with your mother’s sister or your father’s sister
Leviticus20:20 If a man lies with his uncle’s wife
Leviticus20:21 If a man lies with his brother’s wife
Leviticus20:22 You shall therefore keep all My statutes and all My decrees and do them
Leviticus20:23 You shall not walk in the customs of the nation that I am driving out before you
Leviticus20:24 But I have said to you: You shall inherit their land
Leviticus20:25 You shall therefore make distinction between the clean animal from the unclean
Leviticus20:26 You shall be holy unto Me
Leviticus20:27 A man or woman who speaks to spirits as a medium or necromancer shall surely be put to death. They shall stone them. Their blood guilt shall be upon them.

Leviticus22:4 If a man of the offspring of Aaron is a leper or has a discharge
Leviticus22:5 or whoever touches any crawling thing whereby he may be made unclean
Leviticus22:6 the person who has touched any such thing shall be unclean until evening
Leviticus22:7 When the sun goes down
Leviticus22:8 That which dies of itself or is torn by animals he shall not eat
Leviticus22:9 They shall therefore keep My ordinance
Leviticus22:10 There shall be no outsider who eats of a holy thing
Leviticus22:11 But if a priest buys a person with his money
Leviticus22:12 If the priest’s daughter marries an outsider
Leviticus22:13 But if the priest’s daughter is a widow or divorced
Leviticus22:14 If a man eats the holy thing unwittingly
Leviticus22:15 They shall not defile the holy things of the Israelites
Leviticus22:16 and cause them to suffer and to bear the iniquity of guilt when they eat their holy things

Leviticus22:18 Speak to Aaron
Leviticus22:19 then if it is to be accepted for you
Leviticus22:20 But whatever has a blemish you shall not offer
Leviticus22:21 Whoever offers a sacrifice of peace offerings to the Lord to fulfill his vow or a freewill offering
Leviticus22:22 Blind
Leviticus22:23 A herd animal or a flock animal that has a limb that is too long or short you may offer for a freewill offering
Leviticus22:24 You shall not offer to the Lord an animal that is bruised or crushed or torn or cut; nor shall you make any offering of them in your land.
Leviticus22:25 Nor from a foreigner shall you offer an animal as the food of your God
Leviticus22:26 The Lord spoke to Moses
Leviticus22:27 When an ox
Leviticus22:28 But you shall not slaughter on the same day an ox or a sheep and her young.
Leviticus22:29 When you offer a sacrifice of thanksgiving to the Lord
Leviticus22:30 On the same day it shall be eaten. You shall leave none of it until the next day: I am the Lord.
Leviticus22:31 Therefore you shall keep My commandments and do them: I am the Lord.
Leviticus22:32 You shall not defile My holy name
