Leviticus23:1 And the Lord spoke to Moses
Leviticus23:2 Speak to the Israelites
Leviticus23:3 For six days work shall be done
Leviticus23:4 These are the appointed feasts of the Lord
Leviticus23:5 On the fourteenth day of the first month at evening is the Lord ’s Passover.
Leviticus23:6 On the fifteenth day of the same month is the Feast of Unleavened Bread to the Lord. For seven days you must eat unleavened bread.
Leviticus23:7 On the first day you shall have a holy convocation. You shall do no regular work.
Leviticus23:8 But you shall offer a food offering made by fire to the Lord for seven days. On the seventh day is a holy convocation. You shall do no regular work.
Leviticus23:9 The Lord spoke to Moses
Leviticus23:10 Speak to the Israelites
Leviticus23:11 And he shall wave the sheaf before the Lord so that you may be accepted. On the day after the Sabbath the priest shall wave it.
Leviticus23:12 You shall offer that day when you wave the sheaf a year-old male lamb without blemish for a burnt offering to the Lord.
Leviticus23:13 The grain offering shall be two-tenths of an ephah of wheat flour mixed with oil
Leviticus23:14 You shall eat neither bread nor grain
Leviticus23:15 You shall count seven full weeks from the next day after the Sabbath
Leviticus23:16 You shall count fifty days to the day after the seventh Sabbath; then you shall offer a new grain offering to the Lord.
Leviticus23:17 You shall bring out of your habitations two wave loaves of two-tenths of an ephah. They shall be of wheat flour
Leviticus23:18 You shall offer with the bread seven lambs without blemish of the first year
Leviticus23:19 Then you shall sacrifice one male goat for a sin offering
Leviticus23:20 The priest shall wave them with the bread of the firstfruits for a wave offering before the Lord with the two lambs. They shall be holy to the Lord for the priest.
Leviticus23:21 You shall make a proclamation on the same day and shall hold a holy convocation. You shall do no regular work. It shall be a perpetual statute in all your dwellings throughout your generations.
Leviticus23:22 When you reap the harvest of your land
Leviticus23:23 The Lord spoke to Moses
Leviticus23:24 Speak to the Israelites
Leviticus23:25 You shall do no regular work
Leviticus23:26 The Lord spoke to Moses
Leviticus23:27 Also on the tenth day of this seventh month there shall be the Day of Atonement. It shall be a holy convocation to you
Leviticus23:28 You shall do no work on that same day
Leviticus23:29 For whoever is not humbled on that same day
Leviticus23:30 And whoever does any work in that same day
Leviticus23:31 You shall do no manner of work. It shall be a perpetual statute throughout your generations in all your dwellings.
Leviticus23:32 It shall be to you a sabbath of complete rest
Leviticus23:33 The Lord spoke to Moses
Leviticus23:34 Speak to the Israelites
Leviticus23:35 On the first day shall be a holy convocation. You shall do no regular work.
Leviticus23:36 For seven days you shall offer food offerings made by fire to the Lord. On the eighth day it shall be a holy convocation to you
Leviticus23:37 These are the appointed feasts of the Lord
Leviticus23:38 besides the Sabbaths of the Lord
Leviticus23:39 On the fifteenth day of the seventh month
Leviticus23:40 You shall take on the first day the branches of majestic trees—branches of palm trees
Leviticus23:41 You shall keep it as a feast to the Lord for seven days in the year. It shall be a perpetual statute in your generations. You shall celebrate it in the seventh month.
Leviticus23:42 You shall dwell in booths for seven days. All who are native Israelites shall dwell in booths
Leviticus23:43 that your generations may know that I made the Israelites dwell in booths when I brought them out of the land of Egypt: I am the Lord your God.
Leviticus23:44 Moses declared to the Israelites the feasts of the Lord.
Leviticus24:1 And the Lord spoke to Moses
Leviticus24:2 Command the Israelites that they bring to you pure olive oil beaten for the lamp
Leviticus24:3 Outside the veil of the sanctuary
Leviticus24:4 He shall arrange the lamps continually on the pure gold lampstand before the Lord.
Leviticus24:5 You shall take wheat flour and bake twelve cakes. Two-tenths of an ephah shall be in each cake.
Leviticus24:6 You shall set them in two rows
Leviticus24:7 You shall put pure frankincense on each row
Leviticus24:8 Every Sabbath he shall set it in order continually before the Lord
Leviticus24:9 It shall be for Aaron and his sons
Leviticus24:10 The son of an Israelite woman
Leviticus24:11 And the Israelite woman’s son blasphemed the name of the Lord and cursed. And they brought him to Moses. (His mother’s name was Shelomith
Leviticus24:12 And they put him in custody
Leviticus24:13 The Lord spoke to Moses
Leviticus24:14 Bring outside the camp him who has cursed
Leviticus24:15 You shall speak to the Israelites
Leviticus24:16 Whoever blasphemes the name of the Lord
Leviticus24:17 Whoever kills any man shall surely be put to death.
Leviticus24:18 Whoever kills an animal shall make restitution
Leviticus24:19 If anyone causes injury to his neighbor
Leviticus24:20 fracture for fracture
Leviticus24:21 Whoever kills an animal shall make restitution. And whoever kills a man shall be put to death.
Leviticus24:22 You shall have one manner of law for the foreigner as for the native
Leviticus24:23 So Moses spoke to the Israelites
Leviticus25:1 Then the Lord spoke to Moses on Mount Sinai
Leviticus25:2 Speak to the Israelites
Leviticus25:3 For six years you shall sow your field
Leviticus25:4 but in the seventh year there shall be a sabbath of complete rest for the land
Leviticus25:5 That which grows by itself from your harvest you shall not reap
Leviticus25:6 The sabbath produce of the land shall be food for you: for you
Leviticus25:7 and for your livestock
Leviticus25:8 You shall count seven sabbath weeks of years
Leviticus25:9 Then you shall sound the horn blasts on the tenth day of the seventh month. On the Day of Atonement you shall make the sound of the horn throughout all your land.
Leviticus25:10 You shall consecrate the fiftieth year
Leviticus25:11 That fiftieth year will be a Jubilee for you. You shall neither sow
Leviticus25:12 For it is the Jubilee. It shall be holy to you. You shall eat the produce of the field.
Leviticus25:13 In the Year of Jubilee you shall return to your property.
Leviticus25:14 If you sell anything to your neighbor or buy anything from your neighbor
Leviticus25:15 According to the number of years after the Jubilee you shall pay your neighbor
Leviticus25:16 According to the increase of years you shall increase the price
Leviticus25:17 You shall not therefore oppress one another
Leviticus25:18 Therefore you shall do My statutes
Leviticus25:19 The land shall yield its fruit
Leviticus25:20 If you shall say
Leviticus25:21 then I will command My blessing upon you in the sixth year
Leviticus25:22 You shall sow in the eighth year
Leviticus25:23 The land shall not be permanently sold
Leviticus25:24 In all the land of your possession you shall grant a redemption for the land.
Leviticus25:25 If your brother becomes poor and has sold some of his possession
Leviticus25:26 If the man has none to redeem it
Leviticus25:27 then let him count the years since the sale and pay back the balance to the man to whom he sold it
Leviticus25:28 But if he is not able to restore it to himself
Leviticus25:29 If a man sells a house in a walled city
Leviticus25:30 If it is not redeemed within the time of a full year
Leviticus25:31 But the houses of the villages that have no wall around them shall be counted as the fields of the land. They may be redeemed
Leviticus25:32 For the cities of the Levites
Leviticus25:33 If a Levite purchases back the house that was sold in the city of his possession
Leviticus25:34 But the fields of the land of their cities may not be sold
Leviticus25:35 If your brother becomes poor and cannot maintain himself with you
Leviticus25:36 Take no usury or interest from him; but fear your God
Leviticus25:37 You shall not lend him your money at interest
Leviticus25:38 I am the Lord your God
Leviticus25:39 If your brother who dwells near you becomes poor and sells himself to you
Leviticus25:40 But as a hired servant and as a sojourner he shall be with you. He shall serve you until the Year of Jubilee.
Leviticus25:41 And then he shall depart from you
Leviticus25:42 For they are My servants
Leviticus25:43 You shall not rule over him with harshness
Leviticus25:44 Both your male and female slaves
Leviticus25:45 Moreover of the foreigners who sojourn among you and of their families who are with you
Leviticus25:46 You may take them as an inheritance for your children after you
Leviticus25:47 If a sojourner or foreigner becomes rich by you
Leviticus25:48 then after he is sold he may be redeemed again. One of his brothers may redeem him
Leviticus25:49 or either his uncle or his cousin may redeem him
Leviticus25:50 He shall calculate with him who bought him from the year that he was sold to the Year of Jubilee
Leviticus25:51 If there are still many years left until the Jubilee
Leviticus25:52 If there remain but a few years until the Year of Jubilee
Leviticus25:53 As a yearly hired servant he shall be treated
Leviticus25:54 If he is not redeemed in these years
Leviticus25:55 For to Me the Israelites are servants. They are My servants whom I brought out of the land of Egypt: I am the Lord your God.
Leviticus26:1 You shall not make for yourselves idols; neither set up a carved image nor a standing stone
Leviticus26:2 You shall keep My Sabbaths and reverence My sanctuary: I am the Lord.
Leviticus26:3 If you walk in My statutes and keep My commandments and do them
Leviticus26:4 then I will give you rain in due season
Leviticus26:5 Your threshing shall last till the grape harvest
Leviticus26:6 I will give peace in the land
Leviticus26:7 You shall chase your enemies
Leviticus26:8 Five of you shall chase a hundred
Leviticus26:9 I will turn toward you and make you fruitful and multiply you
Leviticus26:10 You shall eat the old harvest long stored
Leviticus26:11 I will set My tabernacle among you
Leviticus26:12 I will walk among you
Leviticus26:13 I am the Lord your God
Leviticus26:14 But if you will not listen to Me
Leviticus26:15 if you despise My statutes
Leviticus26:16 then I will do this to you: I will visit you with terror
Leviticus26:17 I will set My face against you
Leviticus26:18 If you will not yet listen to Me after all this
Leviticus26:19 I will break the pride of your power
Leviticus26:20 Your strength shall be spent in vain
Leviticus26:21 If you continue to walk contrary to Me and will not listen to Me
Leviticus26:22 I will also send wild beasts among you
Leviticus26:23 And if by these things you are not turned to Me
Leviticus26:24 then I will also walk contrary to you and will punish you yet seven times for your sins.
Leviticus26:25 I will bring a sword upon you that shall extract vengeance for My covenant. And when you are gathered together within your cities
Leviticus26:26 When I have broken the supply of your bread
Leviticus26:27 If you will not listen to Me for all this
Leviticus26:28 then I will walk contrary to you also in fury
Leviticus26:29 You shall eat the flesh of your sons and the flesh of your daughters.
Leviticus26:30 I will destroy your high places
Leviticus26:31 I will make your cities a waste and bring your sanctuaries to desolation
Leviticus26:32 I will bring the land into desolation
Leviticus26:33 I will scatter you among the nations and I will draw out a sword after you. And your land shall be desolate and your cities a waste.
Leviticus26:34 Then the land shall enjoy its sabbaths as long as it lies desolate
Leviticus26:35 As long as it lies desolate it shall rest because it did not rest during your sabbaths when you lived upon it.
Leviticus26:36 And on those who are left alive of you I will send faintness into their hearts in the lands of their enemies; and the sound of a shaken leaf shall make them flee
Leviticus26:37 They shall fall one upon one another
Leviticus26:38 You shall perish among the nations
Leviticus26:39 And those who are left of you shall rot away in their iniquity in your enemies’ lands
Leviticus26:40 But if they confess their iniquity
Leviticus26:41 and that I also have walked contrary to them and have brought them into the land of their enemies; if then their uncircumcised hearts are humbled
Leviticus26:42 then will I remember My covenant with Jacob
Leviticus26:43 But the land shall be abandoned by them
Leviticus26:44 Yet for all that
Leviticus26:45 But for their sake I will remember the covenant with their fathers
Leviticus26:46 These are the statutes and judgments and laws that the Lord made between Himself and the Israelites on Mount Sinai by the hand of Moses.
Leviticus27:1 And the Lord spoke to Moses
Leviticus27:2 Speak to the Israelites
Leviticus27:3 then the equivalent value of a male from twenty to sixty years old shall be fifty shekels of silver
Leviticus27:4 If the person is a female
Leviticus27:5 If the person is five to twenty years old
Leviticus27:6 If the person is one month to five years old
Leviticus27:7 If the person is sixty years old or older
Leviticus27:8 But if he is too poor to afford the equivalent value
Leviticus27:9 And if it be an animal
Leviticus27:10 He shall not exchange it nor substitute it
Leviticus27:11 If it is any sort of unclean animal that is not permitted as an offering to the Lord
Leviticus27:12 And the priest shall set its value
Leviticus27:13 But if he plans on redeeming it
Leviticus27:14 When a man consecrates his house as holy to the Lord
Leviticus27:15 If he who consecrates the house should redeem it
Leviticus27:16 If a man shall consecrate to the Lord some of his land
Leviticus27:17 If he consecrates his field from the Year of Jubilee
Leviticus27:18 but if he consecrates his field after the Jubilee
Leviticus27:19 And if he who consecrated the field plans on redeeming it
Leviticus27:20 If he does not redeem the field
Leviticus27:21 But when the field is released in the Jubilee
Leviticus27:22 If a man consecrates to the Lord a field that he purchased
Leviticus27:23 then the priest shall calculate for him the amount of the valuation
Leviticus27:24 In the Year of Jubilee the field shall return to the one from whom it was bought
Leviticus27:25 All your estimations shall be according to the shekel of the sanctuary: Twenty gerahs shall be one shekel.
Leviticus27:26 A firstborn of animals
Leviticus27:27 If it is among the unclean animals
Leviticus27:28 Anything that a man shall devote to the Lord from all that he has
Leviticus27:29 No one devoted of men
Leviticus27:30 Any tithe of the land
Leviticus27:31 If a man plans on redeeming some of his tithe
Leviticus27:32 Any tithe of herd or flock
Leviticus27:33 A person shall not differentiate between good or bad
Leviticus27:34 These are the commandments that the Lord commanded Moses for the Israelites on Mount Sinai.
Numbers1:1 And the Lord spoke to Moses in the Wilderness of Sinai in the tent of meeting on the first day of the second month in the second year after they went out from the land of Egypt
Numbers1:2 Take the sum of all the congregation of the Israelites
Numbers1:3 from twenty years old and upward
Numbers1:4 With you there will be a man of every tribe
Numbers1:5 These are the names of the men who will stand with you: of the tribe of Reuben
Numbers1:6 of Simeon
Numbers1:7 of Judah
Numbers1:8 of Issachar
Numbers1:9 of Zebulun
Numbers1:10 of the sons of Joseph: of Ephraim
Numbers1:11 of Benjamin
Numbers1:12 of Dan
Numbers1:13 of Asher
Numbers1:14 of Gad
Numbers1:15 of Naphtali
Numbers1:16 These were the renowned of the congregation
Numbers1:17 Moses and Aaron took these men who had been mentioned by name
Numbers1:18 and they assembled all the congregation on the first day of the second month
Numbers1:19 As the Lord commanded Moses
Numbers1:20 The sons of Reuben
Numbers1:21 those who were numbered of them
Numbers1:22 Of the sons of Simeon
Numbers1:23 those who were numbered of them
Numbers1:24 Of the sons of Gad
Numbers1:25 those who were numbered of them
Numbers1:26 Of the sons of Judah
Numbers1:27 those who were numbered of them
Numbers1:28 Of the sons of Issachar
Numbers1:29 those who were numbered of them
Numbers1:30 Of the sons of Zebulun
Numbers1:31 those who were numbered of them
Numbers1:32 Of the sons of Joseph
Numbers1:33 those who were numbered of them
Numbers1:34 Of the sons of Manasseh
Numbers1:35 those who were numbered of them
Numbers1:36 Of the sons of Benjamin
Numbers1:37 those who were numbered of them
Numbers1:38 Of the sons of Dan
Numbers1:39 those who were numbered of them
Numbers1:40 Of the sons of Asher
Numbers1:41 those who were numbered of them
Numbers1:42 Of the sons of Naphtali
Numbers1:43 those who were numbered of them
Numbers1:44 These are those who were numbered
Numbers1:45 So were all those who were numbered of the Israelites
Numbers1:46 Even all they who were numbered were six hundred and three thousand five hundred and fifty.
Numbers1:47 But the Levites after the tribe of their fathers were not numbered among them.
Numbers1:48 For the Lord had spoken to Moses
Numbers1:49 Only you will not number the tribe of Levi
Numbers1:50 but you will appoint the Levites over the tent of the testimony
Numbers1:51 When the tabernacle sets out
Numbers1:52 The Israelites will pitch their tents
Numbers1:53 But the Levites will camp around the tabernacle of the testimony
Numbers1:54 Thus the Israelites did according to all that the Lord commanded Moses.
Numbers2:1 And the Lord spoke to Moses and to Aaron
Numbers2:2 Every man of the Israelites will camp by his own standard
Numbers2:3 On the east side toward the rising of the sun the standard of the camp of Judah will camp throughout their armies
Numbers2:4 His host
Numbers2:5 Those who camp next to him will be the tribe of Issachar
Numbers2:6 His host
Numbers2:7 Then comes the tribe of Zebulun
Numbers2:8 His host
Numbers2:9 All who were numbered throughout the armies in the camp of Judah were one hundred and eighty-six thousand four hundred. They will march out first.
Numbers2:10 On the south side will be the standard of the camp of Reuben according to their armies
Numbers2:11 His host
Numbers2:12 Those who camp by him will be the tribe of Simeon
Numbers2:13 His host
Numbers2:14 Then comes the tribe of Gad
Numbers2:15 His host
Numbers2:16 All who were numbered in the camp of Reuben were one hundred and fifty-one thousand four hundred and fifty
Numbers2:17 Then the tent of meeting will set out with the camp of the Levites in the middle of the camp. As they camp
Numbers2:18 On the west side will be the standard of the camp of Ephraim according to their armies
Numbers2:19 His host
Numbers2:20 By him will be the tribe of Manasseh
Numbers2:21 His host
Numbers2:22 Then comes the tribe of Benjamin
Numbers2:23 His host
Numbers2:24 All who were numbered of the camp of Ephraim were one hundred and eight thousand one hundred
Numbers2:25 The standard of the camp of Dan will be on the north side by their armies
Numbers2:26 His host
Numbers2:27 Those who camp by him will be the tribe of Asher
Numbers2:28 His host
Numbers2:29 Then the tribe of Naphtali
Numbers2:30 His host
Numbers2:31 All they who were numbered in the camp of Dan were one hundred and fifty-seven thousand six hundred. They will set out in the back with their standards.
Numbers2:32 These are those who were numbered of the Israelites by the house of their fathers. All those who were numbered of the camps throughout their hosts were six hundred and three thousand five hundred and fifty.
Numbers2:33 But the Levites were not numbered among the Israelites
Numbers2:34 The Israelites did according to all that the Lord commanded Moses. So they camped by their standards
Numbers3:1 These also are the generations of Aaron and Moses in the day that the Lord spoke with Moses in Mount Sinai.
Numbers3:2 These are the names of the sons of Aaron: Nadab the firstborn
Numbers3:3 These are the names of the sons of Aaron
Numbers3:4 Nadab and Abihu died before the Lord when they brought strange fire before the Lord in the Wilderness of Sinai
Numbers3:5 The Lord spoke to Moses
Numbers3:6 Bring the tribe of Levi near
Numbers3:7 They will keep his directives
Numbers3:8 They will keep all the instruments of the tent of meeting
Numbers3:9 You will give the Levites to Aaron and to his sons. They are wholly given to him from the Israelites.
Numbers3:10 You will appoint Aaron and his sons
Numbers3:11 The Lord spoke to Moses
Numbers3:12 Behold
Numbers3:13 because all the firstborn are Mine
Numbers3:14 The Lord spoke to Moses in the Wilderness of Sinai
Numbers3:15 Count the Levites by the house of their fathers
Numbers3:16 Moses numbered them according to the word of the Lord
Numbers3:17 These were the sons of Levi by their names: Gershon
Numbers3:18 These are the names of the sons of Gershon by their families: Libni and Shimei;
Numbers3:19 the sons of Kohath by their families: Amram
Numbers3:20 the sons of Merari by their families: Mahli and Mushi. These are the families of the Levites according to the house of their fathers.
Numbers3:21 From Gershon was the family of the Libnites and the family of the Shimeites. These are the families of the Gershonites.
Numbers3:22 Those who were numbered of them
Numbers3:23 The families of the Gershonites were to camp behind the west side of the tabernacle.
Numbers3:24 The chief of the house of the father of the Gershonites was Eliasaph the son of Lael.
Numbers3:25 The charge of the sons of Gershon in the tent of meeting included the tabernacle
Numbers3:26 the hangings of the court
Numbers3:27 Of Kohath was the family of the Amramites
Numbers3:28 The number of all the males
Numbers3:29 The families of the sons of Kohath were to camp on the south side of the tabernacle.
Numbers3:30 The chief of the house of the father of the families of the Kohathites was Elizaphan the son of Uzziel.
Numbers3:31 Their duty included the ark
Numbers3:32 Eleazar the son of Aaron the priest was to be chief over the leaders of the Levites and to have the oversight of those who kept the guard of the sanctuary.
Numbers3:33 Of Merari was the family of the Mahlites and the family of the Mushites. These are the families of Merari.
Numbers3:34 Those who were numbered of them
Numbers3:35 The chief of the house of the father of the families of Merari was Zuriel the son of Abihail. These were to camp on the north side of the tabernacle.
Numbers3:36 Under the oversight and guard of the sons of Merari were the frames of the tabernacle
Numbers3:37 and the pillars of the court all around
Numbers3:38 But those who were to camp before the tabernacle toward the east
Numbers3:39 All who were numbered of the Levites
Numbers3:40 The Lord said to Moses: Number all the firstborn of the males of the Israelites from one month old and older and take the number of their names.
Numbers3:41 You will take the Levites for Me (I am the Lord) instead of all the firstborn among the Israelites
Numbers3:42 Moses numbered
Numbers3:43 All the firstborn males
Numbers3:44 The Lord spoke to Moses
Numbers3:45 Take the Levites instead of all the firstborn among the Israelites
Numbers3:46 For those who are to be redeemed of the two hundred and seventy-three of the firstborn of the Israelites
Numbers3:47 you will take five shekels apiece by the poll; according to the shekel of the sanctuary you will take them (the shekel is twenty gerahs).
Numbers3:48 And you will give the money
Numbers3:49 Moses took the redemption money of those who were over and above those who were redeemed by the Levites.
Numbers3:50 Of the firstborn of the Israelites he took the money
Numbers3:51 And Moses gave the money of those who were redeemed to Aaron and to his sons
Numbers4:1 And the Lord spoke to Moses and to Aaron
Numbers4:2 Take the sum of the sons of Kohath from among the sons of Levi
Numbers4:3 from thirty years old and older
Numbers4:4 This will be the service of the sons of Kohath in the tent of meeting
Numbers4:5 And when the camp sets out
Numbers4:6 and will put on it the covering of porpoise skins
Numbers4:7 Upon the table of showbread they will spread a cloth of blue
Numbers4:8 And they will spread on them a cloth of scarlet
Numbers4:9 They will take a cloth of blue
Numbers4:10 And they will put it and all its vessels within a covering of porpoise skins and will put it on a carrying pole.
Numbers4:11 Upon the golden altar they will spread a cloth of blue
Numbers4:12 And they will take all the instruments of ministry
Numbers4:13 And they will take away the ashes from the altar and spread a purple cloth on it.
Numbers4:14 They shall put on it all the instruments with which they minister there
Numbers4:15 When Aaron and his sons have finished covering the sanctuary
Numbers4:16 To the office of Eleazar
Numbers4:17 The Lord spoke to Moses and to Aaron
Numbers4:18 Do not cut the tribe of the families of the Kohathites from among the Levites.
Numbers4:19 But do this to them
Numbers4:20 But they will not go in to see when the holy things are covered
Numbers4:21 The Lord spoke to Moses
Numbers4:22 Take also the sum of the sons of Gershon
Numbers4:23 From thirty years old and older to fifty years old you will number them
Numbers4:24 This is the service of the families of the Gershonites
Numbers4:25 And they will carry the curtains of the tabernacle
Numbers4:26 and the hangings of the courtyard
Numbers4:27 At the appointment of Aaron and his sons will be all the service of the sons of the Gershonites
Numbers4:28 This is the service of the families of the sons of Gershon in the tent of meeting. And their charge will be under the hand of Ithamar the son of Aaron the priest.
Numbers4:29 As for the sons of Merari
Numbers4:30 From thirty years old and older
Numbers4:31 This is the charge of their burden
Numbers4:32 and the pillars of the court round about
Numbers4:33 This is the service of the families of the sons of Merari
Numbers4:34 Moses
Numbers4:35 from thirty years old and older to fifty years old
Numbers4:36 And those who were numbered by their families were two thousand seven hundred and fifty.
Numbers4:37 These were the ones listed of the families of the Kohathites
Numbers4:38 Those who were numbered of the sons of Gershon
Numbers4:39 from thirty years old and older to fifty years old
Numbers4:40 even those who were numbered of them
Numbers4:41 These were the ones listed by the families of the sons of Gershon
Numbers4:42 Those who were numbered of the families of the sons of Merari
Numbers4:43 from thirty years old and older to fifty years old
Numbers4:44 even those who were numbered by their families
Numbers4:45 These were the ones listed of the families of the sons of Merari
Numbers4:46 All those who were numbered of the Levites
Numbers4:47 from thirty years old and older to fifty years old
Numbers4:48 even those who were numbered were eight thousand five hundred and eighty.
Numbers4:49 According to the commandment of the Lord they were numbered by the hand of Moses
Numbers5:1 And the Lord spoke to Moses
Numbers5:2 Command the Israelites that they put out of the camp every leper
Numbers5:3 Both male and female you will put out. You will put them outside the camp
Numbers5:4 The Israelites did so
Numbers5:5 The Lord spoke to Moses
Numbers5:6 Tell the Israelites: When a man or woman commits any sin against another
Numbers5:7 and shall confess his sin which he has committed
Numbers5:8 But if the man has no relative to repay the offense to
Numbers5:9 Every offering of all the holy things of the Israelites
Numbers5:10 Every man’s holy things will be his. Whatever any man gives the priest
Numbers5:11 The Lord spoke to Moses
Numbers5:12 Speak to the Israelites and say to them: If any man’s wife goes astray
Numbers5:13 and a man lies with her carnally
Numbers5:14 and if the spirit of jealousy comes on him
Numbers5:15 then the man shall bring his wife to the priest and he shall bring her offering for her
Numbers5:16 The priest will bring her near
Numbers5:17 And the priest will take holy water in an earthen vessel
Numbers5:18 And the priest will set the woman before the Lord
Numbers5:19 And the priest will charge her by an oath
Numbers5:20 But if you have gone astray to another instead of your husband
Numbers5:21 then the priest will charge the woman with an oath of cursing
Numbers5:22 And this water that causes the curse will go into your bowels
Numbers5:23 The priest will write these curses in a book
Numbers5:24 And he will cause the woman to drink the bitter water that causes the curse. And the water that causes the curse will enter into her and become bitter.
Numbers5:25 Then the priest will take the grain offering of jealousy out of the woman’s hand and will wave the offering before the Lord and offer it on the altar.
Numbers5:26 And the priest will take a handful of the offering
Numbers5:27 When he has made her to drink the water
Numbers5:28 If the woman is not defiled
Numbers5:29 This is the law of jealousies
Numbers5:30 or when the spirit of jealousy comes on him
Numbers5:31 Then the man will be guiltless from iniquity
Numbers6:1 And the Lord spoke to Moses
Numbers6:2 Speak to the Israelites and say to them: When either a man or woman will make a hard vow
Numbers6:3 he will separate himself from wine and strong drink and will drink no vinegar of wine
Numbers6:4 All the days of his separation he will eat nothing that is made of the grapevine
Numbers6:5 All the days of the vow of his separation no razor will come on his head until the days are fulfilled in which he separates himself to the Lord. He will be holy and will let the locks of the hair of his head grow.
Numbers6:6 All the days that he separates himself to the Lord he will not approach a dead body.
Numbers6:7 He will not defile himself for his father or for his mother
Numbers6:8 All the days of his separation he is holy to the Lord.
Numbers6:9 If any man dies very suddenly beside him
Numbers6:10 On the eighth day he will bring two turtledoves or two young pigeons to the priest
Numbers6:11 And the priest will offer one as a sin offering and the other as a burnt offering and make an atonement for him
Numbers6:12 He will consecrate to the Lord the days of his separation and bring a male lamb in its first year as a guilt offering. But the previous days will be lost because his separation was defiled.
Numbers6:13 This is the law of the Nazirite. When the days of his separation are fulfilled
Numbers6:14 And he will offer his offering to the Lord
Numbers6:15 a basket of unleavened bread
Numbers6:16 The priest will bring them before the Lord and will offer his sin offering and his burnt offering.
Numbers6:17 And he will offer the ram as a sacrifice of a peace offering to the Lord with the basket of unleavened bread. The priest will offer also his grain offering and his drink offering.
Numbers6:18 The Nazirite will shave his consecrated head at the door of the tent of meeting
Numbers6:19 The priest will take the cooked shoulder of the ram
Numbers6:20 And the priest will wave them as a wave offering before the Lord. This is holy for the priest
Numbers6:21 This is the law of the Nazirite who has vowed to the Lord
Numbers6:22 The Lord spoke to Moses
Numbers6:23 Speak to Aaron and to his sons
Numbers6:24 The Lord bless you and keep you;
Numbers6:25 the Lord make His face to shine upon you
Numbers6:26 the Lord lift His countenance upon you
Numbers6:27 They will put My name upon the Israelites
Numbers7:1 And it was on the day that Moses had fully set up the tabernacle and anointed it and sanctified it and all its instruments
Numbers7:2 that the leaders of Israel
Numbers7:3 And they brought their offering before the Lord
Numbers7:4 The Lord spoke to Moses

Numbers8:7 Thus you will do to them to cleanse them: Splash purifying water on them

Numbers8:9 You will bring the Levites before the tent of meeting. And you will gather the whole assembly of the Israelites together.
Numbers8:10 And you will bring the Levites before the Lord. And the Israelites will put their hands on the Levites.

Numbers19:1 And the Lord spoke to Moses and to Aaron
Numbers19:2 This is the statute of the law which the Lord has commanded
Numbers19:3 and you will give it to Eleazar the priest
Numbers19:4 And Eleazar the priest will take from its blood with his finger and sprinkle some of its blood directly before the tent of meeting seven times.
Numbers19:5 Then it will be burned in his sight. Its hide and its flesh and its blood
Numbers19:6 And the priest will take cedar wood
Numbers19:7 Then the priest will wash his clothes
Numbers19:8 He who burns it will wash his clothes in water
Numbers19:9 A man who is clean will gather the ashes of the heifer and deposit them outside the camp in a clean place
Numbers19:10 He that gathers the ashes of the heifer will wash his clothes and be unclean until evening
Numbers19:11 He that touches the dead body of any man will be unclean seven days.
Numbers19:12 He will make himself clean on the third day
Numbers19:13 Whoever touches the body of any man who is dead and does not purify himself defiles the tabernacle of the Lord
Numbers19:14 This is the law
Numbers19:15 Every open vessel which has no covering fastened on it is unclean.
Numbers19:16 Whoever in the field touches one who is slain with a sword
Numbers19:17 For an unclean person they will take from the ashes of the burnt sin offering
Numbers19:18 A clean person will take hyssop
Numbers19:19 The clean person will splash on the unclean person on the third day and on the seventh day. And on the seventh day he shall purify himself
Numbers19:20 But the man who is unclean and does not purify himself
Numbers19:21 It will be an eternal statute to them. He that sprinkles the water of purification will wash his clothes
Numbers19:22 Whatever the unclean person touches will be unclean

Numbers28:1 And the Lord spoke to Moses
Numbers28:2 Command the Israelites and say to them: My offering
Numbers28:3 You will say to them: This is the fire offering which you will bring near to the Lord: two male lambs in their first year
Numbers28:4 The one lamb you will offer in the morning
Numbers28:5 and one-tenth of an ephah of flour as a grain offering
Numbers28:6 It is a regular burnt offering
Numbers28:7 Its drink offering will be one-fourth of a hin for the one lamb. In a holy place you will pour the strong wine to the Lord as a drink offering.
Numbers28:8 The other lamb you will offer at evening. As the morning grain offering and its drink offering
Numbers28:9 On the Sabbath day two lambs in their first year
Numbers28:10 this is the burnt offering of every Sabbath
Numbers28:11 At the beginnings of your months you will offer a burnt offering to the Lord: two young bulls
Numbers28:12 three-tenths of an ephah of flour as a grain offering
Numbers28:13 and one-tenth of an ephah of flour mixed with oil as a grain offering for one lamb; as a burnt offering of a pleasing aroma
Numbers28:14 Their drink offerings will be one-half a hin of wine for a bull
Numbers28:15 Also one male goat as a sin offering to the Lord shall be offered
Numbers28:16 On the fourteenth day of the first month is the Passover of the Lord.
Numbers28:17 On the fifteenth day of this month is the feast. Unleavened bread will be eaten for seven days.
Numbers28:18 On the first day there will be a holy convocation. You will not do any ordinary work
Numbers28:19 but you will offer a sacrifice made by fire as a burnt offering to the Lord: two young bulls
Numbers28:20 And their grain offering will be of flour mixed with oil. Three-tenths of an ephah you will offer for a bull
Numbers28:21 One-tenth of an ephah you will offer for each of the seven lambs
Numbers28:22 and one goat as a sin offering
Numbers28:23 You will offer these in addition to the burnt offering in the morning
Numbers28:24 The same way you will offer daily
Numbers28:25 And on the seventh day you will have a holy assembly. You will not do any ordinary work.
Numbers28:26 And on the day of the firstfruits
Numbers28:27 But you will offer the burnt offering as a pleasing aroma to the Lord: two young bulls
Numbers28:28 and their grain offering of flour mixed with oil
Numbers28:29 one-tenth of an ephah for each of the seven lambs;
Numbers28:30 and one goat
Numbers28:31 Make sure they are without blemish. You will offer them with their drink offerings

Numbers30:4 and her father hears her vow and her bond with which she has bound herself

Numbers30:6 If she had a husband when she vowed
Numbers30:7 and her husband heard it and was silent to her in the day that he heard it
Numbers30:8 But if her husband restrains her on the day that he heard it

Numbers30:11 and her husband heard it and was silent to her and did not restrain her
Numbers30:12 But if her husband has clearly voided them on the day he heard them
Numbers30:13 Every vow
Numbers30:14 But if her husband says nothing to her from day to day
Numbers30:15 But if he clearly makes them void after he has heard them

Numbers33:1 These are the journeys of the Israelites
Numbers33:2 Moses recorded the starting points of their journeys at the command of the Lord. These are their journeys according to their starting points.

Numbers33:4 because the Egyptians buried all their firstborn
Numbers33:5 The Israelites set out from Rameses and camped in Sukkoth.
Numbers33:6 They set out from Sukkoth and camped in Etham
Numbers33:7 They set out from Etham and turned to Pi Hahiroth
Numbers33:8 They set out from before Pi Hahiroth
Numbers33:9 They set out from Marah
Numbers33:10 They set out from Elim and camped by the Red Sea.
Numbers33:11 They set out from the Red Sea and camped in the Wilderness of Sin.
Numbers33:12 They set out from the Wilderness of Sin and camped in Dophkah.
Numbers33:13 They set out from Dophkah and camped in Alush.
Numbers33:14 They set out from Alush and camped at Rephidim
Numbers33:15 They set out from Rephidim and camped in the Wilderness of Sinai.
Numbers33:16 They set out from the Wilderness of Sinai and camped at Kibroth Hattaavah.
Numbers33:17 They set out from Kibroth Hattaavah and camped at Hazeroth.
Numbers33:18 They set out from Hazeroth and camped in Rithmah.
Numbers33:19 They set out from Rithmah and camped at Rimmon Perez.
Numbers33:20 They set out from Rimmon Perez and camped in Libnah.
Numbers33:21 They set out from Libnah and camped at Rissah.

Numbers35:15 For the Israelites
Numbers35:16 If he strikes him with an instrument of iron
Numbers35:17 If he strikes him with a stone in hand
Numbers35:18 Or if he strikes him with a weapon of wood in hand
Numbers35:19 The avenger of blood himself will slay the murderer. When he meets him
Numbers35:20 But if he pushed him out of hatred
Numbers35:21 or in hatred struck him with his hand
Numbers35:22 But if he pushed him suddenly without hatred
Numbers35:23 or used a stone that may cause death
Numbers35:24 then the assembly will judge between the manslayer and the avenger of blood according to these judgments.
Numbers35:25 And the assembly will deliver the manslayer out of the hand of the avenger of blood
Numbers35:26 But if the manslayer will go out at any time beyond the border of the city of his refuge where he fled
Numbers35:27 and the avenger of blood finds him outside the borders of the city of his refuge
Numbers35:28 because the manslayer should have remained in the city of his refuge until the death of the high priest. But after the death of the high priest the manslayer may return to the land of his possession.
Numbers35:29 So these things will be for a statute of judgment to you throughout your generations in all your dwelling places.

Numbers35:31 And you will not take a ransom for the life of a murderer who is guilty of death
Numbers35:32 You will not take a ransom for him who fled to the city of his refuge
Numbers35:33 So you will not defile the land which you are in
Numbers35:34 So do not defile the land which you are dwelling in

Deuteronomy10:13 to keep the commandments of the Lord and His statutes which I am commanding you today for your good?
Deuteronomy10:14 Behold
Deuteronomy10:15 The Lord delighted only in your fathers
Deuteronomy10:16 Therefore
Deuteronomy10:17 For the Lord your God is the God of gods and Lord of lords
Deuteronomy10:18 He executes the judgment of the orphan and the widow and loves the foreigner
Deuteronomy10:19 Therefore
Deuteronomy10:20 You must fear the Lord your God. You must serve Him and cling to Him
Deuteronomy10:21 He is your praise
Deuteronomy10:22 Your fathers went down into Egypt with seventy people
Deuteronomy11:1 You must love the Lord your God and keep His charge
Deuteronomy11:2 Know this day that I am not speaking with your children who have not known and who have not seen the discipline of the Lord your God
Deuteronomy11:3 and His signs and His works which He did in the midst of Egypt to Pharaoh
Deuteronomy11:4 and what He did to the army of Egypt
Deuteronomy11:5 and what He did to you in the wilderness until you came to this place
Deuteronomy11:6 and what He did to Dathan and Abiram
Deuteronomy11:7 But your eyes have seen all the great deeds of the Lord which He did.
Deuteronomy11:8 Therefore you must keep all the commandments which I am commanding you today
Deuteronomy11:9 and that you may prolong your days in the land which the Lord swore to your fathers to give to them and to their descendants
Deuteronomy11:10 For the land which you are entering to possess is not like the land of Egypt from where you came
Deuteronomy11:11 but the land
Deuteronomy11:12 a land for which the Lord your God cares. The eyes of the Lord your God are always on it
Deuteronomy11:13 It will be
Deuteronomy11:14 then I will give you the rain of your land in its season
Deuteronomy11:15 I will provide grass in your fields for your livestock
Deuteronomy11:16 Take heed to yourselves that your heart be not deceived
Deuteronomy11:17 Then the Lord ’s wrath will be inflamed against you
Deuteronomy11:18 Therefore you must fix these words of Mine in your heart and in your soul
Deuteronomy11:19 You shall teach them to your children
Deuteronomy11:20 You shall write them on the doorposts of your house and on your gates
Deuteronomy11:21 so that your days and the days of your children may be multiplied in the land which the Lord swore to your fathers to give them
Deuteronomy11:22 For if you diligently keep all these commandments which I am commanding you to do —to love the Lord your God
Deuteronomy11:23 then the Lord will drive out all these nations from before you
Deuteronomy11:24 Every place where the soles of your feet tread will be yours. Your border will be from the wilderness to Lebanon
Deuteronomy11:25 No man will be able to resist you
Deuteronomy11:26 See
Deuteronomy11:27 the blessing if you obey the commandments of the Lord your God
Deuteronomy11:28 and the curse
Deuteronomy11:29 Now it shall be
Deuteronomy11:30 Are they not on the other side of the Jordan
Deuteronomy11:31 For you will cross over the Jordan to go in to possess the land which the Lord your God is giving you
Deuteronomy11:32 You must be careful to do all the statutes and judgments which I am setting before you today.
Deuteronomy12:1 These are the statutes and judgments which you shall be careful to observe in the land
Deuteronomy12:2 You must utterly destroy all the places where the nations which you will possess served their gods
Deuteronomy12:3 And you shall overthrow their altars
Deuteronomy12:4 You shall not act this way toward the Lord your God.
Deuteronomy12:5 But you must seek only the place which the Lord your God shall choose out of all your tribes to establish His name
Deuteronomy12:6 There you shall bring your burnt offerings
Deuteronomy12:7 There you must eat before the Lord your God
Deuteronomy12:8 You are not to do all the things that we are doing here today
Deuteronomy12:9 For you have not yet come to the rest and to the inheritance which the Lord your God has given you.
Deuteronomy12:10 But when you cross the Jordan
Deuteronomy12:11 then there will be a place which the Lord your God will choose to cause His name to dwell. There you must bring all that I command you: your burnt offerings
Deuteronomy12:12 You will rejoice before the Lord your God
Deuteronomy12:13 Be careful that you do not offer your burnt offerings in every place that you see.
Deuteronomy12:14 Rather
Deuteronomy12:15 Notwithstanding
Deuteronomy12:16 only you must not eat the blood. You shall pour it on the ground like water.
Deuteronomy12:17 You may not eat within your gates the tithe of your grain
Deuteronomy12:18 Rather you must eat them before the Lord your God in the place which the Lord your God will choose —you
Deuteronomy12:19 Take heed to yourself that you do not forsake the Levite as long as you live on the earth.
Deuteronomy12:20 When the Lord your God shall enlarge your border as He has promised you
Deuteronomy12:21 If the place which the Lord your God has chosen to put His name is too far from you
Deuteronomy12:22 Even as the gazelle and the deer are eaten
Deuteronomy12:23 Only be sure that you do not eat the blood
Deuteronomy12:24 You must not eat it. You must pour it on the ground like water.
Deuteronomy12:25 You must not eat it
Deuteronomy12:26 You shall take only your holy things which you have
Deuteronomy12:27 You must offer your burnt offerings
Deuteronomy12:28 Observe and hear all these words which I command you
Deuteronomy12:29 When the Lord your God shall cut off the nations from before you
Deuteronomy12:30 take heed to yourself so that you are not ensnared by following them
Deuteronomy12:31 You shall not do so to the Lord your God
Deuteronomy12:32 Whatever I command you
Deuteronomy13:1 If a prophet or a dreamer of dreams arises among you and gives you a sign or a wonder
Deuteronomy13:2 and the sign or the wonder comes to pass concerning that which he spoke to you
Deuteronomy13:3 you must not listen to the words of that prophet or that dreamer of dreams
Deuteronomy13:4 You must follow after the Lord your God
Deuteronomy13:5 That prophet or that dreamer of dreams must be put to death because he has spoken in order to turn you away from the Lord your God
Deuteronomy13:6 If your brother
Deuteronomy13:7 namely
Deuteronomy13:8 you shall not consent to him or listen to him
Deuteronomy13:9 but you must surely kill him. Your hand must be first upon him to put him to death
Deuteronomy13:10 You shall stone him with stones so that he dies because he has sought to entice you away from the Lord your God who brought you out of the land of Egypt
Deuteronomy13:11 All Israel shall hear and fear and no more do any such wickedness as this among you.
Deuteronomy13:12 If you hear it said in one of your cities
Deuteronomy13:13 “Certain men
Deuteronomy13:14 then you shall inquire
Deuteronomy13:15 you shall surely put the inhabitants of that city to the sword
Deuteronomy13:16 You must gather all the spoils of it into the middle of the street and burn the city with fire along with all the spoils within it for the Lord your God
Deuteronomy13:17 Nothing of the cursed thing there must cling to your hand
Deuteronomy13:18 if you listen to the voice of the Lord your God
Deuteronomy14:1 You are the sons of the Lord your God. You shall not cut yourselves or make any baldness between your eyes for the dead.
Deuteronomy14:2 For you are a holy people to the Lord your God
Deuteronomy14:3 You must not eat any detestable thing.
Deuteronomy14:4 These are the animals which you may eat: the ox
Deuteronomy14:5 the deer
Deuteronomy14:6 You may eat every animal with divided hooves
Deuteronomy14:7 Nevertheless
Deuteronomy14:8 The pig is unclean to you because it divides the hoof
Deuteronomy14:9 These you shall eat of all that are in the water: All that have fins and scales you may eat.
Deuteronomy14:10 Whatever does not have fins and scales you may not eat. It is unclean to you.
Deuteronomy14:11 You may eat of all clean birds.
Deuteronomy14:12 However
Deuteronomy14:13 the red kite
Deuteronomy14:14 every raven after its kind
Deuteronomy14:15 and the ostrich
Deuteronomy14:16 the little owl
Deuteronomy14:17 the pelican
Deuteronomy14:18 the stork
Deuteronomy14:19 Every creeping thing that flies is unclean to you. They must not be eaten.
Deuteronomy14:20 You may eat all the clean birds.
Deuteronomy14:21 You must not eat of anything that dies of itself
Deuteronomy14:22 You must be certain to tithe all the produce of your seed
Deuteronomy14:23 You must eat before the Lord your God
Deuteronomy14:24 If the distance is too long for you
Deuteronomy14:25 then you shall exchange it for money and bind up the money in your hand and go to the place which the Lord your God shall choose.
Deuteronomy14:26 Then you may spend that money for whatever your heart desires
Deuteronomy14:27 and the Levite that is within your gates. You must not forsake him
Deuteronomy14:28 At the end of three years you must bring forth all the tithe of your produce the same year and lay it up within your gates.
Deuteronomy14:29 Then the Levite (because he has no portion or inheritance with you)
Deuteronomy15:1 At the end of every seven years you shall grant a relinquishing of debts.
Deuteronomy15:2 This is the manner of the relinquishing: Every creditor that has loaned anything to his neighbor shall relinquish it. He shall not exact it of his neighbor
Deuteronomy15:3 You may collect it from a foreigner
Deuteronomy15:4 However
Deuteronomy15:5 if only you carefully obey the voice of the Lord your God
Deuteronomy15:6 For the Lord your God will bless you
Deuteronomy15:7 If there be among you a poor man
Deuteronomy15:8 But you shall open your hand wide to him and must surely lend him what is sufficient for his need
Deuteronomy15:9 Beware lest there be a wicked thought in your heart
Deuteronomy15:10 You must surely give to him
Deuteronomy15:11 For the poor will never cease from being in the land. Therefore
Deuteronomy15:12 If your brother
Deuteronomy15:13 When you send him out free from you
Deuteronomy15:14 You shall supply him liberally out of your flock
Deuteronomy15:15 You shall remember that you were a slave in the land of Egypt and the Lord your God redeemed you. Therefore
Deuteronomy15:16 It shall be
Deuteronomy15:17 then you must take an awl and pierce it through his ear into the door
Deuteronomy15:18 It will not seem difficult for you when you send him away free from you
Deuteronomy15:19 You must sanctify all the firstborn males that come out of your herd and flock to the Lord your God. You must do no work with the firstborn of your bulls or shear the firstborn of your sheep.
Deuteronomy15:20 You shall eat it before the Lord your God year by year in the place where the Lord shall choose
Deuteronomy15:21 If there is any defect in it
Deuteronomy15:22 You shall eat it within your gates. The unclean and the clean person alike shall eat it
Deuteronomy15:23 Only you must not eat its blood. You shall pour it on the ground like water.
Deuteronomy16:1 Observe the month of Aviv and keep the Passover to the Lord your God
Deuteronomy16:2 Therefore
Deuteronomy16:3 You must not eat leavened bread with it. For seven days you must eat unleavened bread
Deuteronomy16:4 There must not be any leavened bread seen with you within all your borders for seven days
Deuteronomy16:5 You may not sacrifice the Passover within any of your gates that the Lord your God has given you.
Deuteronomy16:6 But at the place where the Lord your God chooses to place His name
Deuteronomy16:7 You shall roast and eat it in the place where the Lord your God will choose
Deuteronomy16:8 For six days you must eat unleavened bread
Deuteronomy16:9 You must count seven weeks for yourself. Begin counting the seven weeks from the time you begin to put the sickle to the standing grain.
Deuteronomy16:10 You must keep the Feast of Weeks to the Lord your God with a tribute of a freewill offering from your hand
Deuteronomy16:11 You shall rejoice before the Lord your God—you
Deuteronomy16:12 You must remember that you were a slave in Egypt
Deuteronomy16:13 You shall observe the Feast of Tabernacles seven days after you have gathered in your threshing floor and your winepress
Deuteronomy16:14 and you shall rejoice in your feast
Deuteronomy16:15 You are to celebrate the festival for seven days to the Lord your God in the place where the Lord will choose
Deuteronomy16:16 Three times a year all your males must appear before the Lord your God in the place where He will choose: at the Feast of Unleavened Bread
Deuteronomy16:17 Every man must give as he is able
Deuteronomy16:18 You must appoint judges and officers in all your gates
Deuteronomy16:19 You must not pervert judgment nor show partiality. You must not accept a bribe
Deuteronomy16:20 You must follow that which is altogether just
Deuteronomy16:21 You must not plant for yourself an Asherah of any trees near the altar of the Lord your God
Deuteronomy16:22 You shall not set up for yourself any image
Deuteronomy17:1 You must not sacrifice to the Lord your God any bull or sheep that has a blemish or any defect
Deuteronomy17:2 If there be found among you
Deuteronomy17:3 and has gone and served other gods and worshipped them
Deuteronomy17:4 and if it is told to you
Deuteronomy17:5 then you must bring forth that man or that woman who has committed that wicked thing to your gates
Deuteronomy17:6 On the testimony of two or three witnesses he that is to die must be put to death
Deuteronomy17:7 The hands of the witnesses shall be first against him to put him to death
Deuteronomy17:8 If there arises a matter too difficult for you in judgment
Deuteronomy17:9 You must go to the Levitical priests or to the judge in office those days
Deuteronomy17:10 You shall do according to the verdict which they declare to you from the place where the Lord will choose to show you
Deuteronomy17:11 You must do according to the terms of the law which they instruct you and according to the verdict which they tell you. You must not deviate from the sentence which they show you
Deuteronomy17:12 The man who acts presumptuously and does not listen to the priest who stands to minister before the Lord your God
Deuteronomy17:13 Then all the people will hear

Deuteronomy17:15 you must set a king over you whom the Lord your God will choose. You must select a king over you who is from among your brothers. You may not select a foreigner over you who is not your countryman.
Deuteronomy17:16 What is more
Deuteronomy17:17 He shall not acquire many wives for himself
Deuteronomy17:18 It must be
Deuteronomy17:19 It must be with him
Deuteronomy17:20 that his heart will not be lifted up above his brothers and so that he may not turn aside from the commandment

Deuteronomy18:5 For the Lord your God has chosen him out of all your tribes to stand to minister in the name of the Lord
Deuteronomy18:6 If a Levite comes from any of your gates out of all Israel
Deuteronomy18:7 then he shall minister in the name of the Lord his God
Deuteronomy18:8 They must have the same portions to eat

Deuteronomy18:12 For all that do these things are an abomination to the Lord

Deuteronomy19:5 like when a man goes into the forest with his neighbor to cut wood

Deuteronomy19:7 Therefore

Deuteronomy19:15 A single witness must not rise up against a man on account of any iniquity or any sin that he sins. At the testimony of two witnesses or at the testimony of three witnesses shall the matter be established.
Deuteronomy19:16 If a false witness rises up against any man to testify against him to accuse him of doing wrong
Deuteronomy19:17 then both the men between whom the controversy is must stand before the Lord
Deuteronomy19:18 The judges will thoroughly investigate
Deuteronomy19:19 then you must do to him as he conspired to have done to his brother. In this way you must remove the evil from among you.
Deuteronomy19:20 Those who remain will hear and fear
Deuteronomy19:21 You must not show pity. But life will be for life

Deuteronomy22:1 You must not see your brother’s ox or his sheep go astray and hide yourself from them. You must certainly bring them back to your brother.
Deuteronomy22:2 If your brother is not near you or if you do not know him
Deuteronomy22:3 In the same way
Deuteronomy22:4 You are not to see your brother’s donkey or his ox fall down by the way and hide yourself from them. You certainly must help him to lift them up.
Deuteronomy22:5 A woman must not wear man’s clothing
Deuteronomy22:6 If you happen to notice a bird’s nest along the way
Deuteronomy22:7 You must certainly let the mother go
Deuteronomy22:8 When you build a new house
Deuteronomy22:9 You must not sow your vineyard with two kinds of seeds
Deuteronomy22:10 You must not plow with an ox and a donkey together.
Deuteronomy22:11 You must not wear clothing made of a material of wool and linen together.
Deuteronomy22:12 You must make tassels on the four quarters of your clothing with which you cover yourself.
Deuteronomy22:13 If any man takes a wife and has sexual relations with her and then rejects her
Deuteronomy22:14 and accuses her of impropriety and publicly defames her
Deuteronomy22:15 then the father and mother of the girl must produce evidence of the girl’s virginity to the elders of the city at the gate.
Deuteronomy22:16 The girl’s father must say to the elders
Deuteronomy22:17 And behold
Deuteronomy22:18 The elders of that city must take that man and punish him
Deuteronomy22:19 and they must fine him a hundred shekels of silver and give them to the father of the girl
Deuteronomy22:20 But if the accusation is true
Deuteronomy22:21 then they shall bring the girl out to the door of her father’s house
Deuteronomy22:22 If a man is discovered lying with a married woman
Deuteronomy22:23 If a girl who is a virgin is engaged to a man
Deuteronomy22:24 then you must bring them both out to the gate of that city and you must stone them with stones until they die
Deuteronomy22:25 But if a man finds an engaged girl in the field
Deuteronomy22:26 However
Deuteronomy22:27 For he found her in the field
Deuteronomy22:28 If a man finds a girl who is a virgin who is not engaged and seizes her and lies with her and they are discovered
Deuteronomy22:29 then the man who lay with her must give fifty shekels of silver to the girl’s father
Deuteronomy22:30 A man must not marry his father’s wife and in this way dishonor his father.
Deuteronomy23:1 No one who is emasculated or has his male organ cut off may enter the assembly of the Lord.
Deuteronomy23:2 No one of illegitimate birth may enter the assembly of the Lord. Even to his tenth generation no one related to him may enter the assembly of the Lord.
Deuteronomy23:3 No Ammonite or Moabite may enter the assembly of the Lord. Even to their tenth generation they may not enter the assembly of the Lord forever
Deuteronomy23:4 because they refused you bread and water on the way
Deuteronomy23:5 Nevertheless
Deuteronomy23:6 You are not ever to seek their peace nor their prosperity all your days.
Deuteronomy23:7 You are not to abhor an Edomite
Deuteronomy23:8 The children who are born of them may enter the assembly of the Lord in their third generation.

Deuteronomy23:10 If there be among you any man who is not clean because of a nocturnal emission

Joshua7:12 Therefore the Israelites cannot stand before their enemies. They turn their backs to their enemies because they have become dedicated for destruction. I will not be with you anymore if you do not destroy the things dedicated for destruction in your midst.
Joshua7:13 “Get up! Consecrate the people and say
Joshua7:14 “ ‘In the morning you will be brought forward by tribes
Joshua7:15 And he who is taken with the things dedicated for destruction shall be burned with fire
Joshua7:16 So Joshua got up early in the morning and brought forward Israel by their tribes. The tribe of Judah was selected.
Joshua7:17 He brought forward the clans of Judah
Joshua7:18 He brought forward the household of Zimri man by man
Joshua7:19 Then Joshua said to Achan
Joshua7:20 Achan answered Joshua and said
Joshua7:21 When I saw among the plundered goods a beautiful robe from Babylon
Joshua7:22 So Joshua sent messengers
Joshua7:23 They took it from the tent and brought it to Joshua and all the Israelites. They spread it out before the Lord.
Joshua7:24 Then Joshua
Joshua7:25 Then Joshua said
Joshua7:26 Then they erected over him a large pile of stones
Joshua8:1 Then the Lord said to Joshua
Joshua8:2 Do to Ai and its king what you did to Jericho and its king
Joshua8:3 So Joshua and all the fighting men arose to go up to Ai. Joshua chose thirty thousand men
Joshua8:4 He commanded them
Joshua8:5 I and all the people who are with me will approach the city. When they come out to engage us as before
Joshua8:6 They will come after us until we have lured them away from the city
Joshua8:7 Then you shall rise up for the ambush and take possession of the city. The Lord your God will give it into your hands.
Joshua8:8 When you have seized the city
Joshua8:9 So Joshua sent them out. They went to the place of ambush and took up a position between Bethel and Ai
Joshua8:10 Joshua got up early in the morning and mustered the people. He and the elders of Israel went up before the people to Ai.
Joshua8:11 All the fighting men who were with him went up and drew near to the city. They camped north of Ai
Joshua8:12 He took about five thousand men and set them in ambush between Bethel and Ai
Joshua8:13 So they stationed the people
Joshua8:14 When the king of Ai saw this
Joshua8:15 So Joshua and all Israel allowed themselves to be beaten before them
Joshua8:16 All the people who were in Ai were assembled to pursue them
Joshua8:17 Not a man was left in Ai or Bethel who did not go out after Israel. They left the city wide open
Joshua8:18 The Lord said to Joshua
Joshua8:19 When his hand pointed
Joshua8:20 So when the men of Ai looked back
Joshua8:21 When Joshua and all Israel saw that the ambush had captured the city and that smoke rose from it
Joshua8:22 Then the men in the city came out to engage them in battle
Joshua8:23 They took the king of Ai alive and brought him to Joshua.
Joshua8:24 When Israel completed killing all the inhabitants of Ai in the open wilderness where they had pursued them
Joshua8:25 All who had fallen that day
Joshua8:26 Joshua did not draw back his hand with the outstretched sword until he destroyed all the inhabitants of Ai.
Joshua8:27 Israel plundered only the livestock and the spoil of that city for themselves
Joshua8:28 Joshua burned Ai and made it a heap forever
Joshua8:29 The king of Ai hanged on a tree until evening. At sunset
Joshua8:30 Then Joshua built an altar to the Lord God of Israel on Mount Ebal
Joshua8:31 as Moses the servant of the Lord had commanded the Israelites. As is written in the Book of the Law of Moses
Joshua8:32 There in the presence of the Israelites he wrote a copy of the Law of Moses on the stones.
Joshua8:33 All Israel
Joshua8:34 After this
Joshua8:35 There was not a word from all that Moses had commanded that Joshua did not read out before the whole assembly of Israel

Joshua9:2 they gathered together as one to wage war against Joshua and Israel.

Joshua9:4 they acted craftily
Joshua9:5 old and patched sandals on their feet

Joshua9:13 These wineskins that we filled were new
Joshua9:14 So the men examined some of the food provisions

Joshua9:16 Three days after they had made a covenant with them

Joshua9:19 Yet all the leaders said to the whole congregation
Joshua9:20 This is what we will do to them. We will let them live so that wrath will not come upon us due to the oath that we swore to them.”

Joshua10:1 Now when King Adoni-Zedek of Jerusalem heard that Joshua captured Ai and destroyed it
Joshua10:2 he and his people were very afraid. Gibeon was a large city
Joshua10:3 So King Adoni-Zedek of Jerusalem sent this message to King Hoham of Hebron
Joshua10:4 “Come and help me! Let us attack Gibeon
Joshua10:5 So the five kings of the Amorites—the kings of Jerusalem
Joshua10:6 The men of Gibeon sent this message to Joshua at the Gilgal settlement: “Do not abandon your slaves. Come up to us quickly. Save us! Help us! For all the Amorite kings living in the hill country have gathered against us.”
Joshua10:7 So Joshua went up from Gilgal
Joshua10:8 The Lord said to Joshua
Joshua10:9 Then Joshua came upon them suddenly
Joshua10:10 The Lord panicked them before Israel. They struck them with overwhelming force at Gibeon
Joshua10:11 As they fled from Israel on the downslope from Beth-horon
Joshua10:12 On the day the Lord gave over the Amorites to the Israelites
Joshua10:13 So the sun stood still
Joshua10:14 There has not been a day like this either before or after it

Joshua10:24 When they brought out those five kings to Joshua

Joshua10:26 After that
Joshua10:27 At sundown Joshua commanded men to take them down from the trees and throw them into the cave in which they had hidden themselves. Over the mouth of the cave he placed large stones
Joshua10:28 Joshua took Makkedah that day and struck it and its king with the edge of the sword. He destroyed it and all life within it. He did not leave any survivors. He did to the king of Makkedah just as he did to the king of Jericho.
Joshua10:29 Then Joshua passed from Makkedah to Libnah with all of Israel
Joshua10:30 The Lord gave Libnah and its king into the hand of Israel
Joshua10:31 Then Joshua passed from Libnah to Lachish with all of Israel
Joshua10:32 The Lord gave Lachish into the hand of Israel. They captured it on the second day and struck all who lived in it with the edge of the sword
Joshua10:33 King Horam of Gezer came up to help Lachish
Joshua10:34 From Lachish
Joshua10:35 They captured it that day and struck it with the edge of the sword. All who lived in it were destroyed that day
Joshua10:36 Then Joshua and all of Israel went up from Eglon to Hebron and waged war against it.
Joshua10:37 They captured it and struck its king
Joshua10:38 Then Joshua and all of Israel turned to Debir and waged war against it.
Joshua10:39 They captured it
Joshua10:40 So Joshua attacked the whole land: the hill country
Joshua10:41 Joshua attacked the land from Kadesh Barnea to Gaza and the land of Goshen as far as Gibeon.
Joshua10:42 Joshua captured all these kings and their land in one campaign because the Lord God of Israel waged war for Israel.
Joshua10:43 Then Joshua and all of Israel returned to the settlement at Gilgal.

Judges10:1 After the death of Abimelech
Judges10:2 He judged Israel for twenty-three years. Then he died and was buried in Shamir.
Judges10:3 After him Jair the Gileadite arose and judged Israel for twenty-two years.
Judges10:4 He had thirty sons who rode thirty donkeys and owned thirty cities that are in the land of Gilead. They are called Havvoth Jair to this day.
Judges10:5 Jair died and was buried in Kamon.

1Samuel17:43 The Philistine said to David
1Samuel17:44 The Philistine said to David
1Samuel17:45 Then David said to the Philistine
1Samuel17:46 This day will the Lord deliver you into my hand. And I will strike you down and cut off your head. Then I will give the corpses of the Philistine camp this day to the birds of the air and to the beasts of the earth so that all the earth may know that there is a God in Israel.
1Samuel17:47 And then all this assembly will know that it is not by sword and spear that the Lord saves. For the battle belongs to the Lord
1Samuel17:48 When the Philistine arose and came near to meet David
1Samuel17:49 David put his hand in his bag and took from there a stone. And he slung it and struck the Philistine in his forehead. Therefore the stone sunk into his forehead and he fell upon his face to the ground.
1Samuel17:50 So David prevailed over the Philistine with a sling and with a stone. And he struck down the Philistine and slew him
1Samuel17:51 Therefore David ran and stood over the Philistine. Then he took his sword and drew it from out of its sheath
1Samuel17:52 And the fighting men of Israel and Judah arose and shouted. And they pursued the Philistines from the entrance of the Valley of Elah as far as the gates of Ekron. So the Philistine dead lay slain on the road to Shaaraim as far as Gath and Ekron.
1Samuel17:53 Then the Israelites returned from chasing after the Philistines and they plundered their tents.
1Samuel17:54 David took the head of the Philistine and brought it to Jerusalem

2Samuel22:1 Now on the day the Lord delivered him from the hand of all of his enemies and from the hand of Saul
2Samuel22:2 He said: The Lord is my rock and my fortress and my deliverer;
2Samuel22:3 the God of my strength
2Samuel22:4 I call upon the Lord
2Samuel22:5 When the waves of death encompassed me
2Samuel22:6 The ropes of Sheol were wrapped around me; the snares of death were opposite me.
2Samuel22:7 In my distress I called on the Lord
2Samuel22:8 Then the earth quaked and trembled; the foundations of the heavens rumbled and shook
2Samuel22:9 Smoke rose from His nostrils
2Samuel22:10 He bowed the heaven as He came down
2Samuel22:11 He rode upon a cherub as He flew
2Samuel22:12 He made darkness canopies around Him
2Samuel22:13 From the brightness before Him embers of fire are kindled.
2Samuel22:14 The Lord thundered from heaven
2Samuel22:15 He sent out arrows and dispersed them; with lightning He sent them into confusion.
2Samuel22:16 The channels of the sea appeared
2Samuel22:17 He reached from on high and took me; He drew me out of mighty waters.
2Samuel22:18 He rescued me from my strong enemy
2Samuel22:19 They confronted me in the day of my disaster
2Samuel22:20 He brought me to the open expanse; He rescued me
2Samuel22:21 The Lord did for me according to my righteousness; according to the cleanness of my hands He recompensed me.
2Samuel22:22 For I have kept the ways of the Lord
2Samuel22:23 For all His judgments are before me
2Samuel22:24 I am blameless toward Him
2Samuel22:25 The Lord has recompensed me according to my righteousness
2Samuel22:26 With the faithful You prove Yourself faithful; with the blameless You prove Yourself blameless;
2Samuel22:27 with the pure You show Yourself pure; but with the perverse You show Yourself shrewd.
2Samuel22:28 You deliver a humble people; but Your eyes are upon the exalted to bring them down.
2Samuel22:29 For You are my lamp
2Samuel22:30 For by You I can run over a channel; by my God I can leap over a wall.
2Samuel22:31 As for God
2Samuel22:32 For who is God except the Lord? And who is a rock except our God?
2Samuel22:33 God is my strong fortress
2Samuel22:34 He makes my feet like hinds’ feet; He sets me on my high places.
2Samuel22:35 He trains my hand for battle
2Samuel22:36 You have given me a shield of Your salvation
2Samuel22:37 You widen my stride under me
2Samuel22:38 I pursued my enemies and destroyed them; I did not turn back until they were consumed.
2Samuel22:39 I consumed them and shattered them so that they did not rise; they fell under my feet.
2Samuel22:40 You girded me with strength for the battle; You subjugated under me those who rose up against me.
2Samuel22:41 You caused my enemies to retreat from me; I destroyed those who hated me.
2Samuel22:42 They looked
2Samuel22:43 I crushed them like the dust of the land
2Samuel22:44 You have also delivered me from the strivings of my people; You have kept me as the head of the nations. A people whom I have not known will serve me.
2Samuel22:45 Foreigners submit to me
2Samuel22:46 Foreigners lose heart; they gird themselves as they leave their fortresses.
2Samuel22:47 The Lord lives; blessed be my rock. May the God of the rock of my salvation be exalted
2Samuel22:48 the God who gave me retribution and brought down peoples under me
2Samuel22:49 who brought me out from my enemies. You exalted me above those who rose up against me; You delivered me from violent ones.
2Samuel22:50 Therefore
2Samuel22:51 He is a tower of salvation for His king
