1Kings4:1 So King Solomon was king over all Israel.
1Kings4:2 These were the officials in his court: Azariah the son of Zadok was the priest.
1Kings4:3 Elihoreph and Ahijah
1Kings4:4 Benaiah the son of Jehoiada was over the army. Zadok and Abiathar were priests.
1Kings4:5 Azariah the son of Nathan was over the officers
1Kings4:6 Ahishar was over the household
1Kings4:7 Solomon had twelve officers over all Israel who made provision for the king and his household. Each man was assigned a month to make provision.
1Kings4:8 These are their names: the son of Hur in Mount Ephraim;
1Kings4:9 the son of Deker in Makaz and in Shaalbim and Beth-shemesh and Elon-beth-hanan;
1Kings4:10 the son of Hesed in Arubboth (to him belonged Sokoh and all the land of Hepher);
1Kings4:11 the son of Abinadab in all the region of Dor (he had Taphath the daughter of Solomon as his wife);
1Kings4:12 Baana the son of Ahilud in Taanach
1Kings4:13 the son of Geber in Ramoth Gilead (to him pertained the towns of Jair the son of Manasseh which are in Gilead
1Kings4:14 Ahinadab the son of Iddo in Mahanaim;
1Kings4:15 Ahimaaz in Naphtali (he had taken Basemath the daughter of Solomon as his wife);
1Kings4:16 Baana the son of Hushai in Asher and in Aloth;
1Kings4:17 Jehoshaphat the son of Paruah in Issachar;
1Kings4:18 Shimei the son of Ela in Benjamin;
1Kings4:19 Geber the son of Uri in the country of Gilead
1Kings4:20 Judah and Israel had a large populace
1Kings4:21 Solomon reigned over all kingdoms from the Euphrates River to the land of the Philistines and to the border of Egypt. The people brought presents and served Solomon all the days of his life.
1Kings4:22 Solomon’s provision for one day was thirty kors of fine flour and sixty kors of meal
1Kings4:23 ten fat oxen and twenty pasture-fed cattle
1Kings4:24 For he had dominion over all the region on this side of the River from Tiphsah to Gaza
1Kings4:25 Judah and Israel lived safely
1Kings4:26 Solomon had forty thousand stalls of horses for his chariots and twelve thousand horsemen.
1Kings4:27 Those officers made provision for King Solomon and for all who came to King Solomon’s table
1Kings4:28 Barley also and straw for the horses and dromedaries were brought to the place where the officers were
1Kings4:29 God gave Solomon wisdom and great depth of understanding as well as compassion
1Kings4:30 Solomon’s wisdom excelled the wisdom of all the people of the East country and all the wisdom of Egypt.
1Kings4:31 For he was wiser than all other men
1Kings4:32 He spoke three thousand proverbs
1Kings4:33 He spoke of trees
1Kings4:34 People from all over came to hear the wisdom of Solomon
1Kings5:1 Now Hiram king of Tyre sent his servants to Solomon
1Kings5:2 And Solomon sent word to Hiram
1Kings5:3 “You know how David my father was unable to build a house for the name of the Lord his God because of the wars all around him
1Kings5:4 But now the Lord my God has given me peace on every side
1Kings5:5 So know that I plan to build a house to honor the name of the Lord my God
1Kings5:6 “Now therefore command that cedar trees from Lebanon be cut down for me
1Kings5:7 When Hiram heard the words of Solomon
1Kings5:8 Hiram sent to Solomon
1Kings5:9 My servants shall bring them down from Lebanon to the sea
1Kings5:10 So Hiram gave Solomon cedar trees and fir trees as he desired.
1Kings5:11 Solomon gave Hiram twenty thousand kors of wheat for his household and twenty baths of pure oil. This is what Solomon gave to Hiram each year.
1Kings5:12 The Lord gave Solomon wisdom
1Kings5:13 King Solomon drafted men from all Israel
1Kings5:14 He sent them to Lebanon in turns
1Kings5:15 Solomon had seventy thousand porters and eighty thousand stonecutters in the mountains
1Kings5:16 not counting the chief of Solomon’s officers who were over the work
1Kings5:17 At the king’s command
1Kings5:18 Solomon’s builders and Hiram’s builders
1Kings6:1 In the four hundred and eightieth year after the Israelites came out of the land of Egypt
1Kings6:2 The house which King Solomon built for the Lord had a length of sixty cubits
1Kings6:3 The porch in front of the temple was twenty cubits in length
1Kings6:4 He made beveled windows for the house.
1Kings6:5 He also built a structure against the wall of the house
1Kings6:6 The lowest story was five cubits broad
1Kings6:7 The house was built of stone prepared at the quarry
1Kings6:8 The door for the middle chamber was in the right side of the house
1Kings6:9 So he built the house and finished it and covered it with beams and boards of cedar.
1Kings6:10 Then he built chambers against the whole house
1Kings6:11 Now the word of the Lord came to Solomon
1Kings6:12 “Concerning this house which you are building
1Kings6:13 and I will dwell among the people of Israel and will not forsake My people Israel.”
1Kings6:14 So Solomon built the house and finished it.
1Kings6:15 He built the interior walls of the house with boards of cedar. From the floor of the house to the ceiling
1Kings6:16 He lined twenty cubits on the sides of the house
1Kings6:17 The house
1Kings6:18 The cedar of the house within had carvings of gourds and open flowers. All was cedar. There was no stone seen.
1Kings6:19 He prepared the inner sanctuary in the inner part of the house in order to set there the ark of the covenant of the Lord.
1Kings6:20 The inner sanctuary was twenty cubits long
1Kings6:21 So Solomon overlaid the interior of the house with pure gold
1Kings6:22 He overlaid the whole house with gold as well as the whole altar that was by the inner sanctuary.
1Kings6:23 Within the inner sanctuary
1Kings6:24 One wing of the cherub was five cubits
1Kings6:25 The other cherub was ten cubits. Both the cherubim were the same shape and size.
1Kings6:26 The height of the one cherub was ten cubits
1Kings6:27 He set the cherubim within the inner sanctuary
1Kings6:28 He overlaid the cherubim with gold.
1Kings6:29 He carved all the walls of the house with carved figures of cherubim and palm trees and open flowers both inside and out.
1Kings6:30 He overlaid the floor of the house with gold
1Kings6:31 For the entrance to the inner sanctuary
1Kings6:32 The two doors were also made of olive wood. He carved on them cherubim
1Kings6:33 So also he made for the entrance to the nave four-sided posts of olive wood.
1Kings6:34 The two doors were made from fir tree
1Kings6:35 He carved on them cherubim and palm trees and open flowers and covered them with gold fitted upon the carved work.
1Kings6:36 He built the inner court with three rows of hewed stone and a row of cedar beams.
1Kings6:37 In the fourth year
1Kings6:38 and in the eleventh year
1Kings7:1 Solomon was building his own house for thirteen years
1Kings7:2 He built the House of the Forest of Lebanon. Its length was a hundred cubits
1Kings7:3 It was covered with cedar over the top of the beams
1Kings7:4 There were window frames in three rows and window opposite window in three tiers.
1Kings7:5 All the doors and posts were rectangular with the openings facing each other in three tiers.
1Kings7:6 He made a porch of pillars with a length of fifty cubits and a breadth of thirty cubits. There was a porch in front with pillars
1Kings7:7 Then he made a porch for the throne
1Kings7:8 His own house where he lived
1Kings7:9 All these were built with costly stones
1Kings7:10 The foundation was of large
1Kings7:11 Above were costly stones cut to size
1Kings7:12 The great court was enclosed with three rows of hewed stones and a row of cedar beams. So were the inner court of the house of the Lord and the porch of the house.
1Kings7:13 Now King Solomon sent and called Huram out of Tyre.
1Kings7:14 He was the son of a widow from the tribe of Naphtali
1Kings7:15 He cast two pillars of bronze eighteen cubits high each and twelve cubits in circumference.
1Kings7:16 He made two capitals of cast bronze to set on the tops of the pillars. The height of the one capital was five cubits
1Kings7:17 He made lattices of checker work with wreaths of chainwork for the capitals on top of the pillars: seven for one capital and seven for the other.
1Kings7:18 Likewise he made pomegranates in two rows around the one latticework to cover the capital that was on the top of the pillar
1Kings7:19 The capitals that were on top of the pillars in the porch were four cubits high and in the shape of lilies.
1Kings7:20 The capitals on top of the two pillars also had pomegranates above
1Kings7:21 He set up the pillars in the porch of the temple. He set up the right pillar and called it Jakin
1Kings7:22 The tops of the pillars were in the shape of lilies. This completed the work on the pillars.
1Kings7:23 He made a cast metal sea
1Kings7:24 Under the brim all the way around there were gourds
1Kings7:25 It stood on top of twelve oxen with three facing north
1Kings7:26 It was a hand-breadth thick
1Kings7:27 He made ten stands out of bronze
1Kings7:28 The work of the stands looked like this: They had panels
1Kings7:29 And on the panels that were set in the frames were lions
1Kings7:30 Every stand had four bronze wheels and axles of bronze
1Kings7:31 Its opening was within a crown that projected upward one cubit. Its opening was round
1Kings7:32 Underneath the panels were four wheels
1Kings7:33 The wheels worked like chariot wheels in that their axles and rims and spokes and hubs were all cast metal.
1Kings7:34 There were four supports for the four corners of each stand
1Kings7:35 On the top of the stand
1Kings7:36 On the surface of its stays and on its panels
1Kings7:37 In this way he made the ten stands
1Kings7:38 Then he made ten basins of bronze
1Kings7:39 He put five stands on the right side of the house
1Kings7:40 Huram also made the pots
1Kings7:41 the two pillars
1Kings7:42 and the four hundred pomegranates for the two latticeworks
1Kings7:43 the ten stands and ten basins on the stands;
1Kings7:44 one sea and twelve oxen under the sea;
1Kings7:45 the pots
1Kings7:46 In the plain of the Jordan the king cast them
1Kings7:47 Solomon left all the vessels unweighed because there were so many. The weight of the bronze was also never measured.
1Kings7:48 Solomon made all the vessels that were needed for the house of the Lord: the altar of gold
1Kings7:49 the candlesticks of pure gold
1Kings7:50 the cups
1Kings7:51 All the work that King Solomon made for the house of the Lord was completed. And Solomon brought in the things which David his father had dedicated—the silver
1Kings8:1 Then Solomon assembled the elders of Israel and all the heads of the tribes
1Kings8:2 All the men of Israel assembled themselves before King Solomon at the feast in the month Ethanim
1Kings8:3 All the elders of Israel came
1Kings8:4 The priests and Levites brought up the ark of the Lord
1Kings8:5 King Solomon and all the congregation of Israel who were assembled before him stood together in front of the ark
1Kings8:6 The priests brought in the ark of the covenant of the Lord to its place in the inner sanctuary of the house
1Kings8:7 For the cherubim spread forth their two wings over the place of the ark and covered the ark and the poles from above.
1Kings8:8 The poles were so long that the ends of the poles could be seen out in the holy place in front of the inner sanctuary
1Kings8:9 There was nothing in the ark except for the two tablets of stone that Moses put there at Horeb when the Lord made a covenant with the Israelites after they had come out of the land of Egypt.
1Kings8:10 And when the priests came out of the holy place
1Kings8:11 so that the priests could not continue to minister because of the cloud
1Kings8:12 Then Solomon spoke
1Kings8:13 I have surely built You a house to dwell in
1Kings8:14 Then the king turned around and blessed all the congregation of Israel (and all the congregation of Israel stood)
1Kings8:15 and he said
1Kings8:16 ‘Since the day that I brought forth My people Israel out of Egypt
1Kings8:17 “My father David had it in mind to build a house for the name of the Lord God of Israel.
1Kings8:18 The Lord said to my father David
1Kings8:19 Nevertheless
1Kings8:20 “The Lord has fulfilled His word that He spoke
1Kings8:21 I have set a place there for the ark
1Kings8:22 Then Solomon stood in front of the altar of the Lord in the presence of all the congregation of Israel and spread his hands toward heaven.
1Kings8:23 and he said
1Kings8:24 who have kept what You promised Your servant David my father. You spoke also with Your mouth and have fulfilled it with Your hand
1Kings8:25 “Therefore
1Kings8:26 Now
1Kings8:27 “But will God indeed dwell on the earth? Behold
1Kings8:28 Yet give consideration to the prayer of Your servant and to his supplication
1Kings8:29 that Your eyes may be upon this house night and day
1Kings8:30 Please listen to the supplication of Your servant and of Your people Israel when they pray toward this place. May You hear in heaven
1Kings8:31 “If any man sins against his neighbor
1Kings8:32 then may You hear in heaven and act and judge Your servants
1Kings8:33 “When Your people Israel are defeated by their enemies
1Kings8:34 then may You hear in heaven and forgive the sin of Your people Israel and bring them again to the land which You gave to their fathers.
1Kings8:35 “When heaven is shut up
1Kings8:36 then may You hear in heaven and forgive the sin of Your servants and Your people Israel
1Kings8:37 “When there is famine in the land
1Kings8:38 whatever prayer
1Kings8:39 then hear in heaven Your dwelling place
1Kings8:40 so that they may fear You all the days that they live in the land that You gave to our fathers.
1Kings8:41 “Also concerning the foreigner who is not of Your people Israel and comes from a far country for Your name’s sake
1Kings8:42 (for they will hear of Your great name and of Your strong hand and of Your outstretched arm)
1Kings8:43 may You hear in heaven
1Kings8:44 “If Your people go out to battle against their enemy wherever You send them
1Kings8:45 then may You hear their prayer and supplication in heaven and maintain their cause.
1Kings8:46 “If they sin against You (for there is no man who does not sin)
1Kings8:47 yet when they come to their senses while in the land where they were carried captives and repent and make supplication to You
1Kings8:48 and so return to You with all their hearts and with all their souls in the land of their enemies
1Kings8:49 then may You hear their prayers and supplications in heaven
1Kings8:50 and forgive Your people who have sinned against You and all their transgressions which they committed against You and grant them compassion before those who carried them away as captives
1Kings8:51 For they are Your people and Your inheritance whom You brought forth out of Egypt from the midst of the furnace of iron.
1Kings8:52 “Let Your eyes be open to the supplication of Your servant and to the supplication of Your people Israel
1Kings8:53 For You did call them out from among all the people of the earth to be Your inheritance
1Kings8:54 Now when Solomon finished praying this prayer and making supplication to the Lord
1Kings8:55 He stood up and blessed all the congregation of Israel with a loud voice
1Kings8:56 “Blessed be the Lord who has given rest to His people Israel according to all that He promised. Not one word of His promises which He gave by the hand of Moses His servant has failed.
1Kings8:57 The Lord our God be with us
1Kings8:58 that He may incline our hearts to Him
1Kings8:59 And let these my words
1Kings8:60 so that all the people of the earth may know that the Lord is God and that there is none else.
1Kings8:61 Let your hearts
1Kings8:62 The king and all Israel with him offered sacrifices before the Lord.
1Kings8:63 Solomon offered a sacrifice of peace offerings
1Kings8:64 The same day the king consecrated the middle of the court that was in front of the house of the Lord
1Kings8:65 At that time
1Kings8:66 On the eighth day he sent the people away
1Kings9:1 When Solomon had finished building the house of the Lord and the king’s house and all else he desired
1Kings9:2 the Lord appeared to Solomon a second time
1Kings9:3 The Lord said to him
1Kings9:4 “If you will walk before Me
1Kings9:5 then I will establish the throne of your kingdom upon Israel forever
1Kings9:6 “But if you and your sons turn in any way from following Me and do not keep My commandments and My statutes which I have set before you
1Kings9:7 then I will cut Israel out of the land which I have given them
1Kings9:8 And everyone who passes by this high house will be astonished and will hiss
1Kings9:9 And they will answer
1Kings9:10 When twenty years had passed since Solomon had built the two houses
1Kings9:11 (now Hiram the king of Tyre had furnished Solomon with cedar and fir trees
1Kings9:12 Hiram came out from Tyre to see the cities Solomon had given him
1Kings9:13 He said
1Kings9:14 Hiram sent to the king one hundred and twenty talents of gold.
1Kings9:15 This is the account of the forced labor which King Solomon conscripted to build the house of the Lord and his own house
1Kings9:16 For Pharaoh
1Kings9:17 Solomon built Gezer and Lower Beth-horon
1Kings9:18 and Baalath and Tadmor in the wilderness
1Kings9:19 and all the storage cities that Solomon had
1Kings9:20 All the people who were left of the Amorites
1Kings9:21 the descendants of those who were left in the land and whom the people of Israel were not able to utterly destroy
1Kings9:22 But Solomon did not make any Israelites into slaves
1Kings9:23 These were the chief officers over Solomon’s work
1Kings9:24 But Pharaoh’s daughter moved out of the City of David to her house which Solomon had built for her
1Kings9:25 Three times a year Solomon offered burnt offerings and peace offerings on the altar that he built to the Lord
1Kings9:26 King Solomon built a fleet of ships in Ezion Geber
1Kings9:27 Hiram sent shipmen who had knowledge of the sea to serve alongside Solomon’s men.
1Kings9:28 They went to Ophir and acquired four hundred and twenty talents of gold there and brought it to King Solomon.
1Kings10:1 Now when the queen of Sheba heard of Solomon’s fame connected to the name of the Lord
1Kings10:2 She came to Jerusalem with a very great retinue
1Kings10:3 Solomon answered all her questions; there was not anything too difficult for the king which he could not answer.
1Kings10:4 When the queen of Sheba observed Solomon’s wisdom and the house he had built
1Kings10:5 and the meat of his table and the sitting of his servants and the attendance of his ministers and their clothing and his cupbearers and his entryway by which he went up to the house of the Lord
1Kings10:6 She said to the king
1Kings10:7 I did not believe it until I came and saw it with my own eyes! And behold
1Kings10:8 Happy are your men
1Kings10:9 Blessed be the Lord your God
1Kings10:10 She gave the king one hundred and twenty talents of gold and a great amount of spices and precious stones. No one gave as many spices as the queen of Sheba gave to King Solomon.
1Kings10:11 The ships of Hiram
1Kings10:12 The king made pillars for the house of the Lord out of the almug trees and harps also and psalteries for singers for the king’s house. Never before had such almug wood been brought
1Kings10:13 King Solomon gave to the queen of Sheba all she desired
1Kings10:14 Now the weight of gold that came to Solomon in one year was six hundred and sixty-six talents of gold.
1Kings10:15 In addition
1Kings10:16 King Solomon made two hundred large shields of beaten gold made of six hundred shekels of gold each.
1Kings10:17 He made three hundred shields of beaten gold with three minas of gold in each shield. And the king put them in the House of the Forest of Lebanon.
1Kings10:18 The king also made a great throne of ivory and overlaid it with the best gold.
1Kings10:19 The throne had six steps
1Kings10:20 Twelve lions stood on the sides of the six steps
1Kings10:21 All of King Solomon’s drinking cups were made of gold
1Kings10:22 For the king had ships at sea at Tarshish with the ships of Hiram. Every three years the ships of Tarshish came bringing gold
1Kings10:23 So King Solomon exceeded all the kings of the earth in terms of riches and wisdom.
1Kings10:24 All the earth came to Solomon to hear his wisdom
1Kings10:25 Everyone brought an annual tribute in the form of presents
1Kings10:26 Solomon gathered together chariots and horsemen. He had one thousand four hundred chariots and twelve thousand horsemen whom he stationed in the cities for chariots and with the king at Jerusalem.
1Kings10:27 The king made silver to be in Jerusalem as plentiful as stones
1Kings10:28 Solomon had horses brought out of Egypt along with linen yarn. The king’s merchants received the linen yarn at a price.
1Kings10:29 He brought chariots from Egypt at a price of six hundred shekels of silver and a horse for one hundred and fifty. And he exported them to all the kings of the Hittites and the kings of Aram
1Kings11:1 But King Solomon loved many foreign women in addition to the daughter of Pharaoh
1Kings11:2 from the nations which the Lord warned the Israelites about
1Kings11:3 He had seven hundred wives who were princesses and three hundred concubines
1Kings11:4 For when Solomon was old
1Kings11:5 For Solomon went after Ashtoreth
1Kings11:6 Solomon did what was evil in the sight of the Lord and did not fully follow the Lord as his father David had done.
1Kings11:7 Then Solomon built a high place for Chemosh
1Kings11:8 He did the same for all his foreign wives
1Kings11:9 The Lord was angry with Solomon because he turned his heart away from the Lord God of Israel
1Kings11:10 and had warned him about this
1Kings11:11 Therefore the Lord said to Solomon
1Kings11:12 I will not do this in your lifetime for your father David’s sake
1Kings11:13 However
1Kings11:14 The Lord stirred up an adversary against Solomon
1Kings11:15 For when David was in Edom
1Kings11:16 (for six months Joab stayed there with his men until he had killed every male in Edom).
1Kings11:17 But Hadad fled to Egypt
1Kings11:18 They set out from Midian and went to Paran
1Kings11:19 Pharaoh had great affection for Hadad
1Kings11:20 Tahpenes’ sister gave birth to his son
1Kings11:21 When Hadad heard in Egypt that David slept with his fathers and that Joab
1Kings11:22 Then Pharaoh said to him
1Kings11:23 God stirred up another adversary against him
1Kings11:24 He gathered a group of men and became leader over a band when David killed the men of Zobah
1Kings11:25 He was an enemy of Israel all the days of Solomon in addition to the troubles caused by Hadad
1Kings11:26 Jeroboam the son of Nebat
1Kings11:27 This is what led to his rebellion against the king. Solomon built Millo and repaired the wall of the City of David his father.
1Kings11:28 Jeroboam was a mighty man of valor
1Kings11:29 At that time
1Kings11:30 Ahijah took off the new garment that he wore and tore it into twelve pieces
1Kings11:31 and he said to Jeroboam: Take ten pieces
1Kings11:32 (but he shall have one tribe for My servant David’s sake and for the sake of Jerusalem
1Kings11:33 because they have forsaken Me and have worshipped Ashtoreth
1Kings11:34 “However
1Kings11:35 But I will take the kingdom out of his son’s hand and will give ten tribes to you.
1Kings11:36 To his son will I give one tribe
1Kings11:37 I will take you
1Kings11:38 It shall be
1Kings11:39 I will thus afflict the seed of David
1Kings11:40 Solomon therefore sought to kill Jeroboam. And Jeroboam arose and fled to Egypt to Shishak
1Kings11:41 The rest of the acts of Solomon and all that he did and his wisdom
1Kings11:42 And the time that Solomon reigned in Jerusalem over all Israel was forty years.
1Kings11:43 Solomon slept with his fathers and was buried in the City of David his father
1Kings12:1 And Rehoboam went to Shechem
1Kings12:2 When Jeroboam the son of Nebat
1Kings12:3 they called and sent for him. And Jeroboam and all the congregation of Israel came and spoke to Rehoboam
1Kings12:4 “Your father made our yoke unbearable. Now
1Kings12:5 He said to them
1Kings12:6 King Rehoboam consulted with the old men who advised his father Solomon while he was still alive and asked
1Kings12:7 And they spoke to him
1Kings12:8 But he rejected the advice that the old men gave him and consulted with the young men who grew up with him and who stood before him
1Kings12:9 and he said to them
1Kings12:10 And the young men who grew up with him said
1Kings12:11 Whereas my father loaded you with a heavy yoke
1Kings12:12 So Jeroboam and all the people came to Rehoboam on the third day
1Kings12:13 The king answered the people roughly and forsook the counsel the old men gave him
1Kings12:14 and instead spoke to them following the advice of the young men. He said
1Kings12:15 Thus the king did not listen to the people
1Kings12:16 So when all Israel saw that the king did not listen to them
1Kings12:17 But the people of Israel living in the cities of Judah were ruled over by Rehoboam.
1Kings12:18 Then King Rehoboam sent Adoniram
1Kings12:19 So Israel has been in rebellion against the house of David to this day.
1Kings12:20 When all Israel heard that Jeroboam had returned
1Kings12:21 When Rehoboam arrived at Jerusalem
1Kings12:22 But the word of God came to Shemaiah the man of God
1Kings12:23 Speak to Rehoboam the son of Solomon
1Kings12:24 “Thus says the Lord: You shall not go up
1Kings12:25 Then Jeroboam built Shechem in Mount Ephraim and lived there and went out from there and built Peniel.
1Kings12:26 Jeroboam said in his heart
1Kings12:27 If this people go up to do sacrifice in the house of the Lord at Jerusalem
1Kings12:28 At that point
1Kings12:29 He set one in Bethel
1Kings12:30 This was a sin
1Kings12:31 He also made houses on high places and appointed priests from among all the people who were not Levites.
1Kings12:32 Jeroboam ordained a feast in the eighth month
1Kings12:33 So he made offerings on the altar that he had made in Bethel on the fifteenth day of the eighth month
1Kings13:1 And behold
1Kings13:2 He cried against the altar by the word of the Lord and said
1Kings13:3 He gave a sign the same day
1Kings13:4 When King Jeroboam heard the saying of the man of God who had cried against the altar in Bethel
1Kings13:5 The altar also was torn
1Kings13:6 The king said to the man of God
1Kings13:7 The king said to the man of God
1Kings13:8 The man of God said to the king
1Kings13:9 for so I was commanded by the word of the Lord
1Kings13:10 So he went another way and did not return by the same way he came to Bethel.
1Kings13:11 Now there lived an old prophet in Bethel
1Kings13:12 Their father said to them
1Kings13:13 He said to his sons
1Kings13:14 He went after the man of God and found him sitting under an oak
1Kings13:15 Then he said to him
1Kings13:16 He said
1Kings13:17 for I was commanded by the word of the Lord: You shall eat no bread and drink no water there
1Kings13:18 He said to him
1Kings13:19 So he went back with him to his house and ate bread and drank water.
1Kings13:20 Then as they sat at the table
1Kings13:21 and he cried out to the man of God who came from Judah
1Kings13:22 but instead came back and have eaten bread and drunk water in the place of which the Lord told you to eat no bread and drink no water
1Kings13:23 After he had eaten bread and had drunk
1Kings13:24 As he was going
1Kings13:25 And behold
1Kings13:26 When the prophet who brought him back from the way heard about it
1Kings13:27 He said to his sons
1Kings13:28 He then went and found his body thrown in the road
1Kings13:29 The prophet picked up the body of the man of God and laid it on his donkey and brought it back. The old prophet came to the city to mourn and to bury him.
1Kings13:30 He laid his body in his own tomb
1Kings13:31 And after he had buried him
1Kings13:32 For the saying that he cried out by the word of the Lord against the altar in Bethel and against all the houses of the high places that are in the cities of Samaria shall surely come to pass.”
1Kings13:33 After this event Jeroboam did not turn from his evil ways
1Kings13:34 This matter became sin to the house of Jeroboam
1Kings14:1 At that time
1Kings14:2 Jeroboam said to his wife
1Kings14:3 Take ten loaves
1Kings14:4 Jeroboam’s wife did so and arose and went to Shiloh
1Kings14:5 The Lord said to Ahijah
1Kings14:6 And so when Ahijah heard the sound of her feet as she came in the door
1Kings14:7 Go tell Jeroboam
1Kings14:8 and took the kingdom away from the house of David and gave it you. Yet you have not been as My servant David
1Kings14:9 but you have sinned more than all who were before you
1Kings14:10 “ ‘Therefore
1Kings14:11 Descendants of Jeroboam who die in the city will be eaten by dogs
1Kings14:12 “Arise therefore and go to your own house. When your feet enter the city
1Kings14:13 All Israel will mourn for him and bury him
1Kings14:14 “Moreover the Lord will raise up a king over Israel who will destroy the house of Jeroboam this day and from now on.
1Kings14:15 For the Lord will smite Israel
1Kings14:16 He shall give Israel up because of the sins of Jeroboam who sinned and who led Israel to sin.”
1Kings14:17 Jeroboam’s wife arose and departed and came to Tirzah. As she arrived at the threshold of the door
1Kings14:18 and they buried him
1Kings14:19 Now the rest of the acts of Jeroboam
1Kings14:20 Jeroboam reigned twenty-two years
1Kings14:21 Rehoboam the son of Solomon reigned in Judah. Rehoboam was forty-one years old when he began to reign
1Kings14:22 Judah did evil in the sight of the Lord
1Kings14:23 For they also built high places and images and Asherah poles on every high hill and under every green tree.
1Kings14:24 There were also male cult prostitutes in the land
1Kings14:25 In the fifth year of King Rehoboam
1Kings14:26 He took away all the treasures of the house of the Lord and the treasures of the king’s house
1Kings14:27 King Rehoboam replaced them with bronze shields and committed them to the hands of the chief of the guard who guarded the king’s house.
1Kings14:28 And whenever the king entered the house of the Lord
1Kings14:29 Now the rest of the acts of Rehoboam and all that he did
1Kings14:30 There was war between Rehoboam and Jeroboam all their days.
1Kings14:31 Rehoboam slept with his fathers and was buried with his fathers in the City of David. And his mother’s name was Naamah
1Kings15:1 Now in the eighteenth year of King Jeroboam the son of Nebat
1Kings15:2 He reigned three years in Jerusalem. His mother’s name was Maacah
1Kings15:3 He walked in all the sins of his father that he had done before him
1Kings15:4 Nevertheless
1Kings15:5 because David did that which was right in the eyes of the Lord and did not turn aside from anything that He commanded him all the days of his life
1Kings15:6 The war begun between Rehoboam and Jeroboam continued all the days of his life.

1Kings15:9 In the twentieth year of Jeroboam king of Israel
1Kings15:10 He reigned forty-one years in Jerusalem
1Kings15:11 Asa did what was right in the eyes of the Lord
1Kings15:12 He expelled the male cult prostitutes from the land and removed all the idols that his fathers had made.
1Kings15:13 In addition he even deposed his grandmother Maacah as queen
1Kings15:14 But the high places were not all removed
1Kings15:15 He brought into the house of the Lord the things that both his father and he himself had dedicated
1Kings15:16 There was war between Asa and Baasha king of Israel all their days.
1Kings15:17 Baasha king of Israel went up against Judah and fortified Ramah so he could prevent anyone from going out or coming in to Asa king of Judah.
1Kings15:18 Then Asa took all the silver and the gold that were left in the treasures of the house of the Lord
1Kings15:19 “There is a treaty between me and you and between my father and your father. Behold

1Kings15:21 When Baasha heard about it
1Kings15:22 Then King Asa made a proclamation throughout all Judah—none was exempted—and they took away the stones of Ramah and the timber with which Baasha had built
1Kings15:23 The rest of all the acts of Asa and all his might and all that he did and the cities that he built
1Kings15:24 Asa then slept with his fathers and was buried with them in the city of his father David
1Kings15:25 Nadab the son of Jeroboam began to reign over Israel in the second year of Asa king of Judah and reigned over Israel two years.
1Kings15:26 He did evil in the sight of the Lord and walked in the way of his father and in his sin with which he made Israel to sin.
1Kings15:27 Baasha the son of Ahijah of the house of Issachar conspired against him
1Kings15:28 It was in the third year of Asa king of Judah that Baasha killed him and took his throne.
1Kings15:29 When he became king
1Kings15:30 because of the sins of Jeroboam which he sinned and which he made Israel sin

1Kings16:8 In the twenty-sixth year of Asa king of Judah

1Kings16:22 But the people who followed Omri defeated the people who followed Tibni

1Kings16:25 But Omri did evil in the eyes of the Lord and did worse than all who preceded him

1Kings16:33 Ahab made an Asherah and did more to provoke the Lord God of Israel to anger than all the kings of Israel who preceded him.

2Kings17:1 In the twelfth year of Ahaz king of Judah
2Kings17:2 He did evil in the sight of the Lord
2Kings17:3 Shalmaneser king of Assyria came up against him. Hoshea became his servant and gave him gifts.
2Kings17:4 But the king of Assyria found conspiracy in Hoshea
2Kings17:5 Then the king of Assyria went throughout all the land
2Kings17:6 In the ninth year of Hoshea
2Kings17:7 This happened because the Israelites had sinned against the Lord their God
2Kings17:8 and walked in the statutes of the nations
2Kings17:9 The Israelites ascribed things to the Lord their God that were not so
2Kings17:10 They set up standing stones and Asherah poles on every high hill and under every green tree.
2Kings17:11 There they burned incense on all the high places
2Kings17:12 for they served idols
2Kings17:13 But the Lord warned Israel and Judah by all the prophets and by all the seers
2Kings17:14 But they would not listen. They stiffened their necks
2Kings17:15 They rejected His statutes and His covenant that He had made with their fathers and the decrees He had given them. They followed idols
2Kings17:16 They forsook all the commandments of the Lord their God
2Kings17:17 They caused their sons and daughters to pass through the fire
2Kings17:18 Therefore the Lord was very angry with Israel and removed them from His presence. None remained except the tribe of Judah.
2Kings17:19 Judah also did not keep the commandments of the Lord their God
2Kings17:20 The Lord rejected all the seed of Israel
2Kings17:21 For He had torn Israel from the house of David
2Kings17:22 For the Israelites walked in all the sins which Jeroboam committed. They did not turn aside from them
2Kings17:23 until the Lord removed Israel from His presence as He had said by all His servants the prophets. So Israel was exiled from their land to Assyria until this day.
2Kings17:24 Then the king of Assyria brought people from Babylon
2Kings17:25 Right at the beginning of their settling there
2Kings17:26 So they said to the king of Assyria
2Kings17:27 Then the king of Assyria commanded
2Kings17:28 Then one of the priests whom they had exiled from Samaria came and lived in Bethel. He taught them how they should fear the Lord.
2Kings17:29 But each nation was making its own gods
2Kings17:30 The men of Babylon made Sukkoth Benoth
2Kings17:31 the Avvites made Nibhaz and Tartak. The Sepharvites were burning their children in fire to Adrammelek and Anammelek
2Kings17:32 They feared the Lord and made from amongst themselves priests of the high places
2Kings17:33 They feared the Lord
2Kings17:34 To this day they continue to practice their former customs. They do not fear the Lord
2Kings17:35 The Lord had made a covenant and commanded them
2Kings17:36 Rather
2Kings17:37 The statutes
2Kings17:38 The covenant that I have made with you
2Kings17:39 Rather the Lord your God you shall fear
2Kings17:40 But they did not listen; rather they were practicing their former customs.
2Kings17:41 So these nations feared the Lord and were serving their carved images

2Kings18:2 He was twenty-five years old when he became king

2Chronicles3:1 So Solomon began to build the house of the Lord in Jerusalem on Mount Moriah
2Chronicles3:2 He began to build in the second month on the second day during the fourth year of his reign.
2Chronicles3:3 These are the foundation measurements that Solomon used for building the house of God. The cubit length in the former standard measure was sixty cubits and the width twenty cubits.
2Chronicles3:4 The porch vestibule that was in front of the nave hall had its length
2Chronicles3:5 And he paneled the great house with cypress trees and then covered it with fine gold. Then he decorated it with palm trees and chain work.
2Chronicles3:6 And he overlaid the house with precious stones for decoration
2Chronicles3:7 And he covered the house—its beams
2Chronicles3:8 Then he made the dwelling of the Most Holy Place. Its length was as the width of twenty cubits
2Chronicles3:9 And the weight in gold for the nails was fifty shekels. And he covered the upper chamber in gold.
2Chronicles3:10 Then in the dwelling of the Most Holy Place
2Chronicles3:11 And the wings of the cherubim were the length of twenty cubits. The wing of one cherub was five cubits
2Chronicles3:12 And the wing of this cherub was five cubits
2Chronicles3:13 So the wings of these cherubim spread out were twenty cubits; they were upright on their feet facing inward toward the house.
2Chronicles3:14 Then he made the curtain of violet
2Chronicles3:15 And he made two pillars in front of the house thirty-five cubits high
2Chronicles3:16 Then he made ornamental chain work
2Chronicles3:17 He then raised the pillars in front of the temple
2Chronicles4:1 Then he made a bronze altar that was twenty cubits long
2Chronicles4:2 And he made a cast metal sea as a water basin. It was round and ten cubits from edge to edge
2Chronicles4:3 Figures like oxen were underneath it
2Chronicles4:4 It stood on twelve oxen
2Chronicles4:5 The thickness of the sea was a palm
2Chronicles4:6 Then he made ten water basins
2Chronicles4:7 And he made ten lampstands of gold according to the specifications
2Chronicles4:8 Then he made ten tables and put them in the temple
2Chronicles4:9 Then he made the court of the priests and the great enclosure and the doors for the enclosure
2Chronicles4:10 And he set the sea basin at the southeast side
2Chronicles4:11 And Huram made the pots
2Chronicles4:12 the two pillars
2Chronicles4:13 and the four hundred pomegranates for the two pieces of grating
2Chronicles4:14 And he made the supporting stands and the basins on top of the stands
2Chronicles4:15 and the sea basin and the twelve oxen underneath it;
2Chronicles4:16 the pots
2Chronicles4:17 The king cast these vessels in the district of the Jordan in earthen foundries between Sukkoth and Zeredatha.
2Chronicles4:18 And Solomon made all of these items in a great abundance such that they did not consider the weight of the bronze.
2Chronicles4:19 So Solomon made all the vessels that were in the house of God: the gold altar and the tables displaying the showbread
2Chronicles4:20 and the finely hammered lampstands with their lamps to burn before the Most Holy Place as prescribed;
2Chronicles4:21 the golden flower-shaped ornaments
2Chronicles4:22 the snuffers
2Chronicles5:1 Thus all the work that Solomon did for the house of the Lord was finished. And Solomon brought in the things that David his father had dedicated: the silver and the gold and all the furnishings in the treasury of the house of God.
2Chronicles5:2 Then Solomon assembled the elders of Israel and all the heads of the tribes and the leaders of the houses of the fathers among the Israelites to bring up the ark of the covenant of the Lord from the City of David
2Chronicles5:3 And all the men of Israel were assembled before the king at the feast
2Chronicles5:4 All the elders of Israel came
2Chronicles5:5 Then they brought up the ark
2Chronicles5:6 And King Solomon and all the congregation of Israel
2Chronicles5:7 The priests brought the ark of the covenant of the Lord to its place—to the inner sanctuary of the temple
2Chronicles5:8 Then the cherubim wings were spread out over the place of the ark so that the cherubim covered the ark and its poles.
2Chronicles5:9 The poles were extended so that the ends of the poles of the ark were seen in front of the Most Holy Place
2Chronicles5:10 In the ark there was nothing except the two tablets that Moses had given at Mount Horeb where the Lord made a covenant with the Israelites when they went out from Egypt.
2Chronicles5:11 When the priests came out from the Most Holy Place—for all the priests who were present had consecrated themselves
2Chronicles5:12 and all the Levitical singers
2Chronicles5:13 it happened
2Chronicles5:14 And the priests were not able to stand in order to serve because of the cloud
2Chronicles6:1 Then Solomon said
2Chronicles6:2 But I have built for You an exalted house
2Chronicles6:3 Then the king turned his face around
2Chronicles6:4 And he said: “Blessed be the Lord God of Israel who spoke with David my father and fulfilled His promise
2Chronicles6:5 ‘Since the day that I brought out My people from the land of Egypt
2Chronicles6:6 but I have chosen Jerusalem for My name to dwell there
2Chronicles6:7 “And it was in the heart of David my father to build a house for the name of the Lord God of Israel.
2Chronicles6:8 But the Lord said to David my father
2Chronicles6:9 only you will not be the one to build the house. For your son
2Chronicles6:10 “The Lord has fulfilled the word that He spoke. For I have risen up in the place of David my father and sit on the throne of Israel
2Chronicles6:11 And there I have set the ark
2Chronicles6:12 Then Solomon stood before the altar of the Lord before all the assembly of Israel and spread out his hands.
2Chronicles6:13 For Solomon made a bronze platform and set it in the midst of the enclosure. It was five cubits in length
2Chronicles6:14 And he said: “O  Lord God of Israel
2Chronicles6:15 You have kept what You promised Your servant David my father. You have both spoken with Your mouth and fulfilled it with Your hand
2Chronicles6:16 “And now
2Chronicles6:17 And now
2Chronicles6:18 “For will God indeed dwell with man on the earth? Behold
2Chronicles6:19 But respond to the prayer of Your servant and to his plea
2Chronicles6:20 that Your eyes might be open toward this house both day and night
2Chronicles6:21 And listen to the pleas of Your servant and Your people Israel when they pray toward this place. And may You respond from heaven
2Chronicles6:22 “If a man sins against his companion
2Chronicles6:23 then You will hear from heaven
2Chronicles6:24 “If Your people Israel are struck before enemies because they have sinned against You
2Chronicles6:25 then You will hear from heaven and forgive the sin of Your people Israel
2Chronicles6:26 “When the sky is shut up and there is no rain because they have sinned against You
2Chronicles6:27 then You will hear from heaven and forgive the sin of Your servants and Your people Israel because You will teach them the good path in which they will walk
2Chronicles6:28 “When there is famine in the land or when there is pestilence
2Chronicles6:29 whatever prayer or plea that is made by any man or by all your people Israel
2Chronicles6:30 then You will hear from heaven
2Chronicles6:31 so that they may fear You and walk in Your ways all the days that they live on the land that You have given to our fathers.
2Chronicles6:32 “When foreigners are not from Your people Israel and come from a distant land
2Chronicles6:33 then hear from heaven
2Chronicles6:34 “When Your people go out to battle against their enemies
2Chronicles6:35 then hear from heaven their prayer and plea and act for their cause.
2Chronicles6:36 “When they sin against You (for there is no one who does not sin) and You are angry against them and give them to their enemies
2Chronicles6:37 and they turn their hearts in the land that they have been taken captive
2Chronicles6:38 and if they turn in repentance to You with all their heart and all their soul in the land of their captivity that they were taken to
2Chronicles6:39 then hear from heaven
2Chronicles6:40 “Now
2Chronicles6:41 “Now rise up
2Chronicles6:42 O  Lord God
2Chronicles7:1 And when Solomon finished praying
2Chronicles7:2 And the priests were not able to enter into the house of the Lord
2Chronicles7:3 And all the Israelites saw when the fire came down and the glory of the Lord came on the temple
2Chronicles7:4 Then the king and all the people were making sacrifices before the Lord.
2Chronicles7:5 King Solomon sacrificed twenty-two thousand oxen and one hundred and twenty thousand sheep. So the king and all the people dedicated the house of God.
2Chronicles7:6 The priests stood at their positions
2Chronicles7:7 And Solomon consecrated the middle of the court that was in front of the house of the Lord
2Chronicles7:8 And at the appointed time
2Chronicles7:9 Then on the eighth day they made a solemn assembly because they had made a consecration of the altar for seven days and then the feast for seven days more.
2Chronicles7:10 Then on the twenty-third day of the seventh month Solomon sent the people away to their homes. They were joyful and good of heart because of what the Lord had done for David
2Chronicles7:11 Thus Solomon finished the house of the Lord and the king’s house. And Solomon successfully accomplished everything that came into his heart to do in the house of the Lord and in his own house.
2Chronicles7:12 Then the Lord appeared to Solomon at night
2Chronicles7:13 “When I shut up the heaven and there is no rain
2Chronicles7:14 if My people
2Chronicles7:15 Now My eyes will be open and My ears attentive to the prayer of this place.
2Chronicles7:16 So now I have chosen and consecrated this house that My name be there continually. My eyes and heart will be there for all days.
2Chronicles7:17 “And you
2Chronicles7:18 then I will set the throne of your kingdom as I made a covenant with David your father saying
2Chronicles7:19 “But if the people turn aside and abandon My statutes and commandments that I have given to you
2Chronicles7:20 then I will uproot them from My land that I have given to them; and the house that I have consecrated for My name
2Chronicles7:21 And even though this house was majestic
2Chronicles7:22 Then they will say
2Chronicles8:1 It came that after twenty years
2Chronicles8:2 and Solomon even rebuilt the cities that Hiram had given to him
2Chronicles8:3 Then Solomon went to Hamath Zobah and seized it.
2Chronicles8:4 He also built Tadmor in the wilderness and all the store cities that he built in Hamath.
2Chronicles8:5 He also built the Upper and Lower Beth-horon
2Chronicles8:6 and Baalath
2Chronicles8:7 And all the people who remained from the Hittites
2Chronicles8:8 and from their descendants who remained from these peoples in the land and whom the Israelites did not finish destroying
2Chronicles8:9 But from the Israelites Solomon did not make servants for his work. These were his soldiers
2Chronicles8:10 And these were the two hundred and fifty chief officers for King Solomon who governed the people.
2Chronicles8:11 And Solomon brought up the daughter of Pharaoh from the City of David to the house that he built for her
2Chronicles8:12 At that time Solomon offered up burnt offerings on the altar of the Lord that he built before the vestibule
2Chronicles8:13 according to the daily duty to offer up as the commandment of Moses for Sabbaths
2Chronicles8:14 According to the ruling of David his father
2Chronicles8:15 And they did not turn from the commandment of the king
2Chronicles8:16 So all the work of Solomon was established from the day of the foundation of the house of the Lord and until its completion; and the house of the Lord was complete.
2Chronicles8:17 Then Solomon went to Ezion Geber and Elath at the shore of the sea in Edom.
2Chronicles8:18 And Hiram sent him ships by the hand of his servants
2Chronicles9:1 The queen of Sheba heard a report of Solomon
2Chronicles9:2 Solomon declared to her everything that she asked
2Chronicles9:3 When the queen of Sheba had observed the wisdom of Solomon
2Chronicles9:4 the food on his table
2Chronicles9:5 Then she said to the king
2Chronicles9:6 But I did not believe their reports until I came and my eyes saw; and behold
2Chronicles9:7 How happy your men must be! How happy these servants
2Chronicles9:8 May the Lord your God be blessed
2Chronicles9:9 Then she gave the king one hundred and twenty talents of gold and a great abundance of spices and precious stones. And there were no spices like those that the queen of Sheba gave to King Solomon.
2Chronicles9:10 Moreover
2Chronicles9:11 And the king used the algum wood for steps for the house of the Lord and the palace of the king
2Chronicles9:12 And King Solomon gave everything to the queen of Sheba in which she had pleasure
2Chronicles9:13 The weight of gold that came to Solomon every year was six hundred and sixty-six talents of gold.
2Chronicles9:14 In addition to what the explorers and merchants brought in
2Chronicles9:15 King Solomon made two hundred large shields of hammered gold
2Chronicles9:16 And he made three hundred shields of hammered gold; three hundred pieces of gold were used for each shield. And the king put them in the House of the Forest of Lebanon.
2Chronicles9:17 And the king made a great throne of ivory
2Chronicles9:18 And there were six steps and a gold footstool attached to the throne
2Chronicles9:19 So twelve lions stood there on the six steps
2Chronicles9:20 Even all the drinking vessels of King Solomon were gold
2Chronicles9:21 The ships of Solomon went to Tarshish with the servants of Hiram. Once every three years the ships of Tarshish returned carrying gold
2Chronicles9:22 So King Solomon was greater than all the kings of the earth in wealth and wisdom.
2Chronicles9:23 All the kings of the earth sought out an audience before Solomon to hear his wisdom that God gave to his mind.
2Chronicles9:24 Every year each man brought his own tribute
2Chronicles9:25 Solomon had four thousand stalls for horses and chariots and twelve thousand horses
2Chronicles9:26 He ruled over all the kings from the Euphrates River to the land of the Philistines and to the border of Egypt.
2Chronicles9:27 So the king made silver in Jerusalem as abundant as stones
2Chronicles9:28 The horses of Solomon were imported from Egypt and from all other lands.
2Chronicles9:29 And the rest of the acts of Solomon
2Chronicles9:30 So Solomon reigned in Jerusalem over all Israel for forty years.
2Chronicles9:31 And Solomon slept with his fathers
2Chronicles10:1 Rehoboam went to Shechem because all Israel journeyed to Shechem to make him king.
2Chronicles10:2 It happened that Jeroboam the son of Nebat heard this—he was in Egypt
2Chronicles10:3 So they sent for him and called him. And Jeroboam and all Israel came and spoke to Rehoboam saying
2Chronicles10:4 “Your father made our yoke heavy. Now therefore
2Chronicles10:5 He responded
2Chronicles10:6 Then King Rehoboam consulted with the elders who stood in the presence of Solomon his father when he was alive saying
2Chronicles10:7 They said to him
2Chronicles10:8 But he abandoned the counsel of the elders that they gave to him. And he consulted the young men who grew up and stood before him.
2Chronicles10:9 Rehoboam said to them
2Chronicles10:10 The young men who had grown up with him said
2Chronicles10:11 Whereas my father placed a heavy yoke on you
2Chronicles10:12 So Jeroboam and all the people came to Rehoboam on the third day
2Chronicles10:13 Then the king answered them harshly. King Rehoboam rejected the advice of the elders.
2Chronicles10:14 He spoke to them with the advice of the young men
2Chronicles10:15 The king did not listen to the people
2Chronicles10:16 When all Israel saw that the king would not listen to them
2Chronicles10:17 The Israelites dwelt in the cities of Judah
2Chronicles10:18 Then King Rehoboam sent Hadoram
2Chronicles10:19 So Israel has rebelled against the house of David until this day.
2Chronicles11:1 When Rehoboam came to Jerusalem
2Chronicles11:2 But the word of the Lord came to Shemaiah the man of God saying:
2Chronicles11:3 Speak to Rehoboam
2Chronicles11:4 “So says the Lord: You are not to go up and fight against your brothers. Each man must return to his house
2Chronicles11:5 So Rehoboam dwelt in Jerusalem and built siege cities in Judah.
2Chronicles11:6 He built Bethlehem
2Chronicles11:7 Beth-zur in the east
2Chronicles11:8 Gath
2Chronicles11:9 Adoraim
2Chronicles11:10 Zorah
2Chronicles11:11 He strengthened the fortresses and set leaders in them with stockpiles of food
2Chronicles11:12 And in all the cities he placed shields and spears
2Chronicles11:13 And the priests and Levites in all Israel came before him from all their territories.
2Chronicles11:14 For the Levites left their pasturelands and properties
2Chronicles11:15 And he set for himself priests for the high places and for the goat and calf idols that he made.
2Chronicles11:16 And those who set their hearts to seek the Lord God of Israel followed after them from all the tribes of Israel
2Chronicles11:17 So they strengthened the kingdom of Judah and supported Rehoboam
2Chronicles11:18 And Rehoboam took Mahalath for a wife
2Chronicles11:19 and she bore sons to him: Jeush
2Chronicles11:20 Then after her he took Maacah the daughter of Absalom. And she bore to him Abijah
2Chronicles11:21 And Rehoboam loved Maacah the daughter of Absalom more than his other wives and concubines (for he took eighteen wives and sixty concubines)
2Chronicles11:22 And Rehoboam set Abijah the son of Maacah as chief and head over his brothers for he planned to make him king.
2Chronicles11:23 And he was discerning and spread out all his sons to all the lands of Judah and Benjamin
2Chronicles12:1 Now when the reign of Rehoboam was established and strong
2Chronicles12:2 And in the fifth year of King Rehoboam
2Chronicles12:3 with twelve hundred chariots and sixty thousand horses. The people were without number who came with him from Egypt—Libyans
2Chronicles12:4 And he captured the fortified cities that were in Judah
2Chronicles12:5 Then Shemaiah the prophet came to Rehoboam and the rulers of Judah who were assembled in Jerusalem before Shishak
2Chronicles12:6 Then the king and the rulers of Israel humbled themselves and said
2Chronicles12:7 So when the Lord observed that they had humbled themselves
2Chronicles12:8 For they will be slaves to him so that they experience My labor and the labor of the kingdoms of other lands.”
2Chronicles12:9 So Shishak king of Egypt went up against Jerusalem and took away the treasures of the house of the Lord and of the palace of the king. He took everything
2Chronicles12:10 And King Rehoboam made bronze shields in their place and entrusted them to the hands of the rulers of the guards
2Chronicles12:11 And it happened that whenever the king came to the house of the Lord
2Chronicles12:12 And when he humbled himself
2Chronicles12:13 So King Rehoboam was strong and reigned in Jerusalem. For Rehoboam was forty-one years old when he became king
2Chronicles12:14 And he acted evil because he did not set his heart to seek out the Lord.
2Chronicles12:15 Now
2Chronicles12:16 And Rehoboam slept with his fathers and was buried in the City of David
2Chronicles13:1 In the eighteenth year of King Jeroboam
2Chronicles13:2 He reigned for three years in Judah
2Chronicles13:3 Abijah set the battle in order with an army of valiant men of war
2Chronicles13:4 Then Abijah went up to Mount Zemaraim that is in the hills of Ephraim
2Chronicles13:5 Do you all not know that the Lord God of Israel has perpetually given the kingdom of Israel to David
2Chronicles13:6 And Jeroboam the son of Nebat
2Chronicles13:7 Then vain
2Chronicles13:8 “And now you think that you can withstand before the kingdom of the Lord by the hand of the sons of David. And you all are a great multitude
2Chronicles13:9 Have you all not driven out the priests of the Lord
2Chronicles13:10 “But for us
2Chronicles13:11 They sacrifice burnt offerings to the Lord every morning and evening and put out an incense of spices. They also set the showbread in place on the ritual table and set the golden lampstand with its lamps to burn every evening. For we keep the duty of the Lord our God

2Chronicles13:16 The men of Israel fled before Judah

2Chronicles13:21 So Abijah grew strong and took fourteen wives and had twenty-two sons and sixteen daughters.

2Chronicles14:1 And Abijah slept with his fathers
2Chronicles14:2 Asa did what was good and right in the eyes of the Lord his God.
2Chronicles14:3 He took down the foreign altars and high places
2Chronicles14:4 Then he urged Judah to seek the Lord the God of their fathers and to keep the law and commandments.
2Chronicles14:5 And he also removed all the high places and incense altars in the cities in Judah. And the kingdom was at rest under Asa.
2Chronicles14:6 He built fortified cities in Judah because the land was quiet; and there was no war in these years
2Chronicles14:7 And he said to Judah
2Chronicles14:8 And Asa had an army from Judah carrying large shields and spears numbering three hundred thousand. And from Benjamin he had an army carrying shields and bows numbering two hundred and eighty thousand. All of these were mighty men of valor.
2Chronicles14:9 But Zerah the Cushite came out against them with an army of one million men and three hundred chariots. And he came up to Mareshah.
2Chronicles14:10 Then Asa went out to engage him
2Chronicles14:11 And Asa cried out to the Lord his God
2Chronicles14:12 So the Lord struck down the Cushites before Asa and Judah
2Chronicles14:13 Then Asa and those with him pursued them until Gerar. And the Cushites fell till there was not one left alive because they were struck down before the Lord and His army. And those in Judah carried off a very great plunder.
2Chronicles14:14 And they struck down all the cities surrounding Gerar because the terror of the Lord was on them. And they plundered all the cities because there was a great amount of possessions in them.
2Chronicles14:15 And they also struck the tents holding livestock
2Chronicles15:1 Then the Spirit of God came on Azariah the son of Oded
2Chronicles15:2 and he went out to meet Asa and said to him
2Chronicles15:3 For a long time Israel has been without the true God
2Chronicles15:4 but when in their trouble they turned to the Lord God of Israel
2Chronicles15:5 And at that time there was no peace to the one traveling in or out because there was a great panic on all those who dwelt in the land.
2Chronicles15:6 They were broken to pieces
2Chronicles15:7 But you all must be strong and not lose heart
2Chronicles15:8 And when Asa heard these words of the prophecy of Azariah son of Oded the prophet
2Chronicles15:9 And he gathered together all of Judah and Benjamin and those who had settled among them from Ephraim
2Chronicles15:10 These were gathered in Jerusalem in the third month in the fifteenth year in the reign of Asa.
2Chronicles15:11 They sacrificed to the Lord that day from the plunder that they brought in
2Chronicles15:12 And they entered into a covenant to seek the Lord God of their fathers with all their heart and soul.
2Chronicles15:13 But whoever would not seek the Lord God of Israel would be put to death
2Chronicles15:14 They swore an oath to the Lord in a loud voice
2Chronicles15:15 And all Judah rejoiced over this oath because they swore with their whole heart and they sought Him with all their desire; and He was found by them
2Chronicles15:16 King Asa even removed his mother Maacah from being queen mother because she had made a detestable image for Asherah. Asa cut down her image
2Chronicles15:17 But they did not remove the high places from Israel. Nevertheless the heart of Asa was wholly committed all his days.
2Chronicles15:18 He brought into the house of God the sacred gifts of his father and his own sacred gifts—silver
2Chronicles15:19 And there was no war until the thirty-fifth year of the reign of Asa.

2Chronicles16:2 Then Asa removed silver and gold from the storehouses of the house of the Lord and palace of the king

2Chronicles16:5 And it happened when Baasha heard this
2Chronicles16:6 Then King Asa took all of Judah
2Chronicles16:7 And at that time Hanani the seer came to King Asa of Judah saying
2Chronicles16:8 Were not the Cushites and Libyans a very large army with chariots and horses
2Chronicles16:9 For the eyes of the Lord move about on all the earth to strengthen the heart that is completely toward Him. You have acted foolishly in this
2Chronicles16:10 Then Asa was angry with the seer and placed him in prison stocks
2Chronicles16:11 The events of Asa from beginning to end are written in the book of the kings of Judah and Israel.
2Chronicles16:12 In the thirty-ninth year of the reign of Asa
2Chronicles16:13 So Asa slept with his fathers; he died in the forty-first year of his reign.
2Chronicles16:14 And they buried him in the tomb that he cut for himself in the City of David. And they laid him on a bier that had been filled with all types of spices mixed with ointments

2Chronicles17:2 He put an army in all the fortified cities of Judah and set military garrisons in the land of Judah
2Chronicles17:3 Now the Lord was with Jehoshaphat
2Chronicles17:4 but sought the God of his father and walked in His commandments
2Chronicles17:5 So the Lord established the kingdom in his hand
2Chronicles17:6 His heart was raised up for the ways of the Lord. He even removed the high places and Asherah poles from Judah.
2Chronicles17:7 In the third year of his reign he sent officials
2Chronicles17:8 and with them were also the Levites: Shemaiah
2Chronicles17:9 They taught the people in Judah
2Chronicles17:10 The terror of the Lord came on all the kingdoms of the land that surrounded Judah
2Chronicles17:11 Some from the Philistines brought Jehoshaphat presents and silver for tribute
2Chronicles17:12 Jehoshaphat continued to increase in greatness
2Chronicles17:13 And he had much property in the cities of Judah
2Chronicles17:14 This was their divisions by the houses of their father: From Judah
2Chronicles17:15 Beside him was Jehohanan the commander with two hundred and eighty thousand mighty men of war.
2Chronicles17:16 Beside him was Amasiah the son of Zikri
2Chronicles17:17 From Benjamin: Eliada a mighty man of war with two hundred thousand men armed with bow and shield.
2Chronicles17:18 Beside him was Jehozabad with one hundred and eighty thousand equipped for warfare.
2Chronicles17:19 These were serving the king in addition to those the king placed in the fortified cities throughout Judah.

2Chronicles32:1 After these things and these acts of faithfulness
2Chronicles32:2 When Hezekiah observed that Sennacherib had come and that he turned to war against Jerusalem
2Chronicles32:3 he consulted with his officials and military men to stop up the waters of the springs that were outside the city
2Chronicles32:4 So
2Chronicles32:5 He then worked hard to build up all the walls that were broken down and to raise up towers. Then he built another wall outside that one and strengthened the Millo in the City of David. And he made weapons and shields in abundance.
2Chronicles32:6 And he designated commanders for fighting over the people
2Chronicles32:7 “Be strong and brave. Do not fear or have terror before the king of Assyria or before all this army that is with him because there are more who are with us than with him.
2Chronicles32:8 With this king is a strong arm of flesh
2Chronicles32:9 After all this Sennacherib king of Assyria sent his servants to Jerusalem —while he was at Lachish with all his military force that was with him—to Hezekiah king of Judah and to all Judah that was in Jerusalem stating
2Chronicles32:10 “Thus says Sennacherib king of Assyria: By what do you all trust that you remain in a besieged Jerusalem?
2Chronicles32:11 Is not Hezekiah leading you all astray to give you over to death by famine and thirst when he tells you
2Chronicles32:12 Has not Hezekiah himself taken down this god’s high places and altars by ordering Judah and Jerusalem
2Chronicles32:13 “Do you not know what I and my fathers have done to all the peoples of other lands? Were the gods of the nations of these lands surely able to rescue their lands from my hand?
2Chronicles32:14 Who from among all the gods of these nations that my fathers utterly destroyed was able to rescue his people from my hand? For will your god be able to rescue you all from my hand?
2Chronicles32:15 Now do not let Hezekiah trick or lead you all astray in this. And do not believe him
2Chronicles32:16 And his servants continued to speak against the Lord God and Hezekiah his servant.
2Chronicles32:17 He also wrote letters to insult the Lord God of Israel
2Chronicles32:18 And they proclaimed it also in a loud voice in the Judean language against the people of Jerusalem who were on the wall to frighten and terrify them in order to capture the city.
2Chronicles32:19 They spoke about the God of Jerusalem like the gods of the other peoples of the earth
2Chronicles32:20 So Hezekiah the king and Isaiah the prophet son of Amoz prayed concerning this. And they called out to heaven.
2Chronicles32:21 So the Lord sent an angel and destroyed the mighty army
2Chronicles32:22 So the Lord saved Hezekiah and the inhabitants of Jerusalem from the hand of Sennacherib king of Assyria
2Chronicles32:23 Many brought tribute to the Lord in Jerusalem and choice gifts to Hezekiah king of Judah so that he was lifted up before the eyes of all the nations from then on.
2Chronicles32:24 In those days Hezekiah became ill even to the point of death
2Chronicles32:25 But Hezekiah did not make recompense for what was given to him because his heart was proud. So divine anger was on him
2Chronicles32:26 Then Hezekiah humbled himself from his arrogant heart
2Chronicles32:27 And Hezekiah had a vast amount of riches and honor
2Chronicles32:28 He made storehouses for the produce of grain
2Chronicles32:29 He also built cities for himself and acquired numbers of flocks and herds
2Chronicles32:30 And Hezekiah shut up the upper outlet of the waters of the Gihon and directed them downward to the western side of the City of David. And Hezekiah found success in all his works.
2Chronicles32:31 But the envoys came from the officials of Babylon who were sent to him to inquire about the sign that had been given in the land. God left him alone in order to test Hezekiah
2Chronicles32:32 Now the rest of the acts of Hezekiah and his kind acts
2Chronicles32:33 So Hezekiah slept with his fathers
2Chronicles33:1 Manasseh was twelve years old when he became king
2Chronicles33:2 But he did evil in the sight of the Lord
2Chronicles33:3 And he turned again to build the high places that his father Hezekiah had torn down
2Chronicles33:4 And he built altars in the house of the Lord where the Lord said
2Chronicles33:5 And he built altars for the starry assembly of heaven in the two courtyards of the house of the Lord.
2Chronicles33:6 He even made his sons pass through the fire in the Valley of Ben-hinnom; and he had conjurers
2Chronicles33:7 And the carved image of a statue that he made he set in the house of God
2Chronicles33:8 And I will not again remove the foot of Israel from the ground that I have designated to your fathers
2Chronicles33:9 So Manasseh made Judah and those living in Jerusalem to wander and to perform more evil than the nations that the Lord destroyed from before Israel.
2Chronicles33:10 The Lord spoke to Manasseh and his people
2Chronicles33:11 So the Lord brought on them the officials of the army of the king of Assyria. They captured Manasseh with hooks

2Chronicles33:22 And he did what was evil in the eyes of the Lord as his father Manasseh had done. And Amon sacrificed to and served all the idols that Manasseh his father had made.
2Chronicles33:23 He did not humble himself before the Lord as his father humbled himself. But he increased his guilt all the more.
2Chronicles33:24 And his servants plotted against him
2Chronicles33:25 Then the people of the land struck down all those who conspired against King Amon. The people of the land made Josiah his son king in his place.

2Chronicles34:9 When they came to Hilkiah the high priest

2Chronicles34:11 And they gave it to the craftsmen and builders to acquire quarried stones and timber for joists and beams for the buildings that the kings of Judah had ruined.

2Chronicles34:23 And she said to them
2Chronicles34:24 Thus says the Lord: Behold

2Chronicles34:26 And the king of Judah who sent you all to seek out the Lord

2Chronicles35:1 Josiah kept a Passover for the Lord in Jerusalem; they slaughtered the Passover lamb on the fourteenth day of the first month.
2Chronicles35:2 He appointed priests for their duties and encouraged them in their service to the house of the Lord.
2Chronicles35:3 And he said to the Levites who gave insight to all Israel and were consecrated to the Lord

2Chronicles35:5 “And stand in the Holy Place according to the divisions of the fathers’ households of your brothers
2Chronicles35:6 Then slaughter the Passover lamb and consecrate yourselves and prepare for your brothers to do according to the word of the Lord by the hand of Moses.”

2Chronicles35:15 The singers

2Chronicles35:17 And the Israelites who were present had the Passover at that time and the Feast of Unleavened Bread for seven days.
2Chronicles35:18 And no Passover had been made like this in Israel since the days of Samuel the prophet. And none of the kings of Israel had performed a Passover like Josiah had done. And the priests and Levites and all Judah and Israel were present along with those living in Jerusalem.

2Chronicles36:5 Jehoiakim was twenty-five years old when he began to reign

2Chronicles36:10 In the spring of that year King Nebuchadnezzar sent for him and brought him to Babylon with the precious items from the house of the Lord. Then Zedekiah his brother was king over Judah and Jerusalem.
2Chronicles36:11 Zedekiah was twenty-one years old when he began to reign
2Chronicles36:12 He did what was evil in the eyes of the Lord his God and did not humble himself before Jeremiah the prophet
2Chronicles36:13 He also rebelled against King Nebuchadnezzar
2Chronicles36:14 Even the officials over the priests and the people increased in their unfaithfulness in all the detestable practices of the nations

Ezra4:9 (Rehum the commander

Ezra4:16 We notify the king that if this city is rebuilt and the walls repaired by this means the portion Beyond the River will no longer be yours.”
Ezra4:17 The king sent an answer: “To Rehum the commander
Ezra4:18 “The letter which you sent to us has been translated and read before me.
Ezra4:19 I commanded
Ezra4:20 There have also been mighty kings over Jerusalem
Ezra4:21 Command these men to cease now
Ezra4:22 Take heed now that you do not fail to do this. Why should damage increase to the hurt of the king?”
Ezra4:23 Now when the copy of the letter by King Artaxerxes was read before Rehum
Ezra4:24 Then the work of the house of God in Jerusalem ceased. So it ceased until the second year of the reign of Darius king of Persia.

Ezra10:1 Now while Ezra prayed and confessed
Ezra10:2 Shecaniah the son of Jehiel
Ezra10:3 Now therefore let us make a covenant with our God to sever relations with all the women and their children according to the counsel of my lord and those who tremble at the commandment of our God. May this be done in accordance with the law.
Ezra10:4 Arise
Ezra10:5 Then Ezra stood and made the presiding Levitical priests and all Israel to swear an oath to act according to this word. And they swore an oath.
Ezra10:6 Then Ezra stood in front of the house of God and went into the chamber of Jehohanan the son of Eliashib. When he arrived there
Ezra10:7 They made a proclamation throughout Judah and Jerusalem for all the children of the exile to assemble in Jerusalem.
Ezra10:8 Whoever would not come within three days would forfeit all his possessions
Ezra10:9 Then all the men of Judah and Benjamin gathered themselves together to Jerusalem within the three days. It was the ninth month
Ezra10:10 Ezra the priest stood up and said to them
Ezra10:11 Now therefore make confession to the Lord God of your fathers and do what pleases Him: Sever your relationships with the people of the land
Ezra10:12 Then all the congregation answered loudly
Ezra10:13 But there are many people
Ezra10:14 Let now our rulers preside over all the congregation so that
Ezra10:15 Only Jonathan the son of Asahel and Jahzeiah the son of Tikvah opposed this
Ezra10:16 Then the descendants of the captivity did so. Ezra the priest selected men
Ezra10:17 and finished dealing with every man who had married foreign women by the first day of the first month.
Ezra10:18 Some of the sons of the priests were discovered to have wedded foreign women
Ezra10:19 They committed to sever their relations with their women
Ezra10:20 Likewise
Ezra10:21 Of the sons of Harim
Ezra10:22 Of the sons of Pashhur
Ezra10:23 Also
Ezra10:24 Among the singers
Ezra10:25 Further from Israel
Ezra10:26 Of the sons of Elam
Ezra10:27 Of the sons of Zattu

Nehemiah1:2 Hanani
Nehemiah1:3 They said to me
Nehemiah1:4 When I heard these words
Nehemiah1:5 and said: “I beseech You
Nehemiah1:6 Let Your ear now be attentive and Your eyes open
Nehemiah1:7 We have acted very corruptly against You and have not obeyed the commandments
Nehemiah1:8 “Please remember the word that You commanded Your servant Moses
Nehemiah1:9 but if you return to Me and keep My commandments and do them
Nehemiah1:10 “Now these are Your servants and Your people
Nehemiah1:11 O Lord

Nehemiah3:1 Then Eliashib the high priest rose up with his brothers the priests
Nehemiah3:2 Next to him the men of Jericho built
Nehemiah3:3 The sons of Hassenaah built the Fish Gate
Nehemiah3:4 Next to them Meremoth the son of Uriah
Nehemiah3:5 Next to them the Tekoites made repairs
Nehemiah3:6 Joiada the son of Paseah and Meshullam the son of Besodeiah repaired the Old Gate. They constructed its beams
Nehemiah3:7 Next to them repairs were made by Melatiah the Gibeonite and Jadon the Meronothite
Nehemiah3:8 Next to them Uzziel the son of Harhaiah
Nehemiah3:9 Next to him Rephaiah the son of Hur
Nehemiah3:10 Next to him Jedaiah the son of Harumaph made repairs across from his house. Next to him Hattush the son of Hashabneiah made repairs.
Nehemiah3:11 Malkijah the son of Harim and Hasshub the son of Pahath-Moab repaired another section
Nehemiah3:12 Next to them Shallum the son of Hallohesh
Nehemiah3:13 Hanun and the inhabitants of Zanoah repaired the Valley Gate. They rebuilt it
Nehemiah3:14 But the Refuse Gate was repaired by Malkijah the son of Rekab
Nehemiah3:15 And Shallun the son of Col-hozeh
Nehemiah3:16 After him Nehemiah the son of Azbuk
Nehemiah3:17 After him the Levites
Nehemiah3:18 After him their brothers
Nehemiah3:19 Next to them Ezer the son of Jeshua
Nehemiah3:20 After him Baruch the son of Zabbai zealously repaired another section
Nehemiah3:21 After him Meremoth the son of Uriah the son of Hakkoz repaired another section from the door of the house of Eliashib to the end of it.
Nehemiah3:22 After him the priests
Nehemiah3:23 After them Benjamin and Hasshub made repairs across from their house. After them Azariah the son of Maaseiah the son of Ananiah made repairs beside his house.
Nehemiah3:24 After him Binnui the son of Henadad repaired another section from the house of Azariah as far as the corner plus the corner tower.
Nehemiah3:25 Palal the son of Uzai made repairs across from the corner and to the tower protruding out of the king’s upper house next to the courtyard of the guards. After him Pedaiah the son of Parosh made repairs.
Nehemiah3:26 The temple servants living in Ophel made repairs from opposite the Water Gate eastward to the protruding tower.
Nehemiah3:27 After them the Tekoites repaired another section across from the great protruding tower as far as the wall of Ophel.
Nehemiah3:28 Up to the Horse Gate the priests made repairs—each one across from his house.
Nehemiah3:29 After them Zadok the son of Immer repaired across from his house. After him Shemaiah the son of Shecaniah
Nehemiah3:30 After him Hananiah the son of Shelemiah and Hanun the sixth son of Zalaph repaired another section. After them Meshullam the son of Berekiah made repairs across from his chamber.
Nehemiah3:31 After him Malkijah the goldsmith’s son made repairs as far as the house of the temple servants and the merchants. This was across from the Mustering Gate extending as far as the upper room of the corner tower.
Nehemiah3:32 Between the upper room of the corner tower and the Sheep Gate
Nehemiah4:1 When Sanballat heard that we were rebuilding the wall
Nehemiah4:2 He spoke before his relatives and the army of Samaria and said
Nehemiah4:3 Now Tobiah the Ammonite was beside him
Nehemiah4:4 Hear
Nehemiah4:5 No longer cover their iniquity nor blot out their sin
Nehemiah4:6 So we rebuilt the wall until all of it was solidified up to half its height. The people had a passion for the work.
Nehemiah4:7 When Sanballat
Nehemiah4:8 So they all conspired together to fight against Jerusalem in order to cause it chaos.
Nehemiah4:9 Nevertheless we prayed to our God
Nehemiah4:10 Judah had said
Nehemiah4:11 Our adversaries said
Nehemiah4:12 When the Jews living near them came
Nehemiah4:13 Therefore I set guards at the lowest positions behind the wall
Nehemiah4:14 After I looked around
Nehemiah4:15 Now when our enemies heard that this had become known to us and that God had brought their counsel to nothing
Nehemiah4:16 After that day
Nehemiah4:17 Those who rebuilt the wall and those hauling the loads were working with one hand doing the task
Nehemiah4:18 And each of the builders had his sword bound to his side while he built. He who sounded the trumpet was beside me.
Nehemiah4:19 I said to the nobles
Nehemiah4:20 assemble to us there at the place where you hear the trumpet sounded. Our God shall fight for us.”
Nehemiah4:21 So we labored in the work with half of them holding spears from sunrise to the rising of the stars.
Nehemiah4:22 Likewise at the same time I said to the people
Nehemiah4:23 So neither I nor my brothers nor my servants nor the men of the guard who followed me took off our clothes. Each carried his weapon

Nehemiah5:3 Others were saying
Nehemiah5:4 Still others were saying
Nehemiah5:5 Now our flesh is the same as the flesh of our countrymen. Our children are like their children

Nehemiah5:7 So I contemplated about this for myself and
Nehemiah5:8 and I said to them

Nehemiah5:11 Please restore to them
Nehemiah5:12 Then they said
Nehemiah5:13 Also I shook out the front of my garment and said
Nehemiah5:14 Moreover from the time that I was appointed to be their governor in the land of Judah (from the twentieth year even until the thirty-second year of King Artaxerxes) twelve years had passed. And my companions and I had not eaten the governor’s food allotment.
Nehemiah5:15 The former governors preceding me had extracted a heavy burden on the people
Nehemiah5:16 Furthermore
Nehemiah5:17 Moreover there were regularly at my table one hundred and fifty Jews and officials
Nehemiah5:18 Daily there were one ox and six choice sheep prepared for me. Fowls were also prepared for me. Once in ten days all sorts of wine were supplied in abundance. Yet for all this
Nehemiah5:19 Remember me
