Nehemiah9:1 Now on the twenty-fourth day of this month the Israelites were assembled with fasting and sackcloth
Nehemiah9:2 The offspring of Israel separated themselves from all the foreigners and then stood and confessed their sins and the iniquities of their fathers.
Nehemiah9:3 They stood in their place and read from the Book of the Law of the Lord their God for one-fourth of the day. And for another fourth
Nehemiah9:4 Jeshua
Nehemiah9:5 Then the Levites
Nehemiah9:6 You alone are the Lord. You have made heaven
Nehemiah9:7 “You are the Lord God who chose Abram and brought him out of Ur of the Chaldeans. You gave him the name Abraham
Nehemiah9:8 and found his heart faithful before You. And You made a covenant with him to give him the land of the Canaanites
Nehemiah9:9 “When You saw the affliction of our fathers in Egypt and heard their cry by the Red Sea
Nehemiah9:10 You enacted signs and wonders against Pharaoh
Nehemiah9:11 You divided the sea before them
Nehemiah9:12 By day You led them with a pillar of cloud
Nehemiah9:13 “You came down on Mount Sinai and spoke with them from heaven and gave them just requirements
Nehemiah9:14 You also revealed to them Your Holy Sabbath and
Nehemiah9:15 You gave them bread from heaven for their hunger and brought water out of the rock for them for their thirst. You told them to enter in order to possess the land which You had sworn to give them.
Nehemiah9:16 “But they and our fathers acted proudly and hardened their necks and did not obey Your commandments.
Nehemiah9:17 They refused to obey and were not mindful of Your wonders that You performed among them. But they hardened their necks and in their rebellion appointed a leader to return to their bondage. But You are a God ready to pardon
Nehemiah9:18 “Even when they had made themselves a molded calf and said
Nehemiah9:19 yet You in Your great mercy did not forsake them in the wilderness: The pillar of the cloud did not depart from them by day
Nehemiah9:20 You gave Your good Spirit to instruct them
Nehemiah9:21 Forty years You sustained them in the wilderness
Nehemiah9:22 “You gave them kingdoms and nations
Nehemiah9:23 Their descendants You increased like the stars of heaven
Nehemiah9:24 So the descendants went in and possessed the land
Nehemiah9:25 They captured unassailable cities and a fertile land. They possessed houses full of all goods
Nehemiah9:26 “Nevertheless they became disobedient
Nehemiah9:27 Therefore You delivered them into the hand of their enemies
Nehemiah9:28 “But after they had rest
Nehemiah9:29 “You warned them in order to restore them again to Your law
Nehemiah9:30 For many years You endured them and warned them by Your Spirit in Your prophets
Nehemiah9:31 Nevertheless
Nehemiah9:32 “Now therefore
Nehemiah9:33 You are righteous for everything that has come upon us! You have acted faithfully while we have done wickedly.
Nehemiah9:34 For our kings
Nehemiah9:35 For whether in their kingdom or in Your abundant goodness that You gave them
Nehemiah9:36 “Behold
Nehemiah9:37 because its abundant produce belongs to the kings whom You have set over us due to our sins. They have control over our bodies and over our livestock
Nehemiah9:38 So on the basis of all this

Job12:22 He uncovers deep things out of darkness and brings the shadow of death to light.
Job12:23 He increases the nations and destroys them; He enlarges the nations and guides them.
Job12:24 He takes away the understanding of the chiefs of the people of the earth
Job12:25 They grope in the dark without light
Job13:1 “Behold
Job13:2 What you know
Job13:3 Surely I would speak to the Almighty
Job13:4 But you are plasterers of falsehood; you are all physicians of no value.
Job13:5 Oh
Job13:6 Hear now my reasoning
Job13:7 Will you speak wickedly for God? And talk deceitfully for Him?
Job13:8 Will you take His side? Will you plead for God?
Job13:9 Is it good that He would search you out? Or as one man mocks another
Job13:10 He will surely rebuke you
Job13:11 Will not His excellence make you afraid
Job13:12 Your reminders are parables made of ashes; your answers are answers made of clay.
Job13:13 “Hold your peace; leave me alone
Job13:14 Why do I take my flesh in my teeth and put my life in my hand?
Job13:15 Though He slay me
Job13:16 He also will be my salvation
Job13:17 Listen carefully to my speech
Job13:18 Behold
Job13:19 Who is he who will plead with me? For now
Job13:20 “Only grant me two things
Job13:21 Withdraw Your hand far from me
Job13:22 Then call
Job13:23 How many are my iniquities and sins? Make known to me my transgression and my sin.
Job13:24 Why do You hide Your face and regard me as Your enemy?
Job13:25 Will You frighten a leaf driven to and fro? And will You pursue dry stubble?
Job13:26 For You write bitter things against me and make me inherit the iniquities of my youth.
Job13:27 You put my feet in the stocks and watch closely all my paths; You draw a line around the soles of my feet.
Job13:28 “Man
Job14:1 “Man who is born of a woman is of few days and full of trouble.
Job14:2 He comes forth like a flower and withers; he flees like a shadow and does not continue.
Job14:3 Do You open Your eyes on such a one
Job14:4 Who can bring a clean thing out of an unclean? There is no one.
Job14:5 Seeing his days are determined
Job14:6 turn from him
Job14:7 “For there is hope for a tree
Job14:8 Though its root may grow old in the earth
Job14:9 yet at the scent of water it will bud and bring forth boughs like a plant.
Job14:10 But man dies and wastes away; yes
Job14:11 As the waters disappear from the sea
Job14:12 so man lies down and does not rise; until the heavens are no more
Job14:13 “Oh
Job14:14 If a man dies
Job14:15 You will call
Job14:16 For now You number my steps; do You not observe my sin?
Job14:17 My transgression is sealed up in a bag
Job14:18 “Surely the mountain falling comes to nothing
Job14:19 The waters wear away the stones; its overflow washes away the dust of the earth; and You destroy the hope of man.
Job14:20 You prevail forever against him
Job14:21 His sons come to honor
Job14:22 But his flesh on him will have pain
Job15:1 Then Eliphaz the Temanite answered:
Job15:2 “Should a wise man reply with empty knowledge and fill his lungs with the east wind?
Job15:3 Should he reason with unprofitable talk or with speeches with which he can do no good?
Job15:4 Yes
Job15:5 For your mouth utters your iniquity
Job15:6 Your own mouth condemns you
Job15:7 “Are you the first man who was born? Or were you made before the hills?
Job15:8 Have you heard the counsel of God? And do you restrict wisdom to yourself?
Job15:9 What do you know that we do not know? What do you understand that is not in us?
Job15:10 Both the gray-haired and very aged are among us — much older than your father.
Job15:11 Are the consolations of God too small for you? Or the word spoken gently to you?
Job15:12 Why does your heart carry you away? And what do your eyes wink at
Job15:13 that you turn your spirit against God
Job15:14 “What is man that he should be pure? And he who is born of a woman
Job15:15 Behold
Job15:16 How much more abhorred and corrupt is man
Job15:17 “I will tell you; hear me
Job15:18 what wise men have told
Job15:19 to whom alone the land was given
Job15:20 The wicked man travails with pain all his days
Job15:21 A dreadful sound is in his ears; in prosperity the destroyer will come upon him.
Job15:22 He does not believe that he will return from darkness
Job15:23 He wanders about for bread
Job15:24 Trouble and anguish will make him afraid; they will prevail against him as a king ready to the battle.
Job15:25 For he stretches out his hand against God
Job15:26 He rushes upon Him
Job15:27 “He has covered his face with his fatness and gathered fat upon his waist.
Job15:28 He dwells in desolate cities and in houses which no man inhabits
Job15:29 He will not be rich
Job15:30 He will not depart out of darkness; the flame will dry out his branches
Job15:31 Let him who is deceived not trust in futility
Job15:32 It will be accomplished before his time
Job15:33 He will shake off his unripe grape like the vine
Job15:34 For the company of hypocrites will be desolate
Job15:35 They conceive mischief
Job16:1 Then Job answered:
Job16:2 “I have heard many such things; miserable comforters are you all!
Job16:3 Will windy words have an end? Or what provokes you that you answer?
Job16:4 I also could speak as you do
Job16:5 but I would strengthen you with my mouth
Job16:6 “Though I speak
Job16:7 But now He has made me weary; You have made desolate all my company.
Job16:8 You have filled me with wrinkles
Job16:9 He has torn me in His wrath
Job16:10 They have gaped upon me with their mouth; they have struck me upon the cheek with reproach; they have gathered themselves together against me.
Job16:11 God has delivered me to the ungodly and turned me over into the hands of the wicked.
Job16:12 I was at ease
Job16:13 His archers surround me; He splits open my kidneys and does not pity; He pours out my gall upon the ground.
Job16:14 He pierces me with thrust after thrust; He rushes upon me like a warrior.
Job16:15 “I have sewn sackcloth over my skin and thrust my horn into the dust.
Job16:16 My face is inflamed with weeping
Job16:17 though not for any violence in my hands
Job16:18 “O earth
Job16:19 Even now
Job16:20 My friends scorn me; my eyes pour out tears unto God.
Job16:21 Oh
Job16:22 “For when a few years have passed
Job17:1 “My spirit is broken
Job17:2 Are not mockers with me? And does not my eye dwell on their provocation?
Job17:3 “Now put down a pledge for me with Yourself. Who is he who will shake hands with me?
Job17:4 For You have hidden their heart from understanding. Therefore will You not exalt them.
Job17:5 He who speaks flattery to his friends
Job17:6 “And He has made me a byword of the people
Job17:7 My eye also is dim because of sorrow
Job17:8 Upright men will be astonished at this
Job17:9 The righteous also will hold to his way
Job17:10 “But as for you all
Job17:11 My days are past
Job17:12 They change the night into day; the light is short because of darkness.
Job17:13 If I wait
Job17:14 I have said to the pit
Job17:15 Where now is my hope? As for my hope
Job17:16 Will they go down to the gates of Sheol? Will we descend together in the dust?”
Job18:1 Then Bildad the Shuhite answered:
Job18:2 “How long will it be until you put an end to words? Gain understanding
Job18:3 Why are we counted as beasts and regarded as stupid in your sight?
Job18:4 You who tear yourself in anger
Job18:5 “Yes
Job18:6 The light will be dark in his tent
Job18:7 The steps of his strength will be shortened
Job18:8 For he is cast into a net by his own feet
Job18:9 The trap will take him by the heel
Job18:10 The snare is laid for him in the ground
Job18:11 Terrors will make him afraid on every side and will drive him to his feet.
Job18:12 His strength will be hungry
Job18:13 It will devour the parts of his skin; the firstborn of death will devour his limbs.
Job18:14 His confidence will be rooted out of his tent
Job18:15 They dwell in his tent who have no part with him; brimstone will be scattered upon his habitation.
Job18:16 His roots will be dried up beneath
Job18:17 The memory of him will perish from the earth
Job18:18 He will be driven from light into darkness
Job18:19 He has neither descendant nor posterity among his people
Job18:20 They who come after him will be astonished at his day
Job18:21 Surely such are the dwellings of the wicked
Job19:1 Then Job answered:
Job19:2 “How long will you torment my soul and break me in pieces with words?
Job19:3 These ten times you have reproached me; you are not ashamed that you have wronged me.
Job19:4 And if indeed I have erred
Job19:5 If indeed you exalt yourselves against me and plead against me with my disgrace
Job19:6 know now that God has bent me and has surrounded me with His net.
Job19:7 “Behold
Job19:8 He has fenced up my way that I cannot pass
Job19:9 He has stripped me of my glory and taken the crown from my head.
Job19:10 He has destroyed me on every side
Job19:11 He has also kindled His wrath against me
Job19:12 His troops come together and build up their road against me; they set up camp all around my tent.
Job19:13 “He has removed my brothers far from me
Job19:14 My relatives have failed
Job19:15 Those who dwell in my house
Job19:16 I called my servant
Job19:17 My breath is offensive to my wife; I am loathsome to the children of my own body.
Job19:18 Yes
Job19:19 All my close friends abhorred me
Job19:20 My bones cling to my skin and to my flesh
Job19:21 “Have pity upon me
Job19:22 Why do you persecute me as God does

Job19:26 and after my skin is destroyed
Job19:27 whom I will see for myself

Job20:1 Then Zophar the Naamathite answered:
Job20:2 “Therefore my thoughts cause me to answer
Job20:3 I have heard the rebuke that disgraces me
Job20:4 “Do you not know this of old
Job20:5 that the triumphing of the wicked is short
Job20:6 Though his loftiness extends to the heavens
Job20:7 yet he will perish forever like his own excrement; those who have seen him will say
Job20:8 He will fly away like a dream and will not be found; yes
Job20:9 The eye that saw him will see him no more; nor will his place behold him anymore.
Job20:10 His children will seek favor from the poor
Job20:11 His bones are full of his youthful vigor
Job20:12 “Though evil is sweet in his mouth
Job20:13 though he spares it and does not forsake it
Job20:14 yet his food in his stomach turns sour; it becomes the venom of cobras within him.
Job20:15 He has swallowed down riches
Job20:16 He will suck the poison of cobras; the viper’s tongue will slay him.
Job20:17 He will not see the streams
Job20:18 He will give back the produce of labor and will not swallow it down. According to his wealth the restitution will be
Job20:19 because he has oppressed and has forsaken the poor. He has violently taken away a house that he did not build.
Job20:20 “Because he knows no quietness in his belly
Job20:21 Nothing is left for him to eat; therefore his prosperity will not endure.
Job20:22 In his self-sufficiency he will be in distress; every hand of misery will come upon him.
Job20:23 When he is about to fill his belly
Job20:24 He will flee from the iron weapon; a bronze bow will pierce him through.
Job20:25 It is drawn and comes out of the body; yes
Job20:26 Total darkness is stored up for his treasures. An unfanned fire will consume him; what is left in his tent will be consumed.
Job20:27 The heavens will reveal his iniquity
Job20:28 The increase of his house will depart
Job20:29 This is the wicked man’s portion from God
Job21:1 But Job answered:
Job21:2 “Listen carefully to my speech
Job21:3 Bear with me that I may speak
Job21:4 “As for me

Job21:7 Why do the wicked live and become old; yes
Job21:8 Their descendants are established with them in their sight
Job21:9 Their houses are safe from fear
Job21:10 Their bull breeds without failure; their cow gives calves without miscarriage.
Job21:11 They send forth their little ones like a flock
Job21:12 They take up the tambourine and harp and rejoice at the sound of the flute.
Job21:13 They spend their days in wealth
Job21:14 Therefore they say to God
Job21:15 Who is the Almighty
Job21:16 Indeed
Job21:17 “How often is the lamp of the wicked put out? How often does their destruction come upon them

Job21:19 God stores up their iniquity for their children; let Him reward the people themselves

Job21:21 For what do they care about their households after them

Job21:23 One dies in his full strength
Job21:24 His pails are full of milk
Job21:25 Another dies in the bitterness of his soul

Job21:27 “Behold
Job21:28 For you say
Job21:29 Have you not asked them who travel the road? And do you not know their signs?
Job21:30 For the wicked are reserved for the day of destruction; they will be brought forth on the day of wrath.
Job21:31 Who will declare his way to his face? And who will repay him for what he has done?
Job21:32 Yet will he be brought to the grave and will remain in the tomb.
Job21:33 The clods of the valley will be sweet unto him; and everyone will follow him

Job22:2 “Can a man be profitable to God

Job22:5 Is not your wickedness great
Job22:6 For you have taken a pledge from your brother for nothing

Job22:8 But as for the mighty man
Job22:9 You have sent widows away empty
Job22:10 Therefore snares are all around you
Job22:11 or darkness

Job22:13 And you say

Job23:1 Then Job answered:
Job23:2 “Today also my complaint is bitter; my hand is heavy because of my groaning.
Job23:3 Oh
Job23:4 I would present my case before Him and fill my mouth with arguments.
Job23:5 I would know the words that He would answer me and understand what He would say to me.
Job23:6 Will He plead against me with His great power? No! But He would take note of me.
Job23:7 There the righteous might dispute with Him; so would I be delivered forever from my Judge.
Job23:8 “Behold
Job23:9 when He works on the left hand
Job23:10 But He knows the way that I take; when He has tested me
Job23:11 My foot has held fast to His steps; I have kept His way and have not turned aside.
Job23:12 I have not departed from the commandment of His lips; I have esteemed the words of His mouth more than my necessary food.
Job23:13 “But He is resolute
Job23:14 For He performs what is appointed for me
Job23:15 Therefore I am troubled at His presence; when I consider
Job23:16 For God makes my heart soft
Job23:17 because I was not cut off from the presence of darkness
Job24:1 “Since times of judgment are not kept by the Almighty
Job24:2 Some remove the landmarks; they violently take away flocks and feed on them.
Job24:3 They drive away the donkey of the orphan; they take the widow’s ox for a pledge.
Job24:4 They turn away the needy; the poor of the earth are forced to hide.
Job24:5 Behold
Job24:6 They gather their fodder in the field
Job24:7 They cause the naked to lodge without clothing
Job24:8 They are wet with the showers of the mountains and embrace the rock for want of a shelter.
Job24:9 They snatch the fatherless from the breast and take a pledge from the poor.
Job24:10 They cause him to go naked without clothing
Job24:11 They press out oil within their walls and tread their winepresses
Job24:12 Men groan from outside the city
Job24:13 “They are those who rebel against the light; they do not know its ways nor abide in its paths.
Job24:14 The murderer
Job24:15 Also the eye of the adulterer waits for the twilight
Job24:16 In the dark they break into houses
Job24:17 For the morning is the same to them as the shadow of death; if someone recognizes them
Job24:18 “He is swift as the waters. Their portion is cursed in the earth. They do not turn toward the way of the vineyards.
Job24:19 Drought and heat consume the snow waters; so the grave consumes those who have sinned.
Job24:20 The womb will forget him; the worm will feed sweetly on him; he will be remembered no more
Job24:21 He preys on the barren who do not bear
Job24:22 But God draws the mighty away with His power; He rises up
Job24:23 He gives them security
Job24:24 They are exalted for a little while but are gone and brought low; they are taken out of the way like all others and cut off as the tops of the grain.

Job25:2 “Dominion and fear are with Him; He makes peace in His high places.

Job26:2 “How have you helped him who is without power? How have you saved the arm that has no strength?

Job26:4 To whom have you uttered words? And whose breath came from you?
Job26:5 “The departed spirits tremble under the waters
Job26:6 The underworld is naked before Him
Job26:7 He stretches out the north over empty space
Job26:8 He binds up the waters in His thick clouds
Job26:9 He covers the face of the full moon and spreads His cloud over it.
Job26:10 He has circled the waters with boundaries
Job26:11 The pillars of heaven tremble and are astonished at His reproof.
Job26:12 He divides the sea with His power
Job26:13 By His breath He has made fair the heavens; His hand has formed the fleeing serpent.
Job26:14 Indeed
Job27:1 Moreover Job continued his discourse:
Job27:2 “As God lives
Job27:3 all the while my spirit is in me
Job27:4 my lips will not speak wickedness
Job27:5 God forbid that I should justify you. Until I die I will not put away my integrity from me.
Job27:6 My righteousness I hold fast and will not let it go; my heart will not reproach me as long as I live.
Job27:7 “Let my enemy be like the wicked
Job27:8 For what is the hope of the hypocrite
Job27:9 Will God hear his cry when trouble comes upon him?
Job27:10 Will he delight himself in the Almighty? Will he always call upon God?
Job27:11 “I will teach you about the hand of God; what is with the Almighty I will not conceal.
Job27:12 Behold
Job27:13 “This is the portion of a wicked man with God
Job27:14 If his children are multiplied
Job27:15 Those who survive him will be buried in death
Job27:16 Though he heaps up silver like the dust and piles up clothing like the clay—
Job27:17 he may pile it up
Job27:18 He builds his house like a moth
Job27:19 The rich man will lie down
Job27:20 Terrors overtake him like floodwaters; a tempest steals him away in the night.
Job27:21 The east wind carries him away
Job27:22 For God will cast it upon him and not spare; he would flee from its power.
Job27:23 Men will clap their hands at him and will hiss him out of his place.
Job28:1 “Surely there is a mine for the silver
Job28:2 Iron is taken out of the earth
Job28:3 Man puts an end to darkness
Job28:4 He breaks open a shaft away from the inhabitants; in places forgotten by feet they hang far away from men; and they totter.
Job28:5 As for the earth
Job28:6 Its stones are the source of sapphires
Job28:7 There is a path that no bird knows
Job28:8 the lion cubs have not trodden it
Job28:9 He puts forth his hand upon the rock; he overturns the mountains by the roots.
Job28:10 He cuts out rivers among the rocks
Job28:11 He prevents the floods from overflowing
Job28:12 “But where will wisdom be found? And where is the place of understanding?
Job28:13 Man does not know its price
Job28:14 The depth says
Job28:15 It cannot be bought for gold
Job28:16 It cannot be valued in the gold of Ophir
Job28:17 The gold and the crystal cannot equal it
Job28:18 No mention will be made of coral or of pearls
Job28:19 The topaz of Cush will not equal it
Job28:20 “From where then does wisdom come? And where is the place of understanding?
Job28:21 It is hidden from the eyes of all living and concealed from the birds of the air.
Job28:22 Destruction and Death say
Job28:23 God understands its way
Job28:24 For He looks to the ends of the earth
Job28:25 to make the weight of the wind

Job28:27 then He saw it and declared it; He prepared it

Job29:4 as I was in the days of my autumn youth
Job29:5 when the Almighty was still with me

Job29:10 The nobles held their peace
Job29:11 When the ear heard

Job29:14 I put on righteousness
Job29:15 I was eyes for the blind
Job29:16 I was a father to the poor
Job29:17 I broke the jaws of the wicked and plucked the victim from his teeth.

Job29:20 My glory was fresh in me

Job29:24 If I mocked at them

Job30:1 “But now those who are younger than I mock me
Job30:2 Yes
Job30:3 For want and famine they gnawed the parched land; fleeing into the wilderness in former time
Job30:4 Who pluck mallow by the bushes
Job30:5 They were driven out from among men; they shout after them as after a thief.
Job30:6 They had to dwell in the rocky riverbeds
Job30:7 Among the bushes they brayed; under the nettles they were gathered together.
Job30:8 They were children of fools
Job30:9 “Now I am their taunting song; yes
Job30:10 They abhor me
Job30:11 Because He has loosed my bowstring and afflicted me
Job30:12 At my right hand their brood arises; they push away my feet
Job30:13 They tear apart my path
Job30:14 They came upon me as a wide breach
Job30:15 Terrors are turned on me; they pursue my dignity like the wind
Job30:16 “Now my soul is poured out within me; the days of affliction have taken hold of me.
Job30:17 My bones are pierced in me at night
Job30:18 By the great force of my disease my garment is changed; it binds me about as the collar of my coat.
Job30:19 He has cast me into the mire
Job30:20 “I cry unto You
Job30:21 You have become cruel to me; with Your strong hand You oppose me.
Job30:22 You lift me up to the wind and cause me to ride on it; You dissolve my success.
Job30:23 For I know that You will bring me to death
Job30:24 “Surely He will not stretch out His hand to the grave
Job30:25 Did I not weep for him who was in trouble? Was not my soul grieved for the poor?
Job30:26 When I looked for good
Job30:27 My insides boiled and did not rest; the days of affliction have met me.
Job30:28 I went mourning without the sun; I stood up
Job30:29 I am a brother of jackals and a companion of owls.
Job30:30 My skin is black upon me
Job30:31 My harp is turned to mourning
Job31:1 “I made a covenant with my eyes; why then should I look upon a young woman?
Job31:2 For what portion of God is there from above? And what inheritance of the Almighty from on high?
Job31:3 Does not destruction belong to the wicked
Job31:4 Does He not see my ways
Job31:5 “If I have walked in vanity
Job31:6 let me be weighed in an even balance that God may know my integrity.
Job31:7 If my step has turned out of the path
Job31:8 then let me sow
Job31:9 “If my heart has been deceived by a woman
Job31:10 then let my wife grind for another
Job31:11 For this is a heinous crime; yes
Job31:12 For it is a fire that consumes to destruction and would root out all my increase.
Job31:13 “If I have despised the cause of my male or female servant when they complained against me
Job31:14 what then will I do when God rises up? And when He visits
Job31:15 Did not He who made me in the womb make him? And did not the same One fashion us in the womb?
Job31:16 “If I have kept the poor from their desire
Job31:17 or have eaten my morsel by myself
Job31:18 (but from my youth he was brought up with me
Job31:19 if I have seen anyone perish for lack of clothing
Job31:20 if his body has not blessed me
Job31:21 if I have lifted up my hand against the fatherless
Job31:22 then let my arm fall from my shoulder blade
Job31:23 For destruction from God is a terror to me
Job31:24 “If I have made gold my hope
Job31:25 if I rejoiced because my wealth was great
Job31:26 if I saw the sun when it shined
Job31:27 and my heart has been secretly enticed
Job31:28 this also would be an iniquity to be punished by the judge
Job31:29 “If I rejoiced at the destruction of him who hated me
Job31:30 (nor have I allowed my mouth to sin by wishing for a curse on his soul);
Job31:31 if the men of my tent have not said
Job31:32 (the stranger has not lodged in the street
Job31:33 if I covered my transgressions as any man
Job31:34 did I fear a great multitude
Job31:35 “Oh
Job31:36 Surely I would carry it on my shoulder and bind it on me like a crown.
Job31:37 I would declare to Him the number of my steps; like a prince I would approach Him.
Job31:38 “If my land cries out against me
Job31:39 if I have eaten its fruit without money
Job31:40 let thistles grow instead of wheat
Job32:1 So these three men ceased answering Job
Job32:2 Then the wrath of Elihu the son of Barakel the Buzite
Job32:3 His wrath was aroused also against his three friends because they had found no answer and yet had condemned Job.
Job32:4 Now Elihu had waited until Job had spoken because they were older than he.
Job32:5 When Elihu saw that there was no answer in the mouth of these three men
Job32:6 Elihu the son of Barakel the Buzite answered: “I am young
Job32:7 I said
Job32:8 But there is a spirit in man
Job32:9 Great men are not always wise
Job32:10 “Therefore I say
Job32:11 Indeed
Job32:12 Yes
Job32:13 Lest you say
Job32:14 Now he has not directed his words against me
Job32:15 “They are amazed; they answer no more; they cease speaking.
Job32:16 And I have waited
Job32:17 I said
Job32:18 For I am full of words; the spirit within me compels me.
Job32:19 Behold
Job32:20 I will speak
Job32:21 Let me not
Job32:22 For I do not know how to give flattering titles; in so doing my maker would soon take me away.
Job33:1 “Therefore
Job33:2 Behold
Job33:3 My words will be from the uprightness of my heart
Job33:4 The Spirit of God has made me
Job33:5 If you can answer me
Job33:6 Surely I am before God like you; I also am formed out of the clay.
Job33:7 Indeed
Job33:8 “Surely you have spoken in my hearing
Job33:9 ‘I am clean
Job33:10 Behold
Job33:11 He puts my feet in the stocks
Job33:12 “Behold
Job33:13 Why do you strive against Him? For He does not give an accounting for any of His words.
Job33:14 For God speaks once
Job33:15 In a dream
Job33:16 then He opens the ears of men
Job33:17 that He might turn aside man from his purpose
Job33:18 He keeps back his soul from the pit
Job33:19 “He is also chastened with pain on his bed
Job33:20 so that his life abhors bread
Job33:21 His flesh is consumed away that it cannot be seen
Job33:22 Yes
Job33:23 If there is a messenger for him
Job33:24 then He is gracious to him
Job33:25 His flesh will be fresher than a child’s; he will return to the days of his youth;
Job33:26 he will pray to God
Job33:27 Then he looks at men and says
Job33:28 He will deliver his soul from going down to the pit
Job33:29 “Behold
Job33:30 to bring back his soul from the pit
Job33:31 “Pay attention
Job33:32 If you have anything to say
Job33:33 If not
Job34:1 Then Elihu continued and said:
Job34:2 “Hear my words
Job34:3 For the ear tests words
Job34:4 Let us choose justice for ourselves; let us know among ourselves what is good.
Job34:5 “For Job has said
Job34:6 Should I lie concerning my right? My wound is incurable
Job34:7 What man is like Job
Job34:8 who goes in company with the workers of iniquity and walks with wicked men?
Job34:9 For he has said
Job34:10 “Therefore listen to me
Job34:11 He will repay a man for his work
Job34:12 Yes
Job34:13 Who has given Him charge over the earth? Or who has set in order the whole world?
Job34:14 If He sets His heart on man
Job34:15 all flesh will perish together
Job34:16 “If now you have understanding
Job34:17 Should he who hates justice govern? And will you condemn Him who is most just?
Job34:18 Is it fitting to say to a king
Job34:19 Yet He is not partial to princes
Job34:20 In a moment they will die
Job34:21 “For His eyes are upon the ways of man
Job34:22 There is no darkness nor shadow of death where the workers of iniquity may hide themselves.
Job34:23 For He will not lay upon man more than right
Job34:24 He will break in pieces mighty men without number
Job34:25 Therefore He knows their works
Job34:26 He strikes them as wicked men in the open sight of others
Job34:27 because they turned back from Him and would not consider any of His ways
Job34:28 so that they cause the cry of the poor to come unto Him; and He hears the cry of the afflicted.
Job34:29 When He gives quietness
Job34:30 That the hypocrite should not reign
Job34:31 “Should anyone say to God
Job34:32 teach me what I do not see; if I have done iniquity
Job34:33 Should it be according to your mind? He will repay it
Job34:34 “Let men of understanding say to me
Job34:35 ‘Job has spoken without knowledge
Job34:36 My desire is that Job may be tried unto the end
Job34:37 For he adds rebellion unto his sin; he claps his hands among us
Job35:1 Elihu spoke again and said:
Job35:2 “Do you think this is right
Job35:3 For you said
Job35:4 “I will answer you
Job35:5 Look unto the heavens and see
Job35:6 If you sin
Job35:7 If you are righteous
Job35:8 Your wickedness may hurt a man like you
Job35:9 “Because of the many oppressions they cry out; they cry out because of the arm of the mighty.
Job35:10 But none says
Job35:11 who teaches us more than the beasts of the earth
Job35:12 There they cry out
Job35:13 Surely God will not hear vanity
Job35:14 Although you say you do not see Him
Job35:15 But now
Job35:16 therefore Job opens his mouth in vain; he multiplies words without knowledge.”
Job36:1 Elihu continued and said:
Job36:2 “Bear with me a little
Job36:3 I will bring my knowledge from afar and will ascribe righteousness to my maker.
Job36:4 For truly my words will not be false; He who is perfect in knowledge is with you.
Job36:5 “Behold
Job36:6 He does not preserve the life of the wicked
Job36:7 He does not withdraw His eyes from the righteous; but they are on the throne with kings
Job36:8 If they are bound in chains and held in cords of affliction
Job36:9 then He shows them their work
Job36:10 and He opens their ear to discipline
Job36:11 If they obey and serve Him
Job36:12 But if they do not obey
Job36:13 “But the hypocrites in heart heap up wrath; they do not cry out when He binds them.
Job36:14 They die in youth
Job36:15 He delivers the poor in their affliction
Job36:16 “Even so He would have removed you out of distress into a broad place where there is no restraint; and that which was set on your table would be full of richness.
Job36:17 But you are filled with the judgment due the wicked; judgment and justice take hold of you.
Job36:18 Because there is wrath
Job36:19 Will He esteem your riches? No
Job36:20 Do not desire the night
Job36:21 Take heed! Do not turn to iniquity
Job36:22 “Behold
Job36:23 Who has prescribed His way for Him? Or who can say
Job36:24 Remember to magnify His work
Job36:25 Every man may see it; man may behold it from afar.
Job36:26 Behold
Job36:27 “For He draws up the drops of water; they distill rain according to its mist
Job36:28 which the clouds drop down
Job36:29 Indeed
Job36:30 Behold
Job36:31 For by these He judges the people; He gives food in abundance.
Job36:32 With clouds He covers the light
Job36:33 Its thunder declares it
Job37:1 “At this also my heart trembles and moves out of its place.
Job37:2 Hear attentively the thunder of His voice
Job37:3 He lets it loose under the whole heaven
Job37:4 After it a voice roars; He thunders with His majestic voice
Job37:5 God thunders marvelously with His voice; He does great things that we cannot comprehend.
Job37:6 For He says to the snow
Job37:7 He seals up the hand of every man
Job37:8 Then the beasts go into dens and remain in their places.
Job37:9 Out of the south comes the whirlwind
Job37:10 By the breath of God frost is given
Job37:11 Also He loads the thick clouds with moisture; He scatters His bright clouds
Job37:12 and they are turned about by His guidance
Job37:13 He causes it to come
Job37:14 “Listen to this
Job37:15 Do you know when God dispatches them and causes the light of His cloud to shine?
Job37:16 Do you know how the clouds are balanced
Job37:17 How your garments are warm
Job37:18 Have you
Job37:19 “Teach us what we will say to Him
Job37:20 Will it be told Him that I speak? If a man speaks
Job37:21 Now men do not see the bright light which is in the clouds
Job37:22 Fair weather comes out of the north; with God is terrible majesty.
Job37:23 Concerning the Almighty
Job37:24 Men therefore fear Him; He shows no partiality to those who are wise in heart.”
Job38:1 Then the Lord answered Job out of the whirlwind and said:
Job38:2 “Who is this who darkens counsel by words without knowledge?
Job38:3 Prepare yourself like a man; for I will question you
Job38:4 “Where were you when I laid the foundations of the earth? Declare
Job38:5 Who has determined its measurements
Job38:6 To what are its foundations fastened? Or who laid its cornerstone
Job38:7 when the morning stars sang together
Job38:8 “Or who shut up the sea with doors when it broke forth and went out of the womb?
Job38:9 When I made the cloud its garment
Job38:10 and broke up for it My decreed place
Job38:11 and said
Job38:12 “Have you commanded the morning in your days
Job38:13 that it might take hold of the ends of the earth
Job38:14 It is turned like clay by the seal
Job38:15 From the wicked their light is withheld
Job38:16 “Have you entered into the springs of the sea? Or have you walked in search of the depths?
Job38:17 Have the gates of death been opened to you? Or have you seen the doors of the shadow of death?
Job38:18 Have you perceived the breadth of the earth? Declare
Job38:19 “Where is the path where light dwells? And as for darkness
Job38:20 that you should take it to its boundary
Job38:21 Do you know it because you were born then
Job38:22 “Have you entered the treasuries of the snow? Or have you seen the treasuries of the hail
Job38:23 which I have reserved against the time of trouble
Job38:24 By what way is the light diffused
Job38:25 Who has divided a channel for the overflowing waters
Job38:26 to cause it to rain on the earth where no man is
Job38:27 to satisfy the desolate waste
Job38:28 Does the rain have a father? Or who has produced the drops of dew?
Job38:29 From whose womb came the ice? And the frost of heaven
Job38:30 The waters harden like stone
Job38:31 “Can you tie the cords to the Pleiades or loosen the belt of Orion?
Job38:32 Can you bring out the constellation in its season? Or can you guide the Bear with its cubs?
Job38:33 Do you know the ordinances of heaven? Can you set their dominion over the earth?
Job38:34 “Can you lift up your voice to the clouds
Job38:35 Can you send lightning that they may go forth and say to you
Job38:36 Who has put wisdom in the inward parts
Job38:37 Who can number the clouds by wisdom? Or who can empty out the bottles of heaven
Job38:38 when the dust turns into hard clumps and the clods stick together?
Job38:39 “Will you hunt the prey for the lion? Or fill the appetite of the young lions
Job38:40 when they crouch in their dens and lie in the thicket to wait in ambush?
Job38:41 Who provides for the raven his food when his young ones cry unto God
Job39:1 “Do you know the time when the wild mountain goats produce offspring? Or can you observe when the deer gives birth?
Job39:2 Can you number the months that they fulfill
Job39:3 They bow themselves
Job39:4 Their young ones are healthy
Job39:5 “Who has sent out the wild donkey free? Or who has loosed the bands of the wild donkey
Job39:6 whose home I have made the wilderness
Job39:7 He scorns the multitude in the city and does not regard the shouts of the driver.
Job39:8 The range of the mountains is his pasture
Job39:9 “Will the wild ox be willing to serve you or spend the night by your manger?
Job39:10 Can you bind the wild ox in the furrow with ropes? Or will he plow the valleys behind you?
Job39:11 Will you trust him because his strength is great? Or will you leave your labor to him?
Job39:12 Will you trust him to bring home your grain and gather it into your barn?
Job39:13 “Did you give the beautiful wings to the peacocks? Or wings and feathers to the ostrich
Job39:14 who leaves her eggs in the earth
Job39:15 and forgets that a foot may crush them
Job39:16 She is hardened against her young ones
Job39:17 because God has deprived her of wisdom
Job39:18 When she lifts up herself on high
Job39:19 “Have you given the horse strength? Have you clothed his neck with thunder?
Job39:20 Can you make him afraid like a grasshopper? The glory of his nostrils is awesome.
Job39:21 He paws in the valley and rejoices in his strength; he goes on to meet the armed men.
Job39:22 He mocks at fear and is not frightened
Job39:23 The quiver rattles against him
Job39:24 He devours the distance with fierceness and rage
Job39:25 At the sound of the trumpet he says
Job39:26 “Does the hawk fly by your wisdom
Job39:27 Does the eagle mount up at your command and make her nest on high?
Job39:28 She dwells and remains on the rock
Job39:29 From there she seeks the prey
Job39:30 Her young ones also suck up blood
Job40:1 Moreover
Job40:2 “Will he who argues with the Almighty instruct Him? He who rebukes God
Job40:3 Then Job answered the Lord and said:
Job40:4 “Behold
Job40:5 Once have I spoken
Job40:6 Then the Lord answered Job out of the whirlwind and said:
Job40:7 “Prepare yourself now like a man; I will question you
Job40:8 “Will you indeed annul My judgment? Will you condemn Me
Job40:9 Have you an arm like God? Or can you thunder with a voice like Him?
Job40:10 Adorn yourself now with majesty and excellence
Job40:11 Let loose the rage of your wrath
Job40:12 look on everyone who is proud and bring him low
Job40:13 Hide them in the dust together
Job40:14 Then I will also confess to you that your own right hand can save you.
Job40:15 “Look now at the behemoth which I made along with you; he eats grass like an ox.
Job40:16 Behold
Job40:17 He moves his tail like a cedar; the sinews of his thighs are wrapped together.
Job40:18 His bones are like strong pieces of brass; his bones are like bars of iron.
Job40:19 He is the first of the works of God; He who made him can bring near His sword.
Job40:20 Surely the mountains bring forth food for him
Job40:21 He lies under the shady trees
Job40:22 The shady trees cover him with their shadow; the willows of the brook surround him.
Job40:23 Behold
Job40:24 Can anyone catch it by its eyes
Job41:1 “Can you draw out Leviathan with a hook or snare his tongue with a line which you let down?
Job41:2 Can you put a cord into his nose
Job41:3 Will he make many supplications to you? Will he speak soft words to you?
Job41:4 Will he make a covenant with you? Will you take him for a servant forever?
Job41:5 Will you play with him as with a bird? Or will you put him on a leash for your maidens?
Job41:6 Will your companions make a banquet of him? Will they divide him among the merchants?
Job41:7 Can you fill his skin with harpoons or his head with fishing spears?
Job41:8 Lay your hand on him; remember the battle—you will do it no more.
Job41:9 Behold
Job41:10 No one is so fierce that he dares to stir him up. Who then is able to stand before Me?
Job41:11 Who has preceded Me that I should repay him? Everything under heaven is Mine.
Job41:12 “I will not conceal his limbs
Job41:13 Who can remove his outer garment? Or who can approach him with a double bridle?
Job41:14 Who can open the jaws of his face? His teeth are terrible all around.
Job41:15 His scales are his pride
Job41:16 One is so near to another that no air can come between them.
Job41:17 They are joined to each other; they stick together that they cannot be separated.
Job41:18 His sneezing flashes forth light
Job41:19 Out of his mouth go burning lights
Job41:20 Out of his nostrils goes smoke as out of a seething pot or cauldron.
Job41:21 His breath kindles coals
Job41:22 In his neck remains strength
Job41:23 The folds of his flesh are joined together; they are firm on him; they cannot be moved.
Job41:24 His heart is as firm as a stone
Job41:25 When he raises up himself even the gods are afraid; because of his crashings they are beside themselves.
Job41:26 The sword that reaches him cannot avail
Job41:27 He counts iron as straw
Job41:28 The arrow cannot make him flee; slingstones are turned into stubble by him.
Job41:29 Arrows are counted as straw; he laughs at the shaking of a spear.
Job41:30 Sharp stones are his underside; he leaves a mark in the mire like a sharp threshing sledge.
Job41:31 He makes the deep to boil like a pot; he makes the sea like a pot of ointment.
Job41:32 He leaves a shining wake behind him; one would think the deep had white hair.
Job41:33 On earth there is nothing like him
Job41:34 He beholds all high things; he is a king over all the children of pride.”
Job42:1 Then Job answered the Lord and said:
Job42:2 “I know that You can do everything
Job42:3 ‘Who is he who hides counsel without knowledge?’ Therefore I have uttered what I did not understand
Job42:4 “ ‘Hear
Job42:5 I have heard of You by the hearing of the ear
Job42:6 Therefore I abhor myself
Job42:7 And so it was
Job42:8 And now
Job42:9 So Eliphaz the Temanite
Job42:10 And the Lord restored the fortunes of Job when he prayed for his friends
Job42:11 Then all of his brothers
Job42:12 So the Lord blessed the latter days of Job more than his beginning
Job42:13 He had also seven sons and three daughters.
Job42:14 He called the name of the first Jemimah; and the name of the second Keziah; and the name of the third Keren-Happuch.
Job42:15 In all the land
Job42:16 After this
Job42:17 So Job died
Psalms1:1 Blessed is the man who does not walk in the counsel of the wicked
Psalms1:2 but his delight is in the law of the Lord
Psalms1:3 He will be like a tree planted by the rivers of water
Psalms1:4 The wicked are not so
Psalms1:5 Therefore the wicked will not stand in the judgment
Psalms1:6 For the Lord knows the way of the righteous
Psalms2:1 Why do the nations rage
Psalms2:2 The kings of the earth set themselves
Psalms2:3 “Let us tear off their bonds and cast away their ropes from us.”
Psalms2:4 He who sits in the heavens laughs; the Lord ridicules them.
Psalms2:5 Then He will speak to them in His wrath and terrify them in His burning anger:
Psalms2:6 “I have set My king on Zion
Psalms2:7 I will declare the decree of the Lord: He said to me
Psalms2:8 Ask of Me
Psalms2:9 You will break them with a rod of iron; you will dash them in pieces like a potter’s vessel.”
Psalms2:10 Now then
Psalms2:11 Serve the Lord with fear; and tremble with trepidation!
Psalms2:12 Kiss the son
Psalms3:1 Lord
Psalms3:2 Many are saying about my life
Psalms3:3 But You
Psalms3:4 I cried to the Lord with my voice
Psalms3:5 I lay down and slept; I awoke
Psalms3:6 I will not be afraid of multitudes of people who have set themselves against me all around.
Psalms3:7 Arise
Psalms3:8 Salvation belongs to the Lord. Your blessing is on Your people.    Selah
Psalms4:1 Answer me when I call
Psalms4:2 O people
Psalms4:3 Know that the Lord set apart the faithful for Himself; the Lord hears when I call to Him.
Psalms4:4 Tremble in awe
Psalms4:5 Offer sacrifices of righteousness
Psalms4:6 There are many who say
Psalms4:7 You have placed gladness in my heart
Psalms4:8 I will both lie down in peace and sleep; for You
Psalms5:1 Give ear to my words
Psalms5:2 Listen to the voice of my cry
Psalms5:3 O  Lord
Psalms5:4 For You are not a God who has pleasure in wickedness
Psalms5:5 Those who boast will not stand in Your sight; You hate all workers of iniquity.
Psalms5:6 You will destroy those who speak lies; the Lord abhors the bloodthirsty and deceitful man.
Psalms5:7 But as for me
Psalms5:8 Lead me
Psalms5:9 For there is no uprightness in their mouth; destruction is in their midst; their throat is an open tomb; they flatter with their tongue.
Psalms5:10 Declare them guilty
Psalms5:11 But let all those who take refuge in You rejoice; let them ever shout for joy
Psalms5:12 For You
Psalms6:1 O  Lord
Psalms6:2 Be gracious to me
Psalms6:3 My soul is greatly troubled
Psalms6:4 Return
Psalms6:5 For in death there is no remembrance of You; in Sheol who will give You thanks?
Psalms6:6 I am weary with my groaning; all night I flood my bed with tears; I drench my couch with my weeping.
Psalms6:7 My eyes waste away from grief; they grow weak because of all my enemies.
Psalms6:8 Depart from me
Psalms6:9 The Lord has heard my supplication; the Lord accepts my prayer.
Psalms6:10 May all my enemies be ashamed and greatly troubled; may they turn back and be suddenly ashamed.
Psalms7:1 O  Lord my God
Psalms7:2 lest they tear my soul like a lion
Psalms7:3 O  Lord my God
Psalms7:4 if I have repaid evil to him who was at peace with me
Psalms7:5 then may the enemy pursue my life and overtake me; may my enemy trample my life to the ground
Psalms7:6 Arise
Psalms7:7 So shall the congregation of the peoples surround You; For their sakes
Psalms7:8 The Lord will judge the peoples; grant me justice
Psalms7:9 May the evil of the wicked come to an end; may You vindicate the righteous; You are a righteous God who examines the minds and hearts.
Psalms7:10 My defense depends on God
Psalms7:11 God is a righteous judge
Psalms7:12 If a man does not repent
Psalms7:13 He has prepared for Himself deadly weapons; He makes His arrows flaming shafts.
Psalms7:14 Behold
Psalms7:15 He who digs a hole and hollows it will then fall into his own pit.
Psalms7:16 His mischief will return on his own head; his violence will descend on the crown of his own head.
Psalms7:17 I will thank the Lord according to His righteousness
Psalms8:1 O  Lord
Psalms8:2 Out of the mouth of babes and nursing infants You have ordained strength because of Your enemies
Psalms8:3 When I consider Your heavens
Psalms8:4 what is man that You are mindful of him
Psalms8:5 For You have made him a little lower than the angels
Psalms8:6 You have given him dominion over the works of Your hands; You have put all things under his feet
Psalms8:7 all sheep and oxen
Psalms8:8 the birds of the air
Psalms8:9 O  Lord
Psalms9:1 I will give thanks to You
Psalms9:2 I will be glad and rejoice in You; I will sing praise to Your name
Psalms9:3 When my enemies are turned back
Psalms9:4 For You have maintained my right and my cause; You sat on the throne judging what is right.
Psalms9:5 You have rebuked the nations
Psalms9:6 O enemy
Psalms9:7 But the Lord remains forever; He has established His throne for judgment.
Psalms9:8 He will judge the world in righteousness; He will give judgment to the peoples in uprightness.
Psalms9:9 The Lord also will be a refuge for the oppressed
Psalms9:10 Those who know Your name will put their trust in You
Psalms9:11 Sing praises to the Lord who dwells in Zion; declare His deeds among the people.
Psalms9:12 He who avenges deaths remembers them; He does not forget the cry of the humble.
Psalms9:13 Be gracious to me
Psalms9:14 that I may recount all Your praise in the gates of the daughter of Zion
Psalms9:15 The nations have sunk down in the pit that they made; their own foot is caught in the net which they hid.
Psalms9:16 The Lord is known by the judgment that He executes; the wicked is snared in the work of his own hands. Higgaion.   Selah
Psalms9:17 The wicked will depart to Sheol
Psalms9:18 For the needy will not always be forgotten
Psalms9:19 Arise

Psalms10:1 Why do You stand far off
Psalms10:2 In arrogance the wicked persecutes the poor; let them be caught in the devices they have planned.
Psalms10:3 For the wicked boasts of his soul’s desire
Psalms10:4 The wicked
Psalms10:5 His ways are always prosperous; Your judgments are high and distant from him; as for all his enemies
Psalms10:6 He says in his heart
Psalms10:7 His mouth is filled with cursing and deceit and oppression; under his tongue is mischief and iniquity.
Psalms10:8 He sits in the lurking places of the villages; in the secret places he murders the innocent; his eyes lurk against the unfortunate.
Psalms10:9 He lies in wait secretly as a lion in his den; he lies in wait to catch the poor; he catches the poor
Psalms10:10 He crouches; he lies low
Psalms10:11 He says in his heart
Psalms10:12 Arise
Psalms10:13 Why do the wicked despise God? He says in his heart
Psalms10:14 You have seen it
Psalms10:15 Break the arm of the wicked and the evil man; seek out his wickedness until You find none.
Psalms10:16 The Lord is King forever and ever; the nations perished from His land.
Psalms10:17 The desire of the humble You have heard
Psalms10:18 to judge the orphan and the oppressed; man on earth no longer trembles.

Psalms15:1 Lord
Psalms15:2 He who walks uprightly
Psalms15:3 he who does not slander with the tongue and does no evil to his neighbor
Psalms15:4 in whose eyes a vile person is despised
Psalms15:5 he who does not put his money out to usury
Psalms16:1 Preserve me
Psalms16:2 I have said to the Lord
Psalms16:3 For the holy ones in the land
Psalms16:4 Those who choose another god multiply their sorrows; their drink offerings of blood I will not offer nor take their names upon my lips.
Psalms16:5 O  Lord
Psalms16:6 The boundary lines have fallen to me in pleasant places; yes
Psalms16:7 I will bless the Lord who gives me counsel; in the night also my heart instructs me.
Psalms16:8 I have set the Lord always before me; because He is at my right hand
Psalms16:9 Therefore my heart is glad
Psalms16:10 For You will not leave my soul in Sheol
Psalms16:11 You will show me the path of life. In Your presence is fullness of joy; at Your right hand are pleasures forevermore.
Psalms17:1 Hear a just cause
Psalms17:2 Let my vindication come from Your presence; let Your eyes behold the things that are right.
Psalms17:3 You have tried my heart; You have visited me by night; You have tested me and found nothing; I have purposed that my mouth will not transgress.
Psalms17:4 Concerning the works of men
Psalms17:5 Keep my steps in Your paths
Psalms17:6 I have called upon You
Psalms17:7 Wondrously show Your lovingkindness by Your right hand
Psalms17:8 Keep me as the apple of Your eye; hide me in the shadow of Your wings
Psalms17:9 from the wicked who oppress me
Psalms17:10 They close their hard hearts; with their mouths they speak proudly.
Psalms17:11 They have now surrounded us in our steps; they have set their eyes to cast me to the ground
Psalms17:12 like a lion eager to rip its prey
Psalms17:13 Arise
Psalms17:14 from men by Your hand
Psalms17:15 As for me
Psalms18:1 I love You
Psalms18:2 The Lord is my rock and my fortress and my deliverer; my God
Psalms18:3 I will call upon the Lord
Psalms18:4 The cords of death encompassed me; the torrents of destruction assailed me.
Psalms18:5 The cords of Sheol surrounded me; the snares of death confronted me.
Psalms18:6 In my distress I called upon the Lord
Psalms18:7 Then the earth shook and trembled; the foundations of the hills also quaked
Psalms18:8 Smoke went up from His nostrils
Psalms18:9 He bowed the heavens and came down; darkness was under His feet.
Psalms18:10 He rode on a cherub and flew; He flew swiftly on the wings of the wind.
Psalms18:11 He made darkness His secret place; His pavilion surrounding Him was dark waters and thick clouds of the skies.
Psalms18:12 Out of the brightness before Him
Psalms18:13 The Lord thundered in the heavens
Psalms18:14 He sent out His arrows and scattered the enemy; He shot out lightnings and vanquished them.
Psalms18:15 Then the channels of the sea were seen
Psalms18:16 He sent from above
Psalms18:17 He delivered me from my strong enemy
Psalms18:18 They confronted me in the day of my calamity
Psalms18:19 He also brought me forth into a broad place; He delivered me because He delighted in me.
Psalms18:20 The Lord rewarded me according to my righteousness; according to the cleanness of my hands He has recompensed me.
Psalms18:21 For I have kept the ways of the Lord
Psalms18:22 For all His decrees were before me
Psalms18:23 I was also upright before Him
Psalms18:24 Therefore the Lord has rewarded me according to my righteousness
Psalms18:25 With the merciful You will show Yourself merciful; with the blameless man You will show Yourself blameless;
Psalms18:26 with the pure You will show Yourself pure; and with the crooked You will show Yourself shrewd.
Psalms18:27 For You will save the humble people
Psalms18:28 For You will light my lamp; the Lord my God will enlighten my darkness.
Psalms18:29 For by You I can outrun a troop
Psalms18:30 As for God
Psalms18:31 For who is God
Psalms18:32 It is God who girds me with strength
Psalms18:33 He makes my feet like the feet of deer
Psalms18:34 He trains my hands for war
Psalms18:35 You have given me the shield of Your salvation
Psalms18:36 You widened my path for my steps under me
Psalms18:37 I pursued my enemies and overtook them; I did not return until they were consumed.
Psalms18:38 I wounded them
Psalms18:39 For You girded me with strength for the battle; You subdued under me those who rose up against me.
Psalms18:40 You gave me the necks of my enemies
Psalms18:41 They cried for help
Psalms18:42 Then I beat them fine
Psalms18:43 You have delivered me from strife with the people
Psalms18:44 As soon as they hear of me
Psalms18:45 The foreigners lose heart
Psalms18:46 The Lord lives! Blessed be my rock! Let the God of my salvation be exalted.
Psalms18:47 It is God who avenges me and subdues the peoples under me;
Psalms18:48 He delivers me from my enemies. You exalt me above those who rise up against me; You have delivered me from the violent man.
Psalms18:49 Therefore I will give thanks to You
Psalms18:50 Great deliverance He gives to His king

Psalms19:2 Day unto day utters speech

Psalms19:4 Their voice has gone out through all the earth
Psalms19:5 which is like a bridegroom coming out of his chamber
Psalms19:6 Its rising is from one end of the heavens
Psalms19:7 The law of the Lord is perfect
Psalms19:8 the statutes of the Lord are right
Psalms19:9 the fear of the Lord is clean
Psalms19:10 More to be desired are they than gold
Psalms19:11 Moreover by them is Your servant warned

Psalms19:14 Let the words of my mouth and the meditation of my heart be acceptable in Your sight

Psalms21:1 The king will rejoice in Your strength
Psalms21:2 You have given him his heart’s desire
Psalms21:3 For You meet him with the blessings of goodness; You set a crown of pure gold on his head.
Psalms21:4 He asked life of You
Psalms21:5 His glory is great in Your salvation; honor and majesty You bestow on him.
Psalms21:6 For You have made him most blessed forever; You have made him glad with the joy of Your presence.
Psalms21:7 For the king trusts in the Lord
Psalms21:8 Your hand will find out all Your enemies; Your right hand will find out those who hate You.
Psalms21:9 You will make them like a fiery furnace in the time of Your anger; the Lord will swallow them up in His wrath
Psalms21:10 You will destroy their offspring from the earth
Psalms21:11 For they intended evil against You; they devised a plot which they are not able to perform.
Psalms21:12 Therefore You will put them to flight; You will aim Your bows at their faces.

Psalms22:1 My God
Psalms22:2 O my God
Psalms22:3 But You are holy
Psalms22:4 Our fathers trusted in You; they trusted
Psalms22:5 They cried to You
Psalms22:6 But I am a worm
Psalms22:7 All who see me mock me; they sneer with the lip; they shake the head
Psalms22:8 “Trust in the Lord
Psalms22:9 But You are He who took me out of the womb; You kept me safe on my mother’s breasts.
Psalms22:10 Upon You was I cast from birth; from my mother’s womb You have been my God.
Psalms22:11 Be not far from me
Psalms22:12 Many bulls surround me; strong bulls of Bashan encircle me.
Psalms22:13 They open wide their mouths at me
Psalms22:14 I am poured out like water
Psalms22:15 My strength is dried up like a potsherd
Psalms22:16 For dogs have encompassed me; the assembly of the wicked has encircled me; they pierced my hands and my feet;
Psalms22:17 I can count all my bones; they look and stare at me.
Psalms22:18 They divide my garments among themselves
Psalms22:19 But You
Psalms22:20 Deliver my soul from the sword
Psalms22:21 Save me from the lion’s mouth! From the horns of the wild oxen You have rescued me.
Psalms22:22 I will declare Your name to my brethren; in the midst of the assembly I will praise You.
Psalms22:23 You who fear the Lord
Psalms22:24 For He has not despised nor abhorred the affliction of the afflicted; nor has He hidden His face from him; but heard when he cried to Him.
Psalms22:25 My praise will be from You in the great congregation; I will pay my vows before those who fear Him.
Psalms22:26 The meek will eat and be satisfied; those who seek Him will praise the Lord. May your hearts live forever.
Psalms22:27 All the ends of the world will remember and turn to the Lord
Psalms22:28 For kingship belongs to the Lord
Psalms22:29 All the prosperous ones of the earth will eat and worship; all who go down to the dust will bow before Him
Psalms22:30 Their descendants will serve Him; the next generation will be told about the Lord;
Psalms22:31 they will come and declare His righteousness to a people yet to be born

Psalms71:5 For You are my hope
Psalms71:6 I have leaned on You since my birth; You are He who took me out of my mother’s womb. My praise will be continually of You.
Psalms71:7 I have become a marvel to many
Psalms71:8 Let my mouth be filled with Your praise and with Your glory all the day.
Psalms71:9 Cast me not away in the time of old age; forsake me not when my strength fails.

Psalms71:19 Your righteousness

Psalms72:1 Give the king Your justice
Psalms72:2 May he judge Your people with righteousness and Your poor with justice.
Psalms72:3 May the mountains bring peace to the people
Psalms72:4 May he vindicate the afflicted of the people
Psalms72:5 May they fear You while the sun endures and as long as the moon shines
Psalms72:6 May he descend like rain upon the mown grass
Psalms72:7 In his days may the righteous flourish and peace abound
Psalms72:8 May he have dominion from sea to sea
Psalms72:9 May those who dwell in the wilderness bow before him
Psalms72:10 May the kings of Tarshish and of the isles bring gifts; may the kings of Sheba and Seba render tribute.
Psalms72:11 May all kings bow down before him
Psalms72:12 For he will deliver the needy when he cries
Psalms72:13 He will have compassion on the poor and needy
Psalms72:14 He will redeem their life from oppression and violence
Psalms72:15 So may he live
Psalms72:16 May there be an abundance of grain in the land; may it wave on the tops of the mountains; may its fruit be like Lebanon; and may those from the city flourish like grass of the earth.
Psalms72:17 May his name endure forever; may his name continue as long as the sun shines. And may men be blessed in him; and may all nations call him blessed!
Psalms72:18 Blessed be the Lord God
Psalms72:19 And blessed be His glorious name forever; and may the whole earth be filled with His glory. Amen and Amen.

Psalms77:1 My voice rises to God
Psalms77:2 In the day of my trouble I sought the Lord; in the night my hand was stretched out without ceasing; my soul refused to be comforted.
Psalms77:3 I remembered God
Psalms77:4 You hold my eyelids open; I am so troubled that I cannot speak.
Psalms77:5 I have considered the days of old
Psalms77:6 I call to remembrance my song in the night; I meditate within my heart
Psalms77:7 “Will the Lord reject forever? Will He be favorable no more?
Psalms77:8 Has His mercy ceased forever? Have His promises failed for all time?
Psalms77:9 Has God forgotten to be gracious? Has He in anger withheld His tender mercies?”   Selah
Psalms77:10 Then I said
Psalms77:11 I will remember the works of the Lord; surely I will remember Your wonders of old.
Psalms77:12 I will meditate on all Your work and muse on Your mighty deeds.
Psalms77:13 Your way
Psalms77:14 You are the God who works wonders; You have declared Your strength among the peoples.
Psalms77:15 You have with Your arm redeemed Your people
Psalms77:16 The waters saw You
Psalms77:17 The clouds poured out water; the skies gave forth thunder; Your arrows flashed back and forth.
Psalms77:18 The sound of Your thunder was in the whirlwind; the lightnings lit up the world; the earth trembled and shook.
Psalms77:19 Your way was through the sea

Psalms78:1 Give ear
Psalms78:2 I will open my mouth in a parable; I will speak mysteries of old
Psalms78:3 which we have heard and known
Psalms78:4 We will not hide them from their children
Psalms78:5 For He established a testimony in Jacob and appointed a law in Israel
Psalms78:6 that the generation to come might know them
Psalms78:7 so that they should set their hope in God and not forget the works of God
Psalms78:8 and that they may not be like their fathers
Psalms78:9 The Ephraimites
Psalms78:10 They did not keep the covenant of God and refused to walk in His law;
Psalms78:11 they forgot His works and the wonders that He had shown them.
Psalms78:12 In the sight of their fathers He performed marvelous wonders in the land of Egypt
Psalms78:13 He divided the sea and caused them to pass through
Psalms78:14 In the daytime He led them with a cloud
Psalms78:15 He split rocks in the wilderness and gave them plenty to drink like the depths.
Psalms78:16 He brought streams also out of the rock and caused waters to run down like rivers.
Psalms78:17 But they sinned still more against Him by rebelling against the Most High in the desert.
Psalms78:18 They tested God in their heart by asking for the food they craved.
Psalms78:19 Then they spoke against God
Psalms78:20 Behold
Psalms78:21 Therefore the Lord heard this and was full of wrath; a fire was kindled against Jacob
Psalms78:22 because they did not believe in God and did not trust in His salvation.
Psalms78:23 Yet He had commanded the skies above and opened the doors of heaven
Psalms78:24 and He rained down manna upon them to eat and gave them the grain of heaven.
Psalms78:25 Men ate the bread of angels; He sent them food in abundance.
Psalms78:26 He caused an east wind to blow in the heavens
Psalms78:27 He rained meat on them like dust
Psalms78:28 then He let them fall in the midst of their camp
Psalms78:29 So they ate and were well filled
Psalms78:30 They were not separated from their desire; but while their food was still in their mouths
Psalms78:31 the wrath of God came upon them
Psalms78:32 For all this they still sinned
Psalms78:33 Therefore their days did He consume like a breath
Psalms78:34 When He killed them
Psalms78:35 They remembered that God was their rock
Psalms78:36 Nevertheless they flattered Him with their mouths
Psalms78:37 for their heart was not steadfast toward Him
Psalms78:38 But He
Psalms78:39 for He remembered that they were but flesh
Psalms78:40 How often they provoked Him in the wilderness and grieved Him in the desert!
Psalms78:41 Again and again they tested God
Psalms78:42 They did not remember His power
Psalms78:43 when He performed His signs in Egypt and His wonders in the fields of Zoan
Psalms78:44 and turned their rivers into blood
Psalms78:45 He sent swarms of flies among them
Psalms78:46 He gave their crops to the grasshopper and the fruit of their labor to the locust.
Psalms78:47 He destroyed their vines with hail and their sycamore trees with frost.
Psalms78:48 He gave up their cattle to the hail and their flocks to fiery lightning.
Psalms78:49 He cast upon them the fierceness of His anger
Psalms78:50 He made a path for His anger; He did not spare them from death
Psalms78:51 and struck down all the firstborn in Egypt
Psalms78:52 But He led forth His own people like sheep and guided them in the wilderness like a flock;
Psalms78:53 He led them on safely
Psalms78:54 He brought them to His holy border
Psalms78:55 He cast out the nations before them
Psalms78:56 Yet they tested and provoked the Most High God
Psalms78:57 but turned back and acted unfaithfully like their fathers; they turned aside like a deceitful bow.
Psalms78:58 For they provoked Him to anger with their high places and moved Him to jealousy with their graven images.
Psalms78:59 When God heard this
Psalms78:60 so that He forsook the tabernacle at Shiloh
Psalms78:61 and delivered His strength to captivity and His glory to the enemy’s hand.
Psalms78:62 He gave His people over to the sword
Psalms78:63 The fire consumed their young men
Psalms78:64 Their priests fell by the sword
Psalms78:65 Then the Lord awoke as one out of sleep
Psalms78:66 He routed His enemies back; He made them a perpetual reproach.
Psalms78:67 Moreover
Psalms78:68 but chose the tribe of Judah
Psalms78:69 He built His sanctuary like the high heavens
Psalms78:70 He chose David His servant and took him from the sheepfolds;
Psalms78:71 from following the nursing ewes He brought him to shepherd Jacob His people
Psalms78:72 So he shepherded them according to the integrity of his heart and guided them by the skillfulness of his hands.

Psalms91:2 I will say of the Lord
Psalms91:3 Surely He shall deliver you from the snare of the fowler and from the deadly pestilence.
Psalms91:4 He shall cover you with His pinions
Psalms91:5 You shall not be afraid of the terror by night
Psalms91:6 nor of the pestilence that walks in darkness
Psalms91:7 A thousand may fall at your side
Psalms91:8 Only with your eyes shall you behold and see the recompense of the wicked.
Psalms91:9 Because you have made the Lord
Psalms91:10 no evil will befall you
Psalms91:11 for He will give His angels charge over you to guard you in all your ways.
Psalms91:12 They will bear you up in their hands
Psalms91:13 You will tread upon the lion and the adder; the young lion and the serpent will you trample underfoot.
Psalms91:14 “Because he has set his love upon Me
Psalms91:15 He will call upon Me
Psalms91:16 With long life will I satisfy him and show him My salvation.”

Psalms92:2 to declare Your lovingkindness in the morning

Psalms92:4 For You
Psalms92:5 O  Lord
Psalms92:6 A brutish man does not know
Psalms92:7 that though the wicked spring up like grass and though all the workers of iniquity flourish
Psalms92:8 But You
Psalms92:9 For behold
Psalms92:10 But You have exalted my horn like the horn of the wild ox; You have anointed me with fresh oil.
Psalms92:11 My eyes have seen the downfall of my enemies
Psalms92:12 The righteous shall flourish like the palm tree and grow like a cedar in Lebanon.
Psalms92:13 Those who are planted in the house of the Lord shall flourish in the courts of our God.
Psalms92:14 They shall still bear fruit in old age; they shall be healthy and flourishing
Psalms92:15 to declare that the Lord is upright; He is my rock

Psalms93:4 The Lord on high is mightier than the noise of many waters

Psalms102:1 Hear my prayer
Psalms102:2 Do not hide Your face from me in the day of my trouble; incline Your ear to me; in the day when I call
Psalms102:3 For my days pass away like smoke
Psalms102:4 My heart is stricken and withered like grass
Psalms102:5 Because of the sound of my groaning my bones cling to my skin.
Psalms102:6 I am like an owl of the wilderness; like an owl of the desert.
Psalms102:7 I lie awake
Psalms102:8 My enemies reproach me all the day; those who deride me use my name for a curse.
Psalms102:9 For I eat ashes like bread and mingle my drink with tears
Psalms102:10 because of Your indignation and Your wrath; for You have lifted me up and cast me away.
Psalms102:11 My days are like an evening shadow; I wither away like grass.
Psalms102:12 But You
Psalms102:13 You will arise and have mercy on Zion
Psalms102:14 For Your servants take pleasure in her stones
Psalms102:15 So the nations will fear the name of the Lord
Psalms102:16 For the Lord will build up Zion; He will appear in His glory.
Psalms102:17 He will regard the prayer of the destitute
Psalms102:18 Let this be recorded for the generation to come
Psalms102:19 For He looked down from the height of His sanctuary; from heaven the Lord beheld the earth
Psalms102:20 to hear the groaning of the prisoners
Psalms102:21 to declare the name of the Lord in Zion
Psalms102:22 when the peoples are gathered together
Psalms102:23 He has weakened my strength in midcourse; He has shortened my days.
Psalms102:24 I said
Psalms102:25 Of old You laid the foundation of the earth
Psalms102:26 They will perish
Psalms102:27 But You are the same
Psalms102:28 The children of Your servants shall continue; and their descendants shall be established before You.

Psalms104:1 Bless the Lord
Psalms104:2 covering Yourself with light as with a garment
Psalms104:3 He lays the beams of His upper chambers on the waters; He makes the clouds His chariot; He walks upon the wings of the wind;
Psalms104:4 He makes His angels spirits
Psalms104:5 You laid the earth on its foundations
Psalms104:6 You covered it with the deep as with a garment; the waters stood above the mountains.
Psalms104:7 At Your rebuke they fled; at the sound of Your thunder they hurried away.
Psalms104:8 The mountains rose; the valleys sank down to the place which You established for them.
Psalms104:9 You set a boundary that they may not pass over
Psalms104:10 You send the springs into the valleys; they flow between the hills.
Psalms104:11 They give drink to every beast of the field; the wild donkeys quench their thirst.
Psalms104:12 By them the birds of the heavens have their habitation; they sing among the branches.
Psalms104:13 You water the mountains from Your upper chambers; the earth is satisfied with the fruit of Your works.
Psalms104:14 You cause the grass to grow for the cattle
Psalms104:15 and wine that makes glad the heart of man
Psalms104:16 The trees of the Lord are full of sap
Psalms104:17 where the birds make their nests; the stork has its home in the fir trees.
Psalms104:18 The high mountains are for the wild goats; the rocks are a refuge for the rock badgers.
Psalms104:19 He made the moon for the seasons; the sun knows its place for setting.
Psalms104:20 You make darkness
Psalms104:21 The young lions roar after their prey
Psalms104:22 When the sun rises
Psalms104:23 Man goes out to his work and to his labor until the evening.
Psalms104:24 O  Lord
Psalms104:25 So is this great and wide sea
Psalms104:26 There the ships sail along; and there is Leviathan
Psalms104:27 These all wait upon You
Psalms104:28 You give to them; they gather it up; You open Your hand
Psalms104:29 You hide Your face; they are troubled; You take away their breath; they die and return to their dust.
Psalms104:30 You send forth Your Spirit; they are created
Psalms104:31 May the glory of the Lord endure forever; may the Lord rejoice in His works.
Psalms104:32 He looks at the earth
Psalms104:33 I will sing to the Lord as long as I live; I will sing praise to my God while I have my being.
Psalms104:34 May my meditation be sweet to Him; I will be glad in the Lord.
Psalms104:35 May sinners be consumed from the earth

Psalms105:7 He is the Lord our God; His judgments are in all the earth.
Psalms105:8 He remembers His covenant forever

Psalms105:10 which He confirmed to Jacob as a statute
Psalms105:11 saying
Psalms105:12 When they were few in number
Psalms105:13 and they wandered from one nation to another
Psalms105:14 He permitted no one to do them wrong; He rebuked kings for their sakes
Psalms105:15 saying
Psalms105:16 Moreover He called for a famine upon the land; He broke the whole supply of bread.
Psalms105:17 He sent a man ahead of them
Psalms105:18 They hurt his feet with fetters; he was put in irons.
Psalms105:19 Until the time that his word came to pass
Psalms105:20 The king sent and released him; the ruler of the people let him go free.
Psalms105:21 He made him lord of his house and ruler of all his possessions
Psalms105:22 to imprison his princes at his pleasure
Psalms105:23 Israel also came to Egypt
Psalms105:24 He increased His people greatly
Psalms105:25 He turned their heart to hate His people
Psalms105:26 He sent Moses
Psalms105:27 They performed His signs among them
Psalms105:28 He sent darkness
Psalms105:29 He turned their waters into blood
Psalms105:30 Their land swarmed with frogs
Psalms105:31 He spoke
Psalms105:32 He gave them hail for rain
Psalms105:33 He struck their vines and their fig trees
Psalms105:34 He spoke and locusts came
Psalms105:35 which ate all the vegetation in their land
Psalms105:36 He also struck all the firstborn in their land
Psalms105:37 He also brought them out with silver and gold
Psalms105:38 Egypt was glad when they departed
Psalms105:39 He spread a cloud for a covering
Psalms105:40 The people asked
Psalms105:41 He opened the rock
Psalms105:42 For He remembered His holy promise
Psalms105:43 He led His people out with joy
Psalms105:44 He gave them the lands of the nations

Psalms106:2 Who can utter the mighty deeds of the Lord? Who can declare all His praise?
Psalms106:3 Blessed are those who keep justice
Psalms106:4 Remember me
Psalms106:5 that I may see the prosperity of Your chosen ones
Psalms106:6 We
Psalms106:7 Our fathers in Egypt did not understand Your wonders; they did not remember the abundance of Your mercy
Psalms106:8 Nevertheless He saved them for His name’s sake
Psalms106:9 He rebuked the Red Sea
Psalms106:10 He saved them from the hand of him who hated them
Psalms106:11 The waters covered their enemies; not one of them was left.
Psalms106:12 Then they believed His words; they sang His praise.
Psalms106:13 But they soon forgot His works; they did not wait for His counsel
Psalms106:14 but lusted exceedingly in the wilderness
Psalms106:15 He gave them their request
Psalms106:16 They envied Moses in the camp
Psalms106:17 The earth opened and swallowed up Dathan
Psalms106:18 A fire broke out in their company; the flame burned up the wicked.
Psalms106:19 They made a calf at Horeb
Psalms106:20 Thus they exchanged their glory for the image of an ox that eats grass.
Psalms106:21 They forgot God
Psalms106:22 wondrous works in the land of Ham
Psalms106:23 Therefore He said that He would destroy them
Psalms106:24 Then they despised the pleasant land; they did not believe His word
Psalms106:25 but murmured in their tents
Psalms106:26 Therefore He raised His hand against them to overthrow them in the wilderness
Psalms106:27 and swore that He would overthrow their descendants among the nations
Psalms106:28 They joined themselves to Baal of Peor
Psalms106:29 Thus they provoked Him to anger with their deeds
Psalms106:30 Then Phinehas stood up and intervened
Psalms106:31 And that was counted to him for righteousness to all generations forever.
Psalms106:32 They angered Him also at the waters of Meribah
Psalms106:33 because they provoked His Spirit
Psalms106:34 They did not destroy the peoples
Psalms106:35 but they mingled with the nations and learned their works;
Psalms106:36 they served their idols
Psalms106:37 They sacrificed their sons and their daughters to the demons
Psalms106:38 and shed innocent blood
Psalms106:39 Thus were they defiled by their works and played the whore by their deeds.
Psalms106:40 Therefore the wrath of the Lord was kindled against His people
Psalms106:41 He delivered them into the hand of the nations
Psalms106:42 Their enemies also oppressed them
Psalms106:43 Many times He delivered them
Psalms106:44 Nevertheless
Psalms106:45 For their sake He remembered His covenant
Psalms106:46 He also caused them to be pitied by all those who carried them away captive.
Psalms106:47 Save us
Psalms106:48 Blessed be the Lord God of Israel

Psalms109:1 Do not be silent
Psalms109:2 For the mouth of the wicked and the deceitful have they opened against me; they have spoken against me with a lying tongue.
Psalms109:3 They have also encircled me with words of hatred and fought against me without cause.
Psalms109:4 In return for my love they are my accusers
Psalms109:5 So they have rewarded me evil for good
Psalms109:6 Appoint a wicked man against him
Psalms109:7 When he is judged
Psalms109:8 Let his days be few
Psalms109:9 Let his children be fatherless and his wife a widow.
Psalms109:10 Let his children wander continuously and beg; let them seek their bread far from their desolate homes.
Psalms109:11 Let the creditor seize all that he has
Psalms109:12 Let there be none to extend mercy to him
Psalms109:13 Let his posterity be cut off
Psalms109:14 Let the iniquity of his fathers be remembered before the Lord
Psalms109:15 Let them be before the Lord continually
Psalms109:16 For he did not remember to show kindness
Psalms109:17 He loved cursing; so let it come on him; He did not delight in blessing; so let it be far from him.
Psalms109:18 He clothed himself with cursing as his garment; so let it enter his body like water
Psalms109:19 Let it be to him like a garment with which he covers himself
Psalms109:20 Let this be the reward of my accusers from the Lord
Psalms109:21 But You
Psalms109:22 For I am poor and needy
Psalms109:23 I am gone like a shadow when it lengthens; I am shaken off like a locust.
Psalms109:24 My knees are weak through fasting
Psalms109:25 I also have become a reproach to them; when they look at me
Psalms109:26 Help me
Psalms109:27 that they may know that this is Your hand
Psalms109:28 Let them curse
Psalms109:29 Let my accusers be clothed with shame
Psalms109:30 I will greatly give thanks to the Lord with my mouth; I will praise Him among the multitude.
Psalms109:31 For He stands at the right hand of the poor
