Psalms111:2 Great are the works of the Lord
Psalms111:3 Honorable and majestic is His work
Psalms111:4 He has made His wonderful works to be remembered; the Lord is gracious and compassionate.
Psalms111:5 He has given food to those who fear Him; He will ever be mindful of His covenant.
Psalms111:6 He has shown His people the power of His works
Psalms111:7 The works of His hands are truth and justice; all His precepts are trustworthy.
Psalms111:8 They stand forever and ever
Psalms111:9 He has sent redemption to His people; He has commanded His covenant forever; holy and awesome is His name.
Psalms111:10 The fear of the Lord is the beginning of wisdom; a good understanding have all those who do His commands. His praise endures forever!
Psalms112:1 Praise the Lord! Blessed is the man who fears the Lord
Psalms112:2 His descendants will be mighty on the earth; the generation of the upright will be blessed.
Psalms112:3 Wealth and riches will be in his house
Psalms112:4 Unto the upright arises light in the darkness; he is gracious
Psalms112:5 It is well with the man who is gracious and lends; he will guide his affairs with justice.
Psalms112:6 For he will never be moved; the righteous will be remembered forever.
Psalms112:7 He will not be afraid of evil tidings; his heart is steadfast
Psalms112:8 His heart is established; he will not be afraid
Psalms112:9 He has given freely; he has given to the poor; his righteousness endures forever; his horn will be exalted with honor.
Psalms112:10 The wicked will see it and be angry; he will gnash his teeth and melt away; the desire of the wicked will perish.

Psalms144:13 may our barns be full
Psalms144:14 may our cattle be strong in labor
Psalms144:15 Blessed are the people who are in such a state; blessed are the people whose God is the Lord.

Psalms146:1 Praise the Lord! Praise the Lord
Psalms146:2 While I live I will praise the Lord; I will sing praises to my God while I have my being.

Psalms146:4 His spirit departs

Psalms146:6 who made heaven and earth

Psalms147:1 Praise the Lord! For it is good to sing praises to our God; for it is pleasant
Psalms147:2 The Lord builds up Jerusalem; He gathers together the outcasts of Israel.
Psalms147:3 He heals the brokenhearted and binds up their wounds.
Psalms147:4 He counts the number of the stars; He calls them all by their names.
Psalms147:5 Great is our Lord
Psalms147:6 The Lord lifts up the humble; He casts the wicked down to the ground.
Psalms147:7 Sing to the Lord with thanksgiving; sing praises on the harp to our God
Psalms147:8 who covers the heavens with clouds
Psalms147:9 He gives to the beast its food and to the young ravens that cry.
Psalms147:10 He does not delight in the strength of the horse; He takes no pleasure in the legs of a man.
Psalms147:11 The Lord takes pleasure in those who fear Him
Psalms147:12 Praise the Lord
Psalms147:13 For He has strengthened the bars of your gates; He has blessed your children within you.
Psalms147:14 He makes peace in your borders
Psalms147:15 He sends forth His commandment to the earth; His word runs very swiftly.
Psalms147:16 He gives snow like wool; He scatters the frost like ashes;
Psalms147:17 He casts forth His ice like morsels; who can stand before His cold?
Psalms147:18 He sends out His word and melts them; He causes His wind to blow
Psalms147:19 He declares His word to Jacob
Psalms147:20 He has not dealt so with any nation; and as for His judgments

Psalms150:1 Praise the Lord! Praise God in His sanctuary; praise Him in His mighty firmament!
Psalms150:2 Praise Him for His mighty acts; praise Him according to His excellent greatness!

Proverbs1:1 The proverbs of Solomon the son of David
Proverbs1:2 To know wisdom and instruction
Proverbs1:3 to receive the instruction of wisdom
Proverbs1:4 to give prudence to the simple
Proverbs1:5 a wise man will hear and increase in learning
Proverbs1:6 to understand a proverb and an interpretation
Proverbs1:7 The fear of the Lord is the beginning of knowledge
Proverbs1:8 My son
Proverbs1:9 for they will be a wreath of grace upon your head
Proverbs1:10 My son
Proverbs1:11 If they say
Proverbs1:12 let us swallow them up alive like Sheol
Proverbs1:13 we will find all kinds of precious possessions
Proverbs1:14 cast in your lot among us; let us all have one purse
Proverbs1:15 my son
Proverbs1:16 for their feet run to evil
Proverbs1:17 Surely
Proverbs1:18 but they lie in wait for their own blood; they lurk secretly for their own lives.
Proverbs1:19 So are the ways of everyone who is greedy for gain; it takes away the life of its owners.
Proverbs1:20 Wisdom cries out in the street
Proverbs1:21 She cries out at the entrance of the noisiest street
Proverbs1:22 “How long
Proverbs1:23 Turn to my rebuke; behold
Proverbs1:24 Because I have called and you refused
Proverbs1:25 because you neglected all my counsel
Proverbs1:26 I also will laugh at your calamity; I will mock when your terror comes
Proverbs1:27 when your terror comes like a storm and your destruction comes like a whirlwind
Proverbs1:28 “Then they will call on me
Proverbs1:29 because they hated knowledge and did not choose the fear of the Lord.
Proverbs1:30 They would have none of my counsel and despised all my reproof.
Proverbs1:31 Therefore they shall eat the fruit of their own way
Proverbs1:32 For the turning away of the simple will slay them
Proverbs1:33 But whoever listens to me will dwell safely
Proverbs2:1 My son
Proverbs2:2 so that you incline your ear to wisdom and apply your heart to understanding;
Proverbs2:3 yes
Proverbs2:4 if you seek her as silver and search for her as for hidden treasures
Proverbs2:5 then you will understand the fear of the Lord and find the knowledge of God.
Proverbs2:6 For the Lord gives wisdom; from His mouth come knowledge and understanding;
Proverbs2:7 He stores up sound wisdom for the righteous; He is a shield to those who walk uprightly;
Proverbs2:8 He guards the paths of justice and preserves the way of His faithful ones.
Proverbs2:9 Then you will understand righteousness and justice and equity
Proverbs2:10 for wisdom will enter your heart
Proverbs2:11 discretion will preserve you
Proverbs2:12 to deliver you from the way of evil
Proverbs2:13 from those who leave the paths of uprightness to walk in the ways of darkness
Proverbs2:14 who rejoice in doing evil and delight in the perversity of evil
Proverbs2:15 whose paths are crooked
Proverbs2:16 to deliver you from the wayward woman
Proverbs2:17 who forsakes the companion of her youth and forgets the covenant of her God.
Proverbs2:18 For her house sinks down to death
Proverbs2:19 none who go to her return
Proverbs2:20 Thus you walk in the way of the good and keep to the paths of the righteous.
Proverbs2:21 For the upright will dwell in the land
Proverbs2:22 but the wicked will be cut off from the earth
Proverbs3:1 My son
Proverbs3:2 for length of days and long life and peace will they give you.
Proverbs3:3 Let not mercy and truth forsake you; bind them around your neck; write them on the tablet of your heart.
Proverbs3:4 So will you find favor and high regard in the sight of God and man.
Proverbs3:5 Trust in the Lord with all your heart
Proverbs3:6 In all your ways acknowledge Him
Proverbs3:7 Be not wise in your own eyes; fear the Lord and depart from evil.
Proverbs3:8 It will be healing to your flesh
Proverbs3:9 Honor the Lord with your substance
Proverbs3:10 then will your barns be filled with plenty
Proverbs3:11 My son
Proverbs3:12 for whom the Lord loves He corrects
Proverbs3:13 Blessed is the man who finds wisdom
Proverbs3:14 for her profit is better than the profit of silver
Proverbs3:15 She is more precious than jewels
Proverbs3:16 Length of days is in her right hand; in her left hand are riches and honor.
Proverbs3:17 Her ways are ways of pleasantness
Proverbs3:18 She is a tree of life to those who lay hold of her; blessed are those who retain her.
Proverbs3:19 The Lord by wisdom founded the earth; by understanding He established the heavens;
Proverbs3:20 by His knowledge the depths were broken up
Proverbs3:21 My son
Proverbs3:22 and they will be life to your soul and adornment for your neck.
Proverbs3:23 Then you will walk on your way securely
Proverbs3:24 When you sit down
Proverbs3:25 Do not be afraid of sudden terror or of the desolation by the wicked when it comes
Proverbs3:26 for the Lord will be your confidence and will keep your foot from being caught.
Proverbs3:27 Do not withhold good from those to whom it is due
Proverbs3:28 Do not say to your neighbor
Proverbs3:29 Do not devise evil against your neighbor who dwells securely beside you.
Proverbs3:30 Do not strive with a man without cause
Proverbs3:31 Do not envy the oppressor and do not choose any of his ways
Proverbs3:32 for the perverse is an abomination to the Lord
Proverbs3:33 The curse of the Lord is on the house of the wicked
Proverbs3:34 Surely He scorns the scornful
Proverbs3:35 The wise will inherit honor
Proverbs4:1 Listen
Proverbs4:2 for I give you good teaching; do not forsake my teaching.
Proverbs4:3 When I was a son to my father
Proverbs4:4 he taught me and said to me
Proverbs4:5 Get wisdom; get understanding; do not forget or turn away from the words of my mouth.
Proverbs4:6 Do not forsake her
Proverbs4:7 The beginning of wisdom is this: get wisdom
Proverbs4:8 Exalt her
Proverbs4:9 She will place on your head an ornament of grace; a crown of glory will she bestow on you.”
Proverbs4:10 Hear
Proverbs4:11 I have taught you in the way of wisdom; I have led you in upright paths.
Proverbs4:12 When you walk
Proverbs4:13 Take hold of instruction; do not let her go; guard her
Proverbs4:14 Do not enter the path of the wicked
Proverbs4:15 Avoid it
Proverbs4:16 For they do not sleep unless they have done evil; their sleep is taken away unless they make someone stumble.
Proverbs4:17 For they eat the bread of wickedness and drink the wine of violence.
Proverbs4:18 But the path of the righteous is like the light of dawn
Proverbs4:19 The way of the wicked is like darkness; they do not know what makes them stumble.
Proverbs4:20 My son
Proverbs4:21 Let them not depart from your sight; keep them within your heart.
Proverbs4:22 For they are life to those who find them
Proverbs4:23 Keep your heart with all diligence
Proverbs4:24 Put away from you a deceitful mouth
Proverbs4:25 Let your eyes look straight ahead
Proverbs4:26 Ponder the path of your feet
Proverbs4:27 Do not turn to the right or to the left; turn your foot away from evil.
Proverbs5:1 My son
Proverbs5:2 that you may regard discretion
Proverbs5:3 For the lips of an adulteress drip as honey
Proverbs5:4 but in the end she is bitter as wormwood
Proverbs5:5 Her feet go down to death
Proverbs5:6 She does not ponder the path of life; her ways are unstable
Proverbs5:7 Listen to me now
Proverbs5:8 Keep your way far from her
Proverbs5:9 lest you give your honor to others
Proverbs5:10 lest strangers be filled with your wealth
Proverbs5:11 and you mourn at the end of your life
Proverbs5:12 and you say

Proverbs6:1 My son
Proverbs6:2 you are snared by the words of your mouth; you are taken by the words of your mouth.
Proverbs6:3 Do this now
Proverbs6:4 Give no sleep to your eyes

Proverbs6:7 which
Proverbs6:8 provides her food in the summer and gathers her sustenance in the harvest.
Proverbs6:9 How long will you sleep
Proverbs6:10 A little sleep
Proverbs6:11 so shall your poverty come upon you like a thief
Proverbs6:12 A worthless person
Proverbs6:13 He winks with his eyes
Proverbs6:14 perverseness is in his heart

Proverbs6:17 a proud look
Proverbs6:18 a heart that devises wicked plans
Proverbs6:19 a false witness who speaks lies
Proverbs6:20 My son
Proverbs6:21 Bind them continually on your heart; tie them around your neck.
Proverbs6:22 When you walk
Proverbs6:23 For the commandment is a lamp and the teaching is light
Proverbs6:24 to keep you from the evil woman
Proverbs6:25 Do not desire her beauty in your heart or let her take you with her eyes;
Proverbs6:26 for the price of a prostitute leaves a man with a loaf of bread
Proverbs6:27 Can a man carry fire next to his bosom without burning his clothes?
Proverbs6:28 Can one walk on hot coals without scorching his feet?
Proverbs6:29 So is he who goes in to his neighbor’s wife; whoever touches her will not be innocent.
Proverbs6:30 Men do not despise a thief if he steals to satisfy himself when he is hungry.
Proverbs6:31 Yet if he is found
Proverbs6:32 But he who commits adultery lacks understanding; he who does it destroys his own soul.
Proverbs6:33 Wounds and dishonor will he get
Proverbs6:34 For jealousy enrages a man
Proverbs6:35 He will accept no compensation; he will not rest content though you offer many gifts.
Proverbs7:1 My son
Proverbs7:2 Keep my commandments and live
Proverbs7:3 Bind them on your fingers; write them on the tablet of your heart.
Proverbs7:4 Say to wisdom
Proverbs7:5 that they may keep you from an adulteress
Proverbs7:6 For at the window of my house I looked out through my lattice
Proverbs7:7 and I saw among the simple ones
Proverbs7:8 passing through the street near her corner
Proverbs7:9 in the twilight
Proverbs7:10 And behold
Proverbs7:11 She was loud and stubborn; her feet did not stay at home.
Proverbs7:12 Now she is in the streets
Proverbs7:13 She caught him and kissed him
Proverbs7:14 “I have peace offerings with me; this day I have paid my vows.
Proverbs7:15 Therefore I came out to meet you
Proverbs7:16 I have decked my couch with tapestry
Proverbs7:17 I have perfumed my bed with myrrh
Proverbs7:18 Come
Proverbs7:19 For my husband is not at home; he has gone on a long journey.
Proverbs7:20 He has taken a bag of money with him and will not come home until full moon.”
Proverbs7:21 With her enticing speech she led him astray; with her flattering lips she seduced him.
Proverbs7:22 Immediately he followed her
Proverbs7:23 until an arrow pierces his liver; as a bird darting into the snare
Proverbs7:24 Now therefore
Proverbs7:25 Let not let your heart turn aside to her ways; do not stray into her paths
Proverbs7:26 for many are the wounded she has laid low
Proverbs7:27 Her house is the way to Sheol
Proverbs8:1 Does not wisdom cry out and understanding raise her voice?
Proverbs8:2 On top of the heights beside the way
Proverbs8:3 Beside the gates in front of the city
Proverbs8:4 “To you
Proverbs8:5 O you simple ones
Proverbs8:6 Listen
Proverbs8:7 for my mouth will speak truth; wickedness is an abomination to my lips.
Proverbs8:8 All the words of my mouth are in righteousness; there is nothing crooked or perverse in them.
Proverbs8:9 They are all plain to him who understands
Proverbs8:10 Receive my instruction instead of silver
Proverbs8:11 for wisdom is better than jewels
Proverbs8:12 “I
Proverbs8:13 The fear of the Lord is to hate evil; pride and arrogance and the evil way and perverse speech do I hate.
Proverbs8:14 Counsel and sound wisdom are mine; I have understanding; I have power.
Proverbs8:15 By me kings reign
Proverbs8:16 by me princes rule
Proverbs8:17 I love those who love me
Proverbs8:18 Riches and honor are with me
Proverbs8:19 My fruit is better than gold
Proverbs8:20 I walk in the way of righteousness
Proverbs8:21 endowing those who love me with wealth
Proverbs8:22 “The Lord created me at the beginning of His work
Proverbs8:23 Ages ago I was established
Proverbs8:24 When there were no depths I was brought forth
Proverbs8:25 Before the mountains were settled
Proverbs8:26 before He had made the earth or the fields or the first dust of the world.
Proverbs8:27 When He established the heavens
Proverbs8:28 when He made firm the skies above
Proverbs8:29 when He assigned to the sea its boundary
Proverbs8:30 then I was beside Him
Proverbs8:31 rejoicing in His inhabited world and delighting in the sons of men.
Proverbs8:32 “Now therefore
Proverbs8:33 Listen to instruction and be wise
Proverbs8:34 Blessed is the man who listens to me
Proverbs8:35 For whoever finds me finds life and obtains favor from the Lord
Proverbs8:36 but he who sins against me wrongs his own soul; all those who hate me love death.”

Proverbs30:1 The words of Agur son of Jakeh. An oracle. The man declares to Ithiel

Proverbs31:9 Speak out
Proverbs31:10 Who can find a virtuous wife? Her worth is far above rubies.
Proverbs31:11 The heart of her husband trusts in her; so he will have no lack of gain.
Proverbs31:12 She does him good
Proverbs31:13 She seeks wool and flax
Proverbs31:14 She is like the ships of the merchant; she brings her food from afar.
Proverbs31:15 She rises while it is yet night
Proverbs31:16 She considers a field and buys it; with the fruit of her hands she plants a vineyard.
Proverbs31:17 She girds herself with strength and strengthens her arms.
Proverbs31:18 She perceives that her merchandise is profitable; her lamp does not go out at night.
Proverbs31:19 She puts her hands to the distaff
Proverbs31:20 She extends her palms to the poor and reaches out her hands to the needy.
Proverbs31:21 She is not afraid of the snow for her household
Proverbs31:22 She makes tapestry for herself; her clothing is fine linen and purple.
Proverbs31:23 Her husband is known in the city gates

Ecclesiastes1:1 These are the words of Qoheleth
Ecclesiastes1:2 “Vanity of vanities
Ecclesiastes1:3 What benefit is there to a person in all his labors at which he toils under the sun?
Ecclesiastes1:4 A generation comes
Ecclesiastes1:5 The sun comes up and the sun goes down
Ecclesiastes1:6 It moves to the south
Ecclesiastes1:7 All the streams flow into the sea
Ecclesiastes1:8 All matters are wearisome. A person is not able to speak to them. The eye is not satisfied with what it sees
Ecclesiastes1:9 What has been is the same as what will be
Ecclesiastes1:10 Is there a matter where it is said
Ecclesiastes1:11 There is not a remembrance of the former things
Ecclesiastes1:12 I
Ecclesiastes1:13 I set my heart to seek and to investigate with wisdom everything that is done under heaven. It is a burdensome task that God has given to humanity to be occupied with.
Ecclesiastes1:14 I have seen everything that is done under the sun; and behold
Ecclesiastes1:15 What is bent cannot be straightened
Ecclesiastes1:16 I spoke in my heart saying
Ecclesiastes1:17 And I set my heart to know wisdom and to know the folly of ideas
Ecclesiastes1:18 For in an abundance of wisdom is an abundance of frustration
Ecclesiastes2:1 And I said in my heart
Ecclesiastes2:2 And I said of frivolous fun
Ecclesiastes2:3 I investigated how to cheer up my body with wine
Ecclesiastes2:4 I accomplished great works. I built houses for myself and planted vineyards for myself.
Ecclesiastes2:5 I made myself royal gardens and parks
Ecclesiastes2:6 I made myself pools of water to irrigate the forests of growing trees.
Ecclesiastes2:7 I even bought male and female slaves
Ecclesiastes2:8 I also gathered for myself silver and gold and precious possessions of kings and provinces. I acquired male and female singers
Ecclesiastes2:9 I became great and surpassed anyone who had been in Jerusalem before me. Also my wisdom remained with me.
Ecclesiastes2:10 Everything my eyes wanted
Ecclesiastes2:11 Then I turned to all the work that my hands had done
Ecclesiastes2:12 Then I turned to observe wisdom
Ecclesiastes2:13 Then I saw that there is more benefit in wisdom than in foolishness
Ecclesiastes2:14 A wise man has eyes in his head
Ecclesiastes2:15 Then I said in my heart
Ecclesiastes2:16 For there is no lasting remembrance of the wise or the fool
Ecclesiastes2:17 So I began to hate life
Ecclesiastes2:18 I despised all the toil in which I labored under the sun
Ecclesiastes2:19 And who knows if this man will be wise or foolish? Yet he will gain power over all for which I have toiled and used my wisdom under the sun. This also is vanity.
Ecclesiastes2:20 So I turned to give my heart up to despair concerning all the toil of my labors under the sun.
Ecclesiastes2:21 For there is a person who labors with wisdom
Ecclesiastes2:22 What becomes a man for all his labor and striving in his heart with which he toils under the sun?
Ecclesiastes2:23 All his days are sorrowful
Ecclesiastes2:24 There is nothing better for a person than to eat and drink
Ecclesiastes2:25 For who can even eat or have enjoyment more than I?
Ecclesiastes2:26 To the person who is pleasing before Him
Ecclesiastes3:1 There is a specific time for everything
Ecclesiastes3:2 a time to be born
Ecclesiastes3:3 a time to kill
Ecclesiastes3:4 a time to weep
Ecclesiastes3:5 a time to cast down stones
Ecclesiastes3:6 a time to seek out
Ecclesiastes3:7 a time to tear up
Ecclesiastes3:8 a time to love
Ecclesiastes3:9 What benefit does the worker have in his toil?
Ecclesiastes3:10 I have seen the task that God has given to people to be concerned with.
Ecclesiastes3:11 He has made everything beautiful in its time. He has also put obscurity in their hearts so that people do not come to know the work that God has done from the beginning to the end.
Ecclesiastes3:12 I know that there is nothing better for people than to be glad and do good in their life
Ecclesiastes3:13 and also that everyone should eat and drink and experience good in all their labor. This is a gift of God.
Ecclesiastes3:14 I have perceived that everything that God has done will be lasting. And to this there is nothing to be added
Ecclesiastes3:15 That which is
Ecclesiastes3:16 Moreover
Ecclesiastes3:17 I thought in my heart: “God will bring judgment to the righteous and the wicked
Ecclesiastes3:18 Then I thought in my heart
Ecclesiastes3:19 For there is an event for people and an event for animals
Ecclesiastes3:20 All go to one place; everything came from dust
Ecclesiastes3:21 Who has experienced the spirit of people ascending above
Ecclesiastes3:22 So I saw that there is nothing better than that people take joy in their labor
Ecclesiastes4:1 Again I turned and observed all types of oppression that are done under the sun: Behold
Ecclesiastes4:2 And I thought that the dead
Ecclesiastes4:3 But better than both is the one who has not been
Ecclesiastes4:4 Then I observed that all labor and skill of work come one person’s envy of another. This also is vanity and a chasing after wind.
Ecclesiastes4:5 The fool folds his hands for slothfulness
Ecclesiastes4:6 Better is a full hand of quietness than handfuls of toil and a chasing after wind.
Ecclesiastes4:7 Again
Ecclesiastes4:8 There is a person who is alone
Ecclesiastes4:9 Two are better than one
Ecclesiastes4:10 For if they fall
Ecclesiastes4:11 Again
Ecclesiastes4:12 And if someone might overpower another
Ecclesiastes4:13 Better is a poor and wise youth than an old and foolish king
Ecclesiastes4:14 He went from prison to a throne
Ecclesiastes4:15 Then I observed all those who live and walk under the sun
Ecclesiastes4:16 There was no limit to all the people
Ecclesiastes5:1 Guard your steps when you enter the house of God. Drawing near to obey is better than the sacrifice of fools
Ecclesiastes5:2 Do not be quick to speak with your mouth
Ecclesiastes5:3 For a dream comes when there is a great burden
Ecclesiastes5:4 When you make a vow to God
Ecclesiastes5:5 Better it is that you not make a vow
Ecclesiastes5:6 Do not let your mouth cause you to sin
Ecclesiastes5:7 For when there is an abundance of dreams and futilities
Ecclesiastes5:8 If you observe in a district the oppression of the poor and violation of justice and righteousness
Ecclesiastes5:9 But this is an advantage to the land in every way: a king committed to a cultivated field.
Ecclesiastes5:10 The one who loves silver will not be satisfied with silver; the one who loves money desires revenues in abundance. This also is vanity.
Ecclesiastes5:11 When there is an increase of good things
Ecclesiastes5:12 Sweet is the sleep of one who works hard
Ecclesiastes5:13 There is a grave misery that I have observed under the sun: when riches were kept by an owner to his hurt
Ecclesiastes5:14 and those riches were lost in an unfortunate business deal; and although he has a son
Ecclesiastes5:15 As he came from his mother’s womb
Ecclesiastes5:16 And this also is a grave misery: As he entered
Ecclesiastes5:17 Moreover
Ecclesiastes5:18 This is what I have observed to be good and pleasing: to eat and drink and see benefit in all one’s labor with which he toils under the sun the few days of his life
Ecclesiastes5:19 Likewise
Ecclesiastes5:20 For he will seldom remember the passing days of his life
Ecclesiastes6:1 There is a misfortune that I have observed under the sun
Ecclesiastes6:2 a man to whom God gives wealth
Ecclesiastes6:3 If a man is a father to a hundred children and lives many years
Ecclesiastes6:4 for the child comes in vanity
Ecclesiastes6:5 Moreover
Ecclesiastes6:6 Though the man may live a thousand years twice over
Ecclesiastes6:7 All the labor of a person is for his mouth
Ecclesiastes6:8 For what benefit is there for the wise over the fool? And what more does the poor man know who walks before others?
Ecclesiastes6:9 Better to be content with the sight of eyes than to have a wandering appetite. This is vanity and a chasing after wind.
Ecclesiastes6:10 What has already been has been named and determined
Ecclesiastes6:11 For when there are many debating words
Ecclesiastes6:12 For who knows what is good for a person in life during the number of the days of his vain life
Ecclesiastes7:1 A good name is better than precious oil
Ecclesiastes7:2 It is better to go to a house of mourning for a funeral than to go to a house of feasting for a celebration; for this is the destiny of all humanity; and the living should consider this in their heart.
Ecclesiastes7:3 Grief is better than laughter
Ecclesiastes7:4 The heart of those who are wise is in the house of mourning
Ecclesiastes7:5 It is better for a man to hear the rebuke of the wise than to listen to the song of fools.
Ecclesiastes7:6 For like the crackling sound of thorns under a pot
Ecclesiastes7:7 Surely oppression brings confusion to the wise person
Ecclesiastes7:8 The end of a matter is better than its beginning
Ecclesiastes7:9 Do not be quick in your spirit to be angry
Ecclesiastes7:10 Do not say
Ecclesiastes7:11 Wisdom is good as an inheritance
Ecclesiastes7:12 For the protective shadow of wisdom is like the protective shadow of silver
Ecclesiastes7:13 Consider the work of God: Who can make straight what He made crooked?
Ecclesiastes7:14 In the day of prosperity be joyful
Ecclesiastes7:15 I have seen everything in my vain life: There is the righteous person who dies in his righteousness; and there is the wicked person who prolongs his life in his wickedness.
Ecclesiastes7:16 Do not be excessively righteous
Ecclesiastes7:17 Do not be overly wicked
Ecclesiastes7:18 It is good that you take hold of these two sayings
Ecclesiastes7:19 Wisdom gives the wise person more strength than ten rulers who are in the city.
Ecclesiastes7:20 Surely there is not a righteous man on earth who only does good and refrains from sin.
Ecclesiastes7:21 Moreover
Ecclesiastes7:22 Your heart knows that many times you yourself have spoken a curse against others.
Ecclesiastes7:23 All these matters I have tested by wisdom. I said
Ecclesiastes7:24 That which exists is far off and so deep that it is incomprehensible. Who can discover it?
Ecclesiastes7:25 And I turned my heart to know and seek and search out wisdom in the scheme of things and to know the wickedness of folly
Ecclesiastes7:26 And I find more bitter than death a woman whose heart is like snares and nets
Ecclesiastes7:27 “This is what I have observed
Ecclesiastes7:28 which my soul has continued to seek out
Ecclesiastes7:29 Observe
Ecclesiastes8:1 Who is like a wise man? And who knows the interpretation of a matter? A person’s wisdom makes his face shine
Ecclesiastes8:2 Obey the king’s command because of your oath to God.
Ecclesiastes8:3 Do not be hasty to leave his presence. Do not take a stand in an unpleasant matter
Ecclesiastes8:4 For the word of the king is powerful
Ecclesiastes8:5 The one who keeps the king’s command will know no evil thing
Ecclesiastes8:6 For every matter there is a proper time and just way
Ecclesiastes8:7 For he does not know what is to be
Ecclesiastes8:8 No person has power over the wind to restrain it
Ecclesiastes8:9 All this I observed while applying my heart to all that is done under the sun
Ecclesiastes8:10 Then I saw the wicked approaching. They would enter and leave the holy temple
Ecclesiastes8:11 Because the sentence against an evil deed is not executed speedily
Ecclesiastes8:12 Though one who sins may do evil a hundred times and prolongs his life
Ecclesiastes8:13 But it will not be well with the wicked
Ecclesiastes8:14 There is another vanity that takes place on earth: there are righteous people who are treated according to the deeds of the wicked
Ecclesiastes8:15 So I commend enjoyment
Ecclesiastes8:16 When I applied my heart to know wisdom
Ecclesiastes8:17 then I observed all the work of God
Ecclesiastes9:1 All this I took to heart
Ecclesiastes9:2 The same fate comes to all; there is one fate for the righteous and the wicked
Ecclesiastes9:3 This is an evil in all that is done under the sun
Ecclesiastes9:4 But for the one who is joined with all the living there is hope
Ecclesiastes9:5 For the living know that they will die
Ecclesiastes9:6 Their love
Ecclesiastes9:7 Go
Ecclesiastes9:8 Let your garments always be white
Ecclesiastes9:9 Enjoy life with the wife whom you love
Ecclesiastes9:10 Whatever your hands find to do
Ecclesiastes9:11 Again I observed under the sun that the race is not to the swift
Ecclesiastes9:12 For no one knows his appointed time. Like fish caught in a cruel net
Ecclesiastes9:13 I have observed that this also is wisdom under the sun
Ecclesiastes9:14 There was a small city with a few men in it. A great king came against it
Ecclesiastes9:15 But a poor wise man was found in it
Ecclesiastes9:16 So I said
Ecclesiastes9:17 The words of the wise heard in quietness are better than the shouting of a ruler among fools.
Ecclesiastes9:18 Wisdom is better than weapons of war
Ecclesiastes10:1 Dead flies cause the perfumer’s ointment to give off a stench; so a little folly outweighs wisdom and honor.
Ecclesiastes10:2 The heart of the wise moves him to the right for protection
Ecclesiastes10:3 Even when a fool walks along the road
Ecclesiastes10:4 If the angry spirit of the ruler rises against you
Ecclesiastes10:5 There is an evil that I have observed under the sun
Ecclesiastes10:6 the fool is present in many high places of position
Ecclesiastes10:7 I have observed slaves on horses
Ecclesiastes10:8 The one who digs a pit will fall into it
Ecclesiastes10:9 The one who quarries stones will be hurt by them
Ecclesiastes10:10 If the iron is blunt
Ecclesiastes10:11 If a serpent bites before it is charmed

Ecclesiastes10:13 The beginning of the words from his mouth are folly; so the end of his words are evil madness.

Ecclesiastes10:15 The toil of fools wearies them
Ecclesiastes10:16 Woe to you
Ecclesiastes10:17 Happy are you
Ecclesiastes10:18 By slothfulness the rafters cave in
Ecclesiastes10:19 A feast is made for laughter
Ecclesiastes10:20 Do not curse the king

SongOfSongs7:11 Come
SongOfSongs7:12 let us go out early to the vineyards

Isaiah1:1 The vision of Isaiah the son of Amoz
Isaiah1:2 Hear
Isaiah1:3 the ox knows his owner
Isaiah1:4 Alas
Isaiah1:5 Why should you be beaten again
Isaiah1:6 From the sole of the foot even to the head there is no soundness in it. Wounds
Isaiah1:7 Your country is desolate
Isaiah1:8 The daughter of Zion is left like a shelter in a vineyard
Isaiah1:9 Unless the Lord of Hosts had left to us a very small remnant
Isaiah1:10 Hear the word of the Lord
Isaiah1:11 For what purpose is the multitude of your sacrifices to Me? says the Lord. I am full of the burnt offerings of rams
Isaiah1:12 When you come to appear before Me
Isaiah1:13 Bring no more vain offerings; incense is an abomination to Me. New Moons
Isaiah1:14 My soul hates your New Moons and your appointed feasts; they are a burden to Me; I am weary of bearing them.
Isaiah1:15 When you reach out your hands
Isaiah1:16 Wash yourselves
Isaiah1:17 learn to do good; seek justice
Isaiah1:18 Come now
Isaiah1:19 If you are willing and obedient
Isaiah1:20 but if you refuse and rebel
Isaiah1:21 How the faithful city has become a prostitute! She was full of justice; righteousness lodged in her
Isaiah1:22 Your silver has become dross
Isaiah1:23 Your princes are rebellious and companions of thieves; everyone loves a bribe and follows after rewards. They do not defend the fatherless
Isaiah1:24 Therefore the Lord
Isaiah1:25 And I will turn My hand against you
Isaiah1:26 I will restore your judges as at the first
Isaiah1:27 Zion shall be redeemed with justice and her converts with righteousness.
Isaiah1:28 But the destruction of the transgressors and sinners shall be together
Isaiah1:29 For they shall be ashamed of the oaks which you have desired
Isaiah1:30 For you shall be as an oak whose leaf fades
Isaiah1:31 The strong shall be as tinder
Isaiah2:1 The word that Isaiah the son of Amoz saw concerning Judah and Jerusalem.
Isaiah2:2 In the last days
Isaiah2:3 Many people shall go and say
Isaiah2:4 He shall judge among the nations
Isaiah2:5 O house of Jacob
Isaiah2:6 For You have forsaken Your people
Isaiah2:7 Their land also is full of silver and gold
Isaiah2:8 Their land also is full of idols; they worship the work of their own hands
Isaiah2:9 And the common man bows down
Isaiah2:10 Enter into the rock
Isaiah2:11 The lofty looks of man shall be humbled
Isaiah2:12 For the day of the Lord of Hosts shall be upon everything that is proud and lofty
Isaiah2:13 and it will be upon all the cedars of Lebanon that are high and lifted up
Isaiah2:14 and upon all the high mountains
Isaiah2:15 and upon all the high towers
Isaiah2:16 and upon all the ships of Tarshish
Isaiah2:17 The loftiness of man shall be humbled
Isaiah2:18 the idols He shall utterly abolish.
Isaiah2:19 They shall go into the holes of the rocks
Isaiah2:20 In that day a man shall cast away his idols of silver and his idols of gold
Isaiah2:21 to enter the caverns of the rocks
Isaiah2:22 Cease regarding man
Isaiah3:1 For behold
Isaiah3:2 the mighty man and the man of war
Isaiah3:3 the captain of fifty and the honorable man
Isaiah3:4 I will give youths to be their princes
Isaiah3:5 The people shall be oppressed
Isaiah3:6 When a man shall take hold of his brother in the house of his father saying
Isaiah3:7 in that day he shall swear saying
Isaiah3:8 For Jerusalem is ruined
Isaiah3:9 The expression of their countenance witnesses against them
Isaiah3:10 Say to the righteous that it shall be well with them
Isaiah3:11 Woe to the wicked! It shall be ill with him
Isaiah3:12 As for My people
Isaiah3:13 The Lord stands up to plead and stands to judge the people.
Isaiah3:14 The Lord will enter into judgment with the elders and the princes of His people: For you have eaten up the vineyard; the spoil of the poor is in your houses.
Isaiah3:15 What do you mean that you beat My people to pieces and grind the faces of the poor? says the Lord God of Hosts.
Isaiah3:16 Moreover the Lord says: Because the daughters of Zion are haughty and walk with outstretched necks and wanton eyes
Isaiah3:17 therefore

Isaiah3:19 the chains
Isaiah3:20 the headdresses

Isaiah3:25 Your men shall fall by the sword
Isaiah3:26 Her gates shall lament and mourn
Isaiah4:1 And in that day seven women shall take hold of one man
Isaiah4:2 In that day the branch of the Lord shall be beautiful and glorious
Isaiah4:3 He who is left in Zion and he who remains in Jerusalem shall be called holy
Isaiah4:4 When the Lord has washed away the filth of the daughters of Zion and has purged the blood of Jerusalem from the midst by the spirit of justice and by the spirit of burning
Isaiah4:5 then the Lord will create upon every dwelling place of Mount Zion
Isaiah4:6 There shall be a tabernacle for a shadow in the daytime from the heat
Isaiah5:1 Now I will sing to my well-beloved a song of my beloved concerning His vineyard: My well-beloved has a vineyard in a very fruitful hill.
Isaiah5:2 And He fenced it
Isaiah5:3 Now
Isaiah5:4 What more could have been done to My vineyard that I have not done in it? Why
Isaiah5:5 So now I will tell you what I will do to My vineyard: I will take away its hedge
Isaiah5:6 And I will lay it waste: It shall not be pruned or dug
Isaiah5:7 For the vineyard of the Lord of Hosts is the house of Israel
Isaiah5:8 Woe to those who join house to house
Isaiah5:9 In my ears the Lord of Hosts said: Truly
Isaiah5:10 For ten acres of vineyard shall yield one bath
Isaiah5:11 Woe to those who rise up early in the morning that they may pursue strong drink; who continue late in the evening until wine inflames them!
Isaiah5:12 The lyre and the harp
Isaiah5:13 Therefore My people go into captivity because they have no knowledge; and their honorable men are famished
Isaiah5:14 Therefore Sheol has enlarged itself and opened its mouth without measure; so their glory
Isaiah5:15 The common man shall be brought down
Isaiah5:16 But the Lord of Hosts shall be exalted in judgment
Isaiah5:17 Then the lambs shall feed in their pasture
Isaiah5:18 Woe to those who draw iniquity with cords of falsehood and sin as if with a cart rope
Isaiah5:19 who say
Isaiah5:20 Woe to those who call evil good
Isaiah5:21 Woe to those who are wise in their own eyes and prudent in their own sight!
Isaiah5:22 Woe to those who are mighty to drink wine
Isaiah5:23 who justify the wicked for a reward
Isaiah5:24 Therefore
Isaiah5:25 Therefore the anger of the Lord burns against His people
Isaiah5:26 He will lift up a banner to the nations from afar and will hiss at them from the ends of the earth; and behold
Isaiah5:27 No one shall be weary or stumble among them; no one shall slumber or sleep; neither shall the girdle of their loins be loosed
Isaiah5:28 their arrows are sharp and all their bows bent; their horses’ hooves will seem like flint
Isaiah5:29 Their roaring shall be like a lion
Isaiah5:30 In that day they shall roar against them like the roaring of the sea. And if one looks to the land— behold
Isaiah6:1 In the year that King Uzziah died I saw the Lord sitting on a throne
Isaiah6:2 Above it stood the seraphim. Each one had six wings. With two he covered his face
Isaiah6:3 One cried to another and said: “Holy
Isaiah6:4 The posts of the door moved at the voice of him who cried
Isaiah6:5 And I said: “Woe is me! For I am undone because I am a man of unclean lips
Isaiah6:6 Then one of the seraphim flew to me with a live coal which he had taken with the tongs from off the altar in his hand.
Isaiah6:7 And he laid it on my mouth
Isaiah6:8 Also I heard the voice of the Lord saying
Isaiah6:9 He said
Isaiah6:10 Make the heart of this people dull
Isaiah6:11 Then I said
Isaiah6:12 and the Lord has removed men far away
Isaiah6:13 But yet in it shall be one-tenth
Isaiah7:1 In the days of Ahaz the son of Jotham
Isaiah7:2 It was said to the house of David
Isaiah7:3 Then the Lord said to Isaiah: Go out now to meet Ahaz
Isaiah7:4 and say to him
Isaiah7:5 because Aram
Isaiah7:6 Let us go up against Judah
Isaiah7:7 Thus says the Lord God: It shall not stand
Isaiah7:8 For the head of Aram is Damascus
Isaiah7:9 The head of Ephraim is Samaria
Isaiah7:10 Moreover the Lord spoke again to Ahaz
Isaiah7:11 Ask for a sign from the Lord your God. Make it either as deep as Sheol or as high as heaven.
Isaiah7:12 But Ahaz said
Isaiah7:13 Then he said
Isaiah7:14 Therefore the Lord Himself shall give you a sign: Behold
Isaiah7:15 Curds and honey he shall eat at the time that he knows enough to refuse the evil and choose the good.
Isaiah7:16 For before the child shall know to refuse the evil and choose the good
Isaiah7:17 The Lord shall bring upon you
Isaiah7:18 In that day the Lord shall whistle for the fly that is in the uttermost part of the rivers of Egypt
Isaiah7:19 They shall come
Isaiah7:20 In that same day the Lord shall shave with a hired razor
Isaiah7:21 In that day a man shall nourish a young cow and two sheep
Isaiah7:22 and because of the abundance of milk that they give
Isaiah7:23 In that day every place where there were one thousand vines
Isaiah7:24 With arrows and with bows shall men come there because all the land shall become briers and thorns.
Isaiah7:25 On all hills that used to be cultivated with the hoe

Isaiah9:1 Nevertheless there shall be no more gloom for her who was in anguish. In the former time He contemptuously treated the land of Zebulun and the land of Naphtali
Isaiah9:2 The people who walked in darkness have seen a great light; those who dwell in the land of the shadow of death
Isaiah9:3 You have multiplied the nation and increased the joy; they rejoice before You according to the joy of harvest and as men rejoice when they divide the spoil.
Isaiah9:4 For You have broken the yoke of his burden and the bar of his shoulder
Isaiah9:5 For all the sandals of the tramping warriors and all the garments rolled in blood shall be burned as fuel for the fire.
Isaiah9:6 For unto us a child is born
Isaiah9:7 Of the increase of his government and peace there shall be no end
Isaiah9:8 The Lord sent a word against Jacob
Isaiah9:9 All the people shall know
Isaiah9:10 “The bricks are fallen down
Isaiah9:11 Therefore
Isaiah9:12 The Arameans shall be before and the Philistines behind; and they shall devour Israel with open jaws. For all this His anger is not turned away
Isaiah9:13 For the people do not turn to Him who struck them
Isaiah9:14 Therefore the Lord will cut off from Israel head and tail
Isaiah9:15 The elder and honorable man
Isaiah9:16 For the leaders of this people cause them to err
Isaiah9:17 Therefore the Lord shall have no joy in their young men
Isaiah9:18 For wickedness burns as a fire; it shall devour the briers and thorns
Isaiah9:19 Through the wrath of the Lord of Hosts is the land burned up
Isaiah9:20 They shall snatch on the right hand
Isaiah9:21 Manasseh consumes Ephraim
Isaiah10:1 Woe to those who decree unrighteous decrees and who write unjust judgments which they have prescribed
Isaiah10:2 to turn aside the needy from justice and to take away the right from the poor of My people
Isaiah10:3 And what will you do in the day of punishment and in the desolation which shall come from afar? To whom will you flee for help
Isaiah10:4 Nothing remains but to bow down among the prisoners
Isaiah10:5 Woe to Assyria
Isaiah10:6 I will send him against an ungodly nation
Isaiah10:7 However he does not so intend
Isaiah10:8 For he says
Isaiah10:9 Is not Kalno as Carchemish? Is not Hamath as Arpad? Is not Samaria as Damascus?
Isaiah10:10 As my hand has found the kingdoms of the idols whose graven images were greater than those of Jerusalem and Samaria
Isaiah10:11 shall I not
Isaiah10:12 When the Lord has performed all His work on Mount Zion and on Jerusalem
Isaiah10:13 For he says: “By the strength of my hand and by my wisdom
Isaiah10:14 And my hand has found as a nest the riches of the people
Isaiah10:15 Shall the axe boast itself against him who hews with it? Or shall the saw magnify itself against him who wields it? That is as if the rod wields itself against those who lift it up
Isaiah10:16 Therefore the Lord
Isaiah10:17 The light of Israel shall be a fire
Isaiah10:18 and shall consume the glory of his forest
Isaiah10:19 The rest of the trees of his forest shall be so few that a child may write them down.
Isaiah10:20 In that day the remnant of Israel and those who have escaped of the house of Jacob shall never again depend on him who struck them
Isaiah10:21 The remnant shall return
Isaiah10:22 For though your people
Isaiah10:23 For the Lord God of Hosts shall make a complete destruction
Isaiah10:24 Therefore
Isaiah10:25 For yet a very little while
Isaiah10:26 The Lord of Hosts shall stir up a scourge for him according to the slaughter of Midian at the rock of Oreb; and as His rod was over the sea
Isaiah10:27 In that day his burden shall be taken away from off your shoulder
Isaiah10:28 He has come against Aiath
Isaiah10:29 They are gone over the pass
Isaiah10:30 Lift up your voice
Isaiah10:31 Madmenah has fled
Isaiah10:32 Yet today he shall remain at Nob; he shakes his fist against the mount of the daughter of Zion
Isaiah10:33 Behold
Isaiah10:34 He shall cut down the thickets of the forest with iron

Isaiah11:9 They shall not hurt or destroy in all My holy mountain

Isaiah13:7 Therefore all hands shall be faint
Isaiah13:8 and they shall be afraid. Pangs and sorrows shall take hold of them; they shall be in pain as a woman who travails; they shall be amazed one at another; their faces shall be as flames.
Isaiah13:9 Behold
Isaiah13:10 For the stars of heaven and their constellations shall not give their light; the sun shall be dark when it rises
Isaiah13:11 I will punish the world for its evil
Isaiah13:12 I will make a man more rare than fine gold
Isaiah13:13 Therefore
Isaiah13:14 It shall be as the chased roe

Isaiah13:19 Babylon

Isaiah14:2 The people shall take them and bring them to their place
Isaiah14:3 In the day the Lord gives you rest from your sorrow
Isaiah14:4 you shall take up this proverb against the king of Babylon
Isaiah14:5 The Lord has broken the staff of the wicked
Isaiah14:6 he who struck the people in wrath with unceasing strokes
Isaiah14:7 The whole earth is at rest and is quiet; they break forth into singing.
Isaiah14:8 Indeed
Isaiah14:9 Sheol from beneath is moved for you to meet you at your coming; it stirs up the dead for you
Isaiah14:10 They all shall speak and say to you: “Have you also become as weak as we have? Have you become like us?”
Isaiah14:11 Your pomp is brought down to Sheol
Isaiah14:12 How are you fallen from heaven
Isaiah14:13 For you have said in your heart
Isaiah14:14 I will ascend above the heights of the clouds
Isaiah14:15 Yet you shall be brought down to Sheol
Isaiah14:16 Those who see you shall stare at you and ponder over you: “Is this the man who made the earth to tremble and shook kingdoms
Isaiah14:17 who made the world as a wilderness and destroyed its cities
Isaiah14:18 All the kings of the nations
Isaiah14:19 but you are cast out of your grave like an abominable branch and clothed with those who are slain
Isaiah14:20 You shall not be joined with them in burial because you have destroyed your land and slain your people. The seed of evildoers shall never be renowned.
Isaiah14:21 Prepare a place of slaughter for his children for the iniquity of their fathers; they must not rise nor possess the land
Isaiah14:22 For I will rise up against them
Isaiah14:23 I will also make it a possession for the hedgehog and pools of water
Isaiah14:24 The Lord of Hosts has sworn
Isaiah14:25 that I will break the Assyrian in My land
Isaiah14:26 This is the purpose that is purposed on the whole earth

Isaiah15:4 Heshbon shall cry

Isaiah15:6 for the waters of Nimrim shall be desolate; surely the grass is withered away; the grass fails
Isaiah15:7 Therefore the abundance they have gained
Isaiah15:8 For the cry has gone all around the borders of Moab; its wailing to Eglaim
Isaiah15:9 For the waters of Dimon shall be full of blood; for I will bring more woes on Dimon

Isaiah16:2 For it shall be that as a wandering bird cast out of the nest
Isaiah16:3 “Take counsel
Isaiah16:4 Let my outcasts dwell with you

Isaiah16:6 We have heard of the pride of Moab; he is very proud
Isaiah16:7 Therefore Moab shall wail for Moab; everyone shall wail. For the loss of raisin cakes of Kir Hareseth you shall mourn; surely they are destroyed.
Isaiah16:8 For the fields of Heshbon languish
Isaiah16:9 Therefore I will weep bitterly for Jazer
Isaiah16:10 Gladness and joy are taken away out of the plentiful field
Isaiah16:11 Therefore my heart shall sound like a harp for Moab
Isaiah16:12 When it is seen that Moab is weary on the high place
Isaiah16:13 This is the word that the Lord has spoken earlier concerning Moab.
Isaiah16:14 But now the Lord has spoken

Isaiah17:2 The cities of Aroer are forsaken; they shall be for flocks
Isaiah17:3 The fortress also shall cease from Ephraim

Isaiah17:5 It shall be as when the harvester gathers the grain and reaps the ears with his arm
Isaiah17:6 Yet gleaning grapes shall be left in it

Isaiah17:8 He shall not look to the altars

Isaiah17:11 in the day that you plant it

Isaiah17:13 The nations rumble like the rumbling of many waters; but God shall rebuke them

Isaiah18:1 Woe to the land vibrating with wings
Isaiah18:2 which sends ambassadors by the sea
Isaiah18:3 All you inhabitants of the world
Isaiah18:4 For so the Lord said to me: I will look from My dwelling place quietly
Isaiah18:5 For before the harvest
Isaiah18:6 They shall be left together for the fowl of the mountains and for the beasts of the earth; and the fowl shall spend the summer feeding on them

Isaiah19:1 The oracle of Egypt. Behold
Isaiah19:2 I will set the Egyptians against the Egyptians
Isaiah19:3 The spirit of Egypt shall be discouraged in their midst
Isaiah19:4 The Egyptians I will give over into the hand of a cruel lord
Isaiah19:5 The waters from the sea shall dry up
Isaiah19:6 The canals shall emit a stench; the streams of Egypt shall diminish and dry up; the reeds and rushes shall wither.
Isaiah19:7 The papyrus reeds by the brooks
Isaiah19:8 The fishermen also shall mourn
Isaiah19:9 Moreover those who work with fine flax and those who weave white cloth shall be humiliated;
Isaiah19:10 and the pillars of Egypt shall be crushed. All the hired workers shall be grieved in soul.
Isaiah19:11 Surely the princes of Zoan are fools; the counsel of the wise counselors of Pharaoh has become stupid. How can you say to Pharaoh
Isaiah19:12 Where are they? Where are your wise men? Let them tell you now
Isaiah19:13 The princes of Zoan have become fools; the princes of Memphis are deceived; they have also seduced Egypt
Isaiah19:14 The Lord has mingled a perverse spirit in her; so they have caused Egypt to err in her every work
Isaiah19:15 Nor shall there be any work for Egypt
Isaiah19:16 In that day Egypt shall become like women. And it shall be afraid and be in dread because of the shaking of the hand of the Lord of Hosts which He is about to shake over it.
Isaiah19:17 The land of Judah shall become a terror to Egypt. Everyone to whom it is mentioned shall be in dread of it
Isaiah19:18 In that day five cities in the land of Egypt shall speak the language of Canaan and swear to the Lord of Hosts. One shall be called the City of Destruction.
Isaiah19:19 In that day there shall be an altar to the Lord in the midst of the land of Egypt
Isaiah19:20 It shall be for a sign and for a witness to the Lord of Hosts in the land of Egypt. For they shall cry to the Lord because of the oppressors
Isaiah19:21 The Lord shall be known to Egypt
Isaiah19:22 The Lord shall strike Egypt. He shall strike and heal it. Then they shall return to the Lord
Isaiah19:23 In that day there shall be a highway out of Egypt to Assyria
Isaiah19:24 In that day Israel shall be the third group with Egypt and Assyria
Isaiah19:25 whom the Lord of Hosts has blessed

Isaiah22:1 The oracle of the Valley of Vision. What ails you now
Isaiah22:2 you who were full of noise
Isaiah22:3 All your rulers have fled together; they are captured by the archers. All of you who were found were taken captive together
Isaiah22:4 Therefore
Isaiah22:5 For it is a day of trouble and of treading down and of perplexity by the Lord God of Hosts in the Valley of Vision
Isaiah22:6 Elam took up the quiver with chariots of men and horsemen
Isaiah22:7 Your choicest valleys shall be full of chariots
Isaiah22:8 And He shall remove the protection of Judah. You shall depend in that day on the weapons of the house of the forest;
Isaiah22:9 you have seen also that the breaches of the City of David are many; and you gathered together the waters of the lower pool.
Isaiah22:10 You have numbered the houses of Jerusalem
Isaiah22:11 You also made a reservoir between the two walls for the water of the old pool. But you have not looked to its maker
Isaiah22:12 In that day the Lord God of Hosts called you to weeping and mourning
Isaiah22:13 Behold
Isaiah22:14 It was revealed in my hearing by the Lord of Hosts: Surely this iniquity shall not be forgiven you until you die
Isaiah22:15 Thus says the Lord God of Hosts: Come
Isaiah22:16 What right do you have here? And whom do you have here that you have dug out for yourself a tomb here
Isaiah22:17 Behold
Isaiah22:18 He will surely violently turn and toss you like a ball into a large country; there you shall die
Isaiah22:19 I will drive you from your office and pull you down from your station.
Isaiah22:20 In that day I will call My servant Eliakim the son of Hilkiah.
Isaiah22:21 And I will clothe him with your robe and tie him securely with your sash. And I will entrust him with your authority
Isaiah22:22 The key of the house of David I will lay on his shoulder. Then he shall open
Isaiah22:23 I will fasten him as a nail in a firm place
Isaiah22:24 They shall hang on him all the glory of his father’s house
Isaiah22:25 In that day

Isaiah24:8 The joy of tambourines ceases
Isaiah24:9 They shall not drink wine with song; strong drink shall be bitter to those who drink it.

Isaiah24:11 There is an outcry for wine in the streets
Isaiah24:12 Desolation is left in the city
Isaiah24:13 For thus it shall be in the midst of the earth among the peoples
Isaiah24:14 They lift up their voices; they sing for the majesty of the Lord; they cry aloud from the west.
Isaiah24:15 Therefore glorify the Lord in the east

Isaiah28:26 For his God instructs and teaches him appropriately.
Isaiah28:27 For the dill is not threshed with a threshing instrument
Isaiah28:28 Grain for bread is crushed; so he does not continue to thresh it forever

Isaiah30:16 and you said

Isaiah30:23 Then He shall give you rain for the seed which you shall sow in the ground and bread of the increase of the earth. And it shall be rich and plentiful. On that day your cattle shall feed in large pastures.
Isaiah30:24 The oxen likewise and the young donkeys that work the ground shall eat cured fodder
Isaiah30:25 There shall be on every high mountain and on every high hill rivers and streams of waters in the day of the great slaughter
Isaiah30:26 Moreover the light of the moon shall be as the light of the sun

Isaiah42:13 The Lord shall go forth like a mighty man; He shall stir up zeal like a man of war. He shall cry out
Isaiah42:14 I have for a long time held My peace; I have been still and refrained Myself. Now I will cry like a travailing woman; I will destroy and devour at once.
Isaiah42:15 I will lay waste mountains and hills and dry up all their vegetation
Isaiah42:16 I will bring the blind by a way that they did not know; I will lead them in paths that they have not known. I will make darkness light before them and crooked things straight. These things I will do for them and not forsake them.

Isaiah66:1 Thus says the Lord: Heaven is My throne
Isaiah66:2 For My hand made all those things
Isaiah66:3 He who kills a bull is as if he kills a man; he who sacrifices a lamb

Isaiah66:6 A voice of noise from the city
Isaiah66:7 Before she was in labor
Isaiah66:8 Who has heard such a thing? Who has seen such things? Shall the earth be made to give birth in one day? Shall a nation be born at once? For as soon as Zion was in labor
Isaiah66:9 Shall I bring to the point of birth and not cause delivery? says the Lord. Shall I who cause delivery shut the womb? says your God.
Isaiah66:10 Rejoice with Jerusalem and be glad for her
Isaiah66:11 that you may feed and be satisfied with her consoling breasts
Isaiah66:12 For thus says the Lord: Behold
Isaiah66:13 As one whom his mother comforts
Isaiah66:14 Then you shall see this
Isaiah66:15 For behold
Isaiah66:16 For by fire and by His sword on all flesh
Isaiah66:17 Those who sanctify themselves and purify themselves in the gardens behind one tree in the midst
Isaiah66:18 For I know their works and their thoughts. The time shall come to gather all nations and tongues. And they shall come and see My glory.
Isaiah66:19 I will set a sign among them
Isaiah66:20 They shall bring all your brothers out of all nations as an offering to the Lord on horses
Isaiah66:21 I will also take some of them for priests and for Levites
Isaiah66:22 For as the new heavens and the new earth which I will make shall remain before Me
Isaiah66:23 From one New Moon to another
Isaiah66:24 And they shall go forth and look on the corpses of the men who have transgressed against Me. For neither shall their worm die nor shall their fire be quenched. And they shall be an abhorrence to all flesh.
Jeremiah1:1 The words of Jeremiah the son of Hilkiah
Jeremiah1:2 to whom the word of the Lord came in the days of Josiah the son of Amon
Jeremiah1:3 It came also in the days of Jehoiakim the son of Josiah
Jeremiah1:4 Now the word of the Lord came to me
Jeremiah1:5 “Before I formed you in the womb I knew you; and before you were born I sanctified you
Jeremiah1:6 Then I said
Jeremiah1:7 But the Lord said to me
Jeremiah1:8 Do not be afraid of their faces
Jeremiah1:9 Then the Lord put forth His hand and touched my mouth. And the Lord said to me
Jeremiah1:10 Behold
Jeremiah1:11 Moreover the word of the Lord came to me
Jeremiah1:12 Then the Lord said to me
Jeremiah1:13 The word of the Lord came to me the second time
Jeremiah1:14 Then the Lord said to me: Out of the north calamity will break forth on all the inhabitants of the land.
Jeremiah1:15 For behold
Jeremiah1:16 I will utter My judgments against them concerning all their wickedness
Jeremiah1:17 You therefore gird up your loins and arise
Jeremiah1:18 For behold
Jeremiah1:19 They will fight against you
Jeremiah2:1 Moreover the word of the Lord came to me
Jeremiah2:2 Go and proclaim in the ears of Jerusalem
Jeremiah2:3 Israel is holy to the Lord
Jeremiah2:4 Hear the word of the Lord
Jeremiah2:5 Thus says the Lord: What iniquity have your fathers found in Me
Jeremiah2:6 They did not say
Jeremiah2:7 And I brought you into a plentiful country to eat its fruit and its goodness. But when you entered
Jeremiah2:8 The priests did not say
Jeremiah2:9 Therefore I will yet contend with you
Jeremiah2:10 For pass over the coastlands of Cyprus and see
Jeremiah2:11 Has a nation changed their gods
Jeremiah2:12 Be astonished at this
Jeremiah2:13 For My people have committed two evils. They have forsaken Me
Jeremiah2:14 Is Israel a servant? Is he a homeborn slave? Why has he been plundered?
Jeremiah2:15 The young lions roared at him and roared loudly
Jeremiah2:16 Also the men of Memphis and Tahpanhes have shaved the crown of your head.
Jeremiah2:17 Have you not brought this on yourself
Jeremiah2:18 And now what are you doing in the way of Egypt
Jeremiah2:19 Your own wickedness will correct you
Jeremiah2:20 For long ago I have broken your yoke and burst your bonds; and you said
Jeremiah2:21 Yet I had planted you a noble vine
Jeremiah2:22 For though you wash yourself with lye
Jeremiah2:23 How can you say
Jeremiah2:24 a wild donkey used to the wilderness that sniffs the wind in her passion. In the time of her heat
Jeremiah2:25 Withhold your foot from being unshod and your throat from thirst. But you said
Jeremiah2:26 As the thief is ashamed when he is found
Jeremiah2:27 say to a tree
Jeremiah2:28 But where are your gods that you have made for yourself? Let them arise
Jeremiah2:29 Why will you plead with Me? You all have transgressed against Me
Jeremiah2:30 In vain I have smitten your children; they received no correction. Your own sword has devoured your prophets like a destroying lion.
Jeremiah2:31 O generation
Jeremiah2:32 Can a virgin forget her ornaments
Jeremiah2:33 You prepare your way very well to seek love. Therefore you have also taught the wicked women your ways.
Jeremiah2:34 Also in your skirts is found the lifeblood of the innocent poor
Jeremiah2:35 you say
Jeremiah2:36 Why do you go about so much to change your way? You also shall be ashamed of Egypt
Jeremiah2:37 Indeed
Jeremiah3:1 If a man divorces his wife
Jeremiah3:2 Lift up your eyes to the high places
Jeremiah3:3 Therefore the showers have been withheld
Jeremiah3:4 Shall you not from this time cry to Me
Jeremiah3:5 Shall He reserve His anger forever? Shall He keep it to the end?” Behold
Jeremiah3:6 The Lord said also to me in the days of Josiah the king: Have you seen that which backsliding Israel has done? She has gone up on every high mountain and under every green tree
Jeremiah3:7 I said after she had done all these things
Jeremiah3:8 I saw that for all the adulteries of backsliding Israel
Jeremiah3:9 Through the lightness of her harlotry she defiled the land and committed adultery with stones and with trees.
Jeremiah3:10 Yet for all this
Jeremiah3:11 The Lord said to me: Backsliding Israel has proved herself more righteous than treacherous Judah.
Jeremiah3:12 Go and proclaim these words toward the north
Jeremiah3:13 Only acknowledge your iniquity
Jeremiah3:14 Return
Jeremiah3:15 And I will give you shepherds according to My heart
Jeremiah3:16 It shall come to pass when you are multiplied and increased in the land in those days
Jeremiah3:17 At that time they will call Jerusalem
Jeremiah3:18 In those days the house of Judah will walk with the house of Israel
Jeremiah3:19 But I said: How can I put you among My sons and give you a pleasant land
Jeremiah3:20 Surely as a wife treacherously departs from her husband
Jeremiah3:21 A voice was heard upon the high places
Jeremiah3:22 Return
Jeremiah3:23 Truly in vain is salvation hoped for from the hills
Jeremiah3:24 For shame has devoured the labor of our fathers from our youth
Jeremiah3:25 We lie down in our shame
Jeremiah4:1 If you will return
Jeremiah4:2 You shall swear
Jeremiah4:3 For thus says the Lord to the men of Judah and Jerusalem: Break up your fallow ground
Jeremiah4:4 Circumcise yourselves to the Lord
Jeremiah4:5 Declare in Judah and proclaim in Jerusalem
Jeremiah4:6 Set up the standard toward Zion. Seek refuge
Jeremiah4:7 The lion has come up from his thicket
Jeremiah4:8 For this gird yourself with sackcloth
Jeremiah4:9 It shall come to pass in that day
Jeremiah4:10 Then I said
Jeremiah4:11 At that time it will be said to this people and to Jerusalem: A dry wind of the high places in the wilderness toward the daughter of My people
Jeremiah4:12 even a full wind from those places
Jeremiah4:13 Behold
Jeremiah4:14 O Jerusalem
Jeremiah4:15 For a voice declares from Dan
Jeremiah4:16 “Report it to the nations
Jeremiah4:17 As watchmen of a field
Jeremiah4:18 Your way and your deeds have procured these things to you. This is your wickedness. How bitter it is! How it touches your heart!”
Jeremiah4:19 My soul
Jeremiah4:20 Destruction upon destruction is proclaimed
Jeremiah4:21 How long shall I see the standard and hear the sound of the trumpet?
Jeremiah4:22 “For My people are foolish
Jeremiah4:23 I looked on the earth
Jeremiah4:24 I looked on the mountains
Jeremiah4:25 I looked
Jeremiah4:26 I looked
Jeremiah4:27 For thus says the Lord: The whole land shall be desolate. Yet I will not make a full end.
Jeremiah4:28 For this the earth shall mourn
Jeremiah4:29 The whole city shall flee for the noise of the horsemen and bowmen. They shall go into thickets and climb up upon the rocks. Every city shall be forsaken
Jeremiah4:30 When you are devastated
