Jeremiah5:1 Run to and fro through the streets of Jerusalem
Jeremiah5:2 Though they say
Jeremiah5:3 O  Lord
Jeremiah5:4 Therefore I said
Jeremiah5:5 I will go to the great men and will speak to them
Jeremiah5:6 Therefore a lion out of the forest will slay them
Jeremiah5:7 How shall I pardon you for this? Your children have forsaken Me and sworn by those who are not gods. When I had fed them to the full
Jeremiah5:8 They were as fed horses in the morning; everyone neighed after his neighbor’s wife.
Jeremiah5:9 Shall I not punish for these things? says the Lord
Jeremiah5:10 Go up upon her walls and destroy
Jeremiah5:11 For the house of Israel and the house of Judah have dealt very treacherously against Me
Jeremiah5:12 They have lied about the Lord
Jeremiah5:13 And the prophets shall become wind
Jeremiah5:14 Therefore thus says the Lord God of Hosts: Because you speak this word
Jeremiah5:15 Behold
Jeremiah5:16 Their quiver is as an open sepulcher
Jeremiah5:17 They will eat up your harvest and your bread which your sons and your daughters should eat. They will eat up your flocks and your herds; they will eat up your vines and your fig trees; they will impoverish your fenced cities
Jeremiah5:18 Nevertheless in those days
Jeremiah5:19 It shall come to pass when they say
Jeremiah5:20 Declare this in the house of Jacob and proclaim it in Judah
Jeremiah5:21 Hear this now
Jeremiah5:22 Do you not fear Me? says the Lord. Will you not tremble at My presence? For I have placed the sand for the boundary of the sea by a perpetual decree so that it cannot pass over it. And though the waves toss themselves
Jeremiah5:23 But this people has a revolting and a rebellious heart; they have revolted and gone aside.
Jeremiah5:24 They do not say in their heart
Jeremiah5:25 Your iniquities have turned away these things
Jeremiah5:26 For among My people are found wicked men. They lie in wait
Jeremiah5:27 As a cage is full of birds
Jeremiah5:28 They are fat
Jeremiah5:29 Shall I not punish them for these things? says the Lord. Shall not My soul be avenged on such a nation as this?
Jeremiah5:30 An appalling and horrible thing has been committed in the land.
Jeremiah5:31 The prophets prophesy falsely
Jeremiah6:1 O sons of Benjamin
Jeremiah6:2 I have likened the daughter of Zion to a comely and delicate woman.
Jeremiah6:3 The shepherds with their flocks will come to her. They will pitch their tents against her all around. They will pasture each in his place.
Jeremiah6:4 “Prepare war against her; arise
Jeremiah6:5 “Arise
Jeremiah6:6 For thus says the Lord of Hosts: Hew down trees and build a mound against Jerusalem. This is the city to be punished. She is full of oppression in her midst.
Jeremiah6:7 As a fountain casts out her waters
Jeremiah6:8 Be instructed
Jeremiah6:9 Thus says the Lord of Hosts: They will thoroughly glean the remnant of Israel as a vine. Turn back your hand as a grape gatherer into the baskets.
Jeremiah6:10 To whom shall I speak and give warning that they may hear? Behold
Jeremiah6:11 Therefore I am full of the fury of the Lord; I am weary with holding in. I will pour it out upon the children in the street and upon the assembly of young men together; for even the husband with the wife shall be taken
Jeremiah6:12 Their houses shall be turned to others
Jeremiah6:13 For from the least of them even to the greatest of them
Jeremiah6:14 They have healed also the brokenness of the daughter of My people superficially
Jeremiah6:15 Were they ashamed when they had committed abomination? They were not at all ashamed
Jeremiah6:16 Thus says the Lord: Stand in the ways and see
Jeremiah6:17 Also I set watchmen over you
Jeremiah6:18 Therefore hear
Jeremiah6:19 Hear
Jeremiah6:20 To what purpose does incense from Sheba come to Me
Jeremiah6:21 Therefore thus says the Lord: Behold
Jeremiah6:22 Thus says the Lord: Behold
Jeremiah6:23 They will lay hold on bow and spear; they are cruel and have no mercy. Their voice roars like the sea
Jeremiah6:24 “We have heard the report of it; our hands grow feeble. Anguish has taken hold of us
Jeremiah6:25 Do not go out into the field
Jeremiah6:26 O daughter of my people
Jeremiah6:27 I have set you as an assayer and fortress among My people that you may know and try their way.
Jeremiah6:28 They all are stubborn rebels walking about practicing slander. They are bronze and iron; they all are corrupters.
Jeremiah6:29 The bellows blow fiercely
Jeremiah6:30 Men will call them rejected silver
Jeremiah7:1 The word that came to Jeremiah from the Lord
Jeremiah7:2 Stand in the gate of the house of the Lord and proclaim there this word: Hear the word of the Lord
Jeremiah7:3 Thus says the Lord of Hosts
Jeremiah7:4 Do not trust in lying words
Jeremiah7:5 For if you thoroughly amend your ways and your deeds
Jeremiah7:6 if you do not oppress the stranger
Jeremiah7:7 then I will cause you to dwell in this place
Jeremiah7:8 Behold
Jeremiah7:9 Shall you steal
Jeremiah7:10 and come and stand before Me in this house
Jeremiah7:11 Has this house
Jeremiah7:12 But go now to My place which was in Shiloh
Jeremiah7:13 And now
Jeremiah7:14 therefore I will do to this house
Jeremiah7:15 I will cast you out of My sight as I have cast out all your brothers
Jeremiah7:16 As for you
Jeremiah7:17 Do you not see what they do in the cities of Judah and in the streets of Jerusalem?
Jeremiah7:18 The children gather wood
Jeremiah7:19 Do they provoke Me to anger? says the Lord. Do they not provoke themselves to the shame of their own faces?
Jeremiah7:20 Therefore thus says the Lord God: Behold
Jeremiah7:21 Thus says the Lord of Hosts
Jeremiah7:22 For I spoke not to your fathers
Jeremiah7:23 But this thing I commanded them
Jeremiah7:24 But they did not listen
Jeremiah7:25 Since the day that your fathers came out of the land of Egypt to this day
Jeremiah7:26 Yet they did not listen to Me
Jeremiah7:27 Therefore you will speak all these words to them. But they will not listen to you. You will also call to them
Jeremiah7:28 You shall say to them: This is a nation that does not obey the voice of the Lord their God or receive correction. Truth has perished
Jeremiah7:29 Cut off your hair
Jeremiah7:30 For the Judahites have done evil in My sight
Jeremiah7:31 They have built the high places of Topheth
Jeremiah7:32 Therefore
Jeremiah7:33 The corpses of this people shall be food for the fowl of the heaven and for the beasts of the earth; and no one will frighten them away.
Jeremiah7:34 Then I will cause to cease from the cities of Judah and from the streets of Jerusalem the voice of laughter and the voice of gladness
Jeremiah8:1 At that time
Jeremiah8:2 And they will spread them before the sun and the moon and all the host of heaven
Jeremiah8:3 Death will be chosen rather than life by all the rest of those who remain of this evil family
Jeremiah8:4 Moreover you shall say to them
Jeremiah8:5 Why then is this people of Jerusalem turned away by a perpetual backsliding? They hold fast to deceit; they refuse to return.
Jeremiah8:6 I listened and heard
Jeremiah8:7 Indeed
Jeremiah8:8 How do you say
Jeremiah8:9 The wise men are ashamed; they are dismayed and caught. Behold
Jeremiah8:10 Therefore I will give their wives to others
Jeremiah8:11 For they have healed the brokenness of the daughter of My people superficially
Jeremiah8:12 Were they ashamed when they had committed abomination? They were not at all ashamed
Jeremiah8:13 I will surely consume them
Jeremiah8:14 Why do we sit still? Assemble yourselves
Jeremiah8:15 We looked for peace
Jeremiah8:16 The snorting of his horses was heard from Dan; the whole land trembled at the sound of the neighing of his strong ones. For they have come and have devoured the land and all that is in it
Jeremiah8:17 Behold
Jeremiah8:18 My sorrow is beyond healing; my heart is faint within me.
Jeremiah8:19 Behold
Jeremiah8:20 “The harvest is past
Jeremiah8:21 For the brokenness of the daughter of my people am I broken; I mourn; dismay has taken hold of me.
Jeremiah8:22 Is there no balm in Gilead? Is there no physician there? Why then has not the health of the daughter of my people recovered?
Jeremiah9:1 Oh
Jeremiah9:2 Oh
Jeremiah9:3 They bend their tongues like their bow; lies and not truth prevail upon the land; for they proceed from evil to evil
Jeremiah9:4 Let everyone be on guard against his neighbor
Jeremiah9:5 Everyone deceives his neighbor and does not speak the truth. They have taught their tongue to speak lies; they weary themselves in committing iniquity.
Jeremiah9:6 Your habitation is in the midst of deceit; through deceit they refuse to know Me
Jeremiah9:7 Therefore thus says the Lord of Hosts: Behold
Jeremiah9:8 Their tongue is a deadly arrow; it speaks deceit. One speaks peaceably to his neighbor with his mouth
Jeremiah9:9 Shall I not punish them for these things? says the Lord. Shall not My soul be avenged on such a nation as this?
Jeremiah9:10 For the mountains I will take up a weeping and wailing
Jeremiah9:11 I will make Jerusalem a heap of ruins and a den of jackals; and I will make the cities of Judah desolate
Jeremiah9:12 Who is the wise man who may understand this? And who is he to whom the mouth of the Lord has spoken
Jeremiah9:13 And the Lord said: Because they have forsaken My law which I set before them
Jeremiah9:14 but have walked after the imagination of their own heart and after the Baals
Jeremiah9:15 Therefore thus says the Lord of Hosts
Jeremiah9:16 I will scatter them also among the nations
Jeremiah9:17 Thus says the Lord of Hosts: Consider and call for the mourning women
Jeremiah9:18 And let them make haste and take up a wailing for us
Jeremiah9:19 For a voice of wailing is heard out of Zion
Jeremiah9:20 Yet hear the word of the Lord
Jeremiah9:21 “For death has come up into our windows; and has entered our palaces
Jeremiah9:22 Speak
Jeremiah9:23 Thus says the Lord: Let not the wise man glory in his wisdom
Jeremiah9:24 but let him who glories glory in this
Jeremiah9:25 Behold
Jeremiah9:26 Egypt
Jeremiah10:1 Hear the word which the Lord speaks to you
Jeremiah10:2 Thus says the Lord: Do not learn the way of the nations; do not be terrified at the signs of heaven
Jeremiah10:3 For the customs of the people are vain; for with the axe one cuts a tree out of the forest
Jeremiah10:4 They deck it with silver and with gold; they fasten it with nails and with hammers so that it may not move.
Jeremiah10:5 They are as a scarecrow in a cucumber field
Jeremiah10:6 There is no one like You
Jeremiah10:7 Who would not fear You
Jeremiah10:8 But they are altogether unthinking and foolish; the wooden idol is worthless instruction.
Jeremiah10:9 Silver spread into plates is brought from Tarshish
Jeremiah10:10 But the Lord is the true God; He is the living God and an everlasting King. At His wrath the earth trembles
Jeremiah10:11 Thus you shall say to them: The gods that have not made the heavens and the earth
Jeremiah10:12 He has made the earth by His power. He has established the world by His wisdom and has stretched out the heavens by His discretion.
Jeremiah10:13 When He utters His voice
Jeremiah10:14 Every man is stupid
Jeremiah10:15 They are vain and a work of mockery; in the time of their punishment they shall perish.
Jeremiah10:16 The portion of Jacob is not like them
Jeremiah10:17 Gather up your wares out of the land
Jeremiah10:18 For thus says the Lord: Behold
Jeremiah10:19 Woe is me because of my brokenness! My wound is grievous. But I said
Jeremiah10:20 My tabernacle is devastated
Jeremiah10:21 For the shepherds have become unthinking and have not sought the Lord. Therefore they have not prospered
Jeremiah10:22 Behold
Jeremiah10:23 O  Lord
Jeremiah10:24 O  Lord
Jeremiah10:25 Pour out Your fury upon the nations that do not know You and upon the families that do not call on Your name; for they have eaten up Jacob and devoured him
Jeremiah11:1 The word that came to Jeremiah from the Lord
Jeremiah11:2 Hear the words of this covenant
Jeremiah11:3 And say you to them
Jeremiah11:4 which I commanded your fathers in the day that I brought them out of the land of Egypt
Jeremiah11:5 that I may perform the oath which I have sworn to your fathers
Jeremiah11:6 Then the Lord said to me: Proclaim all these words in the cities of Judah and in the streets of Jerusalem: saying
Jeremiah11:7 For I earnestly warned your fathers in the day that I brought them up out of the land of Egypt
Jeremiah11:8 Yet they did not obey or incline their ear
Jeremiah11:9 The Lord said to me: A conspiracy has been found among the men of Judah and among the inhabitants of Jerusalem.
Jeremiah11:10 They have turned back to the iniquities of their forefathers who refused to hear My words. And they have gone after other gods to serve them. The house of Israel and the house of Judah have broken My covenant which I made with their fathers.
Jeremiah11:11 Therefore thus says the Lord
Jeremiah11:12 Then the cities of Judah and inhabitants of Jerusalem will go and cry to the gods to whom they offer incense. But they will not save them at all in the time of their trouble.
Jeremiah11:13 For according to the number of your cities are your gods
Jeremiah11:14 Therefore do not pray for this people
Jeremiah11:15 What right has My beloved in My house
Jeremiah11:16 The Lord called your name
Jeremiah11:17 For the Lord of Hosts
Jeremiah11:18 The Lord has made it known to me and I knew it; then You showed me their deeds.
Jeremiah11:19 But I was like a gentle lamb that is brought to the slaughter
Jeremiah11:20 But
Jeremiah11:21 Therefore thus says the Lord of the men of Anathoth who seek your life
Jeremiah11:22 Therefore thus says the Lord of Hosts: Behold
Jeremiah11:23 And there shall be no remnant of them
Jeremiah12:1 Righteous are You
Jeremiah12:2 You have planted them; indeed
Jeremiah12:3 But You
Jeremiah12:4 How long shall the land mourn and the herbs of every field wither? For the wickedness of those who dwell in it
Jeremiah12:5 If you have run with the footmen
Jeremiah12:6 For even your brothers and the household of your father
Jeremiah12:7 I have forsaken My house
Jeremiah12:8 My inheritance is to Me as a lion in the forest. She cries out against Me; therefore I have hated her.
Jeremiah12:9 My inheritance is to Me as a speckled vulture; the vultures all around are against her. Come
Jeremiah12:10 Many shepherds have destroyed My vineyard; they have trodden My portion underfoot; they have made My pleasant portion a desolate wilderness.
Jeremiah12:11 They have made it desolate
Jeremiah12:12 The destroyers have come upon all high places through the wilderness
Jeremiah12:13 They have sown wheat
Jeremiah12:14 Thus says the Lord: Against all My evil neighbors who touch the inheritance which I have caused My people Israel to inherit
Jeremiah12:15 It shall come to pass that after I have plucked them out
Jeremiah12:16 It shall come to pass if they will diligently learn the ways of My people
Jeremiah12:17 But if they will not obey
Jeremiah13:1 Thus says the Lord to me
Jeremiah13:2 So I bought a waistband according to the word of the Lord and put it on my loins.
Jeremiah13:3 The word of the Lord came to me the second time
Jeremiah13:4 “Take the waistband that you have
Jeremiah13:5 So I went and hid it by the Euphrates
Jeremiah13:6 After many days the Lord said to me
Jeremiah13:7 Then I went to the Euphrates
Jeremiah13:8 Then the word of the Lord came to me:
Jeremiah13:9 Thus says the Lord: After this manner I will destroy the pride of Judah and the great pride of Jerusalem.
Jeremiah13:10 This evil people
Jeremiah13:11 For as the waistband cleaves to the loins of a man
Jeremiah13:12 Therefore you shall speak to them this word: Thus says the Lord God of Israel: Every bottle shall be filled with wine. And when they say to you
Jeremiah13:13 then you shall say to them: Thus says the Lord: Behold
Jeremiah13:14 I will dash them against one another
Jeremiah13:15 Hear and give heed; do not be proud
Jeremiah13:16 Give glory to the Lord your God
Jeremiah13:17 But if you will not listen to it
Jeremiah13:18 Say to the king and to the queen mother: “Humble yourselves
Jeremiah13:19 The cities of the Negev will be shut up
Jeremiah13:20 Lift up your eyes and see those who come from the north. Where is the flock that was given to you
Jeremiah13:21 What will you say when He appoints over you those you yourself had taught to be companions to you? Shall not sorrows take hold of you
Jeremiah13:22 And if you say in your heart
Jeremiah13:23 Can the Cushite change his skin or the leopard his spots? Then you also can do good
Jeremiah13:24 Therefore I will scatter them as the stubble that passes away by the wind of the wilderness.
Jeremiah13:25 This is your lot
Jeremiah13:26 Therefore I Myself have uncovered your skirts over your face so that your shame may appear.
Jeremiah13:27 I have seen your adulteries and your lustful neighings
Jeremiah14:1 The word of the Lord which came to Jeremiah concerning the drought:
Jeremiah14:2 Judah mourns
Jeremiah14:3 Their nobles have sent their servants for water; they came to the cisterns and found no water. They returned with their vessels empty; they were ashamed and humiliated
Jeremiah14:4 Because the ground is chapped
Jeremiah14:5 Indeed
Jeremiah14:6 The wild donkeys stand in the high places; they pant for air like jackals; their eyes fail because there is no grass.
Jeremiah14:7 O  Lord
Jeremiah14:8 O the Hope of Israel
Jeremiah14:9 Why should You be as a man dismayed
Jeremiah14:10 Thus says the Lord to this people: Even so they have loved to wander; they have not restrained their feet. Therefore
Jeremiah14:11 Then the Lord said to me: Do not pray for the good of this people.
Jeremiah14:12 When they fast
Jeremiah14:13 Yet I said
Jeremiah14:14 Then the Lord said to me: The prophets prophesy lies in My name. I have not sent them
Jeremiah14:15 Therefore thus says the Lord concerning the prophets who prophesy in My name though I did not send them
Jeremiah14:16 The people to whom they prophesy will be cast out in the streets of Jerusalem because of the famine and the sword. And they will have no one to bury them
Jeremiah14:17 Therefore you will say this word to them: Let my eyes run down with tears night and day
Jeremiah14:18 If I go out into the field
Jeremiah14:19 Have You utterly rejected Judah? Has Your soul loathed Zion? Why have You stricken us so that there is no healing for us? We looked for peace
Jeremiah14:20 We acknowledge
Jeremiah14:21 Do not abhor us. For Your name’s sake
Jeremiah14:22 Are there any among the idols of the nations that can cause rain? Or can the heavens give showers? Is it not You
Jeremiah15:1 Then the Lord said to me: Even though Moses and Samuel were to stand before Me
Jeremiah15:2 It shall come to pass if they say to you
Jeremiah15:3 I will appoint over them four kinds of disaster
Jeremiah15:4 I will cause them to be removed into all kingdoms of the earth
Jeremiah15:5 For who shall have pity on you
Jeremiah15:6 You who have forsaken Me
Jeremiah15:7 I will winnow them with a winnowing fork in the gates of the land; I will bereave them of children. I will destroy My people
Jeremiah15:8 Their widows will increase before Me more than the sand of the seas; I will bring against them
Jeremiah15:9 She who has borne seven sons languishes; she has given up the spirit; her sun has gone down while it was yet day; she has been ashamed and humiliated. And the rest of them I will deliver to the sword before their enemies
Jeremiah15:10 Woe is me
Jeremiah15:11 The Lord said: Truly I will set you free for good purposes. Truly I will cause the enemy to entreat you in the time of evil and in the time of affliction.
Jeremiah15:12 Can anyone break the northern iron or the bronze?
Jeremiah15:13 Your wealth and your treasures I will give to the destroyer without price
Jeremiah15:14 I will make you to pass with your enemies into a land which you do not know; for a fire is kindled in My anger
Jeremiah15:15 O  Lord
Jeremiah15:16 Your words were found and I ate them. And Your word became to me the joy and rejoicing of my heart
Jeremiah15:17 I did not sit in the assembly of mockers
Jeremiah15:18 Why is my pain perpetual and my wound incurable
Jeremiah15:19 Therefore thus says the Lord: If you return
Jeremiah15:20 I will make you to this people a fortified bronze wall; and they shall fight against you
Jeremiah15:21 I will deliver you out of the hand of the wicked
Jeremiah16:1 The word of the Lord came also to me
Jeremiah16:2 You shall not take a wife
Jeremiah16:3 For thus says the Lord concerning the sons and concerning the daughters who are born in this place
Jeremiah16:4 They will die of deadly diseases. They will not be lamented
Jeremiah16:5 For thus says the Lord: Do not enter a house of mourning
Jeremiah16:6 Both the great and the small will die in this land. They will not be buried; neither will men lament for them
Jeremiah16:7 Neither will men tear themselves for them in mourning
Jeremiah16:8 Also you shall not go into the house of feasting
Jeremiah16:9 For thus says the Lord of Hosts

Jeremiah17:1 The sin of Judah is written with an iron pen and with a diamond point; it is engraved on the tablet of their heart and on the horns of their altars;
Jeremiah17:2 as they remember their children
Jeremiah17:3 O My mountain in the field
Jeremiah17:4 You
Jeremiah17:5 Thus says the Lord: Cursed is the man who trusts in man and makes flesh his strength
Jeremiah17:6 For he will be like a bush in the desert and will not see when good comes
Jeremiah17:7 Blessed is the man who trusts in the Lord
Jeremiah17:8 For he shall be as a tree planted by the waters
Jeremiah17:9 The heart is more deceitful than all things and desperately wicked; who can understand it?
Jeremiah17:10 I
Jeremiah17:11 As the partridge sits on eggs which it has not laid
Jeremiah17:12 A glorious high throne from the beginning is the place of our sanctuary.

Jeremiah17:14 Heal me
Jeremiah17:15 Behold
Jeremiah17:16 As for me
Jeremiah17:17 Do not be a terror to me; You are my hope in the day of disaster.
Jeremiah17:18 Let those who persecute me be humiliated

Jeremiah17:20 And say to them: Hear the word of the Lord
Jeremiah17:21 Thus says the Lord: Take heed to yourselves
Jeremiah17:22 You shall not carry a load out of your houses on the Sabbath day
Jeremiah17:23 But they did not obey or incline their ears

Jeremiah17:26 They will come from the cities of Judah and from the places about Jerusalem
Jeremiah17:27 But if you will not listen to Me to sanctify the Sabbath day and not to bear a load

Jeremiah22:17 But your eyes and your heart are intent only on your covetousness and on shedding innocent blood and on oppression and violence.

Jeremiah23:1 Woe to the shepherds who destroy and scatter the sheep of My pasture! says the Lord.
Jeremiah23:2 Therefore thus says the Lord God of Israel against the shepherds that feed My people: You have scattered My flock and driven them away and have not visited them. Behold
Jeremiah23:3 I will gather the remnant of My flock out of all countries wherever I have driven them and bring them again to their folds
Jeremiah23:4 I will also set up shepherds over them who will feed them; and they will fear no more
Jeremiah23:5 Behold
Jeremiah23:6 In his days Judah will be saved
Jeremiah23:7 Therefore
Jeremiah23:8 but
Jeremiah23:9 My heart is broken within me
Jeremiah23:10 For the land is full of adulterers; for the land mourns because of the curse. The pleasant places of the wilderness have dried up. And their course is evil and their might is not right.
Jeremiah23:11 For both prophet and priest are profane; indeed
Jeremiah23:12 Therefore their way will be as slippery ways to them; they shall be driven into the darkness and fall in it; for I will bring disaster upon them
Jeremiah23:13 In addition
Jeremiah23:14 I have seen also among the prophets of Jerusalem a horrible thing. They commit adultery and walk in lies. They also strengthen the hands of evildoers
Jeremiah23:15 Therefore thus says the Lord of Hosts concerning the prophets: “Behold
Jeremiah23:16 Thus says the Lord of Hosts: Do not listen to the words of the prophets who prophesy to you. They lead you into vanity; they speak a vision of their own heart and not out of the mouth of the Lord.
Jeremiah23:17 They still say to those who despise Me
Jeremiah23:18 For who has stood in the counsel of the Lord and has perceived and heard His word? Who has given heed to His word and listened to it?
Jeremiah23:19 Behold
Jeremiah23:20 The anger of the Lord will not turn back until He has executed and performed the thoughts of His heart. In the latter days you will understand it perfectly.
Jeremiah23:21 I have not sent these prophets
Jeremiah23:22 But if they had stood in My counsel and had caused My people to hear My words
Jeremiah23:23 Am I a God who is near
Jeremiah23:24 Can a man hide himself in secret places so that I do not see him? says the Lord. Do I not fill heaven and earth? says the Lord.
Jeremiah23:25 I have heard what the prophets have said who prophesy lies in My name
Jeremiah23:26 How long shall this be in the heart of the prophets who prophesy lies? Indeed
Jeremiah23:27 who plan to cause My people to forget My name by their dreams
Jeremiah23:28 The prophet who has a dream
Jeremiah23:29 Is not My word like fire
Jeremiah23:30 Therefore
Jeremiah23:31 Behold
Jeremiah23:32 Behold
Jeremiah23:33 When this people or the prophet or a priest asks you
Jeremiah23:34 As for the prophet and the priest and the people who say
Jeremiah23:35 Thus each of you will say to his neighbor and to his brother
Jeremiah23:36 And the oracle of the Lord you shall mention no more
Jeremiah23:37 Thus you will say to that prophet
Jeremiah23:38 But if you say
Jeremiah23:39 Therefore
Jeremiah23:40 And I will bring an everlasting reproach upon you and perpetual shame which shall not be forgotten.

Jeremiah30:1 The word that came to Jeremiah from the Lord
Jeremiah30:2 Thus says the Lord God of Israel: Write all the words that I have spoken to you in a book.
Jeremiah30:3 For behold
Jeremiah30:4 These are the words that the Lord spoke concerning Israel and Judah.
Jeremiah30:5 For thus says the Lord: I have heard a sound of trembling
Jeremiah30:6 Ask now
Jeremiah30:7 Alas! for that day is great
Jeremiah30:8 For it shall come to pass in that day
Jeremiah30:9 But they shall serve the Lord their God and David their king
Jeremiah30:10 Therefore do not fear
Jeremiah30:11 For I am with you
Jeremiah30:12 For thus says the Lord: Your bruise is incurable and your wound is severe.
Jeremiah30:13 There is no one to plead your cause that you may be bound up. You have no healing medicines.
Jeremiah30:14 All your lovers have forgotten you; they do not seek you; for I have wounded you with the wound of an enemy
Jeremiah30:15 Why do you cry because of your affliction? Your sorrow is incurable. Because of the multitude of your iniquities
Jeremiah30:16 Therefore all who devour you will be devoured; and all your adversaries
Jeremiah30:17 For I will restore health to you
Jeremiah30:18 Thus says the Lord: Behold
Jeremiah30:19 Out of them will proceed thanksgiving and the voice of those who make merry; and I will multiply them
Jeremiah30:20 Their children also will be as before
Jeremiah30:21 Their leader shall be one of them
Jeremiah30:22 You shall be My people
Jeremiah30:23 Behold
Jeremiah30:24 The fierce anger of the Lord shall not return until He has done it and until He has performed the intentions of His heart. In the latter days you will understand it.
Jeremiah31:1 At that time
Jeremiah31:2 Thus says the Lord: The people who survived the sword found grace in the wilderness
Jeremiah31:3 The Lord has appeared to him from afar
Jeremiah31:4 Again I will build you and you will be built
Jeremiah31:5 You will yet plant vines on the mountains of Samaria; the planters will plant and will enjoy them.
Jeremiah31:6 For there will be a day when the watchmen on the hills of Ephraim will proclaim
Jeremiah31:7 For thus says the Lord: Sing with gladness for Jacob
Jeremiah31:8 Behold
Jeremiah31:9 They will come with weeping
Jeremiah31:10 Hear the word of the Lord
Jeremiah31:11 For the Lord has redeemed Jacob and ransomed him from the hand of him who was stronger than he.
Jeremiah31:12 Therefore they will come and sing in the height of Zion
Jeremiah31:13 Then the virgin shall rejoice in the dance
Jeremiah31:14 I will satiate the soul of the priests with abundance
Jeremiah31:15 Thus says the Lord: A voice is heard in Ramah
Jeremiah31:16 Thus says the Lord: Keep your voice from weeping and your eyes from tears; for your work shall be rewarded
Jeremiah31:17 There is hope for your future

Jeremiah31:19 Surely after I turned back
Jeremiah31:20 Is Ephraim My dear son? Is he a pleasant child? For since I spoke against him
Jeremiah31:21 Set up road marks
Jeremiah31:22 How long will you go about
Jeremiah31:23 Thus says the Lord of Hosts
Jeremiah31:24 And Judah and all its cities will dwell there
Jeremiah31:25 For I satiate the weary souls and I replenish every languishing soul.
Jeremiah31:26 Upon this I awoke and looked
Jeremiah31:27 Behold
Jeremiah31:28 It shall come to pass that as I have watched over them to pluck up
Jeremiah31:29 In those days they will say no more: “The fathers have eaten sour grapes
Jeremiah31:30 But everyone will die for his own iniquity. Every man that eats the sour grape
Jeremiah31:31 Behold
Jeremiah31:32 It will not be according to the covenant that I made with their fathers in the day that I took them by the hand to bring them out of the land of Egypt
Jeremiah31:33 But this shall be the covenant that I will make with the house of Israel after those days
Jeremiah31:34 They shall teach no more every man his neighbor and every man his brother
Jeremiah31:35 Thus says the Lord
Jeremiah31:36 If those ordinances depart from before Me

Jeremiah31:38 Behold

Jeremiah32:2 For then the king of Babylon’s army besieged Jerusalem
Jeremiah32:3 For Zedekiah king of Judah had shut him up
Jeremiah32:4 and Zedekiah king of Judah will not escape out of the hand of the Chaldeans
Jeremiah32:5 And he will lead Zedekiah to Babylon
Jeremiah32:6 So Jeremiah said
Jeremiah32:7 Behold
Jeremiah32:8 So Hanamel my uncle’s son came to me in the court of the prison according to the word of the Lord and said to me
Jeremiah32:9 I bought the field of Hanamel my uncle’s son
Jeremiah32:10 I signed and sealed the deed
Jeremiah32:11 So I took the deed of purchase
Jeremiah32:12 And I gave the deed of purchase to Baruch the son of Neriah
Jeremiah32:13 I charged Baruch in their presence
Jeremiah32:14 Thus says the Lord of Hosts
Jeremiah32:15 For thus says the Lord of Hosts
Jeremiah32:16 Now when I had delivered the deed of purchase to Baruch the son of Neriah
Jeremiah32:17 Ah Lord God! Truly
Jeremiah32:18 You show lovingkindness to thousands
Jeremiah32:19 great in counsel and mighty in deed
Jeremiah32:20 You have set signs and wonders in the land of Egypt
Jeremiah32:21 And You have brought your people Israel out of the land of Egypt with signs
Jeremiah32:22 And You have given them this land
Jeremiah32:23 And they entered and possessed it. But they did not obey Your voice or walk in Your law. They have done nothing of all that You commanded them to do; therefore You have caused all this calamity to come upon them.
Jeremiah32:24 Behold
Jeremiah32:25 O Lord God
Jeremiah32:26 Then the word of the Lord came to Jeremiah
Jeremiah32:27 Behold
Jeremiah32:28 Therefore thus says the Lord: Behold
Jeremiah32:29 And the Chaldeans who fight against this city will come and set this city on fire and burn it with the houses
Jeremiah32:30 Indeed the Israelites and the Judahites have only done evil before Me from their youth. For the Israelites have only provoked Me to anger with the work of their hands
Jeremiah32:31 Indeed this city has been to Me a provocation of My anger and My fury from the day that they built it
Jeremiah32:32 because of all the evil of the Israelites and of the Judahites which they have done to provoke Me to anger
Jeremiah32:33 They have turned their back to Me and not their face. Though I taught them
Jeremiah32:34 But they set their abominations in the house which is called by My name
Jeremiah32:35 They built the high places of Baal which are in the Valley of Ben-hinnom to cause their sons and their daughters to pass through the fire to Molech
Jeremiah32:36 Now therefore
Jeremiah32:37 Behold
Jeremiah32:38 And they shall be My people
Jeremiah32:39 And I will give them one heart and one way
Jeremiah32:40 And I will make an everlasting covenant with them that I will not turn away from them
Jeremiah32:41 Indeed
Jeremiah32:42 For thus says the Lord: Just as I have brought all this great calamity upon this people
Jeremiah32:43 Fields will be bought in this land of which you say
Jeremiah32:44 Men will buy fields for money
Jeremiah33:1 Moreover the word of the Lord came to Jeremiah the second time
Jeremiah33:2 Thus says the Lord
Jeremiah33:3 Call to Me
Jeremiah33:4 For thus says the Lord God of Israel concerning the houses of this city
Jeremiah33:5 They come to fight with the Chaldeans
Jeremiah33:6 Behold
Jeremiah33:7 I will restore the fortunes of Judah and the fortunes of Israel
Jeremiah33:8 I will cleanse them from all their iniquity whereby they have sinned against Me. And I will pardon all their iniquities whereby they have sinned and whereby they have transgressed against Me.
Jeremiah33:9 It will be to Me a name of joy
Jeremiah33:10 Thus says the Lord: Again there shall be heard in this place of which you say
Jeremiah33:11 the voice of joy
Jeremiah33:12 Thus says the Lord of Hosts: Again in this place which is desolate
Jeremiah33:13 In the cities of the mountains
Jeremiah33:14 Behold
Jeremiah33:15 In those days and at that time
Jeremiah33:16 In those days Judah will be saved
Jeremiah33:17 For thus says the Lord: David shall never lack a man to sit on the throne of the house of Israel;
Jeremiah33:18 nor shall the Levitical priests lack a man before Me to offer burnt offerings
Jeremiah33:19 The word of the Lord came to Jeremiah
Jeremiah33:20 Thus says the Lord: If you can break My covenant of the day and My covenant of the night so that there should not be day and night in their season
Jeremiah33:21 then also My covenant may be broken with David My servant
Jeremiah33:22 As the host of heaven cannot be numbered nor the sand of the sea measured
Jeremiah33:23 Moreover the word of the Lord came to Jeremiah
Jeremiah33:24 Have you not considered what this people have spoken
Jeremiah33:25 Thus says the Lord: If My covenant for day and night does not stand
Jeremiah33:26 then I would cast away the seed of Jacob and David My servant
Jeremiah34:1 The word which came to Jeremiah from the Lord when Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon and all his army and all the kingdoms of the earth of his dominion and all the people fought against Jerusalem and against all the cities
Jeremiah34:2 Thus says the Lord
Jeremiah34:3 And you will not escape out of his hand
Jeremiah34:4 Yet hear the word of the Lord
Jeremiah34:5 But you will die in peace; and as spices were burned for your fathers
Jeremiah34:6 Then Jeremiah the prophet spoke all these words to Zedekiah king of Judah in Jerusalem
Jeremiah34:7 when the king of Babylon’s army fought against Jerusalem
Jeremiah34:8 This is the word that came to Jeremiah from the Lord after the king Zedekiah had made a covenant with all the people who were in Jerusalem to proclaim liberty to them:
Jeremiah34:9 that every man should let his male slave and every man his female slave
Jeremiah34:10 Now when all the officials and all the people who had entered into the covenant heard that everyone should let his male slave and everyone his female slave go free
Jeremiah34:11 But afterward they turned around and caused the male slaves and the female slaves whom they had set free to return
Jeremiah34:12 Therefore the word of the Lord came to Jeremiah from the Lord
Jeremiah34:13 Thus says the Lord
Jeremiah34:14 “At the end of seven years
Jeremiah34:15 You recently turned and did what was right in My sight by proclaiming liberty
Jeremiah34:16 But then you turned around and profaned My name when every one of you took back his male and female slaves
Jeremiah34:17 Therefore thus says the Lord: You have not obeyed Me in proclaiming liberty
Jeremiah34:18 I will give the men who have transgressed My covenant
Jeremiah34:19 the officials of Judah and the officials of Jerusalem
Jeremiah34:20 I will even give them into the hand of their enemies and into the hand of those who seek their life; and their dead bodies shall be food to the fowl of heaven and to the beasts of the earth.
Jeremiah34:21 Zedekiah king of Judah and his officials I will give into the hand of their enemies and into the hand of those who seek their life and into the hand of the king of Babylon’s army
Jeremiah34:22 Behold

Jeremiah36:1 In the fourth year of Jehoiakim the son of Josiah
Jeremiah36:2 Take a scroll of a book
Jeremiah36:3 It may be that the house of Judah will hear all the disaster which I intend to do to them
Jeremiah36:4 Then Jeremiah called Baruch the son of Neriah. And Baruch wrote on a scroll at the dictation of Jeremiah all the words of the Lord which He had spoken to him.
Jeremiah36:5 Jeremiah commanded Baruch
Jeremiah36:6 Therefore go and read from the scroll that you have written at my dictation the words of the Lord in the hearing of the people in the house of the Lord on a fast day. And also you shall read them in the ears of all Judah who come out of their cities.
Jeremiah36:7 Perhaps their supplication will come before the Lord
Jeremiah36:8 Baruch the son of Neriah did according to all that Jeremiah the prophet commanded him
Jeremiah36:9 In the fifth year of Jehoiakim the son of Josiah
Jeremiah36:10 Then Baruch read from the book the words of Jeremiah in the house of the Lord
Jeremiah36:11 When Micaiah the son of Gemariah
Jeremiah36:12 then he went down to the king’s house
Jeremiah36:13 Then Micaiah declared to them all the words that he had heard when Baruch read the book in the ears of the people.
Jeremiah36:14 Then all the officials sent Jehudi the son of Nethaniah
Jeremiah36:15 They said to him
Jeremiah36:16 When they heard all the words
Jeremiah36:17 They also asked Baruch
Jeremiah36:18 Then Baruch answered them
Jeremiah36:19 Then the officials said to Baruch
Jeremiah36:20 Then they went to the king in the court
Jeremiah36:21 So the king sent Jehudi to fetch the scroll
Jeremiah36:22 Now the king sat in the winter house in the ninth month
Jeremiah36:23 As Jehudi read three or four columns
Jeremiah36:24 Yet they were not afraid
Jeremiah36:25 Although Elnathan and Delaiah and Gemariah entreated the king not to burn the scroll
Jeremiah36:26 But the king commanded Jerahmeel the son of Hammelech
Jeremiah36:27 Then the word of the Lord came to Jeremiah after the king had burned the book and the words which Baruch had written at the mouth of Jeremiah
Jeremiah36:28 Take again another scroll and write in it all the former words that were in the first book
Jeremiah36:29 You shall say to Jehoiakim king of Judah: Thus says the Lord: You have burned this book
Jeremiah36:30 Therefore thus says the Lord concerning Jehoiakim king of Judah: He shall have no one to sit on the throne of David. And his dead body shall be cast out to the heat of the day and the frost of the night.
Jeremiah36:31 I will also punish him and his seed and his servants for their iniquity. And I will bring upon them and upon the inhabitants of Jerusalem and upon the men of Judah all the disaster that I have pronounced against them
Jeremiah36:32 Then Jeremiah took another scroll and gave it to Baruch the scribe

Jeremiah37:2 But neither he nor his servants nor the people of the land listened to the words of the Lord
Jeremiah37:3 But Zedekiah the king sent Jehukal the son of Shelemiah and Zephaniah the son of Maaseiah
Jeremiah37:4 Now Jeremiah was still coming in and going out among the people
Jeremiah37:5 At that time Pharaoh’s army had come out of Egypt
Jeremiah37:6 Then the word of the Lord came to the prophet Jeremiah
Jeremiah37:7 Thus says the Lord
Jeremiah37:8 The Chaldeans will come again and fight against this city
Jeremiah37:9 Thus says the Lord: Do not deceive yourselves
Jeremiah37:10 For though you had struck the whole army of the Chaldeans who fight against you and there remained but wounded men among them

Jeremiah37:12 Jeremiah set out from Jerusalem to go into the land of Benjamin to take possession of his property there among the people.

Jeremiah37:14 But Jeremiah said

Jeremiah37:20 Therefore now please listen
Jeremiah37:21 Then Zedekiah the king commanded that they should commit Jeremiah into the court of the prison and give him daily a piece of bread out of the bakers’ street

Jeremiah38:3 Thus says the Lord: This city shall surely be given into the hand of the king of Babylon’s army

Jeremiah38:13 Then they drew Jeremiah up with cords and took him up out of the cistern

Jeremiah38:15 Then Jeremiah said to Zedekiah

Jeremiah38:20 But Jeremiah said

Jeremiah38:22 Behold
Jeremiah38:23 “So they will bring out all your wives and your children to the Chaldeans. And you will not escape out of their hand
Jeremiah38:24 Then Zedekiah said to Jeremiah
Jeremiah38:25 But if the officials hear that I have talked with you and they come to you and say to you
Jeremiah38:26 then you will say to them
Jeremiah38:27 Then all the officials came to Jeremiah and asked him. And he told them according to all these words that the king had commanded; so they stopped speaking with him
Jeremiah38:28 So Jeremiah remained in the court of the prison until the day that Jerusalem was captured.
Jeremiah39:1 In the ninth year of Zedekiah king of Judah
Jeremiah39:2 In the eleventh year of Zedekiah
Jeremiah39:3 All the officials of the king of Babylon entered and sat in the Middle Gate: Nergal-sharezer
Jeremiah39:4 When Zedekiah the king of Judah saw them and all the men of war
Jeremiah39:5 But the Chaldeans’ army pursued after them and overtook Zedekiah in the plains of Jericho. And when they had taken him
Jeremiah39:6 Then the king of Babylon slew the sons of Zedekiah in Riblah before his eyes. Moreover
Jeremiah39:7 He also put out Zedekiah’s eyes and bound him with chains to carry him to Babylon.
Jeremiah39:8 The Chaldeans burned the king’s house and the houses of the people with fire and broke down the walls of Jerusalem.

Jeremiah39:10 But Nebuzaradan the captain of the guard left behind in the land of Judah the poor people who had nothing

Jeremiah41:2 Ishmael the son of Nethaniah and the ten men that were with him arose and struck Gedaliah the son of Ahikam
Jeremiah41:3 Ishmael also slew all the Jews who were with him
Jeremiah41:4 On the day after he had slain Gedaliah
Jeremiah41:5 eighty men came from Shechem
Jeremiah41:6 Then Ishmael the son of Nethaniah went out from Mizpah to meet them
Jeremiah41:7 Yet it was so when they came into the midst of the city that Ishmael the son of Nethaniah
Jeremiah41:8 But ten men were found among those who said to Ishmael
Jeremiah41:9 Now the cistern in which Ishmael had cast all the dead bodies of the men
Jeremiah41:10 Then Ishmael carried away captive all the rest of the people who were in Mizpah
Jeremiah41:11 But when Johanan the son of Kareah and all the captains of the forces that were with him heard of all the evil that Ishmael the son of Nethaniah had done
Jeremiah41:12 then they took all the men and went to fight with Ishmael the son of Nethaniah
Jeremiah41:13 And when all the people who were with Ishmael saw Johanan the son of Kareah and all the captains of the forces that were with him
Jeremiah41:14 So all the people whom Ishmael had carried away captive from Mizpah turned about and returned
Jeremiah41:15 But Ishmael the son of Nethaniah escaped from Johanan with eight men and went to the Ammonites.

Jeremiah41:17 And they departed and lived in the habitation of Geruth Kimham
Jeremiah41:18 because of the Chaldeans. They were afraid of them

Jeremiah42:6 Whether it is good or whether it is bad

Jeremiah42:9 and said to them

Jeremiah42:11 Do not be afraid of the king of Babylon of whom you are now afraid. Do not be afraid of him

Jeremiah42:14 saying

Jeremiah42:20 For you have only deceived yourselves when you sent me to the Lord your God
Jeremiah42:21 Now I have this day declared it to you. But you have not obeyed the voice of the Lord your God
Jeremiah42:22 Now therefore certainly know that you will die by the sword

Jeremiah44:8 provoking Me to wrath with the works of your hands

Jeremiah44:22 So the Lord could no longer bear it because of the evil of your deeds and because of the abominations which you have committed. Therefore your land is a desolation
Jeremiah44:23 Because you have burned incense and because you have sinned against the Lord and have not obeyed the voice of the Lord

Jeremiah46:2 Concerning Egypt
Jeremiah46:3 Prepare the small and large shields
Jeremiah46:4 Harness the horses; mount the steeds! Take your positions with your helmets
Jeremiah46:5 Why have I seen them dismayed and drawing back? Their mighty ones are beaten down and have taken refuge in flight
Jeremiah46:6 Let not the swift flee away
Jeremiah46:7 Who is this that comes up as the Nile
Jeremiah46:8 Egypt rises up like the Nile
Jeremiah46:9 Come up
Jeremiah46:10 For this is the day of the Lord God of Hosts
Jeremiah46:11 Go up into Gilead and take balm
Jeremiah46:12 The nations have heard of your shame
Jeremiah46:13 The word that the Lord spoke to Jeremiah the prophet
Jeremiah46:14 Declare in Egypt and proclaim in Migdol
Jeremiah46:15 Why have your valiant men become prostrate? They do not stand because the Lord has thrust them down.
Jeremiah46:16 He made many to fall. Indeed
Jeremiah46:17 They cried there
Jeremiah46:18 As I live
Jeremiah46:19 O daughter dwelling in Egypt
Jeremiah46:20 Egypt is like a very fair heifer
Jeremiah46:21 Also her mercenaries are in her midst like fat bulls
Jeremiah46:22 Its sound goes along like a serpent
Jeremiah46:23 They have cut down her forest
Jeremiah46:24 The daughter of Egypt has been humiliated; she has been delivered into the hand of the people of the north.
Jeremiah46:25 The Lord of Hosts
Jeremiah46:26 And I shall deliver them into the hand of those who seek their lives and into the hand of Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon and into the hand of his servants. But afterwards it will be inhabited
Jeremiah46:27 But do not fear
Jeremiah46:28 Do not fear
Jeremiah47:1 The word of the Lord which came to Jeremiah the prophet against the Philistines
Jeremiah47:2 Thus says the Lord: Behold
Jeremiah47:3 at the noise of the stamping of the hoofs of his strong horses
Jeremiah47:4 because of the day that comes to destroy all the Philistines and to cut off from Tyre and Sidon every helper that remains. For the Lord will destroy the Philistines
Jeremiah47:5 Baldness has come upon Gaza; Ashkelon has been cut off. O remnant of their valley
Jeremiah47:6 O sword of the Lord
Jeremiah47:7 How can it be quiet
Jeremiah48:1 Concerning Moab: Thus says the Lord of Hosts
Jeremiah48:2 There is no more praise for Moab; in Heshbon they have devised evil against her: “Come and let us cut it off from being a nation!” Also you will be cut down
Jeremiah48:3 A sound of crying will be from Horonaim: “Devastation and great destruction!”
Jeremiah48:4 “Moab is destroyed”; her little ones have caused a cry of distress to be heard;
Jeremiah48:5 for by the Ascent of Luhith they will go up with continual weeping; for at the descent of Horonaim they have heard a cry of destruction.
Jeremiah48:6 Flee
Jeremiah48:7 For because you have trusted in your works and in your treasures
Jeremiah48:8 The destroyer will come upon every city
Jeremiah48:9 Give wings to Moab
Jeremiah48:10 Cursed be he who does the work of the Lord negligently
Jeremiah48:11 Moab has been at ease from his youth
Jeremiah48:12 Therefore
Jeremiah48:13 Moab shall be ashamed of Chemosh
Jeremiah48:14 How do you say
Jeremiah48:15 Moab has been devastated and gone up out of her cities
Jeremiah48:16 The calamity of Moab will come soon
Jeremiah48:17 All you who are about him
Jeremiah48:18 O daughter who inhabits Dibon
Jeremiah48:19 O inhabitant of Aroer
Jeremiah48:20 Moab has been humiliated
Jeremiah48:21 And judgment has come upon the plain country: upon Holon and Jahzah and Mephaath
Jeremiah48:22 and Dibon and Nebo and Beth-diblathaim
Jeremiah48:23 and Kiriathaim and Beth-gamul and Beth-meon
Jeremiah48:24 and Kerioth and Bozrah
Jeremiah48:25 The horn of Moab has been cut off and his arm has been broken
Jeremiah48:26 Make him drunk
Jeremiah48:27 For was not Israel a derision to you? Or was he caught among thieves? For each time you speak of him
Jeremiah48:28 O you who dwell in Moab
Jeremiah48:29 We have heard of the pride of Moab (he is very proud)
Jeremiah48:30 I know his wrath
Jeremiah48:31 Therefore I will howl for Moab
Jeremiah48:32 O vine of Sibmah
Jeremiah48:33 Joy and gladness are taken away from the plentiful field
Jeremiah48:34 From the outcry of Heshbon even to Elealeh
Jeremiah48:35 Moreover I will cause to cease in Moab
Jeremiah48:36 Therefore My heart will wail for Moab like pipes
Jeremiah48:37 For every head will be bald and every beard clipped; upon all the hands will be gashes
Jeremiah48:38 There will be lamentation everywhere upon all the housetops of Moab and in the streets
Jeremiah48:39 How it is broken down! How they have wailed! How Moab has turned his back with shame! So Moab will be a derision and a terror to all around him.
Jeremiah48:40 For thus says the Lord: Behold
Jeremiah48:41 Kerioth has been captured
Jeremiah48:42 Moab will be destroyed from being a people because he has magnified himself against the Lord.
Jeremiah48:43 Fear and the pit and the snare will be upon you
Jeremiah48:44 He who flees from the terror shall fall into the pit
Jeremiah48:45 The fugitives stand without strength under the shadow of Heshbon; for a fire will come out of Heshbon and a flame from the midst of Sihon
Jeremiah48:46 Woe to you
Jeremiah48:47 Yet I will restore the fortunes of Moab in the latter days
Jeremiah49:1 Concerning the Ammonites: Thus says the Lord: Does Israel have no sons? Or has he no heirs? Why then does their king inherit Gad and his people dwell in his cities?
Jeremiah49:2 Therefore
Jeremiah49:3 Howl
Jeremiah49:4 Why do you glory in the valleys
Jeremiah49:5 Behold
Jeremiah49:6 Yet afterward I will restore the fortunes of the Ammonites
Jeremiah49:7 Concerning Edom. Thus says the Lord of Hosts: Is there no more wisdom in Teman? Has counsel perished from the prudent? Has their wisdom vanished?
Jeremiah49:8 Flee
Jeremiah49:9 If grape gatherers came to you
Jeremiah49:10 But I have made Esau bare
Jeremiah49:11 Leave your fatherless children behind. I will preserve them alive; and let your widows trust in Me.
Jeremiah49:12 For thus says the Lord: They whose judgment was not to drink of the cup have assuredly drunk. And are you the one who shall go unpunished altogether? You will not go unpunished
Jeremiah49:13 For I have sworn by Myself
Jeremiah49:14 I have heard a message from the Lord
Jeremiah49:15 For behold
Jeremiah49:16 As for the terror of you
Jeremiah49:17 Also Edom will become a desolation; everyone who goes by it will be astonished
Jeremiah49:18 As in the overthrow of Sodom and Gomorrah and the neighboring cities
Jeremiah49:19 Behold
Jeremiah49:20 Therefore hear the counsel of the Lord that He has planned against Edom
Jeremiah49:21 The earth has shaken at the noise of their fall; there is an outcry. Its noise has been heard at the Red Sea.
Jeremiah49:22 Behold
Jeremiah49:23 Concerning Damascus: Hamath and Arpad have been humiliated
Jeremiah49:24 Damascus has become helpless; she has turned away to flee
Jeremiah49:25 How the city of praise has not been deserted
Jeremiah49:26 Therefore her young men will fall in her streets
Jeremiah49:27 I will kindle a fire to the wall of Damascus
Jeremiah49:28 Concerning Kedar
Jeremiah49:29 Their tents and their flocks they will take away. They will carry away their curtains for themselves
Jeremiah49:30 Flee
Jeremiah49:31 Arise
Jeremiah49:32 Their camels will become plunder
Jeremiah49:33 Hazor will become a dwelling for jackals and a desolation forever. No man will abide there
Jeremiah49:34 The word of the Lord which came to Jeremiah the prophet against Elam in the beginning of the reign of Zedekiah king of Judah
Jeremiah49:35 Thus says the Lord of Hosts: Behold
Jeremiah49:36 Upon Elam I will bring the four winds from the four quarters of heaven
Jeremiah49:37 For I will cause Elam to be dismayed before their enemies
Jeremiah49:38 And I will set My throne in Elam and will destroy from there the king and the officials
Jeremiah49:39 But it will come to pass in the latter days that I will restore the fortunes of Elam
Jeremiah50:1 The word that the Lord spoke against Babylon and against the land of the Chaldeans by Jeremiah the prophet:
Jeremiah50:2 Declare among the nations and proclaim
Jeremiah50:3 For out of the north there comes up a nation against her
Jeremiah50:4 In those days and at that time
Jeremiah50:5 They will ask for the way to Zion with their faces in its direction. They will come that they may join themselves to the Lord in a perpetual covenant that will not be forgotten.
Jeremiah50:6 My people have been lost sheep. Their shepherds have caused them to go astray; they have turned them away on the mountains. They have gone from mountain to hill and have forgotten their resting place.
Jeremiah50:7 All who found them have devoured them
Jeremiah50:8 Wander away out of the midst of Babylon
Jeremiah50:9 For behold
Jeremiah50:10 Chaldea will become plunder; all who destroy her will be satisfied
Jeremiah50:11 Because you were glad
Jeremiah50:12 your mother will be sorely humiliated; she who bore you will be ashamed. Behold
Jeremiah50:13 Because of the wrath of the Lord she will not be inhabited
Jeremiah50:14 Put yourselves in battle array against Babylon all around
Jeremiah50:15 Shout against her all around; she has given herself up
Jeremiah50:16 Cut off the sower from Babylon
Jeremiah50:17 Israel is a scattered flock. The lions have driven him away. First the king of Assyria devoured him; and this last one who has broken his bones is Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon.
Jeremiah50:18 Therefore thus says the Lord of Hosts
Jeremiah50:19 I will bring Israel again to his habitation
Jeremiah50:20 In those days and at that time
Jeremiah50:21 Go up against the land of Merathaim
Jeremiah50:22 A noise of battle is in the land
Jeremiah50:23 How the hammer of the whole earth has been cut asunder and broken! How Babylon has become a desolation among the nations!
Jeremiah50:24 I have laid a snare for you
Jeremiah50:25 The Lord has opened His armory and has brought out the weapons of His indignation
Jeremiah50:26 Come against her from the utmost border
Jeremiah50:27 Slay all her bulls
Jeremiah50:28 There is the sound of those who flee and escape out of the land of Babylon
Jeremiah50:29 Call together the archers against Babylon. All you who bend the bow
Jeremiah50:30 Therefore her young men shall fall in the streets
Jeremiah50:31 Behold
Jeremiah50:32 The most proud will stumble and fall
Jeremiah50:33 Thus says the Lord of Hosts: The Israelites and the Judahites were oppressed together; and all who took them as captives held them fast; they refused to let them go.
Jeremiah50:34 Their Redeemer is strong; the Lord of Hosts is His name. He will thoroughly plead their case
Jeremiah50:35 A sword is against the Chaldeans
Jeremiah50:36 A sword is against the oracle priests
Jeremiah50:37 A sword is against their horses
Jeremiah50:38 A drought is against her waters
Jeremiah50:39 Therefore the wild beasts of the desert with the wild beasts of the islands will dwell there
Jeremiah50:40 As God overthrew Sodom and Gomorrah and the neighboring cities
Jeremiah50:41 Behold
Jeremiah50:42 They will hold the bow and the lance; they are cruel and will not show mercy. Their voice shall roar like the sea; and they shall ride on horses
Jeremiah50:43 The king of Babylon has heard the report of them
Jeremiah50:44 Behold
Jeremiah50:45 Therefore hear the counsel of the Lord that He has planned against Babylon
Jeremiah50:46 At the shout
Jeremiah51:1 Thus says the Lord: Behold
Jeremiah51:2 And I will send foreigners to Babylon that they may winnow her and empty her land. For in the day of trouble they will be against her all around.
Jeremiah51:3 Let not him who bends his bow bend it
Jeremiah51:4 Thus the slain will fall in the land of the Chaldeans
Jeremiah51:5 For Israel has not been forsaken
Jeremiah51:6 Flee out of the midst of Babylon
Jeremiah51:7 Babylon has been a golden cup in the hand of the Lord
Jeremiah51:8 Babylon is suddenly fallen and destroyed. Howl for her. Take balm for her pain; perhaps she may be healed.
Jeremiah51:9 We applied healing to Babylon
Jeremiah51:10 The Lord has brought forth our righteousness. Come
Jeremiah51:11 Sharpen the arrows. Gather the shields. The Lord has raised up the spirit of the kings of the Medes. For His device is against Babylon
Jeremiah51:12 Set up the standard against the walls of Babylon
Jeremiah51:13 O you who dwell by many waters
Jeremiah51:14 The Lord of Hosts has sworn by Himself
Jeremiah51:15 He has made the earth by His power; He has established the world by His wisdom
Jeremiah51:16 When He utters His voice
Jeremiah51:17 Every man is ignorant by His knowledge; every founder is humiliated by the graven image
Jeremiah51:18 They are vanity
Jeremiah51:19 The portion of Jacob is not like them
Jeremiah51:20 He says: You are My battle-ax and weapon of war: for with you I will break in pieces the nations
Jeremiah51:21 and with you I will break in pieces the horse and his rider; and with you I will break in pieces the chariot and his rider.
Jeremiah51:22 With you also I will break in pieces man and woman; and with you I will break in pieces old and young; and with you I will break in pieces the young man and the young woman.
Jeremiah51:23 I will also break in pieces with you the shepherd and his flock; and with you I will break in pieces the farmer and his yoke of oxen; and with you I will break in pieces captains and rulers.
Jeremiah51:24 I will render to Babylon and to all the inhabitants of Chaldea all their evil that they have done in Zion in your sight
Jeremiah51:25 Behold
Jeremiah51:26 They will not take of you a stone for a corner
Jeremiah51:27 Set up a standard in the land
Jeremiah51:28 Prepare against her the nations
Jeremiah51:29 So the land trembles and sorrows
Jeremiah51:30 The mighty men of Babylon have ceased fighting; they have remained in their strongholds. Their might has failed; they became as women. They have burned her dwelling places; the bars of her gates are broken.
Jeremiah51:31 One courier will run to meet another
Jeremiah51:32 and the passages have been seized
Jeremiah51:33 For thus says the Lord of Hosts
Jeremiah51:34 “Nebuchadnezzar the king of Babylon has devoured me and crushed me; he has made me an empty vessel. He has swallowed me up like a dragon; he has filled his belly with my delicacies
Jeremiah51:35 May the violence done to me and to my flesh be upon Babylon
Jeremiah51:36 Therefore thus says the Lord: Behold
Jeremiah51:37 Babylon will become heaps
Jeremiah51:38 They will roar together like young lions; they will growl as lions’ whelps.
Jeremiah51:39 When they become heated up
Jeremiah51:40 I will bring them down like lambs to the slaughter
Jeremiah51:41 How Sheshak has been captured! And how the praise of the whole earth has been seized! How Babylon has become an astonishment among the nations!
Jeremiah51:42 The sea has come up over Babylon; she has been covered with the multitude of the waves.
Jeremiah51:43 Her cities have become a desolation
Jeremiah51:44 I will punish Bel in Babylon
Jeremiah51:45 My people
Jeremiah51:46 Let not your heart grow faint
Jeremiah51:47 Therefore behold
Jeremiah51:48 Then the heaven and the earth
Jeremiah51:49 As Babylon has caused the slain of Israel to fall
Jeremiah51:50 You who have escaped the sword
Jeremiah51:51 We are humiliated because we have heard reproach. Shame has covered our faces
Jeremiah51:52 Therefore
Jeremiah51:53 Though Babylon should mount up to heaven
Jeremiah51:54 A sound of an outcry comes from Babylon
Jeremiah51:55 because the Lord has devastated Babylon
Jeremiah51:56 Because the destroyer is coming against her
Jeremiah51:57 I will make her officials drunk
Jeremiah51:58 Thus says the Lord of Hosts: The broad wall of Babylon will be utterly broken
Jeremiah51:59 The word which Jeremiah the prophet commanded Seraiah the son of Neriah
Jeremiah51:60 So Jeremiah wrote in a single scroll all the evil that should come upon Babylon
Jeremiah51:61 Jeremiah said to Seraiah
Jeremiah51:62 and say
Jeremiah51:63 It will be that when you have made an end of reading this scroll that you will bind a stone to it
Jeremiah51:64 and say
Jeremiah52:1 Zedekiah was twenty-one years old when he began to reign
Jeremiah52:2 He did what was evil in the eyes of the Lord
Jeremiah52:3 Through the anger of the Lord this came to pass in Jerusalem and Judah until He had cast them out from His presence. Then Zedekiah rebelled against the king of Babylon.
Jeremiah52:4 In the ninth year of his reign
Jeremiah52:5 So the city was besieged until the eleventh year of King Zedekiah.
Jeremiah52:6 In the fourth month
Jeremiah52:7 Then the city was breached
Jeremiah52:8 But the army of the Chaldeans pursued after the king and overtook Zedekiah in the plains of Jericho
Jeremiah52:9 Then they took the king and carried him up to the king of Babylon at Riblah in the land of Hamath where he passed sentence on him.
Jeremiah52:10 The king of Babylon slew the sons of Zedekiah before his eyes; he also slew all the officials of Judah in Riblah.
Jeremiah52:11 Then he put out the eyes of Zedekiah
Jeremiah52:12 Now in the fifth month
Jeremiah52:13 and burned the house of the Lord
Jeremiah52:14 And all the army of the Chaldeans who were with the captain of the guard broke down all the walls of Jerusalem all around.
Jeremiah52:15 Then Nebuzaradan the captain of the guard carried away captive some of the poorest of the people
Jeremiah52:16 But Nebuzaradan the captain of the guard left some of the poorest of the land to be vinedressers and farmers.
Jeremiah52:17 Also the pillars of bronze that were in the house of the Lord
Jeremiah52:18 The cauldrons also
Jeremiah52:19 The captain of the guard also took away the basins
Jeremiah52:20 The two pillars
Jeremiah52:21 Concerning the pillars: the height of one pillar was eighteen cubits
Jeremiah52:22 A capital of bronze was upon it
Jeremiah52:23 There were ninety-six pomegranates on the sides; all the pomegranates were one hundred upon the latticework all around.
Jeremiah52:24 The captain of the guard took Seraiah the chief priest
Jeremiah52:25 He also took out of the city one official who had charge of the men of war
Jeremiah52:26 So Nebuzaradan the captain of the guard took them and brought them to the king of Babylon to Riblah.
Jeremiah52:27 The king of Babylon struck them and put them to death in Riblah in the land of Hamath. Thus Judah was carried away captive out of its own land.
Jeremiah52:28 These are the people whom Nebuchadnezzar carried away captive: in the seventh year
Jeremiah52:29 In the eighteenth year of Nebuchadnezzar
Jeremiah52:30 In the twenty-third year of Nebuchadnezzar
Jeremiah52:31 In the thirty-seventh year of the captivity of Jehoiachin
Jeremiah52:32 and spoke kindly to him
Jeremiah52:33 Therefore Jehoiachin changed his prison garments and continually ate meals before him all the days of his life.
Jeremiah52:34 For his allowance
Lamentations1:1 How lonely sits the city that was full of people! How she has become like a widow
Lamentations1:2 She weeps sorely in the night
Lamentations1:3 Judah has gone into captivity
Lamentations1:4 The roads to Zion mourn because no one comes to the solemn feasts. All her gates are desolate; her priests sigh
Lamentations1:5 Her adversaries have become her masters
Lamentations1:6 From the daughter of Zion all her beauty has departed. Her princes have become like deer that find no pasture; they fled without strength before the pursuer.
Lamentations1:7 In the days of her affliction and misery Jerusalem remembers all her pleasant things that she had in the days of old. When her people fell into the hand of the enemy
Lamentations1:8 Jerusalem has sinned grievously; therefore she has become vile. All who honored her despise her
Lamentations1:9 Her uncleanness is in her skirts; she took no thought of her future; therefore her fall is astounding; she has no comforter. “O  Lord
Lamentations1:10 The adversary has spread his hand over all her precious things; for she has seen the nations enter her sanctuary
Lamentations1:11 All her people groan
Lamentations1:12 Is it nothing to you
Lamentations1:13 From on high He has sent fire into my bones
Lamentations1:14 The yoke of my transgressions was bound; by His hand they were fastened together; they were set upon my neck. He made my strength fail; the Lord delivered me into the hands of those whom I am not able to withstand.
Lamentations1:15 The Lord has trampled underfoot all my mighty men in my midst; He has called an assembly against me to crush my young men; The Lord has trodden the virgin daughter of Judah as in a winepress.
Lamentations1:16 For these things I weep; my eyes flow with tears; for the comforter
Lamentations1:17 Zion spreads out her hands
Lamentations1:18 The Lord is in the right
Lamentations1:19 I called for my lovers
Lamentations1:20 Look
Lamentations1:21 They have heard my groaning
Lamentations1:22 Let all their wickedness come before You
Lamentations2:1 How the Lord has covered the daughter of Zion with a cloud in His anger! He has cast down from heaven to the earth the beauty of Israel; He has not remembered His footstool in the day of His anger.
Lamentations2:2 The Lord has swallowed up without mercy all the habitations of Jacob; in His wrath He has thrown down the strongholds of the daughter of Judah; He has brought them down to the ground; He has profaned the kingdom and its princes.
Lamentations2:3 He has cut off in fierce anger all the might of Israel; He has drawn back His right hand from before the enemy. He has burned against Jacob like a flaming fire
Lamentations2:4 He has bent His bow like an enemy
Lamentations2:5 The Lord has become like an enemy; He has swallowed up Israel
Lamentations2:6 He has violently taken away His tabernacle as if it were a garden; He has destroyed His place of assembly; the Lord has caused the solemn feasts and Sabbaths to be forgotten in Zion. In his fierce indignation He has despised the king and the priest.
Lamentations2:7 The Lord has scorned His altar
Lamentations2:8 The Lord has purposed to destroy the wall of the daughter of Zion. He has stretched out a line; He has not withdrawn His hand from destroying; therefore He caused the rampart and the wall to lament; they languished together.
Lamentations2:9 Her gates have sunk into the ground; He has destroyed and broken her bars. Her king and her princes are among the nations; the Law is no more
Lamentations2:10 The elders of the daughter of Zion sit on the ground in silence; they throw dust on their heads and gird themselves with sackcloth. The virgins of Jerusalem bow their heads to the ground.
Lamentations2:11 My eyes fail with tears
Lamentations2:12 They say to their mothers
Lamentations2:13 What can I say for you
Lamentations2:14 Your prophets have seen for you false and deceptive visions; they have not revealed your iniquity
Lamentations2:15 All who pass by clap their hands at you; they hiss and shake their heads at the daughter of Jerusalem
Lamentations2:16 All your enemies have opened their mouth against you; they hiss and gnash their teeth. They say
Lamentations2:17 The Lord has done what He planned; He has fulfilled His word that He had commanded in the days of old. He has thrown down and has not pitied
Lamentations2:18 Their heart cried to the Lord: O wall of the daughter of Zion
Lamentations2:19 Arise
Lamentations2:20 Look
Lamentations2:21 The young and the old lie on the ground in the streets; my virgins and my young men have fallen by the sword; You have killed them in the day of Your anger
Lamentations2:22 You have invited as to a feast day the terrors all around; in the day of the Lord ’s anger no one escaped or survived; those whom I held and raised my enemy destroyed.
Lamentations3:1 I am the man who has seen affliction by the rod of His wrath.
Lamentations3:2 He has driven and brought me into darkness without any light.
Lamentations3:3 Surely against me has He turned His hand continually
Lamentations3:4 My flesh and my skin He has made waste away; He has broken my bones;
Lamentations3:5 He has besieged and enveloped me with gall and travail.
Lamentations3:6 He has set me in dark places
Lamentations3:7 He has hedged me in so that I cannot get out; He has made my chain heavy.
Lamentations3:8 Even when I cry for help
Lamentations3:9 He has blocked my ways with hewn stone; He has made my paths crooked.
Lamentations3:10 He is to me a bear lying in wait
Lamentations3:11 He has turned aside my ways and torn me in pieces; He has made me desolate.
Lamentations3:12 He has bent His bow and set me as a target for the arrow.
Lamentations3:13 He has caused the arrows of His quiver to pierce my inward parts.
Lamentations3:14 I have become the derision of all my people
Lamentations3:15 He has filled me with bitterness
Lamentations3:16 He has made my teeth grind on gravel
Lamentations3:17 My soul is bereft of peace; I have forgotten prosperity.
Lamentations3:18 So I say
Lamentations3:19 Remember my affliction and my wandering
Lamentations3:20 Surely my soul remembers and is humbled within me.
Lamentations3:21 But this I call to mind
Lamentations3:22 It is of the Lord ’s mercies that we are not consumed; His compassions do not fail.
Lamentations3:23 They are new every morning; great is Your faithfulness.
Lamentations3:24 “The Lord is my portion
Lamentations3:25 The Lord is good to those who wait for Him
Lamentations3:26 It is good that a man should wait quietly for the salvation of the Lord.
Lamentations3:27 It is good for a man to bear the yoke in his youth.
Lamentations3:28 Let him sit alone in silence when it is laid on him;
Lamentations3:29 let him put his mouth in the dust— there may yet be hope.
Lamentations3:30 Let him give his cheek to the one who strikes
Lamentations3:31 For the Lord will not cast off forever.
Lamentations3:32 But though He causes grief
Lamentations3:33 For He does not afflict from His heart
Lamentations3:34 To crush underfoot all the prisoners of the earth
Lamentations3:35 to turn aside the justice due a man in the presence of the Most High
Lamentations3:36 to subvert a man in his cause
Lamentations3:37 Who is he who speaks and it comes to pass
Lamentations3:38 Is it not from the mouth of the Most High that good and bad proceed?
Lamentations3:39 Why should a living man complain
Lamentations3:40 Let us search and try our ways
Lamentations3:41 Let us lift up our hearts and hands to God in heaven:
Lamentations3:42 We have transgressed and rebelled; You have not pardoned.
Lamentations3:43 You have covered Yourself with anger and pursued us; You have killed and not pitied.
Lamentations3:44 You have covered Yourself with a cloud
Lamentations3:45 You have made us filthy refuse in the midst of the peoples.
Lamentations3:46 All our enemies have opened their mouths against us.
Lamentations3:47 Panic and snare have come upon us
Lamentations3:48 My eyes flow with rivers of tears for the destruction of the daughter of my people.
Lamentations3:49 My eyes flow and do not cease
Lamentations3:50 until the Lord from heaven looks down and sees.
Lamentations3:51 My eyes bring suffering to my soul at the fate of all the daughters of my city.
Lamentations3:52 My enemies chased me like a bird
Lamentations3:53 They cut off my life in the pit and cast stones on me.
Lamentations3:54 Waters flowed over my head; I said
Lamentations3:55 I called on Your name
Lamentations3:56 You have heard my plea: “Do not close Your ear to my cry for help!”
Lamentations3:57 You drew near on the day I called on You
Lamentations3:58 O Lord
Lamentations3:59 O  Lord
Lamentations3:60 You have seen all their vengeance
Lamentations3:61 You have heard their reproach
Lamentations3:62 the lips of my enemies and their devices against me all the day.
Lamentations3:63 Behold their sitting down and their rising up; I am their mocking song.
Lamentations3:64 Render to them a recompense
Lamentations3:65 Give them hardness of heart; may Your curse be upon them!
Lamentations3:66 In Your anger pursue and destroy them from under Your heavens
Lamentations4:1 How the gold has become dim! How the most fine gold has changed! The stones of the sanctuary lie scattered at the head of every street.
Lamentations4:2 The precious sons of Zion
Lamentations4:3 Even the jackals offer the breast; they nurse their young
Lamentations4:4 The tongue of the infant cleaves to the roof of his mouth for thirst; the children beg for bread
Lamentations4:5 Those who once ate delicacies are desolate in the streets; those who were brought up in scarlet embrace ash heaps.
Lamentations4:6 For the iniquity of the daughter of my people is greater than the sin of Sodom
Lamentations4:7 Her princes were purer than snow
Lamentations4:8 Their form is blacker than coal; they are not recognized in the streets; their skin cleaves to their bones
Lamentations4:9 Those killed by the sword are better off than those who die of hunger
Lamentations4:10 The hands of compassionate women have boiled their own children; they became their food in the destruction of the daughter of my people.
Lamentations4:11 The Lord has fulfilled His fury
Lamentations4:12 The kings of the earth
Lamentations4:13 This was for the sins of her prophets and the iniquities of her priests
Lamentations4:14 They wandered
Lamentations4:15 They cried out to them
Lamentations4:16 The presence of the Lord scattered them; He will regard them no more; they do not respect the priests
Lamentations4:17 Our eyes failed us
Lamentations4:18 They tracked our steps so that we could not walk in our streets. Our end drew near; our days were numbered
Lamentations4:19 Our pursuers were swifter than the eagles of the skies. They pursued us on the mountains; they lay in wait for us in the wilderness.
Lamentations4:20 The breath of our life
Lamentations4:21 Rejoice and be glad
Lamentations4:22 The punishment of your iniquity
Lamentations5:1 Remember
Lamentations5:2 Our inheritance has been turned over to strangers
Lamentations5:3 We have become orphans and fatherless; our mothers are like widows.
Lamentations5:4 We must pay for the water we drink; our wood is sold to us.
Lamentations5:5 Our pursuers are at our necks; we labor and have no rest.
Lamentations5:6 We have given our hand to Egypt and to Assyria
Lamentations5:7 Our fathers sinned and are no more
Lamentations5:8 Slaves rule over us; there is no one to deliver us from their hand.
Lamentations5:9 We get our bread at the peril of our lives
Lamentations5:10 Our skin is hot as an oven
Lamentations5:11 They ravished the women in Zion
Lamentations5:12 Princes were hung up by their hands
Lamentations5:13 Young men ground at the millstones; boys staggered under loads of wood.
Lamentations5:14 The elders have left the city gate
Lamentations5:15 The joy of our hearts has ceased; our dancing has turned into mourning.
Lamentations5:16 The crown has fallen from our head; woe to us
Lamentations5:17 For this our heart is faint; for these things our eyes grow dim;
Lamentations5:18 because of Mount Zion
Lamentations5:19 You
Lamentations5:20 Why do You forget us forever
Lamentations5:21 Restore us to Yourself
Lamentations5:22 unless You have utterly rejected us
Ezekiel1:1 In the thirtieth year
Ezekiel1:2 On the fifth day of the month
Ezekiel1:3 the word of the Lord came expressly to Ezekiel the priest
Ezekiel1:4 As I looked
Ezekiel1:5 Also out of the midst came the likeness of four living creatures. And this was their appearance: They had the likeness of a man.
Ezekiel1:6 Every one had four faces
Ezekiel1:7 Their legs were straight and the soles of their feet were like the sole of a calf’s hoof. And they gleamed like the color of burnished bronze.
Ezekiel1:8 They had the hands of a man under their wings on their four sides. As for the faces and wings of the four of them
Ezekiel1:9 their wings were joined to one another. Their faces did not turn when they went. Each went straight forward.
Ezekiel1:10 As for the likeness of their faces
Ezekiel1:11 Thus were their faces. And their wings were stretched upward. Two wings of every one were joined to one another
Ezekiel1:12 Each went straight forward. Wherever the spirit was to go
Ezekiel1:13 As for the likeness of the living creatures
Ezekiel1:14 The living creatures ran to and fro as the appearance of a flash of lightning.
Ezekiel1:15 Now as I looked at the living creatures
Ezekiel1:16 The appearance of the wheels and their work was like the color of sparkling beryl. And all four of them had one likeness. And their appearance and their work was
Ezekiel1:17 Whenever they went
Ezekiel1:18 As for their rims
Ezekiel1:19 Whenever the living creatures went
Ezekiel1:20 Wherever the spirit was about to go
Ezekiel1:21 Whenever those went
Ezekiel1:22 The likeness of the expanse over the heads of the living creatures was as the awesome gleam of crystal
Ezekiel1:23 Under the expanse their wings were stretched out straight
Ezekiel1:24 When they went
Ezekiel1:25 There was a voice from the expanse that was over their heads whenever they stood still and let down their wings.
Ezekiel1:26 Above the expanse that was over their heads was the likeness of a throne
Ezekiel1:27 Then I saw as glowing metal
Ezekiel1:28 As the appearance of the rainbow that is in the cloud on a day of rain
Ezekiel2:1 Then He said to me: Son of man
Ezekiel2:2 When He spoke to me
Ezekiel2:3 And He said to me: Son of man
Ezekiel2:4 And as for the impudent and obstinate children
Ezekiel2:5 As for them
Ezekiel2:6 And you
Ezekiel2:7 You shall speak My words to them
Ezekiel2:8 But you
Ezekiel2:9 When I looked
Ezekiel2:10 And He spread it before me. And there was writing on the inside and on the outside
Ezekiel3:1 Moreover He said to me
Ezekiel3:2 So I opened my mouth
Ezekiel3:3 He said to me
Ezekiel3:4 Then He said to me: Son of man
Ezekiel3:5 For you are not being sent to a people of foreign speech and hard language
Ezekiel3:6 nor to many peoples of foreign speech and hard language
Ezekiel3:7 But the house of Israel will not listen to you
Ezekiel3:8 But I have made your face as hard as their faces and your forehead as hard as their foreheads.
Ezekiel3:9 As emery harder than flint
Ezekiel3:10 Moreover He said to me: Son of man
Ezekiel3:11 Go to the captives
Ezekiel3:12 Then the Spirit took me up
Ezekiel3:13 I heard also the noise of the wings of the living creatures that touched one another and the noise of the wheels beside them
Ezekiel3:14 So the Spirit lifted me up and took me away
Ezekiel3:15 Then I came to the captives at Tel-aviv
Ezekiel3:16 At the end of seven days
Ezekiel3:17 Son of man
Ezekiel3:18 When I say to the wicked
Ezekiel3:19 Yet if you warn the wicked and he does not turn from his wickedness or from his wicked way
Ezekiel3:20 Again
Ezekiel3:21 Nevertheless if you warn the righteous man that the righteous should not sin and he does not sin
Ezekiel3:22 The hand of the Lord was upon me there
Ezekiel3:23 Then I arose
Ezekiel3:24 Then the Spirit entered me
Ezekiel3:25 As for you
Ezekiel3:26 And I will make your tongue cleave to the roof of your mouth so that you shall be mute and cannot be one to rebuke them
Ezekiel3:27 But when I speak with you
