Ezekiel4:1 You also
Ezekiel4:2 Then lay siege against it
Ezekiel4:3 Moreover take for yourself an iron plate and set it up for a wall of iron between you and the city. And set your face against it so that it is besieged
Ezekiel4:4 As for you
Ezekiel4:5 For I have laid upon you the years of their iniquity according to the number of the days
Ezekiel4:6 When you have accomplished them
Ezekiel4:7 Therefore you shall set your face toward the siege of Jerusalem
Ezekiel4:8 Behold
Ezekiel4:9 Also take for yourself wheat
Ezekiel4:10 Your food which you shall eat shall be by weight
Ezekiel4:11 You also shall drink water by measure
Ezekiel4:12 You shall eat it as barley cake
Ezekiel4:13 Then the Lord said
Ezekiel4:14 Then I said
Ezekiel4:15 Then He said to me
Ezekiel4:16 Moreover He said to me
Ezekiel4:17 because bread and water will be scarce
Ezekiel5:1 “As for you
Ezekiel5:2 You shall burn with fire one-third in the midst of the city when the days of the siege are fulfilled. Then you shall take one-third and strike it with the sword all around the city. And one-third you shall scatter in the wind. And I will draw out a sword after them.
Ezekiel5:3 You shall also take a few in number from them and bind them in the edges of your robes.
Ezekiel5:4 Then take some of them again and cast them into the midst of the fire and burn them in the fire
Ezekiel5:5 “Thus says the Lord God: This is Jerusalem. I have set it in the midst of the nations
Ezekiel5:6 But she has rebelled against My judgments more wickedly than the nations
Ezekiel5:7 “Therefore thus says the Lord God: Because you have more turmoil than the nations that are round about you and have not walked in My statutes
Ezekiel5:8 therefore thus says the Lord God: Behold
Ezekiel5:9 I will do in you what I have not done
Ezekiel5:10 Therefore the fathers shall eat their sons in your midst
Ezekiel5:11 Therefore
Ezekiel5:12 One-third of you shall die by pestilence or by famine; they shall be consumed in your midst. And one-third shall fall by the sword all around you. And I will scatter one-third into all the winds
Ezekiel5:13 “Thus My anger shall be accomplished
Ezekiel5:14 “Moreover I will make you a desolation and a reproach among the nations that are all around you
Ezekiel5:15 So it shall be a reproach and a taunt
Ezekiel5:16 When I send upon them the deadly arrows of famine which shall be for their destruction and which I will send to destroy you
Ezekiel5:17 So I will send upon you famine and wild beasts
Ezekiel6:1 And the word of the Lord came to me
Ezekiel6:2 Son of man
Ezekiel6:3 and say
Ezekiel6:4 Your altars shall be desolate
Ezekiel6:5 I will lay the dead corpses of the Israelites before their idols. And I will scatter your bones all around your altars.
Ezekiel6:6 In all your dwelling places
Ezekiel6:7 The slain shall fall in your midst
Ezekiel6:8 Yet I will leave a remnant
Ezekiel6:9 Those of you who escape shall remember Me among the nations wherever they shall be carried captive
Ezekiel6:10 They shall know that I am the Lord
Ezekiel6:11 Thus says the Lord God: Clap your hand
Ezekiel6:12 He who is far off shall die of pestilence
Ezekiel6:13 Then you shall know that I am the Lord
Ezekiel6:14 So
Ezekiel7:1 Moreover the word of the Lord came to me
Ezekiel7:2 And you
Ezekiel7:3 Now the end has come upon you
Ezekiel7:4 My eye shall not spare you
Ezekiel7:5 Thus says the Lord God: A disaster! A unique disaster! Behold
Ezekiel7:6 An end is coming
Ezekiel7:7 Your doom has come to you
Ezekiel7:8 Now I will shortly pour out My fury upon you
Ezekiel7:9 My eye shall not spare
Ezekiel7:10 Behold
Ezekiel7:11 Violence has risen up into a rod of wickedness; none of them shall remain
Ezekiel7:12 The time has come
Ezekiel7:13 For the seller shall not return to that which is sold
Ezekiel7:14 They have blown the trumpet even to make all ready
Ezekiel7:15 The sword is outside
Ezekiel7:16 Even when their survivors escape
Ezekiel7:17 All hands shall be feeble
Ezekiel7:18 They shall also gird themselves with sackcloth
Ezekiel7:19 They shall cast their silver in the streets
Ezekiel7:20 They transformed the beauty of His ornaments into pride
Ezekiel7:21 I will give it into the hands of the strangers as plunder and to the wicked of the earth for destruction
Ezekiel7:22 My face also I will turn from them
Ezekiel7:23 Make a chain
Ezekiel7:24 Therefore I will bring the worst of the nations
Ezekiel7:25 When destruction comes
Ezekiel7:26 Mischief shall come upon mischief
Ezekiel7:27 The king shall mourn
Ezekiel8:1 In the sixth year
Ezekiel8:2 Then I looked
Ezekiel8:3 He stretched out the form of a hand and took me by a lock of my head. And the Spirit lifted me up between the earth and the heavens
Ezekiel8:4 Behold
Ezekiel8:5 Then He said to me
Ezekiel8:6 He said furthermore to me
Ezekiel8:7 Then He brought me to the door of the court. And when I looked
Ezekiel8:8 So He said to me
Ezekiel8:9 He said to me
Ezekiel8:10 So I went in and saw every form of creeping things
Ezekiel8:11 There stood before them seventy men of the elders of the house of Israel
Ezekiel8:12 Then He said to me
Ezekiel8:13 He also said to me
Ezekiel8:14 Then He brought me to the door of the gate of the house of the Lord which was toward the north. And behold
Ezekiel8:15 Then He said to me
Ezekiel8:16 He brought me into the inner court of the house of the Lord
Ezekiel8:17 Then He said to me
Ezekiel8:18 Therefore I will indeed deal in fury. My eye shall not spare
Ezekiel9:1 He cried out also in my ears with a loud voice
Ezekiel9:2 And behold
Ezekiel9:3 Then the glory of the God of Israel ascended from the cherub on which it had been
Ezekiel9:4 And the Lord said to him
Ezekiel9:5 To the others He said in my hearing
Ezekiel9:6 Utterly slay old and young
Ezekiel9:7 Then he said to them
Ezekiel9:8 While they were killing
Ezekiel9:9 Then He said to me
Ezekiel9:10 But as for Me
Ezekiel9:11 And behold
Ezekiel10:1 Then I looked
Ezekiel10:2 And He spoke to the man clothed with linen and said
Ezekiel10:3 Now the cherubim stood on the right side of the temple when the man went in
Ezekiel10:4 Then the glory of the Lord went up from the cherub and stood over the threshold of the temple. And the house was filled with the cloud
Ezekiel10:5 The sound of the wings of the cherubim was heard even in the outer court
Ezekiel10:6 When He had commanded the man clothed with linen
Ezekiel10:7 One cherub stretched out his hand from among the cherubim to the fire that was between the cherubs
Ezekiel10:8 There appeared among the cherubim the form of a man’s hand under their wings.
Ezekiel10:9 Then I looked
Ezekiel10:10 As for their appearances
Ezekiel10:11 When they went
Ezekiel10:12 Their whole body
Ezekiel10:13 As for the wheels
Ezekiel10:14 Each one had four faces. The first face was the face of a cherub
Ezekiel10:15 Then the cherubim rose up. They are the living creatures that I saw by the River Kebar.
Ezekiel10:16 When the cherubim went
Ezekiel10:17 When the cherubim stood
Ezekiel10:18 Then the glory of the Lord departed from off the threshold of the temple and stood over the cherubim.
Ezekiel10:19 The cherubim lifted up their wings and mounted up from the earth in my sight. When they went out
Ezekiel10:20 These are the living creatures that I saw under the God of Israel by the River Kebar. So I knew that they were cherubim.
Ezekiel10:21 Every one had four faces apiece
Ezekiel10:22 The likeness of their faces was the same faces which I saw by the River Kebar
Ezekiel11:1 Moreover the Spirit lifted me up and brought me to the east gate of the house of the Lord which faces eastward. And behold
Ezekiel11:2 Then He said to me
Ezekiel11:3 who say
Ezekiel11:4 Therefore prophesy against them; prophesy
Ezekiel11:5 Then the Spirit of the Lord fell upon me
Ezekiel11:6 You have multiplied your slain in this city
Ezekiel11:7 Therefore thus says the Lord God: Your slain whom you have laid in the midst of it
Ezekiel11:8 You have feared the sword
Ezekiel11:9 I will bring you out of the midst of the city and deliver you into the hands of strangers and execute judgments against you.
Ezekiel11:10 You shall fall by the sword. I will judge you in the border of Israel. Then you shall know that I am the Lord.
Ezekiel11:11 This city shall not be your cauldron
Ezekiel11:12 Thus you shall know that I am the Lord. You have not walked in My statutes
Ezekiel11:13 Now while I was prophesying
Ezekiel11:14 Again the word of the Lord came to me
Ezekiel11:15 Son of man
Ezekiel11:16 Therefore say
Ezekiel11:17 Therefore say
Ezekiel11:18 When they come there
Ezekiel11:19 I will give them one heart
Ezekiel11:20 that they may walk in My statutes
Ezekiel11:21 But as for those whose hearts walk after the hearts of their detestable things and their abominations
Ezekiel11:22 Then the cherubim lifted up their wings with the wheels beside them. And the glory of the God of Israel was over them.
Ezekiel11:23 The glory of the Lord went up from the midst of the city and stood on the mountain which is on the east side of the city.
Ezekiel11:24 Afterwards the Spirit took me up and brought me in a vision by the Spirit of God into Chaldea to those of the captivity. So the vision that I had seen went up from me.
Ezekiel11:25 Then I spoke to those of the captivity all the things that the Lord had shown me.
Ezekiel12:1 The word of the Lord also came to me
Ezekiel12:2 Son of man
Ezekiel12:3 Therefore
Ezekiel12:4 You shall bring out your belongings by day in their sight
Ezekiel12:5 Dig a hole through the wall in their sight
Ezekiel12:6 In their sight you shall bear it on your shoulders and carry it out in the twilight. You shall cover your face so that you cannot see the land
Ezekiel12:7 I did so as I was commanded. I brought out my baggage by day as baggage for captivity
Ezekiel12:8 In the morning the word of the Lord came to me
Ezekiel12:9 Son of man
Ezekiel12:10 Say to them
Ezekiel12:11 Say
Ezekiel12:12 The prince who is among them shall bear his baggage on his shoulder in the twilight and go out. They shall dig through the wall to carry it out thereby. He shall cover his face so that he cannot see the land with his eyes.
Ezekiel12:13 My net also I will spread upon him
Ezekiel12:14 I will scatter toward every wind all who are about him
Ezekiel12:15 Then they shall know that I am the Lord when I scatter them among the nations and disperse them among the countries.
Ezekiel12:16 But I will spare a few men of them from the sword
Ezekiel12:17 Moreover the word of the Lord came to me
Ezekiel12:18 Son of man
Ezekiel12:19 And say to the people of the land
Ezekiel12:20 The cities that are inhabited shall be laid waste
Ezekiel12:21 The word of the Lord came to me
Ezekiel12:22 Son of man
Ezekiel12:23 Tell them therefore
Ezekiel12:24 For there shall be no more any vain vision or flattering divination within the house of Israel.
Ezekiel12:25 For I the Lord will speak
Ezekiel12:26 Again the word of the Lord came to me
Ezekiel12:27 Son of man
Ezekiel12:28 Therefore say to them
Ezekiel13:1 And the word of the Lord came to me
Ezekiel13:2 Son of man
Ezekiel13:3 Thus says the Lord God: Woe to the foolish prophets who follow their own spirit and have seen nothing!
Ezekiel13:4 O Israel
Ezekiel13:5 You have not gone up into the gaps
Ezekiel13:6 They have seen vanity and lying divination
Ezekiel13:7 Have you not seen a vain vision
Ezekiel13:8 Therefore thus says the Lord God: Because you have spoken vanity and seen lies
Ezekiel13:9 Thus My hand shall be against the prophets who see vanity and who divine lies. They shall not have a place in the council of My people
Ezekiel13:10 It is surely because they have seduced My people
Ezekiel13:11 Say to those who daub it with whitewash that it shall fall. There shall be an overflowing shower
Ezekiel13:12 When the wall has fallen
Ezekiel13:13 Therefore thus says the Lord God: I will make a violent wind break out in My fury. And there shall be an overflowing shower in My anger
Ezekiel13:14 So I will break down the wall that you have daubed with whitewash
Ezekiel13:15 Thus I will accomplish My wrath upon the wall and upon those who have daubed it with whitewash
Ezekiel13:16 along with the prophets of Israel who prophesy concerning Jerusalem and who see visions of peace for her when there is no peace
Ezekiel13:17 Now you
Ezekiel13:18 and say
Ezekiel13:19 And you pollute Me among My people for handfuls of barley and pieces of bread to slay the souls of those who should not die and to save the souls alive of those who should not live by your lying to My people who hear your lies.
Ezekiel13:20 Therefore thus says the Lord God: Behold
Ezekiel13:21 Your veils also I will tear off and deliver My people out of your hands
Ezekiel13:22 Because with lies you have made the heart of the righteous sad
Ezekiel13:23 therefore you women shall no more see vanity or practice divinations
Ezekiel14:1 Then some of the elders of Israel came to me and sat before me.
Ezekiel14:2 The word of the Lord came to me
Ezekiel14:3 Son of man
Ezekiel14:4 Therefore speak to them and say to them
Ezekiel14:5 in order to seize the heart of the house of Israel who are estranged from Me through all their idols.
Ezekiel14:6 Therefore say to the house of Israel
Ezekiel14:7 For every one of the house of Israel or of the stranger who sojourns in Israel who separates himself from Me
Ezekiel14:8 And I will set My face against that man and will make him a sign and a proverb
Ezekiel14:9 And if the prophet is deceived when he has spoken a word
Ezekiel14:10 They shall bear the punishment of their iniquity. The punishment of the prophet shall be even as the punishment of him who seeks him
Ezekiel14:11 in order that the house of Israel may no more go astray from Me or be polluted anymore with all their transgressions. Thus they shall be My people
Ezekiel14:12 The word of the Lord came to me again
Ezekiel14:13 Son of man
Ezekiel14:14 Even though these three men
Ezekiel14:15 If I cause wild beasts to pass through the land
Ezekiel14:16 though these three men were in it
Ezekiel14:17 Or if I bring a sword on that land
Ezekiel14:18 though these three men were in it
Ezekiel14:19 Or if I send a pestilence into that land and pour out My fury on it in blood to cut off from it man and beast
Ezekiel14:20 though Noah
Ezekiel14:21 For thus says the Lord God: How much more when I send My four sore judgments on Jerusalem
Ezekiel14:22 Yet behold
Ezekiel14:23 Then they shall comfort you when you see their ways and their deeds. And you shall know that I have not done without cause all that I have done in it
Ezekiel15:1 And the word of the Lord came to me
Ezekiel15:2 Son of man
Ezekiel15:3 Can wood be taken from it to do any work
Ezekiel15:4 Behold
Ezekiel15:5 Behold
Ezekiel15:6 Therefore thus says the Lord God: As the wood of the vine tree among the trees of the forest
Ezekiel15:7 I will set My face against them. Though they go out from one fire
Ezekiel15:8 I will make the land desolate
Ezekiel16:1 Again the word of the Lord came to me
Ezekiel16:2 Son of man
Ezekiel16:3 and say
Ezekiel16:4 As for your birth
Ezekiel16:5 No eye pitied you to do any of these to you
Ezekiel16:6 When I passed by you and saw you polluted in your own blood
Ezekiel16:7 I have caused you to multiply as the bud of the field
Ezekiel16:8 When I passed by you and looked upon you
Ezekiel16:9 Then I washed you with water. Indeed
Ezekiel16:10 I clothed you also with embroidered work
Ezekiel16:11 I decked you also with ornaments
Ezekiel16:12 I put a jewel on your forehead
Ezekiel16:13 Thus you were decked with gold and silver. And your raiment was of fine linen
Ezekiel16:14 Then your renown went out among the nations for your beauty. For it was perfect through My comeliness which I had put upon you
Ezekiel16:15 But you trusted in your own beauty
Ezekiel16:16 Of your garments you took and decked your high places with various colors and played the harlot on them
Ezekiel16:17 You have also taken your fair jewels made of My gold and My silver
Ezekiel16:18 Then you took your embroidered garments and covered them. And you have set My oil and My incense before them.
Ezekiel16:19 My bread also which I gave you
Ezekiel16:20 Moreover you have taken your sons and your daughters
Ezekiel16:21 You have slain My children and delivered them up to idols by causing them to pass through the fire.
Ezekiel16:22 Besides all your abominations and your harlotries you have not remembered the days of your youth when you were naked and bare and were polluted in your blood.
Ezekiel16:23 After all your wickedness (Woe
Ezekiel16:24 you also built for yourself a shrine and have made yourself a high place in every street.
Ezekiel16:25 You have built yourself a high place at the head of every street
Ezekiel16:26 You have also committed fornication with the Egyptians
Ezekiel16:27 Therefore
Ezekiel16:28 You have played the harlot also with the Assyrians because you were insatiable. Indeed
Ezekiel16:29 You have moreover multiplied your fornication with the land of merchants
Ezekiel16:30 How weak is your heart
Ezekiel16:31 When you built your shrine at the head of every street and made your high place in every square
Ezekiel16:32 You have been as a wife who commits adultery
Ezekiel16:33 Men give gifts to all harlots. But you give your gifts to all your lovers
Ezekiel16:34 Thus you are different from other women in your harlotries in that no one follows you to commit harlotries
Ezekiel16:35 Therefore
Ezekiel16:36 Thus says the Lord God
Ezekiel16:37 therefore I will gather all your lovers with whom you have taken pleasure and all those whom you have loved with all those whom you have hated. I will even gather them from all around against you and will reveal your nakedness to them so that they may see all your nakedness.
Ezekiel16:38 I will judge you as women who commit adultery or shed blood are judged. And I will bring on you the blood of fury and jealousy.
Ezekiel16:39 I will also give you into their hand
Ezekiel16:40 They shall also bring up a company against you
Ezekiel16:41 They shall burn your houses with fire and execute judgments on you in the sight of many women. And I will cause you to cease from playing the harlot
Ezekiel16:42 So I will make My fury toward you to rest
Ezekiel16:43 Because you have not remembered the days of your youth
Ezekiel16:44 Behold
Ezekiel16:45 You are your mother’s daughter who loathed her husband and her children. And you are the sister of your sisters who loathed their husbands and their children. Your mother was a Hittite
Ezekiel16:46 Now your elder sister is Samaria
Ezekiel16:47 Yet you have not walked after their ways
Ezekiel16:48 As I live
Ezekiel16:49 Behold
Ezekiel16:50 They were haughty and committed abominations before Me. Therefore I took them away when I saw it.
Ezekiel16:51 Moreover
Ezekiel16:52 Also bear your own shame in that you have made judgment favorable to your sisters. Because of your sins that you have committed more abominable than they
Ezekiel16:53 Yet I shall restore their captivity
Ezekiel16:54 in order that you may bear your own shame and may be humiliated in all that you have done in that you are a comfort to them.
Ezekiel16:55 Your sisters
Ezekiel16:56 As the name of your sister Sodom was not mentioned by your mouth in the day of your pride
Ezekiel16:57 before your wickedness was uncovered
Ezekiel16:58 You have borne the punishment of your lewdness and your abominations
Ezekiel16:59 For thus
Ezekiel16:60 Nevertheless I will remember My covenant with you in the days of your youth
Ezekiel16:61 Then you shall remember your ways and be ashamed when you receive your sisters
Ezekiel16:62 Thus I will establish My covenant with you
Ezekiel16:63 in order that you may remember
Ezekiel17:1 And the word of the Lord came to me
Ezekiel17:2 Son of man
Ezekiel17:3 and say
Ezekiel17:4 He cropped off the top of its young twigs and carried it into a land of merchants. He set it in a city of dealers.
Ezekiel17:5 He took also some of the seed of the land and planted it in a fruitful field. He placed it by great waters and set it as a willow tree.
Ezekiel17:6 It grew and became a spreading vine of low stature
Ezekiel17:7 There was also another great eagle with great wings and much plumage. And behold
Ezekiel17:8 It was planted in a good soil by many waters
Ezekiel17:9 Say: Thus says the Lord God: Will it thrive? Will he not pull up its roots and cut off its fruit
Ezekiel17:10 Behold
Ezekiel17:11 Moreover the word of the Lord came to me
Ezekiel17:12 Say now to the rebellious house: Do you not know what these things mean? Tell them: Behold
Ezekiel17:13 He took one of the king’s seed
Ezekiel17:14 that the kingdom might be debased
Ezekiel17:15 But he rebelled against him in sending his ambassadors into Egypt
Ezekiel17:16 As I live
Ezekiel17:17 Nor shall Pharaoh with his mighty army and great company help him in the war by casting up mounts and building forts to cut off many persons.
Ezekiel17:18 Seeing he despised the oath by breaking the covenant
Ezekiel17:19 Therefore thus says the Lord God: As I live
Ezekiel17:20 I will spread My net upon him
Ezekiel17:21 All the choice men in all his troops shall fall by the sword
Ezekiel17:22 Thus says the Lord God: I will also take of the highest branch of the high cedar and set it aside. I will crop off from the top of its young twigs a tender one and will plant it on a high and lofty mountain.
Ezekiel17:23 On the mountain of the height of Israel I will plant it. And it shall bring forth boughs
Ezekiel17:24 All the trees of the field shall know that I the Lord have brought down the high tree
Ezekiel18:1 The word of the Lord came to me again
Ezekiel18:2 What do you mean in using this proverb concerning the land of Israel
Ezekiel18:3 As I live
Ezekiel18:4 Behold
Ezekiel18:5 But if a man is righteous and does that which is lawful and right
Ezekiel18:6 and has not eaten on the mountains; nor has he lifted up his eyes to the idols of the house of Israel
Ezekiel18:7 and he has not oppressed anyone
Ezekiel18:8 if he has not lent at interest
Ezekiel18:9 has walked in My statutes
Ezekiel18:10 If he begets a son who is a robber
Ezekiel18:11 (though he himself does not do any of these things)
Ezekiel18:12 has oppressed the poor and needy
Ezekiel18:13 has lent at interest
Ezekiel18:14 Now if he begets a son who sees all his father’s sins which he has done
Ezekiel18:15 He has not eaten on the mountain shrines
Ezekiel18:16 nor has he oppressed anyone
Ezekiel18:17 he keeps his hand from the poor
Ezekiel18:18 As for his father
Ezekiel18:19 Yet you say
Ezekiel18:20 The soul who sins shall die. The son shall not bear the punishment of the iniquity of the father
Ezekiel18:21 But if the wicked turns from all his sins that he has committed
Ezekiel18:22 All his transgressions that he has committed
Ezekiel18:23 Do I have any pleasure in the death of the wicked
Ezekiel18:24 But when a righteous man turns away from his righteousness
Ezekiel18:25 Yet you say
Ezekiel18:26 When a righteous man turns away from his righteousness and commits iniquity
Ezekiel18:27 Again
Ezekiel18:28 Because he considered and turned away from all his transgressions that he has committed
Ezekiel18:29 Yet the house of Israel says
Ezekiel18:30 Therefore I will judge you
Ezekiel18:31 Cast away from you all your transgressions whereby you have transgressed
Ezekiel18:32 For I have no pleasure in the death of anyone who dies
Ezekiel19:1 Moreover take up a lamentation for the officials of Israel
Ezekiel19:2 and say: What was your mother? A lioness among lions! She lay down among young lions; she nourished her whelps.
Ezekiel19:3 When she brought up one of her whelps
Ezekiel19:4 The nations also heard of him; he was taken in their pit
Ezekiel19:5 Now when she saw as she had waited that her hope was lost
Ezekiel19:6 He went up and down among the lions; he became a young lion
Ezekiel19:7 He destroyed their fortified palaces
Ezekiel19:8 Then the nations set against him on every side from their provinces
Ezekiel19:9 They put him in a cage with hooks and brought him to the king of Babylon; they brought him in hunting nets
Ezekiel19:10 Your mother was like a vine in your vineyard
Ezekiel19:11 It had strong rods for the scepters of those who rule. And its stature was exalted above the clouds
Ezekiel19:12 But it was plucked up in fury
Ezekiel19:13 Now it is planted in the wilderness
Ezekiel19:14 Fire has gone out from its branch; it has devoured its shoots and fruit
Ezekiel20:1 In the seventh year
Ezekiel20:2 Then the word of the Lord came to me
Ezekiel20:3 Son of man
Ezekiel20:4 Will you judge them
Ezekiel20:5 And say to them: Thus says the Lord God: On the day when I chose Israel
Ezekiel20:6 on that day that I lifted up My hand to them to bring them out of the land of Egypt into a land that I had selected for them
Ezekiel20:7 then I said to them: Cast away
Ezekiel20:8 But they rebelled against Me and would not listen to Me. They did not cast away the abominations of their eyes
Ezekiel20:9 But I acted for My name’s sake
Ezekiel20:10 Therefore I caused them to go forth out of the land of Egypt and brought them into the wilderness.
Ezekiel20:11 I gave them My statutes and showed them My judgments
Ezekiel20:12 Moreover I gave them My Sabbaths to be a sign between them and Me
Ezekiel20:13 But the house of Israel rebelled against Me in the wilderness. They did not walk in My statutes
Ezekiel20:14 But I acted for My name’s sake
Ezekiel20:15 Also I lifted up My hand to them in the wilderness that I would not bring them into the land which I had given them
Ezekiel20:16 because they despised My judgments and did not walk in My statutes
Ezekiel20:17 Nevertheless My eye spared them from destroying them
Ezekiel20:18 But I said to their children in the wilderness: Do not walk in the statutes of your fathers
Ezekiel20:19 I am the Lord your God: Walk in My statutes
Ezekiel20:20 And make holy My Sabbaths. And they shall be a sign between Me and you that you may know that I am the Lord your God.
Ezekiel20:21 Yet the children rebelled against Me. They did not walk in My statutes
Ezekiel20:22 Nevertheless I withdrew My hand and acted for My name’s sake
Ezekiel20:23 I lifted up My hand to them also in the wilderness
Ezekiel20:24 because they had not executed My judgments
Ezekiel20:25 Therefore I gave them also statutes that were not good and judgments by which they could not live.
Ezekiel20:26 And I pronounced them unclean because of their gifts
Ezekiel20:27 Therefore
Ezekiel20:28 When I had brought them into the land for which I lifted up My hand to give it to them
Ezekiel20:29 Then I said to them: What is the high place to which you go? And the name is called Bamah to this day.
Ezekiel20:30 Therefore say to the house of Israel
Ezekiel20:31 When you offer your gifts
Ezekiel20:32 That which comes into your mind shall not come about when you say
Ezekiel20:33 As I live
Ezekiel20:34 And I will bring you out from the peoples and will gather you out of the countries in which you are scattered with a mighty hand and with an outstretched arm and with fury poured out.
Ezekiel20:35 I will bring you into the wilderness of the peoples
Ezekiel20:36 As I entered into judgment with your fathers in the wilderness of the land of Egypt
Ezekiel20:37 I will cause you to pass under the rod
Ezekiel20:38 And I will purge from among you the rebels and those who transgress against Me. I will bring them out of the country where they sojourn
Ezekiel20:39 As for you
Ezekiel20:40 For on My holy mountain
Ezekiel20:41 I will accept you with your sweet aroma when I bring you out from the peoples and gather you out of the countries in which you have been scattered. And I will be sanctified among you before the nations.
Ezekiel20:42 You shall know that I am the Lord when I bring you into the land of Israel
Ezekiel20:43 There you shall remember your ways and all your deeds in which you have been defiled. And you shall loathe yourselves in your own sight for all your evils that you have committed.
Ezekiel20:44 You shall know that I am the Lord when I have dealt with you for My name’s sake
Ezekiel20:45 Moreover the word of the Lord came to me
Ezekiel20:46 Son of man
Ezekiel20:47 And say to the forest of the Negev
Ezekiel20:48 All flesh shall see that I the Lord have kindled it. It shall not be quenched.
Ezekiel20:49 Then I said
Ezekiel21:1 And the word of the Lord came to me
Ezekiel21:2 Son of man
Ezekiel21:3 and say to the land of Israel
Ezekiel21:4 Seeing then that I will cut off from you the righteous and the wicked
Ezekiel21:5 that all flesh shall know that I the Lord have drawn forth My sword from its sheath. It shall not return anymore.
Ezekiel21:6 As for you
Ezekiel21:7 It shall be when they say to you
Ezekiel21:8 Again the word of the Lord came to me
Ezekiel21:9 Son of man
Ezekiel21:10 It is sharpened to make a slaughter; it is polished so that it may glisten like lightning. Should we then make cheer
Ezekiel21:11 He has given it to be polished
Ezekiel21:12 Cry out and howl
Ezekiel21:13 Because it is a trial
Ezekiel21:14 You therefore
Ezekiel21:15 That their hearts may faint
Ezekiel21:16 Show yourself sharp
Ezekiel21:17 I will also clap My hands together
Ezekiel21:18 The word of the Lord came to me again
Ezekiel21:19 As for you
Ezekiel21:20 Appoint a way for the sword to come to Rabbah of the Ammonites and to Judah in Jerusalem the fortified.
Ezekiel21:21 For the king of Babylon stands at the parting of the way
Ezekiel21:22 At his right hand was the divination for Jerusalem: to appoint battering rams
Ezekiel21:23 It shall be to them as a false divination in their sight
Ezekiel21:24 Therefore thus says the Lord God: Because you have made your iniquity to be remembered
Ezekiel21:25 You
Ezekiel21:26 thus says the Lord God: Remove the diadem and take off the crown. This shall not be the same anymore. Exalt what is low
Ezekiel21:27 A ruin
Ezekiel21:28 You
Ezekiel21:29 while they see vanity for you
Ezekiel21:30 Return it to its sheath. I will judge you in the place where you were created
Ezekiel21:31 I will pour out My indignation upon you; I will blow against you in the fire of My wrath
Ezekiel21:32 You shall be fuel to the fire; your blood shall be in the midst of the land. You shall be no more remembered
Ezekiel22:1 Moreover the word of the Lord came to me
Ezekiel22:2 Now you
Ezekiel22:3 Then say
Ezekiel22:4 You have become guilty in your blood that you have shed and have defiled yourself with your idols which you have made. And you have caused your day to draw near and have come to your years. Therefore I have made you a reproach to the nations and a mocking to all countries.
Ezekiel22:5 Those who are near and those who are far from you shall mock you
Ezekiel22:6 Behold
Ezekiel22:7 In you they have treated father and mother with contempt. In your midst they have dealt with the stranger by oppression. In you they have vexed the fatherless and the widow.
Ezekiel22:8 You have despised My holy things and have profaned My Sabbaths.
Ezekiel22:9 In you are men who carry slanders to shed blood. And in you they eat at the mountain shrines. In your midst they commit lewdness.
Ezekiel22:10 In you they have uncovered their fathers’ nakedness. In you they have humbled her who was unclean in her menstrual impurity.
Ezekiel22:11 One has committed abomination with his neighbor’s wife
Ezekiel22:12 In you they have taken bribes to shed blood. You have taken usury and increase
Ezekiel22:13 Behold
Ezekiel22:14 Can your heart endure
Ezekiel22:15 I will scatter you among the nations and disperse you in the countries and consume your filthiness out of you.
Ezekiel22:16 You shall profane yourself in the sight of the nations
Ezekiel22:17 The word of the Lord came to me
Ezekiel22:18 Son of man
Ezekiel22:19 Therefore thus says the Lord God: Because you all have become dross
Ezekiel22:20 As they gather silver and bronze and iron and lead and tin into the midst of a furnace to blow fire on it to melt it
Ezekiel22:21 Indeed
Ezekiel22:22 As silver is melted in the midst of a furnace
Ezekiel22:23 The word of the Lord came to me
Ezekiel22:24 Son of man
Ezekiel22:25 There is a conspiracy of her prophets in her midst like a roaring lion tearing the prey. They have devoured souls. They have taken the treasure and precious things. They have made many widows in her midst.
Ezekiel22:26 Her priests have violated My law and have profaned My holy things. They have made no distinction between the holy and profane
Ezekiel22:27 Her officials in her midst are like wolves tearing the prey by shedding blood and destroying lives in order to obtain dishonest gain.
Ezekiel22:28 Her prophets have daubed them with whitewash
Ezekiel22:29 The people of the land have used oppression and exercised robbery and have vexed the poor and needy and have oppressed the sojourner wrongfully.
Ezekiel22:30 I sought for a man among them who would build up the hedge and stand in the gap before Me for the land so that I would not destroy it
Ezekiel22:31 Therefore I have poured out My indignation on them. I have consumed them with the fire of My wrath. Their own way I have recompensed on their heads
Ezekiel23:1 The word of the Lord came again to me
Ezekiel23:2 Son of man
Ezekiel23:3 And they committed harlotries in Egypt. They committed harlotries in their youth. There their breasts were pressed
Ezekiel23:4 Their names were Oholah the elder and Oholibah her sister. And they were mine
Ezekiel23:5 Oholah played the harlot when she was Mine. And she lusted after her lovers
Ezekiel23:6 who were clothed in purple
Ezekiel23:7 Thus she committed her harlotries with them
Ezekiel23:8 She did not forsake her harlotries from the time in Egypt. For in her youth they lay with her
Ezekiel23:9 Therefore I have delivered her into the hand of her lovers
Ezekiel23:10 They uncovered her nakedness. They took her sons and her daughters and killed her with the sword. And she became a byword among women
Ezekiel23:11 Now her sister Oholibah saw this
Ezekiel23:12 She lusted after the Assyrians her neighbors
Ezekiel23:13 Then I saw that she was defiled
Ezekiel23:14 So she increased her harlotries. And she saw men portrayed on the wall
Ezekiel23:15 girded with belts on their loins
Ezekiel23:16 And as soon as she saw them with her eyes
Ezekiel23:17 The Babylonians came to her into the bed of love
Ezekiel23:18 So she uncovered her harlotries and uncovered her nakedness. Then My soul became disgusted with her
Ezekiel23:19 Yet she multiplied her harlotries while remembering the days of her youth in which she had played the harlot in the land of Egypt.
Ezekiel23:20 For she lusted after their paramours
Ezekiel23:21 Thus you longed for the lewdness of your youth when the Egyptians handled your bosom because of the breasts of your youth.
Ezekiel23:22 Therefore
Ezekiel23:23 the Babylonians and all the Chaldeans
Ezekiel23:24 They shall come against you with weapons
Ezekiel23:25 I will set My jealousy against you
Ezekiel23:26 They shall also strip you of your clothes and take away your fair jewels.
Ezekiel23:27 Thus I will make your lewdness and your harlotry brought from the land of Egypt to cease from you so that you shall not lift up your eyes to them or remember Egypt anymore.
Ezekiel23:28 For thus says the Lord God: Behold
Ezekiel23:29 And they shall deal with you hatefully
Ezekiel23:30 I will do these things to you because you have gone whoring after the nations
Ezekiel23:31 You have walked in the way of your sister. Therefore I will give her cup into your hand.
Ezekiel23:32 Thus says the Lord God: You shall drink of your sister’s cup
Ezekiel23:33 You shall be filled with drunkenness and sorrow
Ezekiel23:34 You shall drink it and drain it
Ezekiel23:35 Therefore thus says the Lord God: Because you have forgotten Me and cast Me behind your back
Ezekiel23:36 The Lord moreover said to me: Son of man
Ezekiel23:37 For they have committed adultery
Ezekiel23:38 Moreover they have done this to Me: They have defiled My sanctuary in the same day and have profaned My Sabbaths.
Ezekiel23:39 For when they had slain their children to their idols
Ezekiel23:40 Furthermore
Ezekiel23:41 And you sat on a stately couch with a table prepared before it on which you had set My incense and My oil.
Ezekiel23:42 A sound of a multitude at ease was with her
Ezekiel23:43 Then I said to her who was worn out by adulteries
Ezekiel23:44 Yet they went in to her as they go in to a woman who plays the harlot. So they went in to Oholah and to Oholibah
Ezekiel23:45 But righteous men shall judge them after the manner of adulteresses
Ezekiel23:46 For thus says the Lord God: Bring up a company against them and give them over to terror and devastation.
Ezekiel23:47 The company shall stone them with stones and cut them down with their swords. They shall slay their sons and their daughters
Ezekiel23:48 Thus I will cause lewdness to cease from the land
Ezekiel23:49 Your lewdness shall be recompensed upon you
Ezekiel24:1 And in the ninth year
Ezekiel24:2 Son of man
Ezekiel24:3 Utter a parable to the rebellious house and say to them
Ezekiel24:4 Gather the pieces into it
Ezekiel24:5 take the choicest of the flock
Ezekiel24:6 Therefore thus says the Lord God: Woe to the bloody city
Ezekiel24:7 For her blood is in her midst; she set it on the bare rock; she did not pour it on the ground to cover it with dust.
Ezekiel24:8 That it may cause fury to come up to take vengeance
Ezekiel24:9 Therefore thus says the Lord God: Woe to the bloody city! I will also make the pile great.
Ezekiel24:10 Heap on wood
Ezekiel24:11 Then set it empty on its coals
Ezekiel24:12 She has wearied Me with toil
Ezekiel24:13 In your filthiness is lewdness. Because I would have purged you
Ezekiel24:14 I the Lord have spoken. It shall come to pass and I will act. I will not go back
Ezekiel24:15 Also the word of the Lord came to me
Ezekiel24:16 Son of man
Ezekiel24:17 Groan silently. Make no mourning for the dead. Bind on your turban and put your shoes on your feet and do not cover your mustache and do not eat the bread of men.
Ezekiel24:18 So I spoke to the people in the morning
Ezekiel24:19 And the people said to me
Ezekiel24:20 Then I answered them: The word of the Lord came to me
Ezekiel24:21 Speak to the house of Israel
Ezekiel24:22 You shall do as I have done. You shall not cover your mustache
Ezekiel24:23 Your turbans shall be on your heads
Ezekiel24:24 Thus Ezekiel is a sign to you. According to all that he has done you shall do. And when it comes about
Ezekiel24:25 As for you
Ezekiel24:26 that on that day he who escapes shall come to you to cause you to hear it with your ears?
Ezekiel24:27 On that day your mouth shall be opened to him who escaped
Ezekiel25:1 The word of the Lord came again to me
Ezekiel25:2 Son of man
Ezekiel25:3 and say to the Ammonites
Ezekiel25:4 therefore
Ezekiel25:5 I will make Rabbah a meadow for camels
Ezekiel25:6 For thus says the Lord God: Because you have clapped your hands and stamped your feet and rejoiced in heart with all your spite against the land of Israel
Ezekiel25:7 therefore
Ezekiel25:8 Thus says the Lord God: Because Moab and Seir say
Ezekiel25:9 therefore
Ezekiel25:10 and I will give it for a possession with the Ammonites to the men of the East
Ezekiel25:11 I will execute judgments on Moab. And they shall know that I am the Lord.
Ezekiel25:12 Thus says the Lord God: Because Edom has acted against the house of Judah by taking vengeance and has greatly offended and revenged themselves upon them
Ezekiel25:13 therefore thus says the Lord God: I will also stretch out My hand against Edom and cut off man and beast from it. And I will make it desolate. From Teman even to Dedan they shall fall by the sword.
Ezekiel25:14 I will lay My vengeance upon Edom by the hand of My people Israel. And they shall do in Edom according to My anger and according to My fury. And they shall know My vengeance
Ezekiel25:15 Thus says the Lord God: Because the Philistines have dealt by revenge and have taken vengeance with a spiteful heart to destroy with everlasting hatred
Ezekiel25:16 therefore thus says the Lord God: Behold
Ezekiel25:17 I will execute great vengeance upon them with furious rebukes. And they shall know that I am the Lord when I lay My vengeance upon them.
Ezekiel26:1 In the eleventh year
Ezekiel26:2 Son of man
Ezekiel26:3 therefore thus says the Lord God: Behold
Ezekiel26:4 They shall destroy the walls of Tyre and break down her towers. I will also scrape her dust from her and make her a bare rock.
Ezekiel26:5 She shall be a place for the spreading of nets in the midst of the sea
Ezekiel26:6 Her daughters who are on the mainland shall be slain by the sword. And they shall know that I am the Lord.
Ezekiel26:7 For thus says the Lord God: Behold
Ezekiel26:8 He shall slay your daughters on the mainland with the sword. And he shall set up a siege wall against you and cast up a mound against you and lift up the roof of shields against you.
Ezekiel26:9 He shall set engines of war against your walls
Ezekiel26:10 By reason of the abundance of his horses
Ezekiel26:11 With the hoofs of his horses he shall tread down all your streets. He shall slay your people by the sword
Ezekiel26:12 They shall make a spoil of your riches
Ezekiel26:13 I will cause the noise of your songs to cease. And the sound of your harps shall be heard no more.
Ezekiel26:14 I will make you a bare rock. You shall be a place to spread nets upon. You shall be built no more
Ezekiel26:15 Thus says the Lord God to Tyre: Shall not the coastlands shake at the sound of your fall when the wounded cry
Ezekiel26:16 Then all the officials of the sea shall come down from their thrones and lay away their robes and take off their embroidered garments. They shall clothe themselves with trembling. They shall sit on the ground and tremble every moment and be astonished at you.
Ezekiel26:17 They shall take up a lamentation over you and say to you: How you are destroyed
Ezekiel26:18 Now the coastlands shall tremble on the day of your fall; indeed
Ezekiel26:19 For thus says the Lord God: When I make you a desolate city
Ezekiel26:20 then I shall bring you down with those who descend into the pit
Ezekiel26:21 I will bring terrors on you and you shall be no more. Though you will be sought
Ezekiel27:1 The word of the Lord came again to me
Ezekiel27:2 Now you
Ezekiel27:3 And say to Tyre who is situated at the entrance of the sea
Ezekiel27:4 Your borders are in the midst of the seas. Your builders have perfected your beauty.
Ezekiel27:5 They have made all your ship boards of fir trees from Senir; they have taken cedars from Lebanon to make a mast for you.
Ezekiel27:6 Of the oaks of Bashan they have made your oars; with ivory they have inlaid your deck of boxwood out of the coastlands of Cyprus.
Ezekiel27:7 Fine linen with embroidered work from Egypt was your sail
Ezekiel27:8 The inhabitants of Sidon and Arvad were your mariners; your wise men
Ezekiel27:9 The elders of Byblos and her wise men were in you repairing your seams; all the ships of the sea with their mariners were in you to deal in your merchandise.
Ezekiel27:10 They of Persia and of Lydia and of Put were in your army
Ezekiel27:11 The sons of Arvad and your army were upon your walls all around
Ezekiel27:12 Tarshish was your merchant by reason of the multitude of all kinds of riches. With silver
Ezekiel27:13 Greece
Ezekiel27:14 Those of Beth-togarmah gave horses and horsemen and mules for your wares.
Ezekiel27:15 The sons of Dedan were your merchants. Many coastlands were your merchandise. They brought you for a payment horns of ivory and ebony.
Ezekiel27:16 Aram was your merchant by reason of the multitude of your wares. They paid for your wares with emeralds
Ezekiel27:17 Judah and the land of Israel
Ezekiel27:18 Damascus was your merchant in the multitude of your wares
Ezekiel27:19 Dan and Javan paid for your wares from Izal. Bright iron
Ezekiel27:20 Dedan was your merchant in saddlecloths for chariots.
Ezekiel27:21 Arabia and all the officials of Kedar
Ezekiel27:22 The merchants of Sheba and Raamah
Ezekiel27:23 Harran
Ezekiel27:24 They traded with you in choice garments
Ezekiel27:25 The ships of Tarshish were the carriers for your market. And you were filled and were very glorious in the midst of the seas.
Ezekiel27:26 Your rowers have brought you into great waters; the east wind has broken you in the midst of the seas.
Ezekiel27:27 Your riches and your wares
Ezekiel27:28 The meadowlands shall shake at the sound of the cry of your pilots.
Ezekiel27:29 All who handle the oar
Ezekiel27:30 And they shall cause their voice to be heard against you
Ezekiel27:31 Also they shall make themselves utterly bald for you
Ezekiel27:32 Also
Ezekiel27:33 When your wares went out by sea
Ezekiel27:34 Now that you are broken by the seas in the depths of the waters
Ezekiel27:35 All the inhabitants of the coastlands shall be astonished at you
Ezekiel27:36 The merchants among the peoples hiss at you; you shall be a terror
Ezekiel28:1 The word of the Lord came again to me
Ezekiel28:2 Son of man
Ezekiel28:3 You are wiser than Danel; there is no secret that is a match for you.
Ezekiel28:4 With your wisdom and with your understanding
Ezekiel28:5 by your great wisdom
Ezekiel28:6 Therefore thus says the Lord God: Because you have set your heart as the heart of God
Ezekiel28:7 therefore
Ezekiel28:8 They shall bring you down to the pit
Ezekiel28:9 Will you yet say before him who slays you
Ezekiel28:10 You shall die the death of the uncircumcised by the hand of strangers; for I have spoken! says the Lord God.
Ezekiel28:11 Moreover the word of the Lord came to me
Ezekiel28:12 Son of man
Ezekiel28:13 You were in Eden
Ezekiel28:14 You were the anointed cherub that covers
Ezekiel28:15 You were perfect in your ways from the day that you were created
Ezekiel28:16 By the multitude of your merchandise
Ezekiel28:17 Your heart was lifted up because of your beauty; you have corrupted your wisdom by reason of your brightness; I cast you to the ground
Ezekiel28:18 You have defiled your sanctuaries by the multitude of your iniquities
Ezekiel28:19 All those who know you among the people are astonished at you; you are a terror
Ezekiel28:20 Again the word of the Lord came to me
Ezekiel28:21 Son of man
Ezekiel28:22 and say
Ezekiel28:23 For I will send pestilence into her
Ezekiel28:24 There shall be no more a pricking brier to the house of Israel
Ezekiel28:25 Thus says the Lord God: When I gather the house of Israel from the peoples among whom they are scattered and display My holiness in them in the sight of the nations
Ezekiel28:26 They shall dwell safely in it and shall build houses and plant vineyards and dwell securely when I execute judgments on all those around them who despise them. Then they shall know that I am the Lord their God.
Ezekiel29:1 In the tenth year
Ezekiel29:2 Son of man
Ezekiel29:3 Speak
Ezekiel29:4 But I will put hooks in your jaws
Ezekiel29:5 I will abandon you to the wilderness
Ezekiel29:6 All the inhabitants of Egypt shall know that I am the Lord
Ezekiel29:7 When they took hold of you by your hand
Ezekiel29:8 Therefore thus says the Lord God: Behold
Ezekiel29:9 The land of Egypt shall become a desolation and waste. Then they shall know that I am the Lord. Because you said
Ezekiel29:10 therefore
Ezekiel29:11 No foot of man shall pass through it
Ezekiel29:12 Thus I will make the land of Egypt desolate in the midst of the countries that are desolate
Ezekiel29:13 For thus says the Lord God: At the end of forty years I will gather the Egyptians from the peoples wherever they were scattered.
Ezekiel29:14 And I will turn the fortunes of Egypt and will cause them to return into the land of Pathros
Ezekiel29:15 It shall be the basest of the kingdoms
Ezekiel29:16 It shall be the confidence of the house of Israel no more
Ezekiel29:17 In the twenty-seventh year
Ezekiel29:18 Son of man
Ezekiel29:19 Therefore thus says the Lord God: Behold
Ezekiel29:20 I have given him the land of Egypt for his labor with which he labored against it
Ezekiel29:21 On that day I will cause the horn of the house of Israel to spring forth
Ezekiel30:1 The word of the Lord came to me again
Ezekiel30:2 Son of man
Ezekiel30:3 For the day is near
Ezekiel30:4 The sword shall come upon Egypt
Ezekiel30:5 Cush
Ezekiel30:6 Thus says the Lord: Indeed
Ezekiel30:7 They shall be desolate in the midst of the countries that are desolate
Ezekiel30:8 They shall know that I am the Lord when I have set a fire in Egypt and when all her helpers are destroyed.
Ezekiel30:9 In that day messengers shall go forth from Me in ships to make the careless Cushites afraid
Ezekiel30:10 Thus says the Lord God: I will also make the multitude of Egypt to cease by the hand of Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon.
Ezekiel30:11 He and his people with him
Ezekiel30:12 I will make the rivers dry
Ezekiel30:13 Thus says the Lord God: I will also destroy the idols
Ezekiel30:14 I will make Pathros desolate
Ezekiel30:15 I will pour out My fury upon Pelusium
Ezekiel30:16 I will set fire in Egypt; Pelusium shall have great pain
Ezekiel30:17 The young men of Heliopolis and of Bubastis shall fall by the sword
Ezekiel30:18 In Tahpanhes also the day shall be darkened
Ezekiel30:19 Thus I will execute judgments in Egypt
Ezekiel30:20 In the eleventh year
Ezekiel30:21 Son of man
Ezekiel30:22 Therefore thus says the Lord God: Behold
Ezekiel30:23 I will scatter the Egyptians among the nations and disperse them among the countries.
Ezekiel30:24 For I will strengthen the arms of the king of Babylon and put My sword in his hand. But I will break the arms of Pharaoh so that he groans before him with the groanings of a wounded man.
Ezekiel30:25 So I will strengthen the arms of the king of Babylon
Ezekiel30:26 When I scatter the Egyptians among the nations and disperse them among the countries
Ezekiel31:1 In the eleventh year
Ezekiel31:2 Son of man
Ezekiel31:3 Behold
Ezekiel31:4 The waters made it great; the deep set it up on high. With its rivers it continually ran all around its plants
Ezekiel31:5 Therefore its height was exalted above all the trees of the field
Ezekiel31:6 All the fowl of heaven made their nests in its boughs; and under its branches all the beasts of the field gave birth; and under its shadow all great nations lived.
Ezekiel31:7 Thus it was fair in its greatness
Ezekiel31:8 The cedars in the garden of God could not match it; the fir trees were not like its boughs
Ezekiel31:9 I made it beautiful with an abundance of branches
Ezekiel31:10 Therefore
Ezekiel31:11 I therefore will deliver it into the hand of the mighty one of the nations. He shall surely deal with it. I have driven it out for its wickedness.
Ezekiel31:12 Foreigners
Ezekiel31:13 Upon its ruin all the fowl of the heavens shall remain
Ezekiel31:14 to the end that none of all the trees by the waters exalt themselves for their height
Ezekiel31:15 Thus says the Lord God: On the day when it went down to Sheol I caused mourning. I covered the deep over it and restrained its rivers
Ezekiel31:16 I made the nations to shake at the sound of its fall when I cast him down to Sheol with those who descend into the pit. And all the well-watered trees of Eden
Ezekiel31:17 They also went down into Sheol with it to those who were slain with the sword. And those who were its power lived under its shadow in the midst of the nations.
Ezekiel31:18 To whom are you thus like in glory and in greatness among the trees of Eden? Yet you shall be brought down with the trees of Eden to the nether parts of the earth. You shall lie in the midst of the uncircumcised with those who were slain by the sword. This is Pharaoh and all his multitude
Ezekiel32:1 In the twelfth year
Ezekiel32:2 Son of man
Ezekiel32:3 Thus says the Lord God: I will therefore spread out My net over you with a company of many peoples
Ezekiel32:4 Then I will leave you upon the land; I will cast you out on the open field
Ezekiel32:5 I will lay your flesh upon the mountains
Ezekiel32:6 I will also water the land with your blood
Ezekiel32:7 When I put you out
Ezekiel32:8 All the bright lights in the heavens I will make dark over you
Ezekiel32:9 I will also vex the hearts of many peoples when I bring your destruction among the nations
Ezekiel32:10 Indeed
Ezekiel32:11 For thus says the Lord God: The sword of the king of Babylon shall come upon you.
Ezekiel32:12 By the swords of the mighty
Ezekiel32:13 I also will destroy all the beasts from beside many waters; nor shall the foot of man muddy them anymore
Ezekiel32:14 Then I will make their waters settle
Ezekiel32:15 When I make the land of Egypt desolate
Ezekiel32:16 This is the lamentation
Ezekiel32:17 In the twelfth year
Ezekiel32:18 Son of man
Ezekiel32:19 “Whom do you surpass in beauty? Descend and make your bed with the uncircumcised.”
Ezekiel32:20 They shall fall in the midst of those who are slain by the sword. She is delivered to the sword. They have drawn her and all her multitudes away.
Ezekiel32:21 The strong among the mighty shall speak to him and those who help him out of the midst of Sheol: “They have gone down
Ezekiel32:22 Assyria is there and all her company. Her graves are around her. All of them are slain
Ezekiel32:23 whose graves are set in the sides of the pit and her company is all around her grave. All of them are slain
Ezekiel32:24 There is Elam and all her multitude all around her grave
Ezekiel32:25 They have set a bed for her in the midst of the slain with all her multitude. Her graves are all around it. All of them uncircumcised
Ezekiel32:26 There is Meshech
Ezekiel32:27 They shall not lie with the mighty who are fallen of the uncircumcised
Ezekiel32:28 Indeed
Ezekiel32:29 There is Edom also
Ezekiel32:30 There are also the officials of the north
Ezekiel32:31 Pharaoh shall see them
Ezekiel32:32 Though I have caused a terror of him in the land of the living
Ezekiel33:1 Again the word of the Lord came to me
Ezekiel33:2 Son of man

Ezekiel33:4 then whoever hears the sound of the trumpet and does not take warning
Ezekiel33:5 He heard the sound of the trumpet yet did not take warning. His blood shall be upon himself. But he who takes warning delivers his soul.
Ezekiel33:6 But if the watchman sees the sword come and does not blow the trumpet and the people are not warned and a sword comes and takes a person from among them
Ezekiel33:7 Now as for you

Ezekiel33:9 Nevertheless

Ezekiel33:14 Again
Ezekiel33:15 if the wicked man restores a pledge
Ezekiel33:16 None of his sins that he has committed shall be remembered against him. He has done what is lawful and right. He shall surely live.

Ezekiel33:18 When the righteous turns from his righteousness and commits iniquity
Ezekiel33:19 But if the wicked turns from his wickedness and does that which is lawful and right
Ezekiel33:20 Yet you say

Ezekiel34:1 And the word of the Lord came to me
Ezekiel34:2 Son of man
Ezekiel34:3 You eat the fat and clothe yourself with the wool; you kill those who are fed without feeding the flock.
Ezekiel34:4 The diseased you have not strengthened
Ezekiel34:5 They were scattered because there was no shepherd. And they became meat to all the beasts of the field and were scattered.
Ezekiel34:6 My sheep wandered through all the mountains and upon every high hill. Indeed
Ezekiel34:7 Therefore

Ezekiel34:9 Therefore

Ezekiel34:11 For thus says the Lord God: Behold
Ezekiel34:12 As a shepherd seeks out his flock in the day that he is among his sheep that are scattered
Ezekiel34:13 I will bring them out from the peoples
Ezekiel34:14 I will feed them in a good pasture
Ezekiel34:15 I will feed My flock
Ezekiel34:16 I will seek that which was lost and bring back that which was driven away and bind up that which was broken and will strengthen that which was sick. But I will destroy the fat and the strong. I will feed them with judgment.
Ezekiel34:17 As for you
Ezekiel34:18 Does it seem a small thing to you to have eaten up the good pasture
Ezekiel34:19 And as for My flock

Ezekiel34:22 therefore I will save My flock
Ezekiel34:23 I will set up one shepherd over them
Ezekiel34:24 I the Lord will be their God

Ezekiel34:26 I will make them and the places all around My hill a blessing. And I will cause the showers to come down in their season. They shall be showers of blessing.
Ezekiel34:27 The tree of the field shall yield its fruit
Ezekiel34:28 They shall no more be a prey to the nations
Ezekiel34:29 I will raise up for them a planting place of renown
Ezekiel34:30 Thus shall they know that I
Ezekiel34:31 As for you

Ezekiel36:4 Therefore

Ezekiel36:8 But you

Ezekiel36:26 Also

Ezekiel36:28 You will dwell in the land that I gave to your fathers. And you will be My people
Ezekiel36:29 I will also save you from all your uncleanness. And I will call for the grain and increase it and lay no famine upon you.
Ezekiel36:30 I will multiply the fruit of the tree and the increase of the field so that you shall receive no more reproach of famine among the nations.
Ezekiel36:31 Then you shall remember your evil ways and your deeds that were not good
Ezekiel36:32 Not for your sake am I doing this

Ezekiel38:19 For in My jealousy and in the fire of My wrath I have spoken: Surely in that day there shall be a great earthquake in the land of Israel.
Ezekiel38:20 The fish of the sea and the fowl of the heavens and the beasts of the field and all creeping things that creep upon the earth and all the men who are upon the face of the earth shall shake at My presence

Ezekiel39:1 Moreover you
Ezekiel39:2 And I will turn you back
Ezekiel39:3 And I will strike your bow out of your left hand and will cause your arrows to fall out of your right hand.
Ezekiel39:4 You shall fall upon the mountains of Israel
Ezekiel39:5 You shall fall upon the open field
Ezekiel39:6 I will send a fire on Magog and among those who dwell safely in the coastlands. Then they shall know that I am the Lord.
Ezekiel39:7 So I will make My holy name known in the midst of My people Israel. And I will not let them pollute My holy name anymore. And the nations shall know that I am the Lord
Ezekiel39:8 Behold
Ezekiel39:9 Those who dwell in the cities of Israel shall go and make fires with the weapons and burn them
Ezekiel39:10 They shall take no wood out of the field
Ezekiel39:11 It shall come to pass in that day that I will give to Gog a place of graves there in Israel
Ezekiel39:12 For seven months the house of Israel shall be burying them so that they may cleanse the land.
Ezekiel39:13 Indeed
Ezekiel39:14 They shall separate men who continually pass through the land to bury the travelers
Ezekiel39:15 As the passengers pass through the land and anyone sees a man’s bone
Ezekiel39:16 Also the name of the city shall be Hamonah. Thus they shall cleanse the land.
Ezekiel39:17 As for you
Ezekiel39:18 You shall eat the flesh of the mighty and drink the blood of the officials of the earth as though of rams
Ezekiel39:19 You shall eat fat until you are full and drink blood until you are drunk from My sacrifice which I have sacrificed for you.
Ezekiel39:20 Thus you shall be filled at My table with horses and chariots
Ezekiel39:21 I will set My glory among the nations
Ezekiel39:22 So the house of Israel shall know that I am the Lord their God from that day and forward.
Ezekiel39:23 The nations shall know that the house of Israel went into captivity for their iniquity because they trespassed against Me
Ezekiel39:24 According to their uncleanness and according to their transgressions
Ezekiel39:25 Therefore thus says the Lord God: Now I will restore the fortunes of Jacob and have mercy on the whole house of Israel and will be jealous for My holy name.
Ezekiel39:26 They shall forget their shame and all their trespasses by which they have trespassed against Me when they lived safely in their land and no one made them afraid.
Ezekiel39:27 When I have brought them back from the peoples and gathered them out of the lands of their enemies
Ezekiel39:28 Then they shall know that I am the Lord their God who caused them to be led into captivity among the nations
Ezekiel39:29 Nor will I hide My face from them anymore
Ezekiel40:1 In the twenty-fifth year of our captivity
Ezekiel40:2 In the visions of God He brought me into the land of Israel and set me upon a very high mountain
Ezekiel40:3 When he brought me there
Ezekiel40:4 The man said to me
Ezekiel40:5 And behold
Ezekiel40:6 Then he went to the gateway facing east; and he went up its stairs and measured the threshold of the gate
Ezekiel40:7 Every little chamber was one reed long and one reed wide. And between the little chambers were five cubits. And the threshold of the gate by the vestibule of the inside gate was one reed.
Ezekiel40:8 He measured also the vestibule of the gate within
Ezekiel40:9 Then he measured the vestibule of the gate
Ezekiel40:10 The little chambers of the gate eastward were three on this side and three on that side. The three were of one measurement. And the posts had one measure on this side and on that side.
Ezekiel40:11 He measured the width of the entrance of the gate
Ezekiel40:12 The space also before the little chambers was one cubit on this side
Ezekiel40:13 He measured then the gate from the roof of one little chamber to the roof of another; the width was twenty-five cubits
Ezekiel40:14 He measured the posts
Ezekiel40:15 From the face of the gate of the entrance to the face of the vestibule of the inner gate was fifty cubits.
Ezekiel40:16 There were narrow windows in the little chambers and in their posts inside the gate all around
Ezekiel40:17 Then he brought me into the outer court
Ezekiel40:18 The pavement was by the side of the gates
Ezekiel40:19 Then he measured the width from the front of the lower gate to the outer front of the inner court
Ezekiel40:20 As for the gate of the outer court that faced north
Ezekiel40:21 Its little chambers
Ezekiel40:22 Its windows
Ezekiel40:23 The gate of the inner court was opposite the gate on the north
Ezekiel40:24 After that he brought me toward the south
Ezekiel40:25 There were windows in it and in its arches all around like those windows. The length was fifty cubits
Ezekiel40:26 There were seven steps to go up to it
Ezekiel40:27 There was a gate in the inner court facing south. And he measured from gate to gate toward the south
Ezekiel40:28 He brought me to the inner court by the south gate. And he measured the south gate according to these same measurements.
Ezekiel40:29 And its little chambers and its posts and its arches were according to these same measurements. And there were windows in it and in its arches all around. It was fifty cubits long and twenty-five cubits wide.
Ezekiel40:30 The arches all around were twenty-five cubits long and five cubits wide.
Ezekiel40:31 Its arches faced the outer court. And palm tree ornaments were on its posts. And its staircase had eight steps.
Ezekiel40:32 He brought me into the inner court facing east. And he measured the gate according to these same measurements.
Ezekiel40:33 Its little chambers and its posts and its arches were according to these same measurements. And there were windows in it and in its arches all around. It was fifty cubits long and twenty-five cubits wide.
Ezekiel40:34 Its arches faced the outer court. And palm tree ornaments were on its posts
Ezekiel40:35 He brought me to the north gate and measured it according to these same measurements;
Ezekiel40:36 its little chambers
Ezekiel40:37 Its posts faced the outer court. And palm tree ornaments were on its posts
Ezekiel40:38 The chambers and its entrance were by the posts of the gates where they washed the burnt offering.
Ezekiel40:39 In the vestibule of the gate were two tables on this side and two tables on that side
Ezekiel40:40 On the outside of the vestibule
Ezekiel40:41 Four tables were on this side and four tables on that side by the side of the gate
Ezekiel40:42 The four tables were of hewn stone for the burnt offering
Ezekiel40:43 Within were hooks
Ezekiel40:44 Outside the inner gate were the chambers for the singers in the inner court
Ezekiel40:45 He said to me
Ezekiel40:46 The chamber that faces north is for the priests who have charge of the altar. These are the sons of Zadok among the sons of Levi
Ezekiel40:47 So he measured the court
Ezekiel40:48 Then he brought me to the vestibule of the temple and measured each post of the vestibule
Ezekiel40:49 The length of the vestibule was twenty cubits and the width eleven cubits. And he brought me by the steps whereby they went up to it. And there were pillars by the posts
Ezekiel41:1 Afterward he brought me to the temple and measured the posts
Ezekiel41:2 The width of the door was ten cubits. And the sides of the door were five cubits on the one side and five cubits on the other side. And he measured its length
Ezekiel41:3 Then he went inward and measured the post of the door
Ezekiel41:4 So he measured its length
Ezekiel41:5 Afterward he measured the wall of the temple
Ezekiel41:6 The side chambers were in three stories
Ezekiel41:7 The side chambers became wider with each successive story going up because the structures went up all around the temple. Therefore the width of the temple increased going upwards and thus one went up from the lowest to the highest level through the middle level.
Ezekiel41:8 I saw also the height all around the temple. The foundations of the side chambers were a full reed of six great cubits.
Ezekiel41:9 The thickness of the wall of the side chambers was five cubits. And that which was left was the place of the side chambers that were within.
Ezekiel41:10 Between the chambers was the wideness of twenty cubits all around the temple on every side.
Ezekiel41:11 The doors of the side chambers opened onto the open place
Ezekiel41:12 Now the building that was facing the courtyard on the west side was seventy cubits wide. And the wall of the building was five cubits thick all around
Ezekiel41:13 So he measured the temple
Ezekiel41:14 also the width of the eastern face of the temple and the courtyard was a hundred cubits.
Ezekiel41:15 Then he measured the length of the building facing the yard at the west
Ezekiel41:16 the doorposts and the narrow windows. The galleries all around their three stories opposite the threshold were paneled with wood from the ground to the windows (the windows were covered)
Ezekiel41:17 from the space above the door
Ezekiel41:18 It was made with cherubim and palm trees
Ezekiel41:19 so that the face of a man was toward the palm tree on the one side
Ezekiel41:20 From the ground to the top of the door and on the wall of the nave
Ezekiel41:21 The posts of the temple and the face of the sanctuary were squared. The appearance of the one was as the appearance of the other.
Ezekiel41:22 The altar of wood was three cubits high
Ezekiel41:23 The temple and the sanctuary had two doors.
Ezekiel41:24 The doors had two leaves apiece
Ezekiel41:25 There were made on them
Ezekiel41:26 There were beveled windows and palm trees on both sides
Ezekiel42:1 Then he brought me out into the outer court
Ezekiel42:2 Before the length of a hundred cubits was the north door
Ezekiel42:3 Opposite the twenty cubits that belonged to the inner court
Ezekiel42:4 In front of the chambers was a passage on the inner side
Ezekiel42:5 Now the upper chambers were narrow
Ezekiel42:6 For they were in three stories
Ezekiel42:7 And a wall that was outside was parallel to the chambers
Ezekiel42:8 For the length of the chambers that were in the outer court was fifty cubits. And the length of those before the temple was a hundred cubits.
Ezekiel42:9 From under these chambers was the entrance on the east side as one goes into them from the outer court.
Ezekiel42:10 The chambers were in the thickness of the wall of the court toward the east
Ezekiel42:11 with a passage in front of them; they were like the appearance of the chambers which were toward the north
Ezekiel42:12 Corresponding to the doors of the chambers that were facing south was a door at the head of the passage way
Ezekiel42:13 Then he said to me
Ezekiel42:14 When the priests enter it
Ezekiel42:15 Now when he had made an end of measuring the inner temple
Ezekiel42:16 He measured the east side with the measuring reed
Ezekiel42:17 He measured the north side with the measuring reed round about
Ezekiel42:18 He measured the south side with the measuring reed
Ezekiel42:19 He turned about to the west side and measured five hundred reeds with the measuring reed.
Ezekiel42:20 He measured it by the four sides. It had a wall all around
Ezekiel43:1 Afterward he brought me to the gate
Ezekiel43:2 And behold
Ezekiel43:3 It was according to the appearance of the vision which I saw
Ezekiel43:4 The glory of the Lord came into the temple by the way of the gate facing east.
Ezekiel43:5 So the Spirit took me up and brought me into the inner court; and behold
Ezekiel43:6 Then I heard one speaking to me out of the temple. And a man stood by me.
Ezekiel43:7 He said to me: Son of man
Ezekiel43:8 By setting their threshold by My threshold and their post by My posts and the wall between Me and them
Ezekiel43:9 Now let them put away their harlotry and the corpses of their kings far from Me
Ezekiel43:10 As for you
Ezekiel43:11 If they are ashamed of all that they have done
Ezekiel43:12 This is the law of the temple: The whole territory on the top of the mountain all around shall be most holy. Behold
Ezekiel43:13 These are the measurements of the altar by cubits (the cubit is one cubit and one handbreadth): The base shall be one cubit
Ezekiel43:14 From the bottom on the ground even to the lower ledge shall be two cubits
Ezekiel43:15 So the altar shall be four cubits. And from the altar and upward shall be four horns.
Ezekiel43:16 The altar hearth shall be twelve cubits long by twelve wide
Ezekiel43:17 The ledge shall be fourteen cubits long and fourteen wide in the four squares. And the border about it shall be half a cubit. And its bottom shall be one cubit all around. And its stairs shall face east.
Ezekiel43:18 He said to me: Son of man
Ezekiel43:19 You shall give to the priests the Levites that are of the seed of Zadok who approach Me to minister to Me
Ezekiel43:20 You shall take of its blood and put it on its four horns
Ezekiel43:21 You shall take the bull also of the sin offering
Ezekiel43:22 On the second day you shall offer a male goat without blemish as a sin offering. And they shall cleanse the altar as they cleansed it with the bull.
Ezekiel43:23 When you have made an end of cleansing it
Ezekiel43:24 You shall offer them before the Lord
Ezekiel43:25 For seven days you shall prepare every day a goat as a sin offering. They shall also prepare a young bull and a ram out of the flock
Ezekiel43:26 For seven days they shall purge the altar and purify it. So they shall consecrate it.
Ezekiel43:27 When they have completed the days
Ezekiel44:1 Then he brought me back to the gate of the outer sanctuary
Ezekiel44:2 Then the Lord said to me: This gate shall be shut; it shall not be opened
Ezekiel44:3 As for the prince
Ezekiel44:4 Then he brought me by the way of the north gate before the temple; and I looked
Ezekiel44:5 The Lord said to me: Son of man
Ezekiel44:6 You shall say to the rebellious
Ezekiel44:7 you brought foreigners into My sanctuary
Ezekiel44:8 You have not kept the charge of My holy things. But you have set foreigners to keep charge of My sanctuary.
Ezekiel44:9 Thus says the Lord God: No foreigner
Ezekiel44:10 But the Levites who have gone far from Me when Israel went astray
Ezekiel44:11 Yet they shall be ministers in My sanctuary
Ezekiel44:12 Because they ministered to them before their idols and caused the house of Israel to fall into iniquity
Ezekiel44:13 They shall not come near to Me to do the office of a priest to Me
Ezekiel44:14 But I will make them keepers of the charge of the temple for all its service and for all that shall be done in it.
Ezekiel44:15 But the Levitical priests
Ezekiel44:16 They shall enter My sanctuary
Ezekiel44:17 It shall come to pass that when they enter at the gates of the inner court
Ezekiel44:18 They shall have linen turbans on their heads and shall have linen breeches on their loins. They shall not gird themselves with anything that causes sweat.
Ezekiel44:19 When they go out into the outer court
Ezekiel44:20 They shall not shave their heads
Ezekiel44:21 Nor shall any priest drink wine when they enter the inner court.
Ezekiel44:22 They shall not take for their wives a widow
Ezekiel44:23 They shall teach My people the difference between the holy and profane
Ezekiel44:24 In controversy they shall stand in judgment. And they shall judge it according to My judgments. And they shall keep My laws and My statutes in all My assemblies. And they shall make holy My Sabbaths.
Ezekiel44:25 They shall not go to a dead person to defile themselves. But for father
Ezekiel44:26 After he is cleansed
Ezekiel44:27 In the day that he goes into the sanctuary
Ezekiel44:28 It shall be to them for an inheritance. I am their inheritance. And you shall give them no possession in Israel. I am their possession.
Ezekiel44:29 They shall eat the grain offering and the sin offering and the guilt offering. And every dedicated thing in Israel shall be theirs.
Ezekiel44:30 The first of all the firstfruits of all things and every contribution of all of every sort of your gifts shall be the priest’s. You shall also give to the priest the first of your dough to cause a blessing to rest in your house.
Ezekiel44:31 The priests shall not eat any bird or beast that has died naturally or was torn to pieces.
Ezekiel45:1 Moreover
Ezekiel45:2 Of this there shall be a square plot for the sanctuary
Ezekiel45:3 Of this measure you shall measure the length of twenty-five thousand cubits and the width of ten thousand cubits. And in it shall be the sanctuary
Ezekiel45:4 The holy portion of the land shall be for the priests
Ezekiel45:5 An area twenty-five thousand cubits in length and ten thousand in width shall be for the Levites
Ezekiel45:6 You shall appoint the possession of the city five thousand cubits wide and twenty-five thousand cubits long adjacent to the allotment of the holy portion. It shall be for the whole house of Israel.
Ezekiel45:7 A portion shall be for the prince on the one side and on the other side of the holy allotment and property of the city
Ezekiel45:8 In the land shall be his possession in Israel. And My officials shall no more oppress My people. And the rest of the land they shall give to the house of Israel according to their tribes.
Ezekiel45:9 Thus says the Lord God: Let it suffice you officials of Israel. Remove violence and destruction
Ezekiel45:10 You shall have just balances and a just ephah and a just bath.
Ezekiel45:11 The ephah and the bath shall be of one measure so that the bath may contain one-tenth of a homer
Ezekiel45:12 The shekel shall be twenty gerahs. Twenty shekels
Ezekiel45:13 This is the offering that you shall offer: one-sixth of an ephah of a homer of wheat
Ezekiel45:14 Concerning the ordinance of oil that is the bath of oil
Ezekiel45:15 And one lamb shall be out of the flock
Ezekiel45:16 All the people of the land shall give this offering for the prince in Israel.
Ezekiel45:17 It shall be the prince’s part to give burnt offerings and grain offerings and drink offerings in the feasts and in the New Moons and in the Sabbaths
Ezekiel45:18 Thus says the Lord God: In the first month
Ezekiel45:19 And the priest shall take some of the blood of the sin offering and put it on the doorposts of the temple and on the four corners of the ledge of the altar and on the gateposts of the inner court.
Ezekiel45:20 So you shall do the seventh day of the month for everyone who errs and for him who is naive. So you shall make atonement for the temple.
Ezekiel45:21 In the first month
Ezekiel45:22 On that day the prince shall prepare for himself and for all the people of the land a bull as a sin offering.
Ezekiel45:23 For seven days of the feast he shall prepare a burnt offering to the Lord
Ezekiel45:24 He shall prepare a grain offering of an ephah for a bull and an ephah for a ram and a hin of oil for an ephah.
Ezekiel45:25 In the seventh month
Ezekiel46:1 Thus says the Lord God: The gate of the inner court that faces east shall be shut the six working days. But on the Sabbath it shall be opened
Ezekiel46:2 The prince shall enter by the way of the vestibule of that gate from outside
Ezekiel46:3 Likewise the people of the land shall worship at the door of this gate before the Lord in the Sabbaths and in the New Moons.
Ezekiel46:4 The burnt offering that the prince shall offer to the Lord in the Sabbath day shall be six lambs without blemish and a ram without blemish.
Ezekiel46:5 The grain offering shall be an ephah for a ram and the grain offering for the lambs as he shall be able to give and a hin of oil to an ephah.
Ezekiel46:6 In the day of the New Moon it shall be a young bull without blemish and six lambs and a ram. They shall be without blemish.
Ezekiel46:7 He shall prepare a grain offering
Ezekiel46:8 When the prince enters
Ezekiel46:9 But when the people of the land shall come before the Lord in the solemn feasts
Ezekiel46:10 The prince shall be in their midst. When they enter
Ezekiel46:11 At the feasts and the appointed festivals the grain offering shall be an ephah for a bull
Ezekiel46:12 Now when the prince prepares a voluntary burnt offering or voluntary peace offering to the Lord
Ezekiel46:13 You shall daily prepare a burnt offering to the Lord of a lamb of the first year without blemish. You shall prepare it every morning.
Ezekiel46:14 You shall prepare a grain offering for it every morning
Ezekiel46:15 Thus they shall prepare the lamb and the grain offering and the oil every morning as a continual burnt offering.
Ezekiel46:16 Thus says the Lord God: If the prince gives a gift out of his inheritance to any of his sons
Ezekiel46:17 But if he gives a gift out of his inheritance to one of his servants
Ezekiel46:18 Moreover the prince shall not take of the people’s inheritance by oppression to thrust them out of their possession. But he shall give his sons inheritance out of his own possession so that My people not be scattered

Ezekiel46:20 Then he said to me

Ezekiel46:22 In the four corners of the court there were courts joined of forty cubits long and thirty wide. These four corners were of one measurement.

Ezekiel47:5 Afterward he measured one thousand. And it was a river that I could not pass over
Ezekiel47:6 He said to me
Ezekiel47:7 When I had returned I saw on the bank of the river very many trees on the one side and on the other.
Ezekiel47:8 Then he said to me
Ezekiel47:9 Every living creature that swarms
Ezekiel47:10 It shall come to pass that the fishermen shall stand upon it. From En-gedi even to En-eglaim there shall be a place to spread out nets. Their fish shall be according to their kinds
Ezekiel47:11 But its miry places and its marshes shall not be healed. They shall be given to salt.
Ezekiel47:12 By the river upon its bank
