Daniel1:1 In the third year of the reign of Jehoiakim king of Judah
Daniel1:2 The Lord gave Jehoiakim king of Judah into his hand
Daniel1:3 The king spoke to Ashpenaz the master of his officials that he should bring some of the Israelites and some of the king’s descendants and some of the nobles
Daniel1:4 youths in whom was no blemish
Daniel1:5 The king appointed them a daily provision of the king’s food and of the wine which he drank. They were to be educated for three years
Daniel1:6 Now among them were of the Judahites: Daniel
Daniel1:7 to whom the commander of the officials gave names. And he gave to Daniel the name of Belteshazzar; to Hananiah
Daniel1:8 But Daniel purposed in his heart that he would not defile himself with the portion of the king’s food
Daniel1:9 Now God had brought Daniel into favor and compassion with the master of the officials.
Daniel1:10 The master of the officials said to Daniel
Daniel1:11 Then Daniel said to the steward
Daniel1:12 “Please test your servants for ten days
Daniel1:13 Then let our countenances be looked upon before you
Daniel1:14 So he consented to them in this matter and tested them for ten days.
Daniel1:15 At the end of ten days their countenances appeared fairer and fatter than all the youths who ate the portion of the king’s food.
Daniel1:16 Thus the guard continued to take away the portion of their food and the wine that they were to drink
Daniel1:17 As for these four youths
Daniel1:18 Now at the end of the days that the king had set for them to be brought in
Daniel1:19 The king spoke with them
Daniel1:20 In all matters of wisdom and understanding which the king inquired of them
Daniel1:21 Daniel continued even to the first year of King Cyrus.
Daniel2:1 And in the second year of the reign of Nebuchadnezzar
Daniel2:2 Then the king gave the command to call the magicians and the astrologers and the sorcerers and the Chaldeans to tell the king his dreams. So they came and stood before the king.
Daniel2:3 The king said to them
Daniel2:4 Then the Chaldeans spoke to the king in Aramaic
Daniel2:5 The king answered the Chaldeans
Daniel2:6 But if you tell the dream and its interpretation
Daniel2:7 They answered again and said
Daniel2:8 The king answered
Daniel2:9 If you will not make known to me the dream
Daniel2:10 The Chaldeans answered before the king and said
Daniel2:11 Also
Daniel2:12 For this cause the king was angry and very furious
Daniel2:13 Therefore the decree went out that the wise men should be slain. And they sought for Daniel and his companions to be slain.
Daniel2:14 Then Daniel answered with discretion and wisdom to Arioch the captain of the king’s guard
Daniel2:15 He asked Arioch
Daniel2:16 Then Daniel went in and desired of the king that he would give him time in order that he could tell the king the interpretation.
Daniel2:17 Then Daniel went to his house and made the thing known to Hananiah
Daniel2:18 so that they might ask for compassion from the God of heaven concerning this secret
Daniel2:19 Then the secret was revealed to Daniel in a night vision. Then Daniel blessed the God of heaven.
Daniel2:20 Daniel said: “Blessed be the name of God forever and ever
Daniel2:21 It is He who changes the times and the seasons; He removes kings and sets up kings; He gives wisdom to the wise and knowledge to those who know understanding.
Daniel2:22 He reveals the deep and secret things; He knows what is in the darkness
Daniel2:23 I thank and praise You
Daniel2:24 Therefore Daniel went in to Arioch
Daniel2:25 Then in haste Arioch brought Daniel before the king and said thus to him
Daniel2:26 The king said to Daniel
Daniel2:27 Daniel answered in the presence of the king and said
Daniel2:28 But there is a God in heaven who reveals secrets and makes known to King Nebuchadnezzar what shall be in the latter days. Your dream and the visions of your head upon your bed are these:
Daniel2:29 “As for you
Daniel2:30 But as for me
Daniel2:31 “You saw
Daniel2:32 This image’s head was of fine gold
Daniel2:33 its legs of iron
Daniel2:34 You watched until a stone was cut out without hands which struck the image upon its feet
Daniel2:35 Then the iron
Daniel2:36 “This was the dream. Now we will tell its interpretation before the king.
Daniel2:37 You
Daniel2:38 Wherever the sons of men dwell
Daniel2:39 “After you
Daniel2:40 And the fourth kingdom shall be strong as iron
Daniel2:41 As you saw the feet and toes
Daniel2:42 As the toes of the feet were partly of iron and partly of clay
Daniel2:43 Whereas you saw iron mixed with miry clay
Daniel2:44 “In the days of these kings the God of heaven shall set up a kingdom which shall never be destroyed. And the kingdom shall not be left to another people
Daniel2:45 Inasmuch as you saw that the stone was cut out of the mountain without hands and that it broke in pieces the iron
Daniel2:46 Then King Nebuchadnezzar fell upon his face and did homage to Daniel
Daniel2:47 The king answered Daniel and said
Daniel2:48 Then the king advanced Daniel and gave him many great gifts
Daniel2:49 Then Daniel requested of the king

Daniel5:2 While he tasted the wine
Daniel5:3 Then they brought the golden vessels that were taken out of the temple of the house of God which was at Jerusalem. And the king and his officials
Daniel5:4 They drank wine and praised the gods of gold and of silver

Daniel5:8 Then all the king’s wise men came in

Daniel5:28 Peres***: your kingdom has been divided and given to the Medes and Persians.”

Daniel6:2 and over them three presidents

Daniel6:14 Then the king
Daniel6:15 Then these men came by agreement to the king and said to the king

Daniel6:18 Then the king went to his palace and passed the night fasting
Daniel6:19 Then the king arose very early in the morning and went in haste to the den of lions.
Daniel6:20 When he came to the den
Daniel6:21 Then Daniel said to the king
Daniel6:22 My God has sent His angel and has shut the lions’ mouths so that they have not hurt me
Daniel6:23 Then the king was exceeding glad for him and commanded that they take Daniel up out of the den. So Daniel was taken up out of the den
Daniel6:24 Then the king commanded

Daniel6:27 He delivers and rescues
Daniel6:28 So this Daniel prospered in the reign of Darius and in the reign of Cyrus the Persian.
Daniel7:1 In the first year of Belshazzar king of Babylon
Daniel7:2 Daniel spoke and said: I saw in my vision by night
Daniel7:3 Four great beasts came up from the sea
Daniel7:4 The first was like a lion and had eagle’s wings. I watched until its wings were plucked and it was lifted up from the earth and made to stand on the feet as a man
Daniel7:5 And behold
Daniel7:6 After this I looked
Daniel7:7 After this I saw in the visions at night
Daniel7:8 I considered the horns
Daniel7:9 I watched until the thrones were cast down and the Ancient of Days was seated
Daniel7:10 A fiery stream issued and came out from before Him. One thousand thousands ministered to Him; and ten thousand times ten thousand stood before Him. The judgment was set
Daniel7:11 Then I watched because of the sound of the boastful words which the horn was speaking. I watched even until the beast was slain
Daniel7:12 As for the rest of the beasts
Daniel7:13 I saw in the night visions
Daniel7:14 There was given to Him dominion
Daniel7:15 I
Daniel7:16 I approached one of those who were standing by and asked him the truth of all this. So he told me and made known to me the interpretation of these things.
Daniel7:17 “These great beasts
Daniel7:18 But the saints of the Most High shall take the kingdom and possess the kingdom forever
Daniel7:19 Then I desired to know the truth of the fourth beast
Daniel7:20 and of the ten horns that were in its head
Daniel7:21 I beheld
Daniel7:22 until the Ancient of Days came
Daniel7:23 Thus he said: “The fourth beast shall be the fourth kingdom upon earth
Daniel7:24 The ten horns out of this kingdom are ten kings that shall arise; and another shall rise after them
Daniel7:25 He shall speak words against the Most High and shall wear out the saints of the Most High and plan to change times and law. And they shall be given into his hand until a time and times and half a time.
Daniel7:26 “But the court shall sit for judgment
Daniel7:27 Then the kingdom and dominion
Daniel7:28 At this point the matter ended. As for me

Daniel8:2 I saw in the vision
Daniel8:3 Then I lifted up my eyes and looked
Daniel8:4 I saw the ram pushing westward and northward and southward. No animal could stand before him. There was not any who could deliver out of his hand
Daniel8:5 As I was considering this
Daniel8:6 He came to the ram that had two horns
Daniel8:7 I saw him come close to the ram

Daniel8:9 Out of one of them came a little horn
Daniel8:10 It grew great
Daniel8:11 Indeed
Daniel8:12 Because of rebellion
Daniel8:13 Then I heard one saint speaking
Daniel8:14 And he said to me

Daniel8:24 His power shall be mighty
Daniel8:25 By his cunning

Daniel9:2 in the first year of his reign
Daniel9:3 I set my face toward the Lord God to seek by prayer and supplications with fasting and sackcloth and ashes.

Daniel9:6 We have not listened to Your servants the prophets

Daniel9:8 O Lord
Daniel9:9 To the Lord our God belong mercies and forgiveness

Daniel9:19 O Lord

Daniel9:23 At the beginning of your supplications the command went out
Daniel9:24 “Seventy weeks have been determined for your people and upon your holy city
Daniel9:25 “Know therefore and understand that from the going forth of the command to restore and to rebuild Jerusalem until the Prince Messiah shall be seven weeks
Daniel9:26 After the sixty-two weeks Messiah shall be cut off and shall have nothing. And the troops of the prince who shall come shall destroy the city and the sanctuary. The end of it shall come with a flood. And until the end of the war desolations are determined.
Daniel9:27 And he shall make a firm covenant with many for one week. But in the middle of the week he shall cause the sacrifice and the offering to cease. And on the wing of abominations shall come one who makes desolate
Daniel10:1 In the third year of Cyrus king of Persia a message was revealed to Daniel
Daniel10:2 In those days I
Daniel10:3 I ate no tasty food; no meat or wine entered my mouth
Daniel10:4 On the twenty-fourth day of the first month
Daniel10:5 I lifted up my eyes and looked
Daniel10:6 His body also was like beryl
Daniel10:7 I
Daniel10:8 Therefore I was left alone and saw this great vision
Daniel10:9 Yet I heard the sound of his words; and while I heard the sound of his words
Daniel10:10 And behold
Daniel10:11 He said to me
Daniel10:12 Then he said to me
Daniel10:13 But the prince of the kingdom of Persia withstood me for twenty-one days. So Michael
Daniel10:14 Now I have come to make you understand what shall befall your people in the latter days. For the vision is yet for many days.”
Daniel10:15 When he had spoken such words to me
Daniel10:16 And behold
Daniel10:17 How can the servant of my lord talk with you
Daniel10:18 Then again
Daniel10:19 He said
Daniel10:20 Then he said
Daniel10:21 But I will show you what is inscribed in the Scripture of Truth. Yet there is no one who stands firmly with me against these forces
Daniel11:1 “Also
Daniel11:2 “And now I will tell you the truth. Behold
Daniel11:3 A mighty king shall stand up who shall rule with great dominion and do according to his will.
Daniel11:4 When he shall stand up
Daniel11:5 “The king of the South shall be strong
Daniel11:6 In the end of some years they shall join themselves together
Daniel11:7 “But out of a branch of her roots shall one stand up in his place
Daniel11:8 And he shall also carry captive into Egypt their gods
Daniel11:9 who will enter the kingdom of the king of the South
Daniel11:10 His sons shall wage war and assemble a multitude of armed forces. One shall advance like a flood and pass through
Daniel11:11 “The king of the South shall be moved with rage
Daniel11:12 When he has taken away the multitude
Daniel11:13 For the king of the North shall return and shall raise a multitude greater than the former
Daniel11:14 “In those times many shall stand up against the king of the South. Also the robbers of your people shall exalt themselves to fulfill the vision
Daniel11:15 Then the king of the North shall come and set up a siege mound and capture a fortified city. And the forces of the South shall not withstand him; neither shall his chosen best troops have strength to resist.
Daniel11:16 But he who comes against him shall do according to his own will
Daniel11:17 He shall also set his face to enter with the strength of his whole kingdom
Daniel11:18 After this he shall turn his face to the coastlands and shall capture many. But a commander shall put a stop to his reproach against him
Daniel11:19 Then he shall turn his face toward the fortresses of his own land
Daniel11:20 “Then one shall stand up in his place who shall send an oppressor in the glory of his kingdom. But within a few days he shall be destroyed
Daniel11:21 “In his place a vile person shall stand up
Daniel11:22 A flood of armies shall be swept away before him and be broken
Daniel11:23 After the league is made with him
Daniel11:24 He shall enter peaceably even upon the richest places of the realm. And he shall do that which his fathers have not done
Daniel11:25 “He shall stir up his power and his courage against the king of the South with a great army. And the king of the South shall be stirred up to battle with a very great and mighty army. But he shall not stand
Daniel11:26 Indeed
Daniel11:27 As for both of these kings
Daniel11:28 While turning back to his land with great wealth
Daniel11:29 “At the appointed time he shall return and come into the south
Daniel11:30 For the ships of Kittim shall come against him. Therefore he shall be grieved and return and have indignation against the holy covenant and take action. He shall even return and show regard to those who forsake the holy covenant.
Daniel11:31 “His armies shall rise up and desecrate the sanctuary fortress. They shall abolish the daily sacrifice and set up the abomination that makes desolate.
Daniel11:32 By flatteries he will corrupt those who act wickedly toward the covenant. But the people who know their God will be strong and take action.
Daniel11:33 “The wise among the people shall instruct many. Yet for many days they shall fall by the sword and by flame
Daniel11:34 Now when they fall
Daniel11:35 Some of the wise will fall
Daniel11:36 “The king shall do according to his will. And he shall exalt himself
Daniel11:37 He shall regard neither the gods of his fathers
Daniel11:38 But instead he shall honor the god of forces
Daniel11:39 Thus he shall do in the strongest of fortresses with a foreign god. He shall give great honor to those who acknowledge him
Daniel11:40 “At the time of the end the king of the South shall push at him. But the king of the North shall come against him like a whirlwind
Daniel11:41 He shall enter also the Pleasant Land
Daniel11:42 Then he shall stretch out his hand against the countries
Daniel11:43 But he shall have power over the hidden treasures of gold and of silver
Daniel11:44 But reports from the east and from the north shall trouble him. Therefore he shall go out with great fury to destroy and annihilate many.
Daniel11:45 He shall pitch the tabernacles of his palace between the seas in the beautiful holy mountain. Yet he shall come to his end
Daniel12:1 “And at that time Michael shall stand up
Daniel12:2 Many of those who sleep in the dust of the ground will awake—some to everlasting life
Daniel12:3 Those who are wise shall shine as the brightness of the expanse of heaven
Daniel12:4 But you
Daniel12:5 Then I
Daniel12:6 One said to the man clothed in linen
Daniel12:7 And I heard the man clothed in linen
Daniel12:8 As for me
Daniel12:9 And he said
Daniel12:10 Many shall be purified and made white and tried. But the wicked shall do wickedly
Daniel12:11 “From the time that the daily sacrifice shall be taken away and the abomination that makes desolate set up
Daniel12:12 Blessed is he who waits and comes to the one thousand three hundred and thirty-five days.
Daniel12:13 “But as for you
Hosea1:1 The word of the Lord that came to Hosea son of Beeri
Hosea1:2 When the Lord first spoke by Hosea
Hosea1:3 So he went and took Gomer the daughter of Diblaim as a wife. She conceived
Hosea1:4 The Lord said to him: “Call his name Jezreel
Hosea1:5 On that day
Hosea1:6 Then Gomer conceived again
Hosea1:7 But I will have mercy upon the house of Judah
Hosea1:8 When Gomer had weaned Lo-Ruhamah
Hosea1:9 Then the Lord said: “Call his name Lo-Ammi
Hosea1:10 Yet the number of the Israelites will be as the sand of the sea
Hosea1:11 Then will the Judahites and the Israelites be gathered together
Hosea2:1 Say to your brothers
Hosea2:2 Plead with your mother
Hosea2:3 lest I strip her naked and leave her as in the day that she was born
Hosea2:4 I will not have mercy upon her children
Hosea2:5 For their mother has played the whore. She that conceived them has acted shamefully. For she said
Hosea2:6 Therefore
Hosea2:7 She will pursue her lovers
Hosea2:8 But she did not know that it was I who provided her the grain
Hosea2:9 Therefore I will take back My grain in its time and My wine in its season
Hosea2:10 Therefore I will uncover her shame in the sight of her lovers
Hosea2:11 I will also bring to an end all her joy
Hosea2:12 I will destroy her vines and her fig trees
Hosea2:13 I will punish her for the days of the Baals
Hosea2:14 Therefore
Hosea2:15 From there
Hosea2:16 On that day
Hosea2:17 I will remove the names of the Baals from her mouth
Hosea2:18 On that day
Hosea2:19 I will take you for My wife forever. I will take you for My wife in righteousness and in justice
Hosea2:20 I will take you for My wife in faithfulness
Hosea2:21 On that day I will answer
Hosea2:22 The earth will answer the grain
Hosea2:23 Then I will sow her for Myself in the earth. I will have mercy upon Lo-Ruhamah
Hosea3:1 Then the Lord said to me
Hosea3:2 So I purchased her for myself for fifteen shekels of silver
Hosea3:3 Then I said to her
Hosea3:4 For the Israelites will remain many days without a king and without a prince
Hosea3:5 Afterward the Israelites will return and seek the Lord their God and David their king. They will come in fear to the Lord and to His goodness in the latter days.
Hosea4:1 Hear the word of the Lord
Hosea4:2 Swearing
Hosea4:3 Therefore the land dries up
Hosea4:4 Yet let no one contend
Hosea4:5 You will stumble in the day
Hosea4:6 My people are destroyed for lack of knowledge. Because you have rejected knowledge
Hosea4:7 As they increased
Hosea4:8 They feed upon the sin of My people
Hosea4:9 It will be like people
Hosea4:10 They will eat
Hosea4:11 harlotry; wine and new wine take away understanding.
Hosea4:12 My people seek counsel from their wood
Hosea4:13 They sacrifice upon the tops of the mountains
Hosea4:14 I will not punish your daughters when they play the whore
Hosea4:15 Though you play the whore
Hosea4:16 Like a stubborn heifer
Hosea4:17 Ephraim is joined to idols; let him alone.
Hosea4:18 Their drink has soured
Hosea4:19 A wind has wrapped her in its wings
Hosea5:1 Hear this
Hosea5:2 and a pit dug deep in Shittim; but I will discipline them all.
Hosea5:3 I know Ephraim
Hosea5:4 Their deeds do not allow them to return to their God; for a spirit of harlotry is in their midst
Hosea5:5 The pride of Israel testifies against him. Israel and Ephraim stumble in their iniquity. Judah also stumbles with them.
Hosea5:6 With their flocks and their herds they will go to seek the Lord
Hosea5:7 They have dealt faithlessly with the Lord
Hosea5:8 Blow the horn in Gibeah
Hosea5:9 Ephraim will become desolate in the day of punishment. Among the tribes of Israel I make known that which is sure.
Hosea5:10 The princes of Judah have become like those who remove a boundary marker. Upon them I will pour out My wrath like water.
Hosea5:11 Ephraim is oppressed and broken in judgment
Hosea5:12 Therefore I will be like pus to Ephraim and like rottenness to the house of Judah.
Hosea5:13 When Ephraim saw his sickness and Judah saw his wound
Hosea5:14 For I will be like a lion to Ephraim and like a young lion to the house of Judah. I
Hosea5:15 I will again return to My place until they acknowledge their offense and seek My face. In their affliction they will earnestly seek Me.
Hosea6:1 Come
Hosea6:2 After two days He will revive us. On the third day He will raise us up
Hosea6:3 Let us know
Hosea6:4 What shall I do to you
Hosea6:5 Therefore I have hewn them by the prophets. I have killed them by the words of My mouth
Hosea6:6 For I desired mercy
Hosea6:7 But like men they have transgressed the covenant. There they dealt faithlessly with Me.
Hosea6:8 Gilead is a city of evildoers
Hosea6:9 As robbers lie in wait for someone
Hosea6:10 I have seen a horrible thing in the house of Israel. The harlotry of Ephraim is there; Israel is defiled.
Hosea6:11 Also
Hosea7:1 When I would heal Israel
Hosea7:2 but they did not consider in their hearts that I remember all their wickedness. Now their own deeds surround them; they are before My face.
Hosea7:3 They make the king glad with their wickedness
Hosea7:4 They are all adulterers
Hosea7:5 On the day of our king the princes made him sick from the heat of wine. He stretched out his hand with scorners.
Hosea7:6 For they are kindled like an oven. Their heart burns within them; their anger smolders all night; in the morning it blazes like a flaming fire.
Hosea7:7 They are all hot as an oven
Hosea7:8 Ephraim mixes himself with the people. Ephraim is a cake not turned.
Hosea7:9 Foreigners devour his strength
Hosea7:10 The pride of Israel testifies against him
Hosea7:11 Ephraim is like a dove
Hosea7:12 Wherever they go
Hosea7:13 Woe to them
Hosea7:14 They do not cry to Me with their heart when they howl on their beds. They gather together for grain and wine
Hosea7:15 Though I trained and strengthened their arms
Hosea7:16 They return
Hosea8:1 Set the trumpet to your mouth! One like an eagle is on the house of the Lord
Hosea8:2 Israel cries to Me
Hosea8:3 Israel has cast off the good; the enemy will pursue him.
Hosea8:4 They set up kings
Hosea8:5 Your calf
Hosea8:6 For it is from Israel; a craftsman made it
Hosea8:7 For they sow the wind
Hosea8:8 Israel is swallowed up. Now they are among the nations as a vessel in which there is no pleasure.
Hosea8:9 For they have gone up to Assyria like a wild donkey alone by itself; Ephraim has hired lovers.
Hosea8:10 Though they hire among the nations
Hosea8:11 Because Ephraim has multiplied altars for sin
Hosea8:12 I have written for him the great things of My law
Hosea8:13 For the sacrifices of My offerings
Hosea8:14 Israel has forgotten his maker and built temples

Hosea9:5 What will you do on the appointed day
Hosea9:6 For behold
Hosea9:7 The days of punishment have come; the days of recompense have come. Israel knows! The prophet is a fool; the man of the spirit is insane
Hosea9:8 The watchman of Ephraim is a prophet for my God; yet a fowler’s snare is on all his ways
Hosea9:9 They have deeply corrupted themselves as in the days of Gibeah. He will remember their iniquity
Hosea9:10 Like grapes in the wilderness
Hosea9:11 As for Ephraim
Hosea9:12 Though they bring up their children
Hosea9:13 Just as I saw Ephraim like Tyre
Hosea9:14 Give them
Hosea9:15 All their wickedness is in Gilgal
Hosea9:16 Ephraim is stricken
Hosea9:17 My God will reject them because they did not listen to Him
Hosea10:1 Israel is a fertile vine that brings forth its fruit. As his fruit multiplied
Hosea10:2 Their heart is divided; now they must bear their guilt. He will break down their altars and destroy their pillars.
Hosea10:3 For now they will say
Hosea10:4 They speak mere words

Hosea10:8 The high places of Aven
Hosea10:9 O Israel
Hosea10:10 When I desire
Hosea10:11 Ephraim is a trained heifer that loves to thresh grain

Hosea10:14 therefore a tumult will arise among your people

Hosea11:2 As I called them

Hosea11:6 The sword slashes in their cities
Hosea11:7 My people are bent on backsliding from Me. Though they called to the Most High

Hosea11:9 I will not execute the fierceness of My anger; I will not again destroy Ephraim. For I am God

Hosea12:12 Jacob fled to the land of Aram
Hosea12:13 By a prophet the Lord brought Israel up from Egypt
Hosea12:14 Ephraim provoked Him to anger most bitterly

Hosea13:2 And now they continue sinning and have made a cast image for themselves

Hosea13:13 The pains of childbirth come for him. He is an unwise son

Joel1:1 The word of the Lord
Joel1:2 Hear this
Joel1:3 Tell it to your children
Joel1:4 What the fledging locust left
Joel1:5 Awaken
Joel1:6 For a nation powerful and innumerable has invaded my land; its teeth are like the teeth of a lion
Joel1:7 It has despoiled my vine
Joel1:8 Lament like a virgin wearing sackcloth for the husband of her youth.
Joel1:9 The grain offering and the drink offering are cut off from the house of the Lord; the priests mourn
Joel1:10 The field is ravaged
Joel1:11 Despair
Joel1:12 The vine is dried up
Joel1:13 Put on sackcloth and lament
Joel1:14 Consecrate a fast
Joel1:15 Alas
Joel1:16 Has not the food been cut off before our eyes
Joel1:17 The seeds have shriveled under their shovels
Joel1:18 How the beasts groan! The herds of cattle are confused
Joel1:19 To You
Joel1:20 Even the beasts of the field long for You
Joel2:1 Blow the ram’s horn in Zion
Joel2:2 a day of darkness and gloom
Joel2:3 Before them fire devours
Joel2:4 They have the appearance of horses
Joel2:5 As with the sound of chariots
Joel2:6 Before them
Joel2:7 Like mighty men they run
Joel2:8 They do not jostle one another; each marches in his track. Through the weapons they plunge; they do not break rank.
Joel2:9 They rush on the city
Joel2:10 Before them the earth quakes
Joel2:11 The Lord has sounded His voice before His army
Joel2:12 Yet even now
Joel2:13 Rend your heart
Joel2:14 Who knows? He might turn aside and relent
Joel2:15 Blow the ram’s horn in Zion
Joel2:16 Gather the people
Joel2:17 Between the temple porch and the altar
Joel2:18 Then the Lord became jealous for His land and took pity upon His people.
Joel2:19 So the Lord answered and said to His people
Joel2:20 I will remove the northerner far from you
Joel2:21 Do not be afraid
Joel2:22 Do not be afraid
Joel2:23 And children of Zion
Joel2:24 Then the threshing floors will be filled with grain
Joel2:25 And I will compensate you for the years the locusts have eaten— the larval locust
Joel2:26 You will eat abundantly and be satisfied
Joel2:27 Then you will know that I am in the midst of Israel
Joel2:28 And it will be that
Joel2:29 Even on the menservants and maidservants in those days I will pour out My Spirit.
Joel2:30 Then I will work wonders in the heavens and the earth— blood and fire and columns of smoke.
Joel2:31 The sun will be turned to darkness
Joel2:32 And it will be that everyone who calls on the name of the Lord will be saved. For on Mount Zion and in Jerusalem there will be deliverance
Joel3:1 In those days and at that time
Joel3:2 I will gather all the nations
Joel3:3 They have cast lots for My people
Joel3:4 Also
Joel3:5 For you have taken My silver and My gold
Joel3:6 You have sold the people of Judah and Jerusalem to the Greeks in order to remove them far from their border.
Joel3:7 Behold
Joel3:8 I will sell your sons and your daughters into the hand of the people of Judah
Joel3:9 Proclaim this among the nations: Consecrate a war! Stir up the mighty men! Let all the men of war draw near and rise.

Joel3:11 Hurry and come
Joel3:12 Let the nations be roused
Joel3:13 Swing the sickle
Joel3:14 Multitudes
Joel3:15 The sun and moon darken
Joel3:16 The Lord roars from Zion
Joel3:17 Then you will know that I am the Lord your God
Joel3:18 And it will be that in that day the mountains will drip sweet wine
Joel3:19 Egypt will become a desolation
Joel3:20 But Judah will be inhabited forever
Joel3:21 I will avenge their blood
Amos1:1 The words of Amos
Amos1:2 He said: The Lord roars from Zion and utters His voice from Jerusalem; the pastures of the shepherds languish
Amos1:3 Thus says the Lord: For three transgressions of Damascus
Amos1:4 So I will send fire against the house of Hazael
Amos1:5 I will break the gate bar of Damascus; from the Valley of Aven I will cut off the one enthroned
Amos1:6 Thus says the Lord: For three transgressions of Gaza
Amos1:7 So I will send fire against the wall of Gaza
Amos1:8 From Ashdod I will cut off the one enthroned and from Ashkelon the one who holds the scepter. I will turn My hand against Ekron
Amos1:9 Thus says the Lord: For three transgressions of Tyre
Amos1:10 So I will send fire against the wall of Tyre
Amos1:11 Thus says the Lord: For three transgressions of Edom
Amos1:12 So I will send fire against Teman
Amos1:13 Thus says the Lord: For three transgressions of the Ammonites
Amos1:14 So I will kindle a fire against the wall of Rabbah
Amos1:15 Their king shall go into captivity
Amos2:1 Thus says the Lord: For three transgressions of Moab
Amos2:2 So I will send fire against Moab
Amos2:3 I will cut off the ruler from its midst
Amos2:4 Thus says the Lord: For three transgressions of Judah
Amos2:5 So I will send fire against Judah
Amos2:6 Thus says the Lord: For three transgressions of Israel
Amos2:7 They trample the head of the poor into the dust of the earth
Amos2:8 They recline by every altar on garments taken in pledge
Amos2:9 Yet it was I who destroyed the Amorite before them
Amos2:10 It was I who brought you up from the land of Egypt
Amos2:11 I raised up some of your sons as prophets
Amos2:12 But you made the Nazirites drink wine
Amos2:13 Behold
Amos2:14 Flight will perish from the swift
Amos2:15 The bowman will not stand firm
Amos2:16 He that is courageous among the warriors will flee away naked on that day
Amos3:1 Hear this word that the Lord has spoken against you
Amos3:2 You alone have I known of all the families of the earth; therefore I will punish you for all your iniquities.
Amos3:3 Do two people walk together
Amos3:4 Does a lion roar in the forest
Amos3:5 Does a bird fall into a trap on the ground
Amos3:6 If the trumpet blasts in the city
Amos3:7 Surely the Lord God does nothing without revealing His purpose to His servants the prophets.
Amos3:8 The lion has roared; who will not fear? The Lord God has spoken; who can but prophesy?
Amos3:9 Proclaim to the fortresses in Ashdod
Amos3:10 They do not know how to do right
Amos3:11 Therefore thus says the Lord God: An enemy will surround your land; he will tear down your defenses
Amos3:12 Thus says the Lord: As the shepherd rescues out of the mouth of the lion a pair of legs or a piece of an ear
Amos3:13 Hear and testify against the house of Jacob
Amos3:14 Surely on the day I punish the transgressions of Israel
Amos3:15 I will destroy the winter house as well as the summer house
Amos4:1 Hear this word
Amos4:2 The Lord God has sworn by His holiness: Behold
Amos4:3 You will go out through breached walls
Amos4:4 Come to Bethel and transgress
Amos4:5 Burn leavened bread as a thank offering; announce your voluntary offerings loudly
Amos4:6 Though I gave you cleanness of teeth in all your cities and lack of food in all your places
Amos4:7 I also withheld the rain from you
Amos4:8 So two or three towns wandered to one town to drink water
Amos4:9 I struck you with blight and mildew. Locusts devoured your many gardens and vineyards
Amos4:10 Pestilence like that of Egypt I sent against you. By the sword I killed your young men; your horses were taken captive. The stench of your camps I brought up into your nostrils; yet you did not return to Me
Amos4:11 I destroyed some of you
Amos4:12 Therefore thus will I do to you
Amos4:13 The One who forms the mountains and creates the wind
Amos5:1 Hear this word which I take up as a dirge against you
Amos5:2 Fallen
Amos5:3 For thus says the Lord God: The city that went out with a thousand will be left with a hundred
Amos5:4 Indeed
Amos5:5 But do not seek Bethel
Amos5:6 Seek the Lord and live
Amos5:7 You who turn justice into bitterness and cast righteousness down to the ground!
Amos5:8 The One who made the Pleiades and Orion
Amos5:9 the One who flashes destruction against the strong
Amos5:10 They hate the one who prosecutes at the gate
Amos5:11 Therefore
Amos5:12 For I know your transgressions are many and your sins are grievous
Amos5:13 Therefore the prudent are silent at such a time
Amos5:14 Seek good and not evil
Amos5:15 Hate evil and love good
Amos5:16 Therefore thus says the Lord
Amos5:17 In all vineyards there shall be wailing
Amos5:18 Woe to you that desire the day of the Lord! Why do you want the day of the Lord? It is darkness
Amos5:19 as if someone fled away from a lion
Amos5:20 Will not the day of the Lord be darkness
Amos5:21 I hate
Amos5:22 Though you offer Me burnt offerings or your grain offerings
Amos5:23 Take away from Me the noise of your songs; I will not listen to the melody of your harps.
Amos5:24 But let justice roll down like water
Amos5:25 Did you bring Me sacrifices and offerings those forty years in the wilderness
Amos5:26 But you will carry away Sukuth your king and Kaiwan your star-images
Amos5:27 as I drive you away into exile beyond Damascus
Amos6:1 Woe to those who are at ease in Zion
Amos6:2 Cross over to Kalneh and see
Amos6:3 You who brush off the day of disaster
Amos6:4 who lie upon beds of ivory
Amos6:5 who sing to the sound of the harp and invent musical instruments for themselves like David;
Amos6:6 who drink from bowls of wine and anoint themselves with the finest oils
Amos6:7 Therefore now they will go at the head of the captives into exile
Amos6:8 The Lord God hath sworn by Himself
Amos6:9 If there remain ten people in one house
Amos6:10 And when a relative or one who prepares the bodies picks them up to carry them out of the house
Amos6:11 But indeed the Lord gives a command
Amos6:12 Can horses run on a rocky crag? Can one plow the sea with oxen? But you have turned justice into poison
Amos6:13 you who rejoice in Lo Debar
Amos6:14 Watch: I will raise up against you a nation
Amos7:1 This is what the Lord God showed me: He was forming a plague of locusts when the latter growth was beginning to sprout up
Amos7:2 When they had finished devouring the foliage of the land
Amos7:3 The Lord relented concerning this: “It shall not be
Amos7:4 This is what the Lord God showed me: The Lord God was calling for a judgment by fire. It was consuming the great deep and was devouring the fields.
Amos7:5 I said
Amos7:6 The Lord relented concerning this: “This also shall not be
Amos7:7 This is what He showed me: The Lord was standing by a wall made using a plumb line
Amos7:8 The Lord said to me
Amos7:9 The high places of Isaac will be destroyed
Amos7:10 Then Amaziah the priest of Bethel sent a message to Jeroboam king of Israel
Amos7:11 For this is what Amos said: ‘Jeroboam will die by the sword
Amos7:12 So Amaziah said to Amos
Amos7:13 But do not prophesy any more at Bethel
Amos7:14 But Amos answered Amaziah: “I am no prophet
Amos7:15 But the Lord took me away from the flock
Amos7:16 Now therefore hear the word of the Lord: You say
Amos7:17 “Therefore thus says the Lord: ‘Your wife will be a prostitute in the city
Amos8:1 This is what the Lord God showed me: A basket of summer fruit.
Amos8:2 He said
Amos8:3 “The songs of the temple shall become wailings on that day
Amos8:4 Hear this
Amos8:5 saying
Amos8:6 that we may buy the poor for silver
Amos8:7 The Lord has sworn by the pride of Jacob: Surely I will never forget any of their deeds.
Amos8:8 Will not the land tremble because of this
Amos8:9 On that day
Amos8:10 I will turn your feasts into mourning
Amos8:11 The time is coming
Amos8:12 They will wander from sea to sea
Amos8:13 On that day the beautiful maidens and the young men will faint with thirst.
Amos8:14 Those who swear by Ashimah of Samaria
Amos9:1 I saw the Lord standing upon the altar
Amos9:2 Though they dig down to Sheol
Amos9:3 though they hide on the top of Carmel
Amos9:4 and though they go into captivity before their enemies
Amos9:5 The Lord God of Hosts
Amos9:6 who builds His chambers in the heavens
Amos9:7 Are you not like the Cushites to Me
Amos9:8 The eyes of the Lord God are upon the sinful kingdom
Amos9:9 Behold
Amos9:10 All the sinners of My people will die by the sword
Amos9:11 On that day will I raise up the hut of David that is fallen; I will close up its breached walls
Amos9:12 that they may possess the remnant of Edom
Amos9:13 Indeed
Amos9:14 I will restore the fortunes of My people Israel; they will rebuild the ruined cities and inhabit them; they will plant vineyards and drink their wine; they will make gardens and eat their fruit.
Amos9:15 I will plant them upon their land
Obadiah1:1 The vision of Obadiah. Thus says the Lord God concerning Edom (We have heard a report from the Lord and a messenger has been sent among the nations
Obadiah1:2 Behold
Obadiah1:3 The pride of your heart has deceived you
Obadiah1:4 Though you ascend high like the eagle
Obadiah1:5 If thieves came to you
Obadiah1:6 How Esau will be searched for as his hidden treasures are sought after!
Obadiah1:7 All your confederates have driven you to the border; your allies have deceived and prevailed against you. Those who eat your bread have set a trap for you. You will not detect it.
Obadiah1:8 On that day
Obadiah1:9 Your mighty men shall be shattered
Obadiah1:10 For the slaughter and the violence done to your brother Jacob
Obadiah1:11 On the day that you stood aside
Obadiah1:12 But you should not have gloated on the day of your brother
Obadiah1:13 You should not have entered the gate of My people on the day of their calamity. You should not have gloated over the disaster of Judah on the day of his calamity; you should not have seized his wealth on the day of his calamity.
Obadiah1:14 You should not have stood at the crossroads to cut off his fugitives; you should not have handed over his survivors
Obadiah1:15 For the day of the Lord is near upon all the nations; as you have done
Obadiah1:16 For as you have drunk on My holy mountain
Obadiah1:17 But on Mount Zion there shall be deliverance
Obadiah1:18 The house of Jacob shall be a fire
Obadiah1:19 Those of the Negev shall possess Mount Esau
Obadiah1:20 The exiles of this army of the Israelites
Obadiah1:21 Saviors shall go up to Mount Zion to rule Mount Esau
Jonah1:1 Now the word of the Lord came to Jonah son of Amittai
Jonah1:2 “Get up
Jonah1:3 But Jonah got up to flee to Tarshish from the presence of the Lord. He went down to Joppa and found there a ship going to Tarshish. He paid its fare and went down into it to go with them to Tarshish from the presence of the Lord.
Jonah1:4 But the Lord hurled a great wind upon the sea
Jonah1:5 Then the sailors were afraid
Jonah1:6 The captain came to him and said
Jonah1:7 The sailors said to one another
Jonah1:8 Then the sailors said to Jonah
Jonah1:9 Jonah replied
Jonah1:10 Then the men were very afraid and said to him
Jonah1:11 Then they said to Jonah
Jonah1:12 So Jonah said to them
Jonah1:13 Nevertheless
Jonah1:14 Then they cried to the Lord and said
Jonah1:15 So they picked up Jonah and tossed him into the sea. Then the sea ceased from its raging.
Jonah1:16 Therefore the men were very afraid of the Lord
Jonah1:17 Now the Lord appointed a great fish to swallow Jonah. And Jonah was in the belly of the fish three days and three nights.
Jonah2:1 Then Jonah prayed to the Lord his God from the belly of the fish.
Jonah2:2 He said: “I called to the Lord out of my distress
Jonah2:3 You cast me into the deep
Jonah2:4 Then I said
Jonah2:5 The waters encompassed me; even to my soul the deep surrounded me; weeds were wrapped around my head.
Jonah2:6 I went down to the foundations of the mountains; the earth with its bars was around me forever; yet You have brought up my life from the pit
Jonah2:7 “When my life was ebbing away
Jonah2:8 “Those who follow vain idols forsake their true loyalty.
Jonah2:9 But I will sacrifice to You with the voice of thanksgiving; I will pay what I have vowed. Salvation is of the Lord!”
Jonah2:10 Then the Lord spoke to the fish
Jonah3:1 The word of the Lord came to Jonah a second time
Jonah3:2 “Get up
Jonah3:3 So Jonah got up and went to Nineveh
Jonah3:4 Jonah began to enter the city
Jonah3:5 So the people of Nineveh believed God
Jonah3:6 When the news reached the king of Nineveh
Jonah3:7 Then he made a proclamation in Nineveh: “By decree of the king and his nobles: No man or animal
Jonah3:8 Both man and animals shall cover themselves with sackcloth and cry mightily to God. All shall turn from their evil ways and from the violence that is in their hands.
Jonah3:9 Who knows? God may relent and change His mind. He may turn from His fierce anger
Jonah3:10 When God saw their actions
Jonah4:1 Now this greatly displeased Jonah
Jonah4:2 He prayed to the Lord and said
Jonah4:3 Therefore
Jonah4:4 Then the Lord said
Jonah4:5 So Jonah went out of the city and sat down east of the city and made for himself a booth there. He sat under it in the shade

Jonah4:7 But at dawn the next day
Jonah4:8 When the sun rose
Jonah4:9 Then God said to Jonah
Jonah4:10 The Lord said
Jonah4:11 Should I not
Micah1:1 The word of the Lord
Micah1:2 Hear
Micah1:3 Behold
Micah1:4 The mountains will melt beneath Him
Micah1:5 All this because of the transgression of Jacob and because of the sins of the house of Israel. What is the transgression of Jacob? Is it not Samaria? And what is the high place of Judah? Is it not Jerusalem?
Micah1:6 Therefore I will make Samaria a heap in the field for the planting of vineyards. I will dump her stones into the valley and expose her foundations.
Micah1:7 All her idols will be shattered
Micah1:8 Because of this I will lament and wail
Micah1:9 For her wound is mortal
Micah1:10 Do not tell it in Gath
Micah1:11 Pass on your way
Micah1:12 Indeed the inhabitants of Maroth wait anxiously for good
Micah1:13 Harness the chariot to the steeds
Micah1:14 Therefore you will give parting gifts to Moresheth Gath; the houses of Akzib will be deception to the kings of Israel.
Micah1:15 I will again bring a conqueror to you
Micah1:16 Make yourself bald and shave your head for the children of your delight; make yourself as bald as the eagle

Micah2:2 They covet fields and seize them
Micah2:3 Therefore
Micah2:4 In that day they will take up a taunt against you
Micah2:5 Therefore you will not have anyone to apportion the land by lot in the assembly of the Lord.

Micah2:7 Should it be said
Micah2:8 But lately My people rise up like an enemy. You strip off the rich robe from those who pass by trustingly
Micah2:9 The women of My people you drive out from their delightful homes; from their children you take My adornment forever.
Micah2:10 Get up and go
Micah2:11 If a man
Micah2:12 I will indeed assemble Jacob—all of you; I will indeed gather the remnant of Israel. I will place them together like sheep in a fold
Micah2:13 He who breaks through has gone up before them; they will break through and pass the gate and go out by it. Then their king will pass on before them

Micah3:2 You who hate good and love evil

Micah3:8 But as for me
Micah3:9 Please hear this
Micah3:10 who build Zion with bloodguilt and Jerusalem with wickedness.

Micah3:12 Therefore

Micah4:5 For all the peoples walk
Micah4:6 In that day
Micah4:7 and I will make the lame into a remnant

Micah4:9 Now why do you cry loudly? Have you no king? Has your counselor perished

Micah4:11 But now many nations are gathered against you

Micah7:20 You will give faithfulness to Jacob and benevolence to Abraham
Nahum1:1 An oracle for Nineveh
Nahum1:2 The Lord is a jealous and avenging God; the Lord avenges and is furious. The Lord takes vengeance on His enemies
Nahum1:3 the Lord is slow to anger and great in power
Nahum1:4 He rebukes and dries up the sea
Nahum1:5 The mountains quake before Him
Nahum1:6 Who can stand before His anger? Who will rise up before His burning wrath? His heat is poured out like fire
Nahum1:7 The Lord is good
Nahum1:8 As a flood running forth
Nahum1:9 Why do you all scheme against the Lord? He will bring it to an end. It will not rise up a second time.
Nahum1:10 Because they are like interwoven thorns and as drunkards imbibing
Nahum1:11 Out of you
Nahum1:12 So the Lord says: “Even though they are full and many
Nahum1:13 now I will break apart his yoke from over you
Nahum1:14 The Lord has given a command concerning you: “No longer will your name go forth. I will cut off the carved image and metal image from the house of your gods. I will prepare your grave
Nahum1:15 Behold
Nahum2:1 He who scatters has come to you. Guard the fortifications! Watch the road! Prepare yourself
Nahum2:2 For the Lord is restoring the prominence of Jacob
Nahum2:3 The shields of his mighty men are soaked red
Nahum2:4 The chariots run wildly through the streets
Nahum2:5 He remembers his officers as they stumble about on the road; they hurry on to the wall as the siege tower is set up.
Nahum2:6 The holding gates are opened wide
Nahum2:7 It is decreed: She is uncovered and led away captive; her handmaidens shall lead her as with the voice of doves
Nahum2:8 Nineveh is like a pool whose waters run away. “Halt! Halt!” they cry
Nahum2:9 “Plunder the silver! Plunder the gold! There is no limit to the treasure
Nahum2:10 She is desolate
Nahum2:11 Where is the den of the lions
Nahum2:12 The lion tore enough food for his cubs
Nahum2:13 I am against you
Nahum3:1 Woe to the bloody city! It is full of lies and plunder. The prey never departs.
Nahum3:2 The noise of the whip and the noise of the rattling of the wheels
Nahum3:3 Horsemen charging with flashing sword and glittering spear. Multitude of slain
Nahum3:4 because of the countless harlotries of the seductive harlot
Nahum3:5 I am against you
Nahum3:6 I will throw filth on you
Nahum3:7 All who look at you will flee from you
Nahum3:8 Are you better than Thebes that sat by the Nile
Nahum3:9 Cush and Egypt were her strength
Nahum3:10 Yet she went into exile
Nahum3:11 You also will be drunk; you will go into hiding; you will seek refuge from the enemy.
Nahum3:12 All your fortresses are like fig trees with first-ripe figs: If they are shaken
Nahum3:13 Your troops are women in your midst! The gates of your land are wide open to your enemies; fire has devoured your bars.
Nahum3:14 Draw water for the siege! Strengthen your forts! Go into the clay and tread the mortar! Take hold of the brick mold!
Nahum3:15 There the fire will devour you
Nahum3:16 You have multiplied your merchants more than the stars of heaven. The locust plunders and flies away.
Nahum3:17 Your leaders are like grasshoppers
Nahum3:18 Your shepherds slumber
Nahum3:19 There is no healing of your injury
Habakkuk1:1 The oracle that Habakkuk the prophet saw.
Habakkuk1:2 O  Lord
Habakkuk1:3 Why do You make me see wickedness
Habakkuk1:4 Therefore the law is powerless
Habakkuk1:5 Look among the nations
Habakkuk1:6 For I am raising up the Chaldeans
Habakkuk1:7 They are terrible and dreadful; their justice and their dignity proceed from themselves.
Habakkuk1:8 Their horses are swifter than leopards
Habakkuk1:9 They all come for violence; their forces advance like the east wind. They gather captives like sand.
Habakkuk1:10 They scoff at kings
Habakkuk1:11 Then their mind changes
Habakkuk1:12 Are You not from everlasting
Habakkuk1:13 Your eyes are too pure to look on evil
Habakkuk1:14 You make men like fish of the sea
Habakkuk1:15 They bring all of them up with a hook
Habakkuk1:16 Therefore they sacrifice to their net and burn incense to their dragnet; for by them their portion is extravagant
Habakkuk1:17 Shall they continue to empty their net
Habakkuk2:1 I will stand at my watch and station myself on the watchtower; and I will keep watch to see what He will say to me
Habakkuk2:2 And the Lord answered me: Write the vision
Habakkuk2:3 For the vision is yet for an appointed time; but it speaks of the end
Habakkuk2:4 Behold
Habakkuk2:5 Indeed
Habakkuk2:6 Shall not all these take up a taunt against him
Habakkuk2:7 Shall not your debtors rise up suddenly
Habakkuk2:8 Because you have plundered many nations
Habakkuk2:9 “Woe to him who gets evil gain for his house
Habakkuk2:10 You have given shameful counsel to your house by cutting off many peoples
Habakkuk2:11 For the stone will cry out from the wall
Habakkuk2:12 “Woe to him who builds a town with bloodshed and establishes a city on iniquity!”
Habakkuk2:13 Is it not from the Lord of Hosts that the people labor to feed fire
Habakkuk2:14 For the earth will be filled with the knowledge of the glory of the Lord
Habakkuk2:15 “Woe to him who makes his neighbor drink
Habakkuk2:16 You will be filled with shame instead of glory. You yourself—drink and show your uncircumcision! The cup of the Lord ’s right hand will be turned against you
Habakkuk2:17 The violence done to Lebanon will cover you
Habakkuk2:18 What profit is a carved image when its maker has carved it
Habakkuk2:19 Woe to him who says to the wood
Habakkuk2:20 But the Lord is in His holy temple. Let all the earth keep silence before Him.
Habakkuk3:1 A prayer of Habakkuk the prophet
Habakkuk3:2 O  Lord
Habakkuk3:3 God came from Teman
Habakkuk3:4 His brightness was like the light; rays flashed from His hand
Habakkuk3:5 Pestilence went out before Him
Habakkuk3:6 He stood and measured the earth; He looked and shook the nations. The eternal mountains were scattered
Habakkuk3:7 I saw the tents of Cushan in affliction; the curtains of the land of Midian trembled.
Habakkuk3:8 Were you displeased with the rivers
Habakkuk3:9 You made bare Your bow; oaths were the arrows at Your command.    Selah You divided the earth with the rivers.
Habakkuk3:10 The mountains saw You and trembled; the overflowing water passed by. The deep lifted its voice
Habakkuk3:11 The sun and moon stood still in their places; at the light of Your arrows they went
Habakkuk3:12 You marched throughout the earth in indignation; You threshed the nations in anger.
Habakkuk3:13 You went forth to deliver Your people
Habakkuk3:14 You pierced with his own arrows the leaders of his villages. They came like a whirlwind to scatter me; their rejoicing was like devouring the poor in secret.
Habakkuk3:15 You trampled the sea with Your horses
Habakkuk3:16 I heard
Habakkuk3:17 Though the fig tree does not blossom
Habakkuk3:18 yet I will rejoice in the Lord; I will exult in the God of my salvation.
Habakkuk3:19 The Lord God is my strength; He will make my feet like hinds’ feet

Zephaniah1:17 I will bring distress on men
Zephaniah1:18 Neither their silver nor their gold will be able to deliver them on the day of the Lord ’s wrath. But the whole earth will be consumed by the fire of His jealousy

Zephaniah3:2 She obeys no voice
Zephaniah3:3 Her princes within her are roaring lions; her judges are evening wolves who leave nothing until the morning.

Haggai1:1 In the second year of King Darius
Haggai1:2 Thus says the Lord of Hosts: These people say
Haggai1:3 Then the word of the Lord came by Haggai the prophet
Haggai1:4 Is it time for you yourselves to live in paneled houses
Haggai1:5 Now
Haggai1:6 You have sown much
Haggai1:7 Thus says the Lord of Hosts: Consider your ways.
Haggai1:8 Go up to the mountain and bring wood and rebuild the house
Haggai1:9 You looked for much
Haggai1:10 Therefore the heavens above you have withheld the dew
Haggai1:11 I called for a drought on the land and the mountains
Haggai1:12 Then Zerubbabel the son of Shealtiel
Haggai1:13 Then Haggai
Haggai1:14 And the Lord stirred up the spirit of Zerubbabel the son of Shealtiel
Haggai1:15 on the twenty-fourth day of the sixth month
Haggai2:1 In the seventh month
Haggai2:2 Speak now to Zerubbabel the son of Shealtiel
Haggai2:3 Who is left among you who saw this house in its former glory? How do you see it now? Is it not
Haggai2:4 Yet now be strong
Haggai2:5 According to the covenant that I made with you when you came out of Egypt
Haggai2:6 For thus says the Lord of Hosts: Once more
Haggai2:7 And I will shake all the nations
Haggai2:8 The silver is Mine
Haggai2:9 The glory of this latter house will be greater than the former
Haggai2:10 On the twenty-fourth day of the ninth month
Haggai2:11 Thus says the Lord of Hosts: Now ask the priests concerning the law
Haggai2:12 If a man carries holy meat in the fold of his garment
Haggai2:13 Then Haggai said

Zechariah14:21 And every pot in Jerusalem and Judah will be holy to the Lord of Hosts so that all who come to sacrifice will take from those pots and boil the meat in them. And on that day there will no longer be a Canaanite in the house of the Lord of Hosts.

Matthew1:9 Uzziah was the father of Jotham

Matthew1:16 And Jacob was the father of Joseph

Mark1:1 The beginning of the gospel of Jesus Christ
Mark1:2 As it is written in the Prophets: “Behold
Mark1:3 “The voice of one crying in the wilderness: ‘Prepare the way of the Lord
Mark1:4 John came baptizing in the wilderness and preaching a baptism of repentance for the remission of sins.
Mark1:5 The whole region of Judea and all the people of Jerusalem went out to him and were all baptized by him in the Jordan River
Mark1:6 John was clothed with camel’s hair and with a leather belt around his waist. And he ate locusts and wild honey.
Mark1:7 He preached saying
Mark1:8 I indeed have baptized you with water
Mark1:9 In those days Jesus came from Nazareth in Galilee and was baptized by John in the Jordan.
Mark1:10 Coming up out of the water
Mark1:11 And a voice came from heaven
Mark1:12 The Spirit immediately drove Him into the wilderness.
Mark1:13 And He was there in the wilderness for forty days
Mark1:14 After John was put in prison
Mark1:15 saying

Mark1:17 Jesus said to them
Mark1:18 Immediately they left their nets and followed Him.
Mark1:19 When He had gone a little farther from there

Mark1:21 They went to Capernaum
Mark1:22 They were astonished at His teaching
Mark1:23 In their synagogue there was a man with an unclean spirit.
Mark1:24 And he cried out
Mark1:25 Jesus rebuked him
Mark1:26 When the unclean spirit had convulsed him and cried out with a loud voice
Mark1:27 They were all amazed
Mark1:28 Immediately His fame spread everywhere throughout the region surrounding Galilee.

Mark1:30 The mother of Simon’s wife lay sick with a fever
Mark1:31 So He came and took her by the hand and lifted her up
Mark1:32 In the evening
Mark1:33 The whole city was gathered at the door
Mark1:34 and He healed many who were sick with various diseases and cast out many demons. And He did not let the demons speak

Mark1:36 Simon and those who were with Him followed Him
Mark1:37 and when they found Him
Mark1:38 He said to them

Mark1:41 Then Jesus

Mark1:43 He sternly warned him
Mark1:44 saying

Mark4:1 Again He began to teach by the seaside. A large crowd was gathered before Him
Mark4:2 He taught them many things in parables and said to them in His teaching:
Mark4:3 “Listen! Behold
Mark4:4 As he sowed
Mark4:5 Some seed fell on rocky ground
Mark4:6 But when the sun rose
Mark4:7 Other seed fell among thorns
Mark4:8 And other seed fell on good ground
Mark4:9 Then He said to them
Mark4:10 When He was alone
Mark4:11 He said to them
Mark4:12 so that ‘seeing they may see
Mark4:13 Then He said to them
Mark4:14 The sower sows the word.
Mark4:15 These are those beside the path
Mark4:16 Others
Mark4:17 but have no root in themselves
Mark4:18 And others are seed sown among thorns
Mark4:19 But the cares of this age
Mark4:20 Still others are seed sown on good ground
Mark4:21 He said to them
Mark4:22 For there is nothing hidden except to be revealed; neither is anything kept secret except to be proclaimed.
Mark4:23 If anyone has ears to hear
Mark4:24 He said to them
Mark4:25 For to him who has will more be given. And from him who has not will be taken
Mark4:26 He said
Mark4:27 He sleeps and rises night and day
Mark4:28 For the earth bears fruit by itself: first the blade
Mark4:29 But when the grain is ripe
Mark4:30 He said
Mark4:31 It is like a grain of mustard seed which
Mark4:32 Yet when it is sown
Mark4:33 With many such parables He spoke the word to them as they were able to hear it.
Mark4:34 Without a parable He did not speak to them. But when they were alone
Mark4:35 That same day
Mark4:36 When they had sent the crowd away
Mark4:37 A great windstorm arose
Mark4:38 He was in the stern asleep on a pillow. They woke Him and said
Mark4:39 He rose and rebuked the wind
Mark4:40 He said to them
Mark4:41 They feared greatly and said to one another
Mark5:1 They went to the other side of the sea to the region of the Gadarenes.
Mark5:2 When He had come out of the boat
Mark5:3 He lived among the tombs. And no one could constrain him
Mark5:4 because he had often been bound with shackles and chains. But he had pulled the chains apart and broken the shackles to pieces. And no one could subdue him.
Mark5:5 Always
Mark5:6 But when he saw Jesus afar off
Mark5:7 and cried out with a loud voice
Mark5:8 For Jesus said to him

Luke2:2 This taxation was first made when Quirinius was governor of Syria.
Luke2:3 And everyone went to his own city to be taxed.

Romans1:1 Paul
Romans1:2 which He promised beforehand through His prophets in the Holy Scriptures
Romans1:3 concerning His Son

Romans1:7 To all who are in Rome
Romans1:8 First

Romans1:13 Now I would not have you unaware

Romans1:15 So
Romans1:16 For I am not ashamed of the gospel of Christ. For it is the power of God for salvation to everyone who believes
Romans1:17 For in it the righteousness of God is revealed from faith to faith. As it is written
Romans1:18 The wrath of God is revealed from heaven against all ungodliness and unrighteousness of men
Romans1:19 For what may be known of God has been revealed in them
Romans1:20 The invisible things about Him—His eternal power and deity —have been clearly seen since the creation of the world and are understood by the things that are made
Romans1:21 Because
Romans1:22 Claiming to be wise
Romans1:23 They changed the glory of the incorruptible God into an image made like corruptible man

1Corinthians4:1 Let a man so regard us as the ministers of Christ and stewards of the mysteries of God.
1Corinthians4:2 Moreover it is required in stewards that a man be found faithful.
1Corinthians4:3 But with me it is a very small thing that I should be judged by you or by man’s judgment. I do not even judge myself.
1Corinthians4:4 For I know nothing against myself. Yet I am not justified by this. But He who judges me is the Lord.
1Corinthians4:5 Therefore judge nothing before the appointed time until the Lord comes. He will bring to light the hidden things of darkness and will reveal the purposes of the hearts. Then everyone will have commendation from God.
1Corinthians4:6 Brothers

1Corinthians4:8 Now you are full! Now you are rich! You have begun reigning as kings without us! And I would that you did reign
1Corinthians4:9 For I think that God has exhibited us

1Corinthians4:11 Even to this present hour we both hunger and thirst
1Corinthians4:12 We labor

1Corinthians6:1 Dare any of you

1Corinthians7:2 Nevertheless

1Corinthians7:6 I speak this as a concession and not as a command.
1Corinthians7:7 For I wish that all men were even as I myself. But every man has his proper gift from God
1Corinthians7:8 I say to the unmarried and widows that it is good for them if they live even as I am.
1Corinthians7:9 But if they cannot restrain themselves
1Corinthians7:10 Now to the married I command

1Corinthians7:19 Circumcision is nothing
1Corinthians7:20 Let each man remain in the same condition in which he was called.
1Corinthians7:21 Were you called while a servant? Do not worry about it. But if you may become free
1Corinthians7:22 For he who is called in the Lord while a servant is the Lord’s freeman. Likewise
1Corinthians7:23 You were bought at a price. Do not be the servants of men.
1Corinthians7:24 Brothers
1Corinthians7:25 Now concerning those not having married
1Corinthians7:26 I suppose therefore that this is good because of the present distress
1Corinthians7:27 Are you committed to a wife? Do not seek to be uncommitted. Are you free from a wife? Do not seek a wife.
1Corinthians7:28 But if you marry
1Corinthians7:29 But this I say

1Corinthians7:31 and those who use this world
1Corinthians7:32 But I prefer that you have no concern. He who is unmarried cares for the things of the Lord
1Corinthians7:33 But he who is married cares for the things of the world
1Corinthians7:34 There is a difference between a wife and a virgin. The unmarried woman cares for the things of the Lord
1Corinthians7:35 I say this for your own benefit
1Corinthians7:36 If any man thinks that he is behaving improperly toward his unmarried woman
1Corinthians7:37 Nevertheless he who stands steadfast in his heart without necessity
1Corinthians7:38 So then he who gives her in marriage does well
1Corinthians7:39 The wife is bound by the law as long as her husband lives. But if her husband dies
1Corinthians7:40 But in my judgment she is happier if she so remains as she is. And I think that I have the Spirit of God.

1Corinthians15:2 Through it you are saved

1Corinthians15:8 Last of all
1Corinthians15:9 For I am the least of the apostles and am not fit to be called an apostle

1Corinthians15:13 If there is no resurrection of the dead
1Corinthians15:14 If Christ has not risen

1Corinthians15:16 For if the dead do not rise

1Corinthians15:23 But every man in his own order: Christ the firstfruits; afterward
1Corinthians15:24 Then comes the end when He will deliver up the kingdom to God the Father
1Corinthians15:25 For He will reign until He has put all enemies under His feet.

1Corinthians15:27 For He “has put all things under His feet.” But when He says
1Corinthians15:28 When all things are subjected to Him
1Corinthians15:29 Otherwise

1Corinthians15:32 If

1Corinthians15:37 When you sow
1Corinthians15:38 Then God gives it a body as He pleases
1Corinthians15:39 All flesh is not the same flesh. There is one kind of flesh of men
1Corinthians15:40 There are also celestial bodies and terrestrial bodies. The glory of the celestial is one
1Corinthians15:41 There is one glory of the sun
1Corinthians15:42 So also is the resurrection of the dead. The body that is sown is perishable; it is raised imperishable.
1Corinthians15:43 It is sown in dishonor; it is raised in glory. It is sown in weakness; it is raised in power.
1Corinthians15:44 It is sown a natural body; it is raised a spiritual body. There is a natural body
1Corinthians15:45 So it is written
1Corinthians15:46 However

1Corinthians15:51 Behold

2Thessalonians2:9 even him
2Thessalonians2:10 and with all deception of unrighteousness among those who perish

Hebrews1:4 He was made so much better than the angels as He has inherited a more excellent name than they.
Hebrews1:5 For to which of the angels did He at any time say: “You are My Son; today I have become Your Father”? Or again
Hebrews1:6 And again
Hebrews1:7 Of the angels He says: “He makes His angels spirits

Hebrews4:2 For the gospel was preached to us as well as to them. But the word preached did not benefit them

Hebrews8:2 a minister in the sanctuary and the true tabernacle
Hebrews8:3 For every high priest is appointed to offer gifts and sacrifices. Therefore it is necessary that this priest also have something to offer.
Hebrews8:4 For if He were on earth
Hebrews8:5 They serve in a sanctuary that is an example and shadow of the heavenly one
Hebrews8:6 But now He has obtained a more excellent ministry
Hebrews8:7 For if that first covenant had been faultless
Hebrews8:8 For finding fault with them

Hebrews8:10 This is the covenant that I will make with the house of Israel after those days
Hebrews8:11 No longer shall every man teach his neighbor
Hebrews8:12 For I will be merciful toward their unrighteousness
Hebrews8:13 In speaking of a new covenant He has made the first one old. Now that which is decaying and growing old is ready to vanish away.
Hebrews9:1 Then indeed
Hebrews9:2 A tabernacle was made. In the first outer part of the tabernacle
Hebrews9:3 Behind the second veil was the second inner part of the tabernacle called the Most Holy Place
Hebrews9:4 which contained the golden censer and the ark of the covenant overlaid with gold
Hebrews9:5 Above the ark were the cherubim of glory overshadowing the mercy seat. Concerning these things we cannot now speak in detail.
Hebrews9:6 Now when these things were thus ordained
Hebrews9:7 But only the high priest went into the second part once a year
Hebrews9:8 The Holy Spirit was signifying through this that the way into the Most Holy Place was not yet revealed
Hebrews9:9 This is an illustration for the present time
Hebrews9:10 since they are concerned only with foods and drinks
Hebrews9:11 But Christ
Hebrews9:12 neither by the blood of goats and calves
Hebrews9:13 For if the blood of bulls and goats
Hebrews9:14 how much more shall the blood of Christ
Hebrews9:15 For this reason He is the Mediator of a new covenant
Hebrews9:16 For where there is a will
Hebrews9:17 For a will has force after men are dead
Hebrews9:18 So not even the first covenant was inaugurated without blood.
Hebrews9:19 For when Moses had taught every precept to all the people according to the law
Hebrews9:20 saying
Hebrews9:21 Likewise he sprinkled both the tabernacle and all the vessels of worship with blood.
Hebrews9:22 And according to the law almost everything must be cleansed with blood; without the shedding of blood there is no forgiveness.
Hebrews9:23 It was therefore necessary that the replicas of heavenly things be cleansed with these sacrifices
Hebrews9:24 For Christ did not enter holy places made with hands

Hebrews11:1 Now faith is the substance of things hoped for
Hebrews11:2 For by it the men of old obtained a good report.
Hebrews11:3 By faith we understand that the ages were set by the word of God
Hebrews11:4 By faith Abel offered to God a more excellent sacrifice than Cain offered. Through this he was approved as righteous
Hebrews11:5 By faith Enoch was taken to heaven so that he would not see death. He was not found
Hebrews11:6 And without faith it is impossible to please God
Hebrews11:7 By faith Noah
Hebrews11:8 By faith Abraham obeyed when he was called to go out into a place which he would later receive as an inheritance. He went out not knowing where he was going.
Hebrews11:9 By faith he dwelt in the promised land
Hebrews11:10 for he was looking for a city which has foundations
Hebrews11:11 By faith Sarah herself also received the ability to conceive seed
Hebrews11:12 Therefore from one man
Hebrews11:13 These all died in faith not having received the promises

Hebrews11:23 By faith Moses
Hebrews11:24 By faith Moses
Hebrews11:25 choosing rather to suffer affliction with the people of God than to enjoy the pleasures of sin for a time.

Hebrews11:28 By faith he kept the Passover and the sprinkling of blood
Hebrews11:29 By faith they passed through the Red Sea as on dry land
Hebrews11:30 By faith the walls of Jericho fell down after they were encircled for seven days.
Hebrews11:31 By faith the prostitute Rahab

Hebrews11:40 For God provided something better for us

1John4:1 Beloved
1John4:2 This is how you know the Spirit of God: Every spirit that confesses that Jesus Christ has come in the body is from God

1John4:10 In this is love: not that we loved God
1John4:11 Beloved

1John4:13 We know that we live in Him
1John4:14 And we have seen and testify that the Father sent the Son to be the Savior of the world.

1John4:16 And we have come to know and to believe the love that God has for us. God is love. Whoever lives in love lives in God

1John4:18 There is no fear in love
1John4:19 We love Him because He first loved us.
1John4:20 If anyone says
1John4:21 We have this commandment from Him: Whoever loves God must also love his brother.

1John5:2 By this we know that we love the children of God: when we love God and keep His commandments.
1John5:3 For this is the love of God
1John5:4 for whoever is born of God overcomes the world
1John5:5 Who is it that overcomes the world
1John5:6 This is He who came by water and blood—Jesus Christ. He did not come by water only
1John5:7 There are three who testify in heaven: the Father
1John5:8 There are three that testify on earth: the Spirit
1John5:9 If we receive the testimony of men
1John5:10 Whoever believes in the Son of God has this witness in himself. Whoever does not believe God has made Him out to be a liar
1John5:11 And this is the testimony: that God has given us eternal life
1John5:12 Whoever has the Son has life
1John5:13 I have written these things to you who believe in the name of the Son of God
1John5:14 This is the confidence that we have in Him
1John5:15 So if we know that He hears whatever we ask
1John5:16 If anyone sees his brother commit a sin which does not lead to death
1John5:17 All wrongdoing is sin
1John5:18 We know that whoever is born of God does not keep on sinning. But whoever has been born of God guards himself
1John5:19 We know that we are of God
1John5:20 And we know that the Son of God has come and has given us understanding
1John5:21 Little children

Revelation19:3 Again they said: “Alleluia! Her smoke rises forever and ever.”

Revelation19:5 Then a voice came from the throne
Revelation19:6 Then I heard something like the sound like a great multitude
Revelation19:7 Let us be glad and rejoice and give Him glory

Revelation19:10 I fell at his feet to worship him. But he said to me
Revelation19:11 I saw heaven opened. And behold
Revelation19:12 His eyes are like a flame of fire
Revelation19:13 He is clothed with a robe dipped in blood. His name is called The Word of God.

Revelation19:15 Out of His mouth proceeds a sharp sword
Revelation19:16 On His robe and on His thigh He has a name written: king of kings***and lord of lords***.

Revelation19:18 to eat the flesh of kings
Revelation19:19 Then I saw the beast and the kings of the earth with their armies gathered to wage war against Him who sat on the horse and against His army.
Revelation19:20 But the beast was captured and with him the false prophet who worked signs in his presence
Revelation19:21 The remnant were slain with the sword which proceeded out of the mouth of Him who sat on the horse. And all the birds gorged themselves with their flesh.

Revelation20:6 Blessed and holy is he who takes part in the first resurrection. Over these the second death has no power
Revelation20:7 When the thousand years are ended
Revelation20:8 and will go out to deceive the nations which are in the four corners of the earth

Revelation20:10 The devil
Revelation20:11 Then I saw a great white throne and Him who was seated on it. From His face the earth and the heavens fled away
Revelation20:12 And I saw the dead
Revelation20:13 The sea gave up the dead who were in it
Revelation20:14 Then Death and Hades were cast into the lake of fire. This is the second death.
Revelation20:15 Anyone whose name was not found written in the Book of Life was cast into the lake of fire.
